title: Aal auf französische Art (Eel in the French Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- butter
- thick sour cream
- capers
- lemon

## Directions

1. Skin the eel and clean it out
2. Keep it whole
3. Fry it in the oven in a frying pan with a piece of butter
4. Pour thick sour cream over it
5. Add a tablespoon of capers and half a lemon
6. Finish frying the eel so that it develops a sauce from the cream and capers

title: Aal Blau (Boiled Eel)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g eel
- 10 g salt
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1/2 l water
- 30 g onion slices
- 1 spice measure
- 1 tablespoon soup greens
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Divide the killed eel into uniform pieces of desired length. Clean each piece individually by pushing out the entrails with a whisk handle and cleaning it from the inner skin, rub with salt and wash.
2. Pour boiling water mixed with 1/2 tablespoon vinegar over the fish and cook it in a broth of water, soup greens, spices, onion and some vinegar for a good 1/4 hour until done. Arrange the blue eel pieces warm or cold and serve with the eel broth.

title: Aal im Bier (Eel in Beer)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g eel
- 3/8 liter white beer
- 1 spice dose
- 8 g salt
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 2 teaspoons flour
- 4 teaspoons potato flour
- 3 tablespoons water
- 4 onion slices

## Directions

1. Cut the eel into pieces
2. Clean each piece and wash several times
3. Pack it with spices, onion slices and salt in an earthen pot
4. Pour white beer over it and cook covered, depending on thickness, for approximately 20 minutes until soft
5. Skim it often and thicken the sauce with a small piece of butter stirred into flour
6. Arrange the eel pieces in a deep bowl
7. After tasting the sauce, quickly cook it with diluted potato flour
8. Pour it over the fish pieces and serve the remaining sauce on the side

title: Aal in Bruehe (Eel in Broth)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 g onion slices
- 1 spice dose
- 1 tablespoon soup greens
- 1/2 liter water
- eel (prepared as described above)
- 1/2 teaspoon vinegar
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the prepared eel into uniform pieces of any desired length
2. Clean each piece individually by pushing out the entrails with a whisk handle and then cleaning it of the inner skin
3. Rub with salt and wash
4. Scald the fish with boiling water mixed with 1/2 teaspoon vinegar
5. Cook it in a broth made from water, soup greens, spices, onion and some vinegar for approximately 1/4 hour until done
6. Arrange the blue eel pieces warm or cold
7. Serve with the eel broth

title: Aal in Dillsauce (Eel in Dill Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g eel
- 1/4 liter water
- 1 teaspoon butter
- 1-1/2 teaspoons flour
- 1 teaspoon chopped dill
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 10 g salt
- 10 g onions
- 1 spice dose
- 3/8 liter water

## Directions

1. Prepare the eel according to directions, cut into pieces, clean and scald with boiling water
2. Cook the fish with spice dose, salt, onions and water until done
3. Remove from broth and keep hot
4. In an earthen pot, cook the fish broth with flour sautéed in butter into a good white sauce
5. Season with white pepper and chopped dill or parsley
6. Pour over the eel pieces

title: Abgetrocknete Knöpflein (Dried Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 rolls soaked in water and firmly pressed out
- 1 piece fat
- 1 handful steamed onions and parsley
- 4 to 5 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- butter
- grated bread

## Directions

1. Steam 3 water-soaked, firmly pressed-out rolls in a pan with 1 piece fat
2. Take 1 handful steamed onions and parsley, 4 to 5 eggs, salt, and nutmeg
3. Make small dumplings from the dough, as large as a hen's egg
4. Cook them for a quarter hour in boiling water
5. Braise them with butter and grated bread

title: Aechte Meerspinne (True Sea Spider)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 boiled sea spider
- white meat from the sea spider
- eggs from the sea spider
- butter
- 1 glass wine
- 1 bay leaf
- 1 lemon slice

## Directions

1. After the sea spider is boiled, let it cool
2. Break off all the long legs and remove the inner white meat, keeping it tender
3. Clean the body and wash it out carefully
4. Place the meat back in the body with the eggs
5. Roast everything until nicely golden
6. Pour in a glass of wine and let it come to a boil
7. Add 1 bay leaf and 1 lemon slice
8. Let it steam until soft

title: Aechter Rheinlangen (Authentic Rhine Pike)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fish
- salt
- 3 bottles Bordeaux wine
- 1 whole lemon, sliced
- cloves
- whole pepper
- mace
- beef kidney fat
- handful of chopped parsley
- 2 shallot onions
- lemon zest

## Directions

1. Clean the fish thoroughly, remove the gills, remove the entrails, rub it strongly with salt
2. Place in a fish kettle with 3 bottles Bordeaux wine, one whole lemon sliced, cloves, whole pepper, and mace, cover tightly and cook slowly
3. Finely chop a piece of beef kidney fat and let it melt in a casserole
4. Add a handful of chopped parsley, 2 shallot onions, and some lemon zest to the fat
5. When the fish is tender, place it on a plate and pour the mixture over it

title: Aechter Rheinlangen (True Rhine Eel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 fish
- salt
- 3 bottles Bordeaux wine
- 1 whole lemon, sliced
- cloves
- whole pepper
- nutmeg blossom
- ox kidney fat
- 1 handful chopped parsley
- 2 shallot onions
- 1 lemon, yolk

## Directions

1. Clean the fish thoroughly, cut out the gills, remove the entrails
2. Rub strongly with salt
3. In a fish kettle, cook it with 3 bottles Bordeaux wine, 1 whole lemon cut into slices, cloves, whole pepper, and nutmeg blossom, tightly covered and slowly
4. Finely pound a piece of ox kidney fat, let it melt in a casserole
5. Add 1 handful chopped parsley, 2 shallot onions, and some lemon yolk
6. When the fish is soft, place it on a plate and pour the steamed mixture over it

title: Aechtes Zuckerbrod (True Sugar Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- zest of 1 lemon
- 3 egg whites
- 10 loth flour

## Directions

1. Beat 8 egg yolks with the finely sifted sugar
2. Stir vigorously for half an hour
3. Grate the yellow zest from a lemon (the mixture must be as thick as a porridge)
4. Beat 3 egg whites into stiff snow
5. Mix 10 loth flour underneath the snow
6. Fill into a syringe with a finger-thick tube
7. Pipe long stalks, rounded at top and bottom, onto stiff paper on a black baking sheet
8. Pull through finely sifted sugar
9. Bake until yellow

title: Aepfel in Butter gebacken (Apples Baked in Butter)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Borsdorf apples
- Small and large raisins
- Wine
- Fine butter dough
- Egg

## Directions

1. Peel Borsdorf apples, cut off a small lid at the top and remove the core
2. Cook small and large raisins for half a quarter hour with wine
3. Weigh them and fill the apples with them
4. Roll out fine butter dough to knife back thickness
5. Cut 2 finger-width strips
6. Lay them crosswise and place an apple on top
7. Lift the 2 strips up around the apple
8. Lay 2 other strips on top and bend them downward and glue them to the lower ones with egg
9. Brush all strips with egg
10. Set the apples on a dark baking sheet, bake them and sprinkle with sugar

title: Aepfel und Kartoffeln (Apples and Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) raw peeled potatoes
- 5 g salt
- 1/4 l water
- 375 g apples
- 1/4 l water
- 10 g onions
- 20 g fatty bacon
- vinegar to taste
- 20 g sugar
- 1 pinch salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into quarters. Peel the apples, cut them into quarters and remove the core. Cut the onion into small cubes.
2. Cook the potatoes like salted potatoes. Cook the apples quickly with 1/4 l water and mix with the drained, dry potato pieces.
3. Fry the onion in the rendered bacon fat from which the cracklings have been removed; set the cracklings aside in a warm place.
4. Mix the onion, fat, sugar, salt and vinegar into the dish, season it strongly to taste, arrange it in the vegetable bowl in a mound shape and sprinkle the bacon cracklings over it.

title: Aepfel und Kartoffeln mit Speck (Apples and Potatoes with Bacon)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon, quantity not specified
- Apples, quantity not specified
- Potatoes, quantity not specified
- 1 onion
- Vinegar to taste
- 20 g sugar
- 1 pinch salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut into quarters. Peel the apples, cut into quarters, and remove the core. Cut the onion into small dice.
2. Cook the potatoes like salted potatoes. Quickly cook the apples in 1/4 liter of water and mix with the drained, dry potato pieces. Fry the onion in the rendered, strained bacon fat; set the cracklings aside in a warm place. Mix the onion, fat, sugar, salt and vinegar with the dish, season it strongly, arrange it mountain-like in a vegetable bowl and sprinkle with the bacon cracklings.

title: Aepfel-Compote (Apple Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 18 to 20 Borsdorf apples
- 1 maas water
- 1 schoppen wine
- 8 loth sugar
- cooked cherries

## Directions

1. Cut the apples in half and remove the cores
2. Cook the cores and peels in a pan with 1 maas water until reduced to half
3. Strain through a hair sieve and press out the peels
4. Pour 1 schoppen wine into the juice
5. Add 8 loth sugar and put the apples in to cook until soft
6. Drain the apples on a hair sieve
7. Arrange them on a dish in the form of a beehive, placing cooked cherries between the gaps
8. Reduce the syrup until it spins and pour it over the apples

title: Aepfelauflauf (Apple Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- good apples
- hard rind from 2 rolls or milk breads
- 1 schoppen milk
- 6 loth sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls sour cream, if needed
- egg whites from 5 eggs
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter lightly, cut good apples into small pieces and put them in the butter
2. Cut the hard rind from 2 rolls or milk breads, cook the soft part, finely cut, in a brass pan with 1 schoppen milk like a porridge
3. Let it cool, take it to the butter, add 6 loth sugar, 5 egg yolks, if necessary 3 spoonfuls sour cream
4. Finally fold in the snow from the 5 egg whites
5. Fill the soufflé into a bowl brushed with butter, brown it lightly and sprinkle sugar over it

title: Aepfelauflauf anderer Art (Apple Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 14 to 16 Borsdorfer apples
- a piece of butter
- a piece of sugar
- butter for the dish
- equal weight of eggs, sugar, and almonds
- finely ground sugar
- peeled almonds
- egg whites
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Steam 14 to 16 Borsdorfer apples with a piece of butter and a piece of sugar in a casserole until not very soft.
2. Brush the dish in which you will serve the soufflé with butter and place the apples in it.
3. Take equal weight of eggs, sugar, and almonds. Finely grind the sugar and peel the almonds and grate them with egg white on a grater to a paste.
4. Stir together the sugar, egg yolks, and almonds ground with egg white as for an almond cake.
5. Beat the remaining egg whites to a stiff snow and mix into the mass.
6. Pour this over the apples and bake in the oven until nicely golden.

title: Aepfelcrême (Apple Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Borsdorf apples
- wine (1 schoppen)
- water (1 schoppen)
- sugar (quarter pound)
- milk (half a maas)
- lemon (zest and juice)
- sugar (3 loth)
- egg yolks (4)
- flour (3 finger-widths)
- cold milk
- pistachios

## Directions

1. Peel Borsdorf apples and cut in half
2. Remove the core house
3. Hollow out the apples a little
4. Cook them with one schoppen wine, one schoppen water and a quarter pound sugar until soft
5. For the cream: cook half a maas milk with the zest of a lemon and 3 loth sugar together
6. Stir 4 egg yolks and 3 finger-widths of flour smooth with cold milk
7. Add to the boiling milk
8. Let everything draw on the fire once more
9. Pour the cream through a hair sieve onto the designated plate
10. Drain the apples beforehand (the syrup in which the apples were cooked must be reduced until it forms threads)
11. Lay them on the cream with the hollow facing upward
12. Fill them with the syrup
13. Sprinkle chopped pistachios on top

title: Aepfelcrême anderer Art (Apple Cream, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Borsdorf or Rose apples (6 to 8)
- butter
- sugar
- flour (1 small cooking spoon)
- cold water
- egg yolks (4)
- white wine (1 schoppen)
- sugar (4 loth)
- sugar (for sprinkling)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut finely
2. Stew them very soft with a piece of butter and a piece of sugar
3. Press through a hair sieve
4. For the cream: stir a small cooking spoon of flour with cold water smooth
5. Stir 4 egg yolks into it
6. Pour in one schoppen white wine
7. Cook the cream with 4 loth sugar like a porridge
8. Cook it a bit more with the apples
9. Put it in the designated dish
10. Sprinkle sugar on top
11. Burn it nicely with a glowing shovel

title: Aepfelkrapfen (Apple Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 8 loth melted butter
- 3 loth finely powdered sugar
- 1 knife point cinnamon
- 1 schoppen warm milk
- 5 eggs
- 1 small knife point salt
- 3 spoonfuls beer yeast
- apple purée for filling
- sugar and cinnamon for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 1 pound flour, 8 loth melted butter, 3 loth finely powdered sugar, 1 knife point cinnamon, and 1 schoppen warm milk
2. Beat in 5 eggs and scatter 1 small knife point salt
3. Work the dough on the noodle board until it throws bubbles
4. Work it a bit more with 3 spoonfuls beer yeast
5. Roll it out knife-back thick on the noodle board
6. Cut out little fritters with a mold, fill with apple purée, bake until golden, and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Aepfelkrapfen (Apple Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 8 loth melted butter
- 3 loth finely pounded sugar
- a knife point of cinnamon
- 1 schoppen warm milk
- 5 eggs
- a small knife point of salt
- 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast
- apple sauce (Aepfelmuß)

## Directions

1. Mix flour, melted butter, finely pounded sugar, cinnamon, and warm milk into a dough
2. Beat in 5 eggs and sprinkle salt over it
3. Work the dough on the noodle board until it throws bubbles
4. Work it a little more with 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast
5. Roll out the dough on the noodle board to the thickness of a knife back
6. Cut out small fritters with a model
7. Fill with apple sauce
8. Fry until golden
9. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Aepfelkuchen (Apple Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cake sheet with butter dough
- good apples, sliced
- zest of half a lemon, grated
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- 1 large handful ground sugar
- 2 knife-tips ground cinnamon
- half a schoppen milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Line a cake sheet with butter dough
2. Cover with good, sliced apples
3. Mix the grated zest of half a lemon, 2 spoonfuls of sour cream, a large handful of ground sugar, 2 knife-tips of ground cinnamon, and half a schoppen of milk together well
4. Pour this mixture over the apples
5. Cut some butter over the top
6. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Aepfelkuchen anderer Art (Apple Cake Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- good apples that do not overcook
- 1 Maas water
- 12 Loth sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 1 glass wine
- butter dough
- raw potatoes or whole apples

## Directions

1. Peel and quarter good apples that do not overcook
2. Cook them with their cores and skins in 1 Maas of water in a brass pan for a quarter-hour
3. Pour through a hair sieve
4. Return the water to the pan with 12 Loth sugar, the zest of half a lemon, a piece of whole cinnamon, 1 glass of wine, and the apples
5. Cook the apples until soft but not overcooked
6. Let them drain on a hair sieve and cook the syrup until it spins threads
7. Line a cake sheet with butter dough, placing raw potatoes or whole apples on top so the dough does not shrink
8. Bake the dough
9. Remove the potatoes or apples
10. Arrange the steamed apples around the cake
11. Pour the syrup over them
12. Serve the cake—it should not be baked further

title: Aepfelkuchen mit einem Gitter (Apple Cake with a Grid)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled apples
- butter
- sugar
- quarter-pound raisins and currants
- 2 knife-tips ground cinnamon
- zest of half a lemon, grated
- quarter-pound peeled, finely ground almonds
- quarter-pound butter
- half a pound fine flour
- 3 Loth ground sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- cold water if needed
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cook peeled apples in a casserole with a piece of butter, a piece of sugar, a quarter-pound of raisins and currants, 2 knife-tips of ground cinnamon, and the grated zest of half a lemon until very soft
2. Let them cool in a dish
3. Make an almond dough: combine a quarter-pound of peeled, finely ground almonds, a quarter-pound of butter, half a pound of fine flour, 3 Loth of ground sugar, and 3 egg yolks (add a little cold water if necessary)
4. Roll out the dough, fold it over itself, and roll out again
5. Line a buttered cake sheet with the dough
6. Place the apples in it
7. Make a border around the cake as usual
8. Cut 12 finger-wide strips from the same dough (reserved for this purpose)
9. Lay these strips crosswise like a grid over the cake
10. Coat with eggs
11. Bake

title: Aepfelkuchen mit einer Kruste (Apple Cake with a Crust)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- peeled Breitling apples
- butter dough
- quarter-pound butter
- 3 handfuls grated bread
- 4 Loth ground sugar
- 3 Loth ground almonds
- 2 knife-tips cinnamon

## Directions

1. Place peeled Breitling apples in a cake sheet lined with butter dough
2. Toast 3 handfuls of grated bread in a quarter-pound of butter
3. Add 4 Loth of ground sugar, 3 Loth of ground almonds, and 2 knife-tips of cinnamon
4. Mix together
5. Sprinkle this mixture thickly over the cake
6. Bake

title: Aepfelkunzen (Apple Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 to 8 pieces of Breitling apples
- a piece of butter
- a piece of sugar
- 10 loth butter
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 teaspoons of flour
- 8 egg whites
- butter for the dish
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Peel and cut 6 to 8 Breitling apples and steam them in a piece of butter with a piece of sugar until not quite soft.
2. In a bowl, lightly stir 10 loth butter, 4 loth sugar with a lemon zest grated into it.
3. Add 8 egg yolks one after another to the butter mixture.
4. To each egg yolk add a small teaspoon of flour.
5. Beat the 8 egg whites to a snow.
6. Mix everything together, fill into a bowl brushed with butter.
7. Bake until golden and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Aepfelküchlein (Apple Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- wine
- 6 tablespoons hot lard
- apple slices for baking

## Directions

1. Make a thin dough (Flädchenteig) from flour and wine
2. Stir in 6 tablespoons hot lard, one after another
3. If the dough becomes too thick, help it along with wine
4. Dip apple slices in the dough
5. Fry very slowly in hot lard with frequent shaking
6. Lay on bread slices to drain
7. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon
8. Serve very hot

title: Aepfelküchlein anderer Art (Apple Cakes, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- lukewarm wheat beer
- 5 tablespoons boiling hot lard
- good baking apples
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a thin dough (Flädchenteig) from flour and lukewarm wheat beer in a bowl
2. Stir in 5 tablespoons boiling hot lard, one after another
3. Peel slices of good baking apples and remove the core
4. Mix the apple slices into the dough
5. Fry slowly in hot lard with frequent shaking of the pan
6. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over them

title: Aepfelscharlott (Apple Charlotte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- apples
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound raisins and dried fruit
- 2 knife points of ground cinnamon
- half the peel of a finely chopped lemon
- white bread or milk bread slices
- clarified fat for frying
- butter for the baking sheet
- sugar
- preserved fruits
- 2 spoonfuls of raspberry jelly

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cook them in a casserole with 1/4 pound butter, 1/4 pound raisins and dried fruit, 2 knife points of ground cinnamon, and half the peel of a finely chopped lemon until they form a paste and let cool.
2. Cut white bread or milk bread slices and fry them golden in clarified fat.
3. Brush a fairly deep baking sheet with butter, sprinkle it with sugar, and line the bottom with some of the fried bread slices.
4. Lay a layer of the cooked apples on top, then preserved fruits, then again apples and again fruits, continuing until the apples are used up.
5. Finally, cover the top of the dish with fried bread slices.
6. Place the dish in the oven for 1.5 hours.
7. Pour 2 spoonfuls of raspberry jelly over it and the same amount at the side of the dish so that the bread slices become soft.

title: Aepfelschaum (Apple Foam)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Borsdorf apples
- Quarter pound finely sifted sugar
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Roast 6 Borsdorf apples
2. Scrape out the pulp and stir it with quarter pound finely sifted sugar for half an hour
3. Beat the whites of 3 eggs into stiff snow and mix it with the yolks

title: Aepfelschaum (Apple Foam)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples (6 to 8)
- cinnamon (1 knife point)
- sugar (8 loth, ground)
- egg whites (12)
- butter

## Directions

1. Roast the apples either in hot ashes, on a dish in the oven, or in the roasting oven
2. Scrape out the pulp
3. Stir it with a knife point of cinnamon and 8 loth ground sugar until very foamy
4. Whip a stiff foam from 12 egg whites
5. Slowly mix it with the apple mixture
6. Pour into a deep plate brushed with butter, shaped like a pyramid
7. Place the plate on a black sheet thickly covered with sand or salt
8. Put in a roasting oven and sprinkle with sugar
9. It is ready in half an hour

title: Amsterdamer Waffeln (Amsterdam Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- lukewarm wine
- 2 knife-points of cinnamon
- 4 loth pounded sugar
- snow from 6 eggs
- bacon

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound flour smooth in a bowl with lukewarm wine.
2. Slowly mix in 2 knife-points of cinnamon, 4 loth pounded sugar, and the beaten whites of 6 eggs.
3. Heat a waffle iron in fire or, better yet, in coals, and coat the inside with bacon.
4. Fill it with dough, close the iron slowly, bake the waffle golden on both sides, and sprinkle cinnamon and sugar on top.

title: Anisküchlein (Anise Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- Half pound white sugar
- Pure anise for 2 kreuzer
- Flour

## Directions

1. Beat 5 egg whites into stiff snow
2. Add half pound white sugar and stir for half an hour
3. Mix in pure anise and enough flour so the dough no longer runs
4. Line a dark baking sheet with wafers
5. Set small mounds of dough on them
6. Bake at gentle heat in the oven so they remain completely white

title: Anislaibchen auf Oblaten (Anise Cakes on Wafers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound sugar
- 7 egg whites
- Juice of 1 lemon
- 6 lots fine flour
- 2 lots clean washed anise

## Directions

1. Stir half pound sugar, 7 egg whites and lemon juice into white icing
2. Add 6 lots fine flour and 2 lots clean washed anise
3. Test a small amount of the mixture in the oven to see if it no longer runs
4. Set small cakes on white wafers placed on a dark baking sheet using a coffee spoon
5. Bake slowly in the oven so they remain a nice white color and do not turn yellow

title: Apfelbettelmann für 4 Personen (Apple Beggar's Dish for 4 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g grated bread
- 1/2 teaspoon ground cinnamon
- 2 ground cloves
- 1/2 grated lemon
- 40 g sugar
- 1/2 kg apples
- 65 g sugar
- 4 tablespoons water
- 125 g lard
- 30 g currants
- 20 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate the bread, mix with the spices and 40 g sugar; peel the apples and cut into pieces, clean the currants and let them soak.
2. Stew the apples with 65 g sugar in a little water. Then brown half of the lard in a pan, press half of the bread mixture onto it with a spoon, place the apple compote and currants over it, sprinkle the other half of bread over and press down. After the dish has baked slowly for 1/4 hour, drizzle the remaining lard over it, turn the dish like a pancake, bake it for another 1/4 hour over low heat and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon after serving.

title: Apfelbrotsuppe (Apple Bread Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g old bread
- 1 pinch salt
- 2 crushed cloves
- 40 g sugar
- 4 medium-sized apples (or apple scraps)
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Soften the bread in the water.
2. Cook the apples or apple scraps, bread mixture, spices, salt and sugar together, strain through a sieve, reheat once more and serve.

title: Apfelklösse (Apple Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g apples (sour)
- 250 g flour
- 15 g margarine
- 1-1/2 egg
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/2 teaspoon lemon sugar
- 1 knife-tip baking powder
- 1/8 l skim milk (scant)

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, remove the core and cut into cubes.
2. Make a good dumpling dough from the remaining ingredients, mixing in the apple cubes last. Dumplings are spooned from the dough, either placed on the dish to be prepared and covered tightly to steam until cooked, or boiled in salted water.

title: Apfelkompott (Stewed Apples)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg apples
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 4 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Peel the apples cleanly and remove the cores.
2. Stew them soft and clear in the sugar water, arrange them, cook the juice down a bit more and pour it over the apples.

title: Apfelkuchen (Apple Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Apple pieces
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Distribute apple pieces, sugar, and cinnamon on top
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Apfelmus (Apple Puree)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg apples
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 6 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Peel the apples and cut them into pieces.
2. Cook them quickly with the water in a heated pot until soft, press the cooked apples through a sieve and mix the puree with the sugar.

title: Apfelnauflauf (Apple Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice
- 1/2 maas milk
- 4 loth butter
- 12 Borsdorfer apples
- 1 glass wine
- 2 loth orange peel and citron
- some lemon peel
- 6 peeled almonds
- a handful of raisins
- 5 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 4 loth sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/4 pound rice in boiling water 2 to 3 times, drain it in a hair sieve, cook it with 1/2 maas milk and 4 loth butter in a casserole
2. Peel 12 Borsdorfer apples, remove the core, braise the apples in a casserole with 1 glass wine in butter until half soft, place them on a plate
3. For the filling: weigh 2 loth orange peel and citron, some lemon peel, 6 peeled almonds not very fine, add a handful of raisins, fill the apples with this mixture
4. Stir 5 egg yolks, the snow of 6 egg whites, 4 loth ground sugar into the mass
5. Place half of the mixture on a buttered baking sheet, lay the filled apples on it, place the other half on top, and let the soufflé rise in an oven
6. When ready, place it on a plate, spread a snow of 4 egg whites mixed with sugar on top, and place it once more in the oven on a black baking sheet with sand or salt underneath

title: Apfelnudeln (Apple Noodles)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1-2 tablespoons water
- 100 g flour
- 3 g salt
- 20 g fat
- 1/2 kg apples
- water and salt for cooking the noodles
- 30 g sugar
- 1/8 l water
- 20 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel the apples, remove the core, cut into pieces. Put the sugar water on the stove.
2. Sift the flour onto a board, place the egg beaten with salt in a depression in the middle, distribute the fat over the flour, mix everything together with a broad knife, and work the dough with the hand until a hole pressed in with a finger closes by itself. Divide the dough into 4 parts; roll each one out on a floured board with a floured rolling pin to the thickness of a knife-back, and let the dough sheets dry on clean cloth for 1/4 hour, then cut strips about 5 cm wide from them, lay 6 on top of each other and cut 5 cm long noodles from them, which laid out on the cloth dry for 1/2 hour. The apples are poured into boiling sugar water, cooked until done and kept hot. The dried noodles are boiled for 5 minutes in salted water, drained, dripped dry, mixed with the apples, reheated and sprinkled with sugar and cinnamon before serving.

title: Apfelreis (Apple Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g rice
- 1/2 l water for cooking rice
- 2 large apples or 40 g dried apples and 1/4 l water
- 5 g margarine or fat
- 1 small piece of orange peel
- 10 g sugar
- 5 g salt
- 10 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Peel 2 large apples and cut them into pieces. Cook the apple scraps with 1/4 l water; use this water for cooking the rice.
2. Add 10 g sugar to the apples and cook them with orange peel, salt, and the cooked rice that has been mixed with fat until everything is tender.
3. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over it. You can also use 40 g dried apples, but these should be cooked separately until soft before mixing with the rice.

title: Apfelschaum (Apple Foam)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Borsdorfer apples
- 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 lemon
- wafers (Oblaten)

## Directions

1. Roast the 6 Borsdorfer apples, scrape out the pulp and stir it with 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar for half an hour
2. Beat the whites of 3 eggs to stiff peaks
3. Mix the egg whites with the yolk of half a lemon (grated on a grater) into the apple mass
4. Line a sheet with wafers, place small heaps of the mixture on it
5. Bake in the oven

title: Aprikosen (Preserved Apricots)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- apricots (not overripe)
- boiling water
- sugar (equal weight to apricots)

## Directions

1. Place apricots in a dish and pour boiling water over them
2. Remove the skin and dry on a cloth
3. In a saucepan or brass pan, cook sugar and water until the sugar forms bubbles
4. With a silver spoon, add the apricots and cook as long as hard-boiled eggs
5. Remove with the same spoon to a clean dish
6. Let the syrup cook down somewhat, pour over the apricots and let stand overnight
7. The next day place again in the pan, cook briefly, carefully remove the apricots, cook the syrup down again, and pour over the apricots
8. Repeat this process for 4 days in succession
9. On the fourth day, place the apricots in the dish where they will be stored, draw paper through cherry brandy, place it on top, prick holes in the paper, tie the dish closed and place in a dry location

title: Aprikosenauflauf (Apricot Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen sour cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 3 loth flour
- 4 egg whites
- butter for the dish
- apricots (fresh or preserved)
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Stir 1 schoppen sour cream with 4 egg yolks and 1/4 pound sugar until very thick.
2. Mix 3 loth flour and the snow from 4 egg whites into the mass.
3. Brush the dish that you will serve with butter and line it with apricots (fresh or preserved).
4. Fill the mass on top, bake the soufflé until golden, and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Arakpudding (Arrack Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 spoonfuls pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 loth fine flour
- 1/2 glass arrack
- Snow from the egg whites
- Butter
- 4 good apples
- 1 piece of butter
- 1 schoppen wine
- 1 knife tip of cinnamon
- 1 piece of sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 6 spoonfuls pounded sugar and 6 egg yolks very thick like a biscuit dough
2. Add 4 loth fine flour, 1/2 glass arrack and the snow from the egg whites
3. Mix everything together
4. Coat a tin capsule with butter including the lid
5. Fill the mass into it
6. Bind the capsule firmly shut
7. Place it for a quarter hour in boiling water
8. For the sauce: peel 4 good apples
9. Remove the core
10. Steam them in a piece of butter
11. Push them through a hair sieve
12. Let them cook with 1 schoppen wine, 1 knife tip of cinnamon and 1 piece of sugar
13. Pour the sauce over the pudding

title: Arme Ritter (Poor Knights)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Bread slices
- Fat
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Dried fruit
- Lingonberries
- Apple puree
- Wine sauce

## Directions

1. Fry bread slices in fat until dark brown color
2. Sprinkle thickly with sugar and cinnamon
3. Add dried fruit, lingonberries, and apple puree or serve with wine sauce
4. Alternatively, soften bread slices first in milk or sugar water, prepare a thin pancake batter from 20 g flour and the stated ingredients, dip the softened slices in the batter, and bake

title: Arme Ritter für 4 Personen (Poor Knights for 4 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 grated milk rolls or 8 toasted rusks
- 1 egg, 1 egg white
- 1 pinch salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 3/8 l skim milk
- 1/4 grated lemon
- 2 tablespoons grated bread crumbs
- 25 g lard, fat or margarine for frying
- 20 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut milk rolls or rusks into finger-thick slices and put them in a neat shape, beat 1 egg, salt and sugar with the milk until clear, add lemon and gradually pour the liquid over the bread slices, which are placed on a flat dish and turned often.
2. When the poor knights are thoroughly soaked, turn them in beaten egg white and bread crumbs and fry or bake them in steaming fat to a beautiful light brown color, sprinkle them thickly with sugar and cinnamon, serve with stewed fruit, lingonberries, sauce over wine sauce or serve with apple rice. The poor knights can also first be softened in milk or sugar water, a thin pancake batter made from 20 g flour and the stated ingredients, the softened slices turned in it and then baked.

title: Aufgelaufene Citronentorte (Risen Lemon Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- zest of 4 lemons
- 8 egg yolks
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 loth fine flour
- snow of 8 egg whites
- butter dough for lining

## Directions

1. Finely pound the sugar with the lemon zest in a bowl
2. Beat 8 egg yolks into it
3. Stir this together for half an hour
4. Mix in the juice of 2 lemons, 2 loth fine flour, and the snow of the 8 egg whites into the mass
5. Fill this into a plafond lined with butter dough
6. Bake the torte in the oven

title: Aufgelaufene Geduldzeltchen (Risen Patience Tents)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1/4 pound finest flour
- zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir sugar and egg together in a bowl for half an hour
2. Mix in flour and grated lemon zest
3. Place small heaps on a black baking sheet coated with wax or lard
4. Bake in the oven

title: Aufgelaufenes Mandelbrod (Risen Almond Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Pfund abgeschälte Mandeln
- 4 Eierweiß
- ein Viertelpfund gesiebter Zucker
- 1 Loth feines Mehl
- Oblaten

## Directions

1. 1 Pfund abgeschälte Mandeln der Länge nach so fein als möglich schneiden.
2. Von 4 Eierweiß und einem Viertelpfund gesiebtem Zucker ein dickes Eis rühren.
3. 1 Loth feines Mehl nebst den Mandeln dazunehmen.
4. Oblaten auf ein schwarzes Blech legen.
5. Fingersdick die Masse darauf streichen.
6. Das Mandelbrod im Ofen backen.

title: Aufgezogene Milchspätzlein (Baked Milk Spätzle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 or 5 eggs
- 1 heaping tablespoon flour per egg
- milk, 1 schoppen
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 lemon, zest
- 3 eggs for sauce

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter in a bowl, adding 4 or 5 eggs one by one, adding 1 heaping tablespoon flour for each egg
2. In a saucepan, bring milk to boil
3. Dip a sieve into the milk and press the dough with a cooking spoon into the boiling milk
4. Catch the spätzle with a skimming spoon and place in a bowl
5. Butter a baking sheet and lay the spätzle on it
6. Pound 4 loth sugar rubbed with lemon zest very finely
7. Pour 1 schoppen good milk into it, beat in 3 eggs and mix well
8. Pour a little over the spätzle, add the remaining spätzle sauce, pour the rest over and place in the oven
9. Bake for half an hour until done

title: Aufgezogene Nudeln mit Krebsragout (Drawn Noodles with Crayfish Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour for 2 whole eggs and 3 yolks
- boiling water
- cold water
- 15 large crayfish
- quarter pound butter
- 3 eggs
- melon mold

## Directions

1. Work as much flour as 2 whole eggs and 3 yolks can take on the noodle board.
2. Roll out the dough not very thin, cut noodles as wide as the back of a knife is thick.
3. Boil them in boiling water, pour into a strainer and cold water over.
4. Meanwhile boil 15 large crayfish in boiling water, remove the tails and claws, clean the bodies well.
5. Pound them in the mortar with a quarter pound butter, put into a casserole, place on coals and let them steam well.
6. When the crayfish butter is nicely red, force it through a hair sieve so the butter runs off nicely.
7. Stir it lightly, beat 3 eggs into it, slowly mix the noodles under it.
8. Line a melon mold with butter, pour half the noodles into it.
9. Lay the crayfish tails and claws on the noodles, the other half on top.
10. Place in the oven and let it bake nicely.

title: Aufgezogene Pastete (Puffed Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal sweetbreads
- Calf brain
- Chicken breasts
- Calf udders
- Beef sweetbreads
- fine white veal
- Crab udders
- 3 shallots
- zest of half a lemon
- flour
- cold water
- meat broth
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1/8 pound crab butter
- 4 eggs
- half a quart sweet cream
- 1 quart sour cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 6 spoonfuls flour
- salt
- half a nutmeg

## Directions

1. Make a mélange ragout: this consists of veal sweetbreads, brain, chicken breasts, calf udders, beef sweetbreads, fine white veal, and crab udders
2. Keep all of these, except the crab udders, in boiling water in a large colander as long as hard eggs
3. Remove and place in cold water so it stays white
4. Weigh 3 shallots and the zest of half a lemon, braise these in a casserole, cover for a quarter hour
5. Let the meat drain and braise it in the casserole for half an hour
6. Sprinkle a small cooking spoon of flour over it, add a little cold water, turn it over, pour a soup ladle of good meat broth and the juice of 1 lemon and let it cook together for a quarter hour
7. In a small casserole, take 1/8 pound crab butter, add 4 eggs, pour in half a quart sweet cream, stir it over weak coal fire until it begins to thicken, let it rest in a colander
8. Stir 1 quart sour cream in a bowl thick, add 6 egg yolks, make stiff snow from the 6 egg whites, slowly mix 6 spoonfuls flour, salt, and half a nutmeg under the mass
9. Put the ragout without sauce in a bowl
10. Cut the crab butter as desired and place it on top of the ragout
11. Pour half of the stirred mass over it and let it puff up nicely in a baking oven
12. When nearly done, pour the other half over it and let it finish puffing in the oven
13. When ready to serve, place the puffed pastry on a plate and serve the sauce separately

title: Aufgezogener Eierbrei (Souffléed Egg Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 8 egg yolks
- sugar
- lemons
- egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter, 8 egg yolks, sugar and lemons together
2. Beat the whites of the eggs to snow and add them, place in a suitable dish, add heat above and below and soufflé

title: Aufgezogener Mandelbrei (Souffléed Almond Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound peeled, finely ground almonds
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 drinking glasses sweet cream
- a handful of grated roll

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound peeled, finely ground almonds, 1/2 pound sugar, 8 egg yolks, 2 drinking glasses sweet cream and a handful of grated roll well together
2. Beat a snow from the egg whites, add it and place in a suitable dish, heat above and below, and soufflé

title: Auflauf von geriebener Gerste (Barley Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- flour
- half a Maas milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 Loth sugar
- half a lemon
- 6 egg yolks
- a handful of raisins and citrons
- 2 Loth cut citron peel
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix 2 egg yolks with as much flour as they will absorb, ground as finely as possible
2. Cook this into a thick porridge in half a Maas of milk
3. Let it cool in a bowl
4. Stir 1/4 pound of butter until light
5. Take 4 Loth of sugar grated on half a lemon, 6 egg yolks, a handful of raisins and citrons, 2 Loth of cut citron peel, and the snow from the 6 egg whites, and add to the butter
6. Add the porridge to this mixture and blend everything well together
7. Butter a dish and fill the mass into it
8. Let the soufflé rise beautifully golden in the oven and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Augsburger Gansleberpastete (Augsburg Goose Liver Pie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fine butter dough
- 3 goose livers
- 4 lemon peels
- 1 soaked and pressed rusks
- marrow from 4 lemons
- a handful of chopped and braised onions and parsley
- half a pound lean ham
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- mace
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 3 to 4 goose livers
- 1 spoonful capers
- mushrooms
- quarter pound cleaned truffles
- 2 goose livers
- peeled olives
- bacon slices
- 3 to 4 blanched calf's feet
- wine
- vinegar
- water
- celery
- yellow carrots
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 large onions
- whole cloves
- mace
- whole pepper
- 1 crushed egg
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Make a fine butter dough and line a melon mold with it.
2. Weigh 3 goose livers and cover them in a bowl.
3. Weigh 4 lemon peels, take one soaked and pressed rusk, the marrow from 4 lemons, a handful of chopped and braised onions and parsley, half a pound lean ham, salt, pepper, nutmeg, mace, 2 eggs and 1 egg yolk.
4. Mix everything together thoroughly.
5. Line the bottom of the mold with a handful of the mixture.
6. Cut 3 to 4 goose livers lengthwise and place on the mixture.
7. Add 1 spoonful of capers, place a mushroom on top, quarter pound cleaned truffles cut into leaves and crushed.
8. Add another handful of the mixture.
9. Cut 2 more goose livers lengthwise and place on top.
10. Add peeled olives, 1 spoonful capers, 4 to 6 mushrooms, truffles, and the mixture on top.
11. Place bacon slices on top and make a lid from the same dough.
12. Crimp with a pie crimper, brush the pie with eggs, and bake for 2 hours in the oven.
13. Cut an opening so steam can escape, cover again, and make an aspic.
14. Cook 3 to 4 blanched calf's feet with equal parts wine, vinegar, and water, celery, yellow carrots, 2 bay leaves, 2 large onions, whole cloves, mace, and whole pepper for 3 hours.
15. Pour through a hair sieve and let stand for a quarter hour.
16. Dip filter paper in it so the fat sticks to it.
17. Pour the jelly into a saucepan, avoiding the sediment.
18. Add a crushed egg, return to fire, and beat with a whisk until it boils.
19. Remove from fire, press in the juice from 1 lemon, cover with a lid with coals on top, and let stand for a quarter hour.
20. Pour the jelly through a felt hat and, when cold, pour it over the pie (must pour over 3 to 4 times).
21. Place the lid on top and set the pie in a cold place.

title: Augsburger Kugelhopf (Augsburg Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 loths of butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 loths of finely ground sugar
- 12 loths of fine flour
- 2 tablespoons of good beer yeast
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 9 loths of butter in a bowl until creamy
2. Gradually stir in 6 egg yolks, 3 loths of finely ground sugar, 12 loths of fine flour, and 2 tablespoons of good beer yeast
3. Fold in the stiffly beaten snow from 6 egg whites
4. Let the dough rise
5. Fill into a kugelhopf mold brushed with butter
6. Bake

title: Ausgesteinte Kirschen (Pitted Cherries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Schöne Kirschen (Beautiful cherries)
- Zucker (Sugar)
- Wasser (Water)
- Zimmt (Cinnamon)

## Directions

1. Pit 2 pounds of beautiful cherries, taking care not to lose too much juice
2. For 2 pounds of cherries, use 1½ pounds of sugar and cook it in a brass pan or saucepan with half a bottle's measure of water until the sugar spins thick threads
3. Add the cherries along with some whole cinnamon and cook as long as it takes for hard-boiled eggs
4. Lift out the cherries with a skimming spoon
5. Cook the syrup until it rises above the pan
6. Place the cherries in their designated dish and pour the syrup, after it has been well reduced, over them
7. Let cool and store like other preserved sugar items; they will keep for 2 years

title: Backmeistercrême (Baker Master's Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fine flour
- egg yolks (8)
- sweet cream (half a maas)
- sugar (4 loth)
- lemon (zest)
- fresh butter (3 loth)
- lemon (half, juice)
- sugar (quarter pound)
- water (half a glass)

## Directions

1. Take as much fine flour as can be stirred with 8 egg yolks
2. Pour half a maas sweet cream to it
3. Add 4 loth sugar rubbed from a lemon peel and 3 loth fresh butter
4. Stir the cream on the fire
5. Press the juice of half a lemon to it when removed from the fire
6. Cook a quarter pound sugar with half a glass of water to a caramel
7. Pour the cream into it
8. Stir together so it remains somewhat marbled (white and brown)

title: Backobst (Dried Fruit Compote)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g dried fruit
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 1 piece orange peel
- 2 tablespoons sugar
- 3 g potato flour
- 1 tablespoon water
- 3/8 liter water

## Directions

1. Wash the dry fruit preferably the evening before use and place it in an earthen pot with 3/8 liter fresh water to soak.
2. The next morning, without changing the water, bring the dried fruit to a boil in the same pot with orange peel, cinnamon and half the sugar, then cover, push back and simmer very slowly for 2-3 hours to soften, without stirring; only shake the pot occasionally. If necessary, add some water so that there is plenty of broth. Lift out the fruit, mix the remaining sugar with the juice, cook this down to 1/8 liter and mix with potato flour dissolved in water; arrange the fruit with the sauce poured over it.

title: Baierisch Kraut (Bavarian Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt
- pork fat
- onions
- half glass vinegar
- glass of wine
- meat broth
- flour

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage finely and salt it lightly.
2. In a casserole, steam it soft with a piece of pork fat and onions.
3. When soft, pour half a glass of vinegar over it, a glass of wine, and some meat broth.
4. Sprinkle a little flour over it, mix together, and finish cooking.

title: Baierische Dampfnudeln (Bavarian Steamed Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine flour
- 3 spoonfuls beer yeast
- 1 Schoppen lukewarm milk
- quarter pound butter
- 2 whole eggs and yolks
- flour
- quarter pound butter
- approximately 1 Schoppen milk
- 1 Loth pounded sugar

## Directions

1. In a bowl make a small dough with 2 pounds fine flour, 3 spoonfuls beer yeast and 1 Schoppen lukewarm milk, let it rise well in gentle warmth.
2. Cut quarter pound butter to it, along with 2 whole eggs and yolks, work the dough until it throws bubbles.
3. Let it rise in the warmth, and when it is quite ripe, place cloth on a noodle board, sprinkle flour on it, set the noodles, as large as a hen's egg, on it and let them rise a little more.
4. Let quarter pound butter melt in a suitable dish, but do not let it become hot.
5. Pour approximately 1 Schoppen milk to it, 1 Loth pounded sugar.
6. Set the steamed noodles in the dish and a cover with weak coals on top.
7. Place the dish on a trivet, put a little coals under it and let it cook through.

title: Baierische Käsnudeln (Bavarian Cheese Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- 2 spoonfuls of beer yeast
- 1/2 schoppen of lukewarm milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 3/4 pound cheese
- 3 egg yolks
- a large handful of chopped chives
- salt
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Put flour in a bowl and make a sponge with 2 spoonfuls of beer yeast and 1/2 schoppen of lukewarm milk
2. Let 1/4 pound butter melt in a dish and stir it lightly with 3/4 pound cheese
3. Add 3 egg yolks and work everything together with the aforementioned dough and flour
4. Add a large handful of chopped chives and salt
5. Set the dough in warmth to rise
6. Make small noodles with a spoon and bake them in hot lard

title: Baierische Rüben (Bavarian Turnips)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- pork fat
- meat broth or warm water
- pork meat
- flour
- fat

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the turnips, scrape them.
2. In a casserole with pork fat, some meat broth or warm water, cook them for one hour.
3. When tender, add a piece of pork meat.
4. In a pan, roast a couple of spoonfuls of flour in a piece of fat until brown.
5. Deglaze it with meat broth, stir it smooth, pour it over the turnips.
6. Let them cook another half hour.

title: Baierische Rüben mit Kastanien (Bavarian Turnips with Chestnuts)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- turnips
- sugar
- roasted chestnuts
- pork foot
- pork ear
- roasted bratwurst

## Directions

1. Clean and cut the turnips.
2. Brown them in sugar.
3. Add the roasted chestnuts with a pork foot and pork ear.
4. When everything is tender, cut several roasted bratwursts into small pieces and add them.
5. Arrange the turnips on the plate.

title: Basler Lebkuchen (Basel Gingerbread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound flour
- 1/8 pound blanched, coarsely ground almonds
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 2 loth coarsely chopped citron
- 1/2 loth cinnamon
- 2 knife points cloves
- 2 knife points mace
- 2 egg whites beaten stiff
- 1/4 pound sugar for the glaze
- 2 spoonfuls water for the glaze
- Juice of 3 to 4 lemons

## Directions

1. Mix sugar, flour, almonds, lemon zest, citron, cinnamon, cloves, and mace together
2. Fold in beaten egg whites to form a dough
3. Roll out dough finger-thick on a noodle board
4. Cut out pieces with a tin mold
5. Bake on a flour-dusted sheet until not quite done
6. For the glaze, cook sugar and water together until it forms threads
7. Add lemon juice and bring to a boil again
8. Brush the glaze on the gingerbread and dry in the oven

title: Bauernhefenküchlein (Farmer's Yeast Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds of flour
- 4 whole eggs
- half a schoppen of thick sour cream
- 3 loths of ground sugar
- 2 knife points of salt
- one quarter pound of clean small raisins
- one quarter pound of melted butter
- 2 spoons of white beer yeast

## Directions

1. In a bowl, beat 4 whole eggs into 2 pounds of flour
2. Work the dough together with half a schoppen of thick sour cream, 3 loths of ground sugar, 2 knife points of salt, one quarter pound of clean small raisins, and one quarter pound of melted butter
3. Add 2 spoons of white beer yeast
4. Place the dough back in the bowl and let it rise in gentle warmth
5. When ripe, place on a pasta board and shape into round fritters the size of a hen's egg
6. Pull them apart so they become thin as paper in the middle
7. Place in hot lard and fry until golden yellow
8. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Bauernmocken (Farmer's Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- yeast
- milk
- curd from 1 measure of sour milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 egg yolk
- 3 tablespoons thick sour cream
- 1/8 pound sugar
- salt
- chives
- lard

## Directions

1. Make a pre-dough (Zusang) from flour, yeast, and milk
2. Add curd from 1 measure of sour milk to it
3. Beat in 2 eggs and 1 egg yolk
4. Add 3 tablespoons thick sour cream and 1/8 pound sugar
5. Mix well with salt and chives
6. Beat the dough for another quarter hour until it releases from the bowl
7. Let it stand until it is ripe
8. Use a spoon to remove long cakes and place them in hot lard
9. Fry them slowly

title: Baumnüsse (Candied Walnuts)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Walnuts (50 nuts)
- 1-1/2 pounds sugar
- Cinnamon
- Cloves
- Cinnamon water
- Cherry spirits for soaking paper

## Directions

1. Break the walnuts around Saint John's Day when they are still soft and milky
2. Cut a little off the top and bottom
3. Pierce 2 to 3 holes with a stick on each side
4. Lay them in fresh spring water for 8 days
5. Cook them in a brass pan for a quarter hour in water
6. Pour off the water, add fresh water, and repeat this three times
7. Test the nuts with a small stick to see if they are soft; if not, cook them once more
8. When soft, place them in cold water
9. Drain the water 3 times
10. Lay them on a hair sieve and let them dry
11. Lard the nuts with cinnamon and cloves
12. Lay them in a sugar jar
13. For 50 nuts, take 1-1/2 pounds sugar
14. Clarify this in a brass pan with cinnamon water until it spins a thick thread
15. Pour the clarified sugar over the nuts
16. Let them stand for 2 days
17. On the third day, pour off the sugar again
18. Let it boil once more, add another piece of sugar
19. Pour it over the nuts again and repeat this 3 to 4 times
20. The last time let the nuts cook along with it for a while
21. Preserve them well in a glass
22. Lay paper soaked in cherry spirits on top
23. Store in a dry place

title: Baumwollesuppe (Cotton Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- 4 tablespoons sweet cream
- 5 tablespoons flour
- meat broth
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Beat the butter in a bowl until very light
2. Gradually stir in the eggs, sweet cream, and flour
3. Drop this batter by spoonfuls into boiling meat broth
4. Season with salt and nutmeg

title: Bechamellesauce (Bechamel Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 g magerer Speck
- 30 g Zwiebeln
- 10 g Margarine
- 10 g Mehl
- 1/8 l Wasser
- 1 Essl. weisser Kaese, aufgeloest in 1/8 l Magermilch
- 1 Essl. Parmesankaese
- 1 Prise weisser Pfeffer
- Salz nach Geschmack

## Directions

1. Der schwartenlose Speck wird in Wuerfel, die Zwiebeln werden in Scheiben geschnitten.
2. Den Speck schwitzt man mit den Zwiebelscheiben in der Margarine im irdenen Topf, fuellt das Mehl dazu und vollendet die Sauce, indem man Wasser, Kaesemilch, Parmesankaese, Pfeffer und Salz hinzufuegt; man laesst die Sauce langsam einkochen, ruehrt sie durch ein Haarsieb und erwaermt sie nochmals.

title: Bechemelle (Bechamel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- veal, cut into small pieces
- butter
- onion
- yellow turnips
- celery
- 3 handfuls flour
- 4 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- butter for greasing the mold
- eggs for coating
- grated bread for coating
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Cut veal into small pieces and braise it gently in a saucepan with a piece of butter, onion, yellow turnips, and celery.
2. Add 3 handfuls of flour and braise together again.
3. Press everything through a sieve.
4. Beat 4 eggs into it.
5. Stir it together well with salt and nutmeg in a bowl.
6. Butter a long mold and fill it with the mixture.
7. Poach it in a water bath.
8. Cut or shape small cakes from it, dip them in eggs and grated bread, and fry them in hot lard.

title: Beefsteak (Beef Steak)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of loin roast
- salt
- pepper
- fine oil
- butter
- onion
- gravy jus

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of loin roast into small pieces, but not as thick as English beefsteaks
2. Beat them flat with your hand
3. Rub them with salt and pepper
4. Pour fine oil into a vessel and turn the beefsteaks in it
5. Place them in a pan with a piece of butter
6. Add a weighed onion
7. Let them fry golden brown
8. Arrange the whole on a plate
9. Pour the sauce and a tablespoon of roast jus over them
10. Serve with potatoes

title: Beefsteak auf englische Art (English-Style Beefsteak)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- piece of loin roast (from the loin)
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- parsley
- anchovy
- butter
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of loin roast into finger-thick pieces
2. Beat them a little
3. Rub them with salt and pepper
4. Lay them over good coals on a grill and pour melted butter over them
5. Meanwhile make an anchovy butter as follows: weigh parsley with a carefully cleaned and boned anchovy
6. Work this into a piece of butter
7. When the beefsteak on the grill is done, arrange it on a plate
8. Put a little of the butter on each piece, pour lemon juice over it and serve quickly
9. Note: these beefsteaks should still be juicy in the middle and are done in a quarter hour

title: Beefsteak mit Mostrichsauce (Beef Patty with Mustard Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g ground beef
- 25 g beef fat cracklings
- 1 large cooked potato
- 20 g margarine or mixed fat
- 15 g onion dice
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1/4 l water
- pepper and salt for the sauce

## Directions

1. Grate the potato and weigh the fat cracklings.
2. Mix the beef, cracklings, potato, pepper and salt and form 2 beef patties of 1-1/2 cm thickness using two spoons on a plate. Press diagonal squares into them with a knife, place the patties in hot smoking fat in a skillet and fry them for 6 minutes, turning frequently on both sides.
3. When the patties are brown, brown the onion dice in the fat and place them on the patties, then sweat the flour in the fat until yellow, add 1/4 l water mixed with mustard and vinegar, let the sauce come to a boil, season with salt and pepper and serve separately as mustard sauce.

title: Belgrader Brod (Belgrade Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound peeled almonds
- 1/4 pound almonds with rose water
- 12 Loth sugar
- 10 Loth flour
- 1 Quint pounded cinnamon
- 1 Quint cloves
- 1 lemon
- 1 knife-tip potassium carbonate
- 2 to 3 eggs
- wafers (Oblaten)

## Directions

1. Cut 1/4 pound peeled almonds as finely as possible
2. Pound 1/4 pound almonds with rose water as finely as possible
3. Mix both together in a bowl
4. Add 12 Loth sugar, 10 Loth flour, 1 Quint pounded cinnamon, 1 Quint cloves, the finely chopped yolk of 1 lemon, and 1 knife-tip potassium carbonate
5. Beat in 2 to 3 eggs to form a dough
6. Roll out finger-thick and cut into finger-length pieces
7. Flatten slightly with the knife
8. Let them stand on wafers on a black sheet for a quarter hour
9. Bake in the oven

title: Berliner Pfannenkuchen (Berlin Pancakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 2 spoonfuls good thick beer yeast
- lukewarm milk
- 8 loth butter
- 4 egg yolks
- salt
- sugar
- lukewarm cream
- 1/4 pound large and small raisins
- cinnamon
- lard

## Directions

1. Make a small pre-dough from 1 pound flour, 2 spoonfuls good thick beer yeast, and lukewarm milk, and let it stand and rise
2. Stir 8 loth butter in a bowl until foamy
3. Beat in 4 egg yolks one after another
4. Work the stirred butter and flour together
5. Add salt and sugar, then beat the dough for another quarter hour; lukewarm cream can be added if it is still too stiff
6. Set the dough in warmth and let it rise, but not until completely ripe
7. Meanwhile, take 1/4 pound large and small raisins
8. Roll out half the dough on the noodle board to knife-back thickness
9. Brush with melted butter and cover completely with raisins and currants
10. Roll out the other half of the dough equally thin and lay it on the first cake
11. Cut or punch out cakes with a round model
12. Fry beautifully golden in a pan with hot lard
13. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Bienennester (Bee's Nests)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 3 spoonfuls brewer's yeast
- 1 schoppen milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/4 pound small raisins
- 1/4 pound large raisins
- sugar
- good pears
- 1 schoppen wine
- 1 lemon, zest of
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 3 loth sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough with 2 pounds flour, 3 spoonfuls brewer's yeast and 1 schoppen milk, let it rise in warmth
2. Cut in 1/4 pound butter, 2 egg yolks and 2 whole eggs
3. Beat the dough for a quarter hour, let it rise again
4. Dust a cloth with flour, form the dough into noodles as large as a chicken egg
5. Pull them apart on the cloth, brush with butter
6. Fill with 1/4 pound small and 1/4 pound large raisins
7. Roll up the noodles, bake them golden yellow in a buttered dish sprinkled with sugar
8. For the compote: peel good pears, place in hot water in an earthen dish
9. Pour 1 schoppen wine over, add the yellow of 1 lemon, a piece of whole cinnamon and 3 loth sugar
10. Cook with these, and when the pears are soft, serve with the nests

title: Bierfisch (Beer Fish)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg fish
- 50 g fat
- 20 g onions
- 40 g flour
- 1 spice dose
- 2 tablespoons soup greens
- 1 bread crust
- 15 g salt
- 2 tablespoons syrup
- 1 bottle brown beer

## Directions

1. Cut the prepared fish into hand-width pieces, then clean them so that the pieces remain round and whole and do not fall apart at the belly during cooking
2. Wash the pieces clean and quickly, dry them and salt with 10 g salt
3. Clean, wash and cut the soup greens into small strips
4. Peel the onions and cut into slices
5. Break up the bread crust
6. Render the fat, pour in the flour and stir well
7. Pour the beer over it, add the spices and let everything cook covered until the soup greens are soft
8. Season the sauce with syrup and 5 g salt
9. Place the fish in the boiling sauce, in which it must slowly cook until done
10. To check if the fish is done, pull out a fin; if it comes off easily without being bloody, the fish is done
11. Serve with the sauce in a bowl
12. Instead of syrup, grated gingerbread can be used, but then use less flour
13. Cook roe separately in boiling salt water and arrange on top

title: Bierkaltschale (Cold Beer Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 75 g grated bread
- 1 piece lemon or orange peel
- 1 pinch ground cinnamon
- 65 g sugar
- 1 bottle light beer
- 65 g currants

## Directions

1. Grate the bread and let the currants swell.
2. Mix the bread with spices and sugar, pour the beer over it; add the swollen currants; stir everything together and chill.

title: Biersuppe auf feine Art (Beer Soup, Fine Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 measure beer
- 6 lot sugar
- 1 piece cinnamon
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- butter
- 1 small spoon flour
- 1/2 measure good sweet cream
- 2 egg yolks
- bread
- lard

## Directions

1. Let 1/2 measure beer cook in a brass pan with 6 lot sugar, a piece of cinnamon, and the zest of 1/2 lemon
2. Put a piece of butter in a casserole and let 1 small spoon of flour brown in it
3. Pour in 1/2 measure of good sweet cream
4. Add 2 egg yolks and strain the beer through a hair sieve into the casserole
5. Let everything come to a boil together
6. Cut bread into small pieces, toast them golden in lard, and arrange the soup over them

title: Biersuppe auf gewöhnliche Art (Beer Soup, Common Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white beer
- butter
- 1 spoon flour
- 1 measure of good cooked milk
- bread
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Let the white beer cook in a brass pan
2. Skim off the white foam with a skimming spoon
3. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole
4. Add 1 spoon of flour and let it barely brown
5. Stir it smooth with the beer
6. Pour in 1 measure of good cooked milk
7. Let everything come to a boil together
8. Cut bread into cubes and arrange the soup over it
9. Do not forget salt and nutmeg

title: Biersuppe mit Sago (Beer Soup with Sago)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bottle light beer
- 65 g sugar
- 1 piece lemon peel
- 3 cloves
- 1 pinch salt
- 30 g sago

## Directions

1. Bring the beer to a boil with sugar and spices, sprinkle in the sago and cook for 15-25 minutes in a covered pot depending on the grain firmness; season with salt and remove the spices.

title: Birnen (Fresh Pears)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pears
- 1/4 liter water
- 30 g sugar
- 1 piece orange peel
- 2 g starch flour
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Peel the pears nicely, trim the stems (if the pears are large, cut them in halves, quarters or eighths, remove the hard core) and wash them. Dissolve the starch flour.
2. Let them simmer soft in an earthen pot with water, sugar and orange peel covered, add the starch flour and let the pears boil once more with it.

title: Birnen (Preserved Pears)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- muscat pears
- clove
- cinnamon
- water
- sugar (7/4 pound per 3 pounds pears)

## Directions

1. Peel the pears, remove the core and insert a clove, and whole cinnamon in place of the stem
2. Cook them soft in water, place on a hair sieve and let drain
3. In a brass pan, cook sugar with water from the pears until it spins a thick thread
4. Add the pears and cook as long as hard-boiled eggs
5. Repeat this process for 4 days
6. On the fourth day, fill the pears into a vessel, soak card paper in cherry brandy, place on the pears and bind the vessel tightly closed

title: Birnen in Essig (Pears in Vinegar)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 kg (3 Pfd.) pears
- 3/4 kg (1-1/2 Pfd.) sugar
- 1/2 l wine vinegar, not too sharp
- 1 piece lemon zest
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 10 cloves
- 2 tablespoons wine vinegar
- 125 g sugar
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Preparation: Prepare smaller pears by peeling them whole, larger pears by halving them and carefully coring the seed cavity, or table pears by peeling and cutting into quarters. While preparing the pears, dissolve 3/4 kg (1-1/2 Pfd.) sugar in vinegar and skim.
2. Preparation: Cook the spices (in a muslin bag) with the vinegar and sugar. Blanch the pears in water, sugar and vinegar, then lay them drained into the sugar vinegar and cook them until soft.
3. Fill the pears into glasses or pots and reduce the sugar vinegar until it falls in broad drops from the spoon. After it cools, pour it over the pears (leaving the spices behind) and seal the containers as directed.
4. After a few days, pour the pears onto a colander, cook the thinned vinegar down to the second degree, and after it cools, pour it over the pears returned to the containers.
5. If the liquid is still thinned by the juice from the pears, it must be reduced again; if it has reduced too much, sugar and vinegar must be added.

title: Birnen mit Speck (Pears with Bacon)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g lean bacon
- 1/2 liter water
- 1/2 kg pears
- 40 g sugar
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 5 g potato starch
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Peel the pears. Clean the bacon by rubbing it.
2. Half-cook the bacon in water. Cook the pears gently in the bacon broth, season with sugar and vinegar, and make creamy with the potato starch dissolved in cold water. You can also add 375 g peeled and blanched potatoes and cook together. Season the dish with salt.

title: Birnencompote (Pear Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- pears
- boiling water
- 1/2 lemon peel
- cinnamon stick
- 6 cloves

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and cut them in half
2. Pour enough boiling water over them to cover by a finger's height
3. Cook them soft with half a lemon peel, a whole cinnamon stick, and 6 cloves
4. Form 2 to 3 artichokes on a plate from the pears
5. Reduce the syrup a bit more
6. Strain it through a hair sieve over the pears

title: Birnencompote (Pear Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Pears
- boiling water
- half a lemon peel
- whole cinnamon
- 6 cloves

## Directions

1. Peel the pears and cut them in half
2. Pour boiling water over them such that it reaches a finger's height above the pears
3. Cook them in the water with half a lemon peel, some whole cinnamon and 6 cloves until soft
4. Form 2 to 3 artichokes from the pears on a plate
5. Let the syrup reduce a bit more
6. Pour the syrup through a hair sieve over the pears

title: Birnenlatwerge (Pear Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- good peeled bergamot pears or Zweibutzer pears (Lemon pears are best)
- half pound sugar per 2 pounds pulp
- water

## Directions

1. Cook the peeled pears in a pan in water until soft
2. When the water is completely evaporated, force them through a hair sieve
3. Put the preserve back in a pan (for 2 pounds of pulp add half a pound of sugar)
4. Cook it until it is thick, then let it cool
5. Store it in wooden boxes

title: Bischof (Bishop's Punch)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Maas good red wine
- 3 sweet Seville oranges
- 3 bitter Seville oranges
- 1 Loth whole cinnamon
- 6 cloves
- 12 Loth sugar

## Directions

1. Take 3 sweet and 3 bitter Seville oranges and cut lines around them
2. Broil them on the grate
3. Place the wine on coals
4. Add the whole cinnamon, cloves, sugar and the broiled oranges
5. Let everything cook together for half an hour
6. Strain the wine through a hair sieve into the serving dish
7. It can be served cold or warm

title: Biscuitkugelhopf (Biscuit Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg yolks
- egg whites beaten to stiff peaks
- half a pound of finely ground sugar
- one lemon, grated on the grater

## Directions

1. Stir together 12 egg yolks, the stiffly beaten egg whites, half a pound of finely ground sugar, and one grated lemon for half an hour until the mass becomes very thick
2. Gradually stir in half a pound of very fine flour
3. Brush a kugelhopf mold (or Turkish mold) with butter
4. Fill the mass into it
5. Bake until beautifully golden yellow

title: Biscuittorte (Biscuit Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- 20 egg whites
- one lemon
- 24 Loth of cornstarch
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir together one pound of sugar with 20 egg yolks and the snow (beaten whites) of 20 egg whites for half an hour.
2. When the mixture is thick, add one lemon rubbed on a grater along with its juice and 24 Loth of cornstarch to the mixture.
3. Butter a cake form, fill everything in and bake the cake in the oven.

title: Biscuittorte (Biscuit Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- snow of 20 egg whites
- 1 lemon, zest grated
- juice of 1 lemon
- 24 loth starch flour
- butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the 20 egg yolks and the snow of 20 egg whites for half an hour
2. When the mass is thick, add the lemon zest that has been grated on a grater, along with its juice
3. Add 24 loth starch flour to the mass
4. Brush a cake pan with butter
5. Fill the entire mixture into the pan
6. Bake the torte in the oven

title: Blanc Manger (Court Style) (Blank Manger)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound blanched almonds, of which 2 loth must be bitter
- water
- 3 schoppen water
- 1 Seville orange
- 1 lemon
- half a pound sugar
- 3 loth isinglass dissolved in water
- juice of 1 Seville orange
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 to 3 whole saffron leaves
- 2 spoons full calf's jelly
- 2 tablespoons white wine
- 3 to 4 drops cochineal color
- green carnation petals
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Pound 1 pound blanched almonds (of which 2 loth must be bitter) with water as fine as flour
2. Pour 3 schoppen water to it and press out firmly in a cloth, treating it as if making almond milk
3. Rub a Seville orange and a lemon on sugar, put the rubbed zest with half a pound sugar and 3 loth isinglass dissolved in water, along with the almond milk and juice of 1 Seville orange and 2 lemons, in a brass pan
4. Let everything cook together, press through a fine sieve into a clean dish and let it cool
5. Make 4 colored jellies as follows: for 2 to 3 whole saffron leaves take 2 spoons full calf's jelly and 2 tablespoons white wine; in a second dish pour 3 to 4 drops cochineal color and calf's jelly; in the third dish let calf's jelly melt, pound green carnation petals, press through a fine sieve, pour them with the calf's jelly
6. Brush a mold with almond oil, pour the colored jellies into it, let them set
7. Pour the almond milk over it, place the mold on ice and let everything set
8. Turn the contents onto a plate and garnish to taste

title: Blanc Manger auf grüne Art (Green-Style Blank Manger)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter pound blanched almonds, of which 2 loth must be bitter
- water
- 3 schoppen milk
- meat from 3 calf's feet
- 1 quarter pound sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 2 knife points flour
- good oil

## Directions

1. Pound 1 quarter pound blanched almonds (of which 2 loth must be bitter) with water as fine as flour
2. Prepare with 3 schoppen milk
3. Let the almond milk cook with the meat from 3 calf's feet and a quarter pound sugar
4. Mix 6 egg yolks with 2 knife points flour smooth, stir this with the cooked milk on the fire until very smooth
5. Press the mixture through a fine sieve and let it cool
6. Brush a mold with good oil, fill the cream into it and place on ice until it sets

title: Blaubeerenkompott (Blueberry Compote)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg blueberries
- 50 g sugar
- 1 piece orange peel
- 2 g starch flour
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Sort the blueberries, wash them and toss them with sugar in an earthen pot. Dissolve the starch flour.
2. Place the prepared blueberries on the stove in the earthen pot and simmer slowly with the orange peel for 1/4 hour. Then stir in the starch flour dissolved in water while stirring, let everything boil and arrange the dish.

title: Blaubeerkuchen (Blueberry Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Blueberries
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Distribute blueberries, sugar, and cinnamon on top
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Blaues Kraut (Red Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- onion
- vinegar
- flour
- meat broth
- sugar
- wine
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage finely.
2. In a casserole, steam a finely cut onion.
3. Add the cabbage, pour a small glass of vinegar over it.
4. Steam for one hour.
5. When quite tender, sprinkle a little flour over it, pour meat broth over it, and add sugar to taste.
6. Add half a glass of wine, pepper, and salt to taste.

title: Blinde Chokoladesuppe (Blind Chocolate Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 2 spoons flour
- good milk

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter in a casserole
2. Toast 2 spoons of flour light yellow in it
3. Stir it smooth with good milk
4. Pour in more milk as needed

title: Blinde Chokoladesuppe (Blind Chocolate Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Good milk
- 3 Loth sugar
- Ground cinnamon
- Bread

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole
2. Roast 2 tablespoons of flour in it until light yellow
3. Stir smooth with good milk
4. Add more milk as needed
5. Add 3 Loth sugar and ground cinnamon
6. Let it come to a boil
7. Cut bread into fine pieces for dunking and arrange the soup over the dunked slices

title: Blinde Punschtorte (Blind Punch Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 loth finely ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- zest and juice of half a lemon
- 6 egg whites
- 6 loth fine flour
- butter
- 2 tablespoons raspberries
- 2 tablespoons arrack
- 4 loth sugar
- 2 tablespoons cold water
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 6 loth finely ground sugar with 6 egg yolks and stir together for a quarter hour
2. Add the zest and juice of half a lemon
3. Beat 6 egg whites into stiff peaks
4. Fold 6 loth fine flour into the mixture
5. Butter a baking sheet, spread one quarter of the mixture on it, and let it half-cook in the oven
6. Mix 2 tablespoons raspberries with 2 tablespoons arrack, remove the sheet from the oven, spread the raspberries over it, spread the remaining mixture on top, and bake completely in the oven
7. For the icing: cook 4 loth sugar and 2 tablespoons cold water in a brass pan until it reaches the thread stage
8. Beat 3 egg whites into stiff peaks, stir into the cooked sugar
9. Turn the torte out onto a plate, cover with the egg white icing, and smooth the surface with a knife

title: Blinder Speckkuchen (Blind Bacon Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 loth butter
- 3 loth finely ground sugar
- half a lemon zested
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 loth ground sugar
- 4 loth melted butter
- half a lemon zested
- 8 loth fine flour
- 10 egg whites snow
- 3 loth citron cut into cubes
- one loth coarsely ground rock sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough from quarter pound flour, 2 egg yolks, 4 loth butter, 3 loth finely ground sugar, half a lemon zested and 2 spoonfuls sour cream on the pastry board
2. Work it until no butter is visible
3. Work it together
4. Roll out a cake from it
5. Coat a cake sheet with butter
6. Lay the dough in it
7. Press a rim with fingers as usual
8. Stir together 10 egg yolks, 10 loth ground sugar, 4 loth melted butter, half a lemon zested, 8 loth fine flour with the snow from 10 egg whites
9. Fill the mass onto the dough
10. Bake the cake in a not too hot oven half way
11. Sprinkle it with 3 loth citron cut into cubes mixed with one loth coarsely ground rock sugar
12. Bake it until completely done

title: Blitzkuchen (Lightning Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Loth melted butter
- 12 Loth sugar
- 12 Loth flour
- zest of 1 lemon
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Mix 12 Loth of melted butter, 12 Loth of sugar, 12 Loth of flour, the zest of 1 lemon, and 5 eggs very well together
2. Butter a springform pan
3. Bake the cake beautifully golden

title: Blutreinigende Kräutermilch für Kinder (Blood-Purifying Herb Milk for Children)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Edelleber-, Erdbeeren-, Körbel-, Löffel-, Pfennig- und Sternleberkraut
- Eiternessel
- Gänseblumenstöckchen
- Gundelreben
- Pfaffenöhrleinswurzel
- Sauerampfer
- Sauerklee
- Schafgarben
- Wegwartenwurzel
- Salt
- Milk
- Butter (optional)
- Egg yolk (optional)

## Directions

1. Pick the herbs very clean without washing them
2. Weigh one handful of each herb
3. Chop the herbs as finely as possible
4. Add one knife-tip full of salt
5. Press the juice into a glazed pot
