6. Leave it in the cellar overnight
7. In the morning, heat one Maas of milk
8. Pour 3 to 4 spoonfuls of the juice into the hot milk
9. Give it to the child to drink (a 10-year-old child gets one whole Schoppen, a 5-year-old gets half a Schoppen)
10. Continue this cure for 14 days
11. Make the juice fresh twice a week
12. Optionally add a small piece of butter and an egg yolk

title: Blutreinigende Kräutermilch für Kinder (Blood-Purifying Herbal Milk for Children)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- herb leaves including: noble liverwort, strawberry plant, chervil, spoonwort, pennywort, starred liverwort, fleawort, goose daisy flowers, ground ivy, pfaffenöhrleins root, sorrel, wood sorrel, yarrow, chicory root
- salt
- milk

## Directions

1. Pick the herbs carefully without washing them
2. Weigh a handful of each, made as fine as possible
3. Add a pinch of salt
4. Press the juice into a glazed pot
5. Place it in the cellar overnight
6. In the morning, bring 1 Maas milk to a simmer
7. Add 3 to 4 spoonfuls of the juice

title: Blutwürste auf deutsche Art (German-Style Blood Sausage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one large finely chopped onion
- fresh butter
- half a maas of pork blood
- half a maas of sweet cream
- half a pound of fresh, half-cooked bacon cut into small cubes
- finely chopped basil
- finely chopped thyme
- finely chopped marjoram

## Directions

1. Braise the finely chopped onion in fresh butter in a casserole
2. Pour half a maas of pork blood and half a maas of sweet cream over it
3. Add half a pound of fresh, half-cooked, finely diced bacon and finely chopped basil, thyme, and marjoram
4. Set over the fire and stir until warm
5. Fill the mixture into casings and place the sausages in boiling water
6. Let them simmer twice and then cool
7. Before serving, warm the sausages in hot water or weak broth, then brown them on both sides in a casserole with butter or good fat over strong heat

title: Boeuf à la Mode (Beef à la Mode)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from the hind quarter or a good tail piece
- vinegar
- wine
- roots of various kinds
- lemons
- bay leaves
- whole pepper
- cloves
- black bread
- meat broth or water
- lemon zest
- mace
- brown gingerbread

## Directions

1. Pound the beef until tender and place in a dish
2. Pour equal parts vinegar and wine as desired
3. Cut all sorts of roots and add lemons, bay leaves, whole pepper, and cloves
4. Cover the meat tightly and let it stand 2 to 3 days, turning it daily
5. On the day of cooking, put everything together in a casserole
6. Cut the crust from black bread and add it, pour in 1 spoonful of meat broth or water
7. Cover tightly and cook until soft in the oven or over coals
8. Remove the meat, press everything through a hair sieve, remove the fat
9. Put the sauce back in the casserole, add the meat, and let it cook together for another half hour
10. Add the zest and juice of half a lemon and a knife point of mace
11. If the sauce is not thick enough, soak a piece of brown gingerbread in a little meat broth until soft and stir it into the sauce

title: Bohnen (Beans)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- hot butter
- onions
- pepper
- nutmeg
- beef jus (optional)

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from the beans and split them once or twice
2. Cook with boiling water until soft
3. Drain the water
4. Sauté the beans with hot butter and onions
5. Before serving, add pepper and nutmeg, and if desired, a couple of tablespoons of beef jus

title: Bohnen (Preserved Beans)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- very small, half-grown beans
- salt
- grape leaves
- pepper

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from the beans and cut them as desired, large or small
2. Boil water in a kettle, salt it well and add the beans
3. Cook for 4 minutes, quickly remove them and place in cold water for a quarter hour
4. Place in a basket to drain well and dry on a noodle board
5. Preserve in a well-pitched small barrel as follows: lay grape leaves on the bottom, some coarsely pounded pepper and salt, then a layer of beans, pepper and salt on top, then again beans, continuing until the barrel is full
6. Press the beans down firmly by hand and cover the top of the barrel again with grape leaves

title: Bohnen auf andere Art (Preserved Beans, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fat beans (sword beans are best)
- salt
- grape leaves
- butter
- onion
- parsley
- flour
- meat broth or bouillon

## Directions

1. Cut the beans as for a bean salad (1 handful of salt per bowl of beans)
2. Mix with salt in a clean dish and place in a clean, well-pitched barrel lined with grape leaves on top and bottom
3. Press the beans down firmly, place a well-fitting lid on top and weight it down
4. To cook: let them cook in a dish with boiling water for an hour
5. Pour into a colander and pour cold water over so they stay green
6. Braise in a saucepan with a piece of butter, onion and parsley, sprinkle 3 fingerfulls of flour on top, shake them, add one ladle-full of meat broth or bouillon and cook uncovered so they don't lose their green color

title: Bohnen auf englische Art (Beans in the English Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- onions
- parsley
- butter
- flour
- beef broth
- nutmeg
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook the beans as in the previous recipe but not quite as soft
2. Steam onions and parsley in butter in a casserole
3. Add the beans and steam everything together until soft
4. Sprinkle a little flour over them
5. Add a little beef broth, nutmeg, and some sugar
6. The beans are very tasty and good prepared this way

title: Bohnen auf gewöhnliche Art (Beans in the Ordinary Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beans
- fat
- 2 tablespoons of flour
- onions
- parsley
- bean herb
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Clean, wash, and cook the beans in boiling water
2. When soft, drain them
3. Take a piece of fat in a pan and brown 2 tablespoons of flour in it
4. Add onions, parsley, and bean herb
5. Deglaze with half a large spoon of beef broth and bring to a boil
6. Pour this over the beans and let them cook for another half hour

title: Bohnen in Essig (Beans in Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- young beans without kernels
- salt
- pepper
- grape leaves
- strong wine vinegar
- lard
- Provence oil (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the young beans small
2. Cook water in a kettle, add the beans and let boil twice, quickly remove them and place in cold water
3. Let lie for a quarter hour, pour into a colander and let drain well, then dry on a cloth
4. Line the bottom of the intended dish with grape leaves and coarsely pounded pepper mixed with salt
5. Make a layer of beans, press firmly by hand, sprinkle some salt and pepper on top and another layer of beans, continue until finished
6. Pour a bottle of strong wine vinegar over them
7. Melt lard, pour over the beans (must be half a finger's height above them; Provence oil can be used instead of lard)
8. The beans can be used as a salad or served with beef
9. Bind the dish tightly closed and place in a cool location

title: Bohnen oder Linsen, Saure (Sour Beans or Lentils)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g beans or lentils
- 10 g salt
- 1 pinch baking soda
- 1 liter water
- 30 g bacon
- 20 g onions
- 1 spice mixture
- 1/4 liter water
- 1 tablespoon syrup
- 15 g flour
- 1-2 tablespoons wine vinegar to taste

## Directions

1. Soak the legumes the evening before; dice the bacon and onions.
2. Cook the beans or lentils in salted water with a pinch of baking soda until soft and pour onto a sieve. Brown the bacon; remove the cracklings and keep warm; soften the onion in the fat, add the flour, stir and brown, fill the sauce with the spices, vinegar, syrup and water, cook 1/2 hour open in an earthen pot; mix the soft legumes with it and heat. When serving, sprinkle the dish with the bacon cracklings.

title: Bohnen ohne Brühe (Green Beans Without Broth)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) green beans
- 2 liters water for blanching
- 3/4 liter water
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 30 g lean bacon or 25 g fatty bacon or 1 tablespoon fat
- 1/2 teaspoon diced onion
- 2 teaspoons flour
- 1 1/2 tablespoons mild vinegar
- 1 pinch pepper

## Directions

1. Prepare: Remove the beans, break or cut them and wash; dice the bacon and onions.
2. Blanch the beans and cook them tender in fresh salt water. 1/2 hour before serving, fry the bacon until yellow, add the diced onions and fry them tender, sweat the flour with the bacon and onions until done, stir in the vinegar and pour everything over the beans, which are seasoned with pepper. You can also add boiled potatoes.

title: Bohnen, Getrocknete (Dried Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 g dried snap or breaking beans
- Water for cooking
- 15 g fat
- 8 g flour
- 1/4 liter water
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 sprig pepper herb
- 250 g peeled potatoes
- Water and salt for blanching
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the potatoes into pieces.
2. Put the beans on with cold water, bring to a boil, drain and finish like fresh beans; drain the blanched potatoes, lay them on the beans; both cook together covered for 3/4 hour until soft and are then mixed together.

title: Bohnen, Grosse oder Saubohnen (Large or Fava Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 kg large beans
- 2 liters water
- 8 g salt
- 20 g fat
- 3/8 liter broth
- 15 g onions
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1/2 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 5 g flour
- 1 sprig bean or pepper herb

## Directions

1. Shell and wash the beans; rub the onions; chop the parsley.
2. Half-cook the beans in salted water. Drained, cook them in the broth with the pepper herb until done, add flour, onions sweated in fat and parsley, pepper and salt to taste and serve the beans, thoroughly cooked, very hot. Remove the bean or pepper herb.

title: Bohnen, Gruene (Brechbohnen) (Green Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg green beans
- 2 liters water for blanching
- 3/4 liter mutton broth
- 1 sprig bean or pepper herb
- 1 tablespoon melted beef or mutton fat
- 8 g flour
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Set 2 liters of water in a covered pot on the stove. Remove the strings from the beans and break into even pieces; pick over and chop the parsley.
2. When the water boils, put in the beans and blanch several times, pour onto a colander or drain very dry, pour over with cold water and let drip. Put the beans in a pot and cook slowly covered in the mutton broth with the bean or pepper herb until done. Melt the fat in a small bowl, add the flour and stir with the fat until it bubbles, then pour in several spoonfuls of bean broth, add the whole thing to the soft beans and bring the vegetable to a boil again while stirring. Remove the bean herb. Season the beans with pepper, salt and chopped parsley and serve in a bowl.

title: Bohnen, Gruene und Weisse (Green and White Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg green beans
- 3 liters water for blanching
- 100 g white beans
- 1 liter water
- 3/4 liter mutton broth
- 1 sprig pepper herb
- 1 scant tablespoon flour
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Soak white, dried beans overnight.
2. The next morning cook them with cold water until soft and pour them into the green bean vegetable prepared according to the previous recipe.

title: Bohnen, Grüne, zu Salzen (Salted Green Beans for Storage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 kg (10 Pfd.) beans
- 1 kg (2 Pfd.) salt

## Directions

1. Pick snap beans and string beans while they are still tender and not tough or stringy. Remove the strings, cut them finely or break them, and immediately mix with salt.
2. Spread a layer of salt on the bottom of a large tall stoneware or Bunzlau pot, then layer the salted beans without any other additions and pack them down firmly with a wooden stirring stick, which is important for proper preservation.
3. Lay a piece of clean old linen cloth on top, which prevents mold formation and can later be easily washed if necessary. Then place a porcelain lid or similar on top, which is weighted down with a clean field stone. The pot of beans can be stored in any place that is not too warm.
4. Alternatively: Pack them layer by layer with salt in glasses or pots. Lay a suitable small board on top, weighted down with a stone. As soon as brine has formed, remove the stone and board, pour melted warm fat over the brine, and tie up the pot. Larger quantities, well mixed with salt, can be placed in a stone pot under the weight of a field stone lying on a clean cloth and used as needed. The cloth should be washed frequently.

title: Bohnen, Süss-Saure (Sweet and Sour Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g white beans
- 1 1/2 liters water
- 10 g salt
- 30 g bacon
- 1 medium onion
- 1/2 liter water
- 2 tablespoons syrup
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- vinegar to taste
- 30 g flour
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare: Soak the beans the evening before; dice the bacon and onion; prepare 1/2 liter water for the sauce and season with syrup, vinegar, pepper and salt.
2. Cook the beans with 1 pinch salt and cold, soft water, blanch, drain and cook covered slowly and tender with 1-1/2 liters water and 8 g salt. Meanwhile, fry the bacon cubes in an earthenware pot, remove the cracklings and keep them warm, let the sugar brown in the fat, add the diced onions to the fat and let them brown, as well as the flour. Add the liquid slowly with stirring and it must cook thick with the brown flour. Drain the tender beans, mix with the finished sauce, taste again, pour into a bowl, sprinkle the cracklings over and serve.

title: Bohnen-, Linsen- oder Erbsensuppe (Bean, Lentil or Pea Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g beans, lentils or peas
- 1 pinch soda
- 1 tablespoon celery cubes
- 250 g beef
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 1 teaspoon chopped celery greens or leek
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1/2 tablespoon onion cubes
- 1/2 tablespoon flour
- 2 l water

## Directions

1. Soak the beans, lentils or peas the evening before according to instructions.
2. Place the legumes in 1 l cold water with soda, heat and drain. Add beef, celery cubes, salt and 1 l water to the legumes, seal the pot tightly and cook the contents slowly for 2 hours. In a small bowl, sweat the onions, chopped greens and flour, add a few spoonfuls of soup and cook the flour mixture with the entire dish, which you season with pepper.
3. Serve the meat separately.

title: Bohnen-, Linsen- oder Erbsensuppe ohne Fleisch (Bean, Lentil or Pea Soup without Meat)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g beans, lentils or peas
- 1 pinch soda
- 50 g lean bacon or pork rinds
- 2 l water
- 8 g salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 small onion
- 1/4 teaspoon marjoram

## Directions

1. Soak beans, lentils or peas the evening before according to instructions; scrape and wash the rinds or cut the bacon into small cubes; peel the onion.
2. Place the legumes in 1 l cold water with soda, heat and drain, and cook gently with 1 l water, onion cubes, rinds or bacon cubes, salt and marjoram; crush slightly with a ladle or press through a sieve as desired, then reheat, season with pepper, salt and marjoram and serve.
3. Alternative: the legumes can be cooked with 65 g salted meat instead of rinds or bacon.

title: Bohnensalat (Bean Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1-1/2 pounds) snap beans or wax beans
- 2 liters water and 10 g salt
- 10 g onions
- 1 tablespoon oil
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 tablespoon broth
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1/2 teaspoon parsley or chives

## Directions

1. Prepare: Remove the beans, break them, wash them. Chop the parsley, grate the onion.
2. Cook the beans in plenty of boiling salt water; once they feel very tender, run them under cold water on a sieve. After draining well, mix them with oil, a little broth or hot water and salt, and mix with a spoon; gradually add onions, pepper and vinegar and mix carefully until all liquid is absorbed; then taste the beans carefully, sprinkle chopped parsley or chives over them and arrange the salad in a mound.

title: Boiled Ham (Gesottener Schinken)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Cold water
- Various herbs
- Parsley, fresh
- Breadcrumb flour, grated
- Pepper
- Wine (optional)
- Coarse flour and water (for baking variation)

## Directions

1. Wash the ham thoroughly.
2. Lay it overnight in cold water.
3. In the morning, place it in a large pot or kettle over the fire and let it boil, adding all kinds of herbs.
4. When it is half cooked, remove it from the fire and let it sit in the pot until cold.
5. Remove the rind.
6. Weigh a handful of fresh parsley and an equal amount of grated breadcrumb flour, add 2 knife-tips of pepper, mix together, and sprinkle over the ham.

title: Bouillon (Beef Broth)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds beef
- 2 pounds veal bones
- 1 old hen
- cold water
- salt
- 2 scraped and cut celery roots
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 yellow carrots
- leek
- parsley root
- 1 onion studded with 4 cloves

## Directions

1. Put the beef, veal bones, and hen together in a three-measure pot with cold water and some salt
2. Bring to a boil and skim off the foam diligently
3. Add the scraped and cut celery roots, bay leaves, yellow carrots, leek, parsley root, and the onion studded with cloves
4. Let it cook over gentle heat until the meat is tender
5. Remove the meat, pour the broth through a hair sieve, skim off the fat, and set it in a cool place
6. This bouillon is used for all white sauces

title: Bouletten (German Meatballs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g meat scraps
- 20 g cracklings
- 1/2 old roll
- 1/2 egg
- 1 pinch pepper
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 tablespoon grated breadcrumbs
- 15 g fat or margarine
- 1 knife tip grated onion
- 1/8 l water

## Directions

1. Rub the roll into crumbs, break it up, put it in a pot and pour 1/8 l water over it. Finely chop the cooked lean meat and cracklings on a wooden board with a mincing knife.
2. In a bowl, mix the roll pressed out in a clean cloth or sieve with egg, salt, pepper and grated onion, mix thoroughly with the minced mixture and adjust the seasoning.
3. Heat fat or margarine in a pan until smoking, form 4 equal sized balls from the meat mixture, flatten them, press diagonal squares into them with the back of a knife, roll them in grated breadcrumbs, slowly fry them for 6 minutes on both sides in smoking fat until they have a nice color and serve them poured over with the fat.

title: Bozener Hutzelnbrod (Bolzano Fruit Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 vierling medlars
- 1 pound raisins
- 1 pound wine grapes
- 2 pounds pitted and cleaned plums
- 12 figs
- 1 pound blanched and finely cut almonds
- 2 loth cinnamon
- 1/2 loth cloves
- 6 kreuzer cubeb and cardamom
- 1 pound citron and bitter orange peel
- 3 handfuls flour
- water

## Directions

1. Cook medlars until very soft and cut into small pieces
2. Remove pits and stems
3. Mix together raisins, wine grapes, plums, figs, almonds, spices, citron, and orange peel thoroughly
4. Place flour on a pastry board and make a firm dough with water
5. Roll out very thick as a knife back
6. Spread the fruit mixture inside
7. Form into a round loaf no more than 4 fingers high
8. Press lightly and place in the oven
9. Bake until golden
10. For the icing: stir egg whites, finely ground sugar, and lemon juice into a thick icing
11. Coat the bread thickly with the icing
12. Garnish with candied fruits as desired

title: Braten von Schwarzwildbret (Roast Wild Boar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of young wild boar
- wine
- vinegar
- roots and herbs
- coarsely crushed spices
- bacon
- capers
- smoked herring
- onion
- lemon
- egg whites
- cinnamon
- mace

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the leg of young wild boar and cut the lower bone so that the hooves and skin remain attached.
2. Place it in a mixture of equal parts wine and vinegar with all sorts of roots, herbs, and coarsely crushed spices, turning it often.
3. On the day it is to be served, remove it from the marinade.
4. Make holes in the bottom with a knife and insert a piece of bacon into each hole.
5. Roast it in a casserole in the oven until beautifully golden, along with the marinade.
6. Finely chop one tablespoon of capers, one smoked herring, one onion, and the zest of half a lemon.
7. Mix together two egg whites, a knife-point of cinnamon, the same amount of mace, and the juice of half a lemon in a bowl.
8. When the leg is tender, spread this mixture on top of it to the thickness of a knife-back.
9. Roast it in the oven until golden, basting it 2 to 3 times with the fat.
10. Arrange the roast on a plate, garnish with lemons, place the shank with hooves on top, and serve the jus strained through a fine sieve separately.

title: Bratkartoffeln (Fried Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) potatoes (boiled)
- 50 g bacon or 40 g lard = 2-1/2 tablespoons melted fat
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. The boiled and slightly cooled unpeeled potatoes are peeled and cut into even slices. The bacon is cut into cubes and fried to a good color in a pan; the cracklings are removed; fat can also be used instead of bacon. The potatoes are put in the fat, when it steams, salt is sprinkled over and the potatoes are fried to a nice brown, stirring frequently with a spoon.

title: Bratknöpflein (Ground Meat Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound veal or beef forcemeat
- 1/2 Maas milk (if not already mixed in by the butcher)
- zest of half a lemon, finely minced
- 1/2 quarter pound rendered butter
- lukewarm water for cooking

## Directions

1. If the 1 pound veal or beef forcemeat is not already prepared with milk by the butcher, stir it with 1/2 Maas milk.
2. Mince the zest of half a lemon finely.
3. Stir 1/2 quarter pound rendered butter with the forcemeat and lemon zest thoroughly.
4. Place the dumplings in lukewarm water but do not cook them in it; if they turn rough, they are overcooked.
5. When they throw up a white foam, they are done.
6. Serve them in meat broth.

title: Bratwurst (Bratwurst Sausage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Bratwurst
- 3 g salt
- 1 tablespoon water
- 10 g flour (optional)
- 1 pinch pepper (optional)
- 1/4 liter broth or wheat beer (optional)
- 1 drop brown sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Wash the bratwurst and divide it into two equal pieces.
2. If the bratwurst is fatty enough, place it in the pan without additional fat, pour one spoon of water over it, and fry the sausage under frequent turning in its own fat to a nice color.
3. Place the finished sausage on a plate and pour the available fat over it.
4. If a vegetable with sauce is added, this is sufficient. Otherwise, add to the fat in the pan 10 g flour, 1 pinch pepper and salt, make a brown roux from it, add 1/4 liter broth or wheat beer while stirring, color the sauce with a drop of brown sugar and adjust the seasoning.
5. Alternatively, prepare the sauce first and let the sausage simmer in it for 1/2 hour.

title: Bratwurst (Bratwurst Sausage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 g bratwurst
- 15 g fat or margarine
- 3 g salt
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Wash the bratwurst and divide it into two equal pieces.
2. If the bratwurst is fatty enough, it does not need to be fried in fat, instead place it in the pan, pour a spoonful of water over it and fry the sausage in its own fat under frequent turning to a nice color, place the finished sausage on a plate and pour the available fat over it.

title: Braune Jussuppe (Brown Meat Broth Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- Onions
- Yellow turnips
- 3 pounds lean beef
- 1 head white cabbage
- Water
- Meat broth
- 1 half Maas water (for dumplings)
- Butter the size of an egg (for dumplings)
- Fine flour (for dumplings)
- 5 to 6 eggs (for dumplings)
- 2 knife points of salt (for dumplings)
- Lard (for frying dumplings)

## Directions

1. Place a piece of butter, cut onions and yellow turnips in a casserole
2. Cut 3 pounds of lean beef into small pieces and brown them nicely in the casserole
3. Add a head of white cabbage cut up
4. Pour a ladle full of water over it
5. When it is brown, cover the casserole for a little while
6. Pour in meat broth and let it cook 2 to 3 hours
7. Pass it through a hair sieve into another casserole and let it come to a boil
8. For the fried peas: bring half a Maas water to a boil in a brass pan with a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
9. Stir in fine flour until it becomes a thick paste
10. Transfer to a bowl and beat in 5 to 6 eggs
11. Add 2 knife points of salt
12. Force this dough through a sparrow sieve into hot lard and fry the peas until nicely yellow

title: Breiauflauf (Porridge Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 4 loth butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 4 loth raisins
- 5 egg whites
- butter for the dish

## Directions

1. Cook a child's porridge from 1/2 maas milk.
2. Lightly stir it with 4 loth butter in a bowl.
3. Add 5 egg yolks one after another.
4. Add 4 loth sugar with a lemon zest grated into it, finely ground.
5. Mix in 4 loth raisins and the snow from 5 egg whites.
6. Brush a porcelain bowl with butter, fill the soufflé into it, and bake at low heat.

title: Brennsuppe (Roux Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 spoons flour
- lard
- cold water
- salt
- nutmeg
- bread

## Directions

1. Roast 2 spoons of flour yellow in hot lard in a pan
2. Extinguish it with cold water
3. Stir it smooth
4. Pour on as much water as needed
5. Add salt and nutmeg
6. Cut bread into small cubes and let it come to a boil, or cut the pieces directly into the serving dish and let the broth run through a sieve over them

title: Brieschen (Calf Sweetbreads)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf sweetbreads
- butter
- onion
- lemon
- bay leaf
- 2 blades of mace
- meat broth
- flour
- water
- salt

## Directions

1. Lay the sweetbreads in lukewarm water to remove the blood
2. Put them in cold water and bring to a boil
3. Remove them and pass through cold water again
4. Remove the membrane and lard them nicely
5. Put a piece of butter in a casserole, lay the sweetbreads with sliced onion, lemon, bay leaf, and mace in the butter
6. Pour a little meat broth over them and braise covered for half an hour
7. Sprinkle three finger widths of flour over the sweetbreads, turn them over, pour a little cold water over, fill with meat broth, and let everything cook together for a quarter hour
8. When serving, lay the sweetbreads on a plate, strain the sauce through a fine sieve over them, and salt to taste

title: Brieschen in Trüffelsauce (Calf Thymus in Truffle Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf thymus
- butter
- flour
- strong jus
- glass of Bordeaux or Markgräfler wine
- 2 to 3 truffles
- anchovy
- salt

## Directions

1. Blanch the thymus, remove the skin, lard it, and brown it nicely in butter in a casserole
2. In a piece of butter, let a spoonful of flour turn light yellow and deglaze with strong jus
3. Pour in a glass of Bordeaux or Markgräfler wine
4. Clean the truffles, cut off the rough parts, pound them in a mortar with a piece of butter and a clean anchovy, and add to the sauce
5. Cut the truffles themselves in a separate dish
6. Pour the sauce through a hair sieve over them
7. Add the thymus with the cooking liquid and salt to taste
8. When serving, arrange the truffles nicely on top of the thymus

title: Brieschensuppe (Sweetbread Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Two sweetbreads
- Onions
- Parsley
- A piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
- Two tablespoons of flour
- Good bouillon
- Meat broth
- Two egg yolks
- Two tablespoons of sour cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take two sweetbreads, blanch slightly, chop on a chopping block
2. Also chop onions and parsley, the latter not very finely
3. Place a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg in a casserole and add the chopped ingredients
4. Braise together for half an hour
5. Stir in two tablespoons of flour, pour in good bouillon
6. Stir everything until smooth with no lumps
7. Pour in as much meat broth as needed for the soup
8. Beat two egg yolks into the soup bowl with two tablespoons of sour cream
9. Arrange the soup over it, add salt and nutmeg

title: Brockelerbsen (Broccoli Peas)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- broccoli peas
- parsley
- onion
- butter
- flour
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Clean and sort the broccoli peas, discarding any damaged by worms
2. Steam parsley and onion in butter in a casserole
3. Add the broccoli peas and steam until soft
4. Sprinkle flour over them
5. Add some beef broth and let cook for a quarter hour

title: Brockelerbsen (Preserved Broccoli Peas)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half-ripe broccoli peas
- salt
- pig's bladder or bottle

## Directions

1. Shell the half-ripe broccoli peas and remove the wormy ones
2. To one measure of kernels add 2 handfuls of salt and let stand overnight
3. Fill a pig's bladder or bottle with the broccoli peas and salt, tie the bladder tightly closed and make a loop of twine so they can be hung

title: Brockelerbsen (Preserved Brockle Peas)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Halfreife Brockelerbsen (Half-ripe brockle peas)
- Salz (Salt)
- Schweinsblase or Bouteille (Pig's bladder or bottle)
- Bindfaden (Twine)
- Stroh or Heu (Straw or hay)

## Directions

1. Select half-ripe brockle peas and remove any wormy ones
2. For one measure of kernels, add 2 handfuls of salt
3. Let them stand overnight
4. Fill a pig's bladder or bottle with the brockle peas and salt together, tie the bladder securely, and make a loop of twine so it can be hung
5. Heat water in a kettle and add straw or hay to it
6. Place the bottle in the water so they do not touch each other; if using a bladder, hang it from a stick and cook it the same length of time as a bottle, but no longer than a couple of hard-boiled eggs
7. Remove from heat and let it stand until the water is cold
8. Hang the bladder in a dry place to preserve
9. If the brockle peas are in a bottle, cork it securely and place it in the cellar

title: Brockelerbsen auf französische Art (Brockle Peas in the French Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Schöne junge Brockelerbsen (Beautiful young brockle peas)
- Petersilie (Parsley)
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean and select beautiful young brockle peas
2. Place them in a saucepan with a small handful of parsley and a piece of butter
3. Braise for a quarter hour
4. Transfer to a tin box and add enough butter so that the peas are completely covered
5. Have the box soldered shut by a tinsmith
6. Cook another quarter hour in steam so that the fat remains on top
7. Preserve in the cellar
8. They will taste like fresh brockle peas

title: Brockelerbsen mit Hühnern (Broccoli Peas with Chicken for 40 People)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- broccoli peas
- butter
- parsley
- onion
- 6 young chickens
- flour
- sugar
- consommé

## Directions

1. Steam the broccoli peas in a casserole with butter, a handful of chopped parsley and onion for half an hour
2. Steam 6 cleaned young chickens for half an hour
3. Pour the chicken juice over the broccoli peas
4. Add a small spoon of flour and 2 loths of sugar
5. Bring to a boil together
6. Cut the breast and legs from the chickens
7. Arrange the broccoli peas on a platter and garnish with the chicken pieces
8. Brush with melted consommé

title: Brod von hartgesottenen Eiern (Bread of Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 4 Loth sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- flour
- melted butter
- bacon
- coarse pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Press the yolk from 6 hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve onto a noodle board
2. Mix in 4 Loth sugar, 2 egg yolks, some yolk from 1 lemon, and flour
3. Pour melted butter on and work like a noodle dough
4. Form quarter-long sticks from this
5. Place them on a black sheet brushed with bacon
6. Brush with an egg and coarse pounded sugar
7. Bake golden in the oven
8. Serve with tea

title: Brodkuchen auf englische Art (Bread Cake English Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 quarter-pound sugar
- 1 pound peeled, ground almonds
- 14 egg yolks
- zest of 2 lemons, grated on a grater
- 1 Loth ground cinnamon
- 6 Loth grated bread
- snow from 6 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Mix 3 quarter-pound of sugar, 1 pound of peeled, ground almonds, 14 egg yolks, and the grated zest of 2 lemons in a bowl, stirring strongly for half an hour
2. Add 1 Loth of ground cinnamon
3. Stir everything together again
4. Add 6 Loth of grated bread and the snow from 6 egg whites
5. Fill the mixture into a buttered baking sheet
6. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Brodpanadesuppe (Bread Panade Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Dunked slices of white bread
- Weak meat broth
- Egg yolk

## Directions

1. Let dunked slices of white bread cook soft in a casserole with weak meat broth
2. Pass them through a hair sieve
3. Let them come together once more in the casserole and stir in an egg yolk

title: Brodschnitten (Bread Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Mundbrode (as much as needed)
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 maas milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 schoppen milk

## Directions

1. Grate Mundbrode on a grater and cut into slices
2. Place in a plafond
3. Beat 4 eggs with 1/2 maas milk and pour over the slices
4. Let stand for half an hour
5. Beat 2 more eggs with 1 schoppen milk
6. Turn the slices in this mixture and bake until golden

title: Broetchen (Bread Roll Sandwich)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 bread roll or salted roll
- 15 g butter

## Directions

1. Cut the roll lengthwise or around into 3 slices
2. Spread each slice evenly with soft butter, or if butter is forbidden, with plum puree or sauerkraut
3. On a plate, cut each slice lengthwise and, if the patient cannot bite, also cut into pieces crosswise
4. Optional: toast the white bread slices on a fork over an open flame and then spread
5. If topping with minced ham, a meat slice, or chopped egg yolk, trim the overhanging edges neatly so the rolls look attractive

title: Brotsuppe mit Backpflaumen (Bread Soup with Prunes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g old bread
- 1/2 tablespoon butter
- 5 g salt
- 1 l water
- 40 g sugar
- 12 prunes
- 1/4 l water
- cinnamon and orange peel

## Directions

1. Crumble the bread and place in a pot, pour 1 l water over it and soften the bread in it. Wash the prunes and place them in a pot with 1/4 l water, where they swell covered.
2. Place the soaked bread with salt on the stove, covered; place the soaked prunes, to which cinnamon and orange peel can be added, in another pot also covered. When the bread is cooked and completely dissolved, strain the soup through a sieve, add butter, sugar, the softened prunes along with their sauce and season the soup to taste.

title: Brühkartoffeln (Broth Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 pounds) potatoes
- 5 g salt
- 1 liter water
- 3/4 liter broth
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley or 1/4 teaspoon dried marjoram

## Directions

1. Prepare: Wash, peel and cut the potatoes into pieces as evenly as possible. Wash and chop the parsley.
2. Put the potato pieces in water and salt, bring to a boil, drain, add to the broth and cook covered until tender. When soft, let the pot stand open for a while so the broth properly combines with the potatoes, then add parsley or marjoram and arrange the dish in a bowl.

title: Brühreis mit Fleisch (Broth Rice with Meat)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 65 g beef
- 30 g rice
- 1/4 bunch celery
- 1 liter water
- 8 g salt
- 1 small carrot

## Directions

1. Prepare: Wash the meat and cut into cubes, peel celery, wash and cut into cubes, clean and grate the carrot. Wash the rice three times in lukewarm water.
2. Put the meat and celery cubes, salt, and carrot in 1 liter water covered; add the rice 3/4 hour before serving and cook until tender. You can also add washed potato pieces to this soup and cook them for 3/4 hour.

title: Brühsuppe mit Nudeln, Griess oder Einlauf und Rindfleisch (Broth Soup with Noodles, Semolina or Egg Drop and Beef)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g beef
- 1 tablespoon soup vegetables
- 1 l water
- 8 g salt
- 1 grated carrot or peel of a red onion

## Directions

1. Place the water with salt and any available cleaned bones on the stove covered; clean, wash and cut the soup vegetables; wash and pound the beef.
2. Once the water with the bones boils, add the meat; after it has cooked with the scum for some time, remove the scum, add the cut soup vegetables, and to color the broth a grated carrot or raw onion peel, seal the pot tightly and let the meat simmer slowly for 2-2½ hours. When the meat is tender, remove it and keep it warm, season the broth, remove the bones, skim the fat from the broth and add noodles, semolina or egg drop, cook the soup for another 10 minutes and serve soup and meat.

title: Butterauflauf (Butter Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 whole lemon
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- fine flour
- 3 tablespoons sour cream
- 14 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter until foamy.
2. Grate a whole lemon into 1/4 pound sugar, finely grind it, and stir in with 12 egg yolks, each time adding a knife point of fine flour.
3. When all the eggs are mixed in, add 3 tablespoons sour cream.
4. Beat the 14 egg whites to a snow and mix into the mass well.
5. Bake in a baking sheet brushed with butter.

title: Butterblumen (Butter Flowers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound butter
- half pound flour
- 4 spoonfuls sugar
- 3 spoonfuls wine
- 1 egg yolk
- ground almonds

## Directions

1. Make a dough from half pound butter, half pound flour, 4 spoonfuls sugar, 3 spoonfuls wine and one egg yolk on the pastry board
2. Roll it out strong knife-back thick
3. Cut out figures using all forms of small metal molds
4. Place them on a black baking sheet
5. Brush them with egg
6. Sprinkle them with ground almonds and bake

title: Butterbretzeln (Butter Pretzels)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound butter
- half pound flour
- one quarter pound sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 yolk
- 1 tablespoon sweet or sour cream
- bitter ground almonds

## Directions

1. Mix together one quarter pound butter, half pound flour, one quarter pound sugar, one egg, one yolk and one tablespoon sweet or sour cream in a bowl or on the pastry board
2. Form small pretzels or rings from it
3. Brush with egg yolk
4. Sprinkle bitter ground almonds on top and bake

title: Butterknöpflein (Butter Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound melted butter
- 6 eggs
- grated breadcrumb flour
- fine flour
- meat broth for cooking

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound melted butter until very foamy.
2. Add 6 eggs, and for each egg add a handful of grated breadcrumb flour and one cooking spoonful of fine flour (the flour is added gradually, not all at once).
3. Form dumplings the size of a walnut.
4. Cook them in boiling meat broth.
5. Add them to the soup.

title: Butterkuchen (Butter Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- Butter pieces

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Distribute sugar, cinnamon, and butter pieces on top
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Butterkuchen auf besondere Art (Butter Cake Special Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 3 quarter-pound butter
- 3 spoonfuls beer yeast
- 2 knife-tips mace
- 3 tablespoons rose water
- 2 Loth finely ground sugar
- quarter-pound butter
- 6 eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough from 1 pound of fine flour, 3 quarter-pound of butter, 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast, 2 knife-tips of mace, 3 tablespoons of rose water, and 2 Loth of finely ground sugar
2. Work it for a long time
3. Set it to a gentle warmth and let it rise
4. Place it on a noodle board and roll it out thumb-thick
5. Work a quarter-pound of butter into it
6. Coat a piece of paper with butter and place the cake on it
7. Put it in a springform pan
8. Beat 6 eggs and pour them over the cake
9. Place it in the oven
10. Bake it beautifully golden
11. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over it

title: Butterlaibchen (Butter Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- one quarter pound butter
- one quarter pound sugar
- one quarter pound finely ground almonds
- 1 to 1.5 quarter pounds flour
- zest of half a lemon
- a knife point of ground cinnamon
- lard
- coarsely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together 2 eggs, one quarter pound butter, one quarter pound sugar and one quarter pound finely ground almonds for a quarter hour
2. Work in 1 to 1.5 quarter pounds flour, the zest of half a lemon grated on a grater, and a knife point of ground cinnamon
3. Place cakes the size of a walnut from this dough onto a baking sheet brushed with lard
4. Brush with egg
5. Sprinkle with coarsely ground sugar and bake

title: Buttermilchsuppe (Buttermilk Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g old bread
- 1 l buttermilk
- 15 g flour
- 5 g salt
- 3 tablespoons syrup or 35 g sugar

## Directions

1. Cut the bread into small pieces and soften it with 3/4 l buttermilk.
2. Bring to a boil while stirring, then bind with the flour mixed into 1/4 l buttermilk, season the mixture with syrup or sugar and salt, bring to a boil, pass through a fine sieve while stirring, heat again and serve.

title: Butterstritzel (Butter Twisted Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls sugar
- water
- ground sugar
- egg white
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough from one pound butter, one pound flour, 4 egg yolks and 2 spoonfuls sugar (if it is too stiff, a little water can be added)
2. Work this together on a pastry board, fold it over, repeat this 4 times
3. The fifth time, roll it out leaf thin
4. Sprinkle ground sugar on it and fold it over 4 times
5. Cut pieces one finger long and two fingers wide from it
6. Place them on a black baking sheet
7. Brush with egg white
8. Sprinkle sugar on top and bake them in the oven until golden yellow

title: Butterteig zu machen (Butter Dough)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound good butter
- 1 pound fine flour
- 2 spoons sour cream
- 2 knife-tips salt
- 1 whole and half egg
- cold water

## Directions

1. Weigh 1 pound fine flour for 1 pound good butter and place flour on a pastry board.
2. Make a depression in the center and add 2 spoons sour cream, 2 knife-tips salt, 1 whole and half egg, and some cold water.
3. Work this dough gently.

title: Butterteig zu machen (Butter Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound good butter
- 1 pound fine flour
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- 2 knife-tips salt
- 1 whole egg and half an egg
- cold water

## Directions

1. Weigh the flour and place it on the noodle board
2. Make a depression in the middle and add the sour cream, salt, eggs, and cold water
3. Work the dough to a gentle firmness and roll it out finger-thick
4. Take the butter, knead it with a little flour and place it in the dough
5. Fold the dough over, roll it out and repeat this three times

title: Butterteig zu Pasteten (Butter Pastry for Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound butter
- 1 pound flour

## Directions

1. Make the dough as in recipe 267
2. Fold this dough five times on top of each other, because it should not rise as much

title: Butterteig-Schnecken (Butter Dough Spirals)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough
- 1/4 pound blanched almonds, not very finely ground
- Egg yolks
- Zest and juice of 1 lemon
- 4 loth sugar

## Directions

1. Roll out butter dough to the thickness of a knife back and cut into strips half a quarter-ell long
2. Mix blanched almonds with egg yolks, lemon zest, lemon juice, and sugar
3. Spread the mixture onto the strips
4. Roll the strips into spiral shapes
5. Bake on a flour-dusted sheet until golden

title: Böhmische Kolatschen (Bohemian Kolaches)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 3 spoons good white beer yeast
- 1 schoppen warm milk
- 5 egg yolks
- 2 loth ground sugar
- 12 loth butter
- apricot jam
- egg white snow
- sugar and vanilla

## Directions

1. Make a starter dough from flour, yeast, and warm milk in a bowl
2. Let it rise well in gentle warmth
3. Work in egg yolks and ground sugar by hand
4. Roll out on a pastry board
5. Distribute 12 loth butter on the dough and fold it over
6. Roll out again; repeat this process until no butter is visible
7. Return dough to bowl and let it rise
8. Form into round heaps and place on a baking sheet
9. Let them rise slowly
10. Press a depression with an egg
11. Fill with apricot jam
12. Top with a little egg white snow
13. Sprinkle with sugar and vanilla
14. Bake for half an hour, not too hot

title: Caffebrod (Coffee Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of flour
- 4 loths of sugar
- one egg
- 2 spoons of thick beer yeast
- lukewarm milk
- melted butter
- lemon peel
- anise

## Directions

1. Mix half a pound of flour, 4 loths of sugar, one egg, and 2 spoons of thick beer yeast with lukewarm milk to form a firm dough
2. Mix in some melted butter, lemon peel, and anise
3. Knead thoroughly on a pasta board
4. Form 2 loaves
5. Roll each one out the length of a shoe
6. Let them rise in gentle warmth
7. Brush with beaten egg
8. Bake in the oven

title: Caffeegefrorenes (Coffee Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 measure of milk or sweet cream
- 1/4 pound roasted coffee, only lightly roasted
- 4 loth sugar
- 9 egg yolks
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Pound the coffee coarsely in a mortar
2. Place the coffee and sugar in the milk
3. Bring everything to a boil over the fire
4. Beat 9 egg yolks in a pot and stir smooth with cold milk
5. Slowly pour the boiling coffee milk into the eggs
6. Let it cook briefly in the pan
7. Strain through a sieve
8. Let it cool completely
9. Treat it in the freezing tin according to the method given in No. 767

title: Caffeeküchlein (Coffee Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Maas milk or water
- one quarter pound butter
- one quarter pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 12 loth flour
- 6 eggs
- lard

## Directions

1. Cook half a Maas milk or water, one quarter pound butter and one quarter pound sugar (with one lemon zested into it) together for 4 minutes
2. Stir 12 loth flour into it and cook everything together until the dough is dry
3. Beat 6 eggs into it
4. Brush a black baking sheet with lard and sprinkle a little flour on it
5. Place heaps of the dough on it with a spoon and bake them in the oven until golden yellow

title: Casserollepastete auf genuesische Art (Casserole Pastry Genoese Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- noodle dough made with egg yolks
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Instead of macaroni, use noodle dough made with egg yolks
2. Cut long ribbons from it, like macaroni, as wide as a small finger
3. Take sour cream and prepare the pastry in the same way as macaroni pastries

title: Cervelas-Würste (Cervelat Sausage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- lean meat from a pork leg
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- basil
- coriander
- 1 pound of bacon to 4 pounds of meat

## Directions

1. Cut the lean meat from the pork leg
2. Mince this together with salt, pepper, cloves, basil, and coriander very finely
3. Pound it further in a mortar
4. Work it together in a bowl
5. Press the mass into a beef casing
6. Make sausages of desired size and hang them to smoke
7. These sausages can be served with peas and lentils

title: Champignons (Mushrooms)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Champignons (Mushrooms)
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the mushrooms
2. Let them drain
3. Cook them for a quarter hour with a large piece of butter
4. Place them in a dish and reduce the sauce further
5. Add enough butter so that the mushrooms are completely covered with it
6. Pour the sauce over them
7. Tie the dish shut and store

title: Chaudeau (Wine Custard)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 lemons
- 1/2 Maas wine
- sugar (totaling 1/2 pound with the lemon zest)
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 4 egg yolks
- cold water

## Directions

1. Rub 2 lemons on sugar to release the zest
2. Pour 1/2 Maas wine into a brass pan
3. Add the rubbed sugar, the juice of the 2 lemons, and enough additional sugar that it makes 1/2 pound total with the zest
4. Add 1 piece of whole cinnamon
5. Let everything cook a little together
6. Beat 4 egg yolks in a dish with a spoonful of cold water
7. Stir them smooth
8. Stir the boiling wine slowly to them
9. Set it back on the fire and let it draw
10. Pour it into the pot and whisk it until it is quite fine

title: Chokolade (Hot Chocolate)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Maas milk (or cream, which is better)
- 6 Loth chocolate
- 2 Loth sugar
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 3 egg yolks
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Calculate 6 Loth chocolate to 1/2 Maas milk
2. In a saucepan or brass pan, stir the chocolate smooth with a little water over the fire
3. Gradually stir in the milk along with 2 Loth sugar and 1 piece of whole cinnamon
4. Let this cook for a time
5. Stir 3 egg yolks smooth in a pot with cold milk
6. Slowly stir the chocolate to it
7. Let it draw once more in the pan and whisk vigorously in the pot with the chocolate until it foams

title: Chokoladeauflauf (Chocolate Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 loth chocolate
- 3 tablespoons water
- 2 loth butter
- 3 loth finely ground almonds
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the dish

## Directions

1. Melt 8 loth chocolate in a brass pan with 3 tablespoons water.
2. Add 2 loth butter and 3 loth finely ground almonds and let them cook together a little.
3. Put the mixture into a bowl and stir in 6 egg yolks one after another.
4. Beat the egg white to a snow.
5. Brush a dish with butter, put the mass in it, place it in the oven, and let it bake nicely. It will be done in about 15 minutes.

title: Chokoladeauflauf mit Brod (Chocolate Soufflé with Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 white bread rolls or milk bread
- 1 schoppen milk
- 6 loth butter
- 4 loth ground sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound grated chocolate
- 5 egg whites
- butter for the dish
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Cut 3 white bread rolls or milk bread finely as for soup and cook with 1 schoppen milk like porridge.
2. Put into a bowl and stir 6 loth butter, 4 loth ground sugar, 5 egg yolks, and 1/4 pound grated chocolate with the porridge for about 15 minutes.
3. Beat the 5 egg whites to a snow and mix into the mass.
4. Brush a bowl with butter, pour the mass into it, let the soufflé bake nicely golden, and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Chokoladegefrorenes (Chocolate Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound chocolate
- 1-1/2 schoppen of milk
- 1/8 pound sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- cold milk

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate in a brass pan
2. Add the milk and sugar to it
3. Let it cook together
4. Beat 8 egg yolks in a pot and stir smooth with cold milk
5. Slowly add the cooked chocolate
6. Set it over the fire again and let it cook briefly
7. Strain through a sieve
8. Let it become completely cold
9. Put it in the freezing tin

title: Chokoladeküchlein (Chocolate Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- half pound finely sifted sugar
- half pound grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Beat 2 egg whites to a stiff snow
2. Stir in half pound finely sifted sugar and half pound grated chocolate
3. Mix together
4. Place heaps of it on a black baking sheet brushed with wax and bake in the oven

title: Chokolademandeltorte (Chocolate Almond Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 egg yolks
- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 25 loth blanched, finely ground almonds
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 strong loth cinnamon
- zest of 1 lemon, minced
- snow of 8 egg whites
- 10 loth grated chocolate
- mussel flour for the pan
- 1/2 loth citron peel
- 1/2 loth orange peel
- 1 egg white for icing
- 1/8 pound sugar for icing
- 2 loth chocolate for icing

## Directions

1. Stir 16 egg yolks for half an hour
2. Take 1 pound finely sifted sugar, 25 loth blanched finely ground almonds, juice of 1 lemon, 1 strong loth cinnamon, and the minced zest of 1 lemon
3. Add the snow of 8 egg whites
4. Stir everything together for one hour
5. Finally add 10 loth grated chocolate
6. Bake the torte in a mold brushed with butter and sprinkled with mussel flour
7. For a better version, also add 1/2 loth citron peel and 1/2 loth orange peel
8. Make an icing from the snow of 1 egg white, 1/8 pound sugar, and 2 loth chocolate
9. Brush the torte with this icing and let it brown once more in the oven

title: Chokoladepudding (Chocolate Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound chocolate
- 2 rolls or milk breads
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound blanched, finely ground almonds
- 1/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- snow from 6 egg whites
- 3 loth chocolate
- milk as needed
- 1 knife-point ground cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate the chocolate on a grater
2. Remove the crusts from the rolls and soak and squeeze out the bread
3. Lightly stir the butter
4. Mix in the blanched almonds, ground sugar, and egg yolks
5. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
6. Coat a metal capsule with butter and dust with sugar
7. Fill the mass into the capsule
8. Cook in steam for one hour
9. For the sauce: stir 3 loth chocolate with as much milk as needed in a brass pan
10. Add one knife-point ground cinnamon
11. Bring to a boil, let it foam and bubble, and pour over the pudding

title: Chokoladepudding auf feine Art (Fine Chocolate Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound vanilla chocolate
- 1/2 cup good sweet cream
- 8 loth butter
- 4 loth fine flour
- 1/4 pound blanched, ground almonds
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound ground sugar
- snow from 6 egg whites
- 1/2 measure milk
- 1 piece vanilla
- 1 piece sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 fingers full flour
- cold milk as needed

## Directions

1. Cook the vanilla chocolate, cream, butter, and fine flour in a brass pan with constant stirring until it separates from the pan
2. Let it cool in a bowl
3. Stir in the blanched almonds, egg yolks, ground sugar, and beaten egg whites
4. Mix this with the above
5. Coat a pudding capsule with butter and dust with sugar
6. Place in a pot of boiling water
7. Cook the pudding for one hour
8. For the sauce: cook half a measure milk with one piece vanilla and one piece sugar together
9. Beat 2 egg yolks, whisk with 3 fingers full flour and cold milk until smooth
10. Pour this into the boiling vanilla milk
11. Cook everything together with constant stirring
12. Pour through a sieve and pour over the pudding

title: Chokoladeschnitten (Chocolate Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound blanched, finely ground almonds
- 1/4 pound finely grated chocolate
- 2 loth starch flour
- 6 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Whisk together 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar and 3 egg yolks for a quarter hour
2. Add 1/4 pound blanched, finely ground almonds and 1/4 pound finely grated chocolate
3. Mix in 2 loth starch flour
4. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 6 eggs
5. Butter a baking sheet and fill with the mixture
6. Bake until nicely golden
7. Cut into slices only on the second day

title: Chokoladetorte (Chocolate Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar, finely ground
- 20 egg yolks
- snow of 16 egg whites
- 1/4 pound grated chocolate
- 1/2 loth ground cinnamon
- 12 loth starch flour
- butter for the pan

## Directions

1. Stir the sugar with the 20 egg yolks and the snow of 16 egg whites for a quarter hour
2. Slowly stir in the grated chocolate, ground cinnamon, and 12 loth starch flour into the mass
3. Bake the torte in a tin pan brushed with butter

title: Citronenauflauf anderer Art (Lemon Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 8 loth butter
- 8 loth ground sugar
- 2 lemons
- 5 egg yolks
- 5 egg whites
- butter for the dish
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Cook a thick porridge from 1/2 maas milk and put into a bowl.
2. Add 8 loth butter, 8 loth ground sugar, 2 lemons grated on a grater, and the pith cut into strips.
3. Stir in 5 egg yolks and stir everything well for 15 minutes.
4. Beat the 5 egg whites to a stiff snow and mix with the porridge.
5. Brush the bowl that you will serve with butter, pour the mass onto it, let the soufflé bake nicely golden, and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Citronenbiscuit (Lemon Biscuit)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole lemon
- sugar (for grating)
- 2 egg whites
- 1/2 pound finely pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Grate a whole lemon on a piece of sugar
2. Stir the grated material with 2 egg whites and 1/2 pound finely pounded sugar to a thick icing
3. Roll out this icing on a noodle board
4. Press broad with the hand
5. Dip a biscuit mold in cold water and place the dough in it
6. Bake the biscuits in a cooled oven

title: Citronenbrei (Lemon Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- porridge
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook a good thick porridge, stir 2 egg yolks into it
2. Rub the yellow of 1 lemon on sugar, pound this and mix it under the porridge

title: Citronenbrei anderer Art (Lemon Porridge Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 5 to 6 egg yolks
- 2 loth butter
- 3 loth ground sugar with lemon zest
- 2 tablespoons cold milk

## Directions

1. Cook a good thick porridge from 1/2 maas milk
2. Stir 5 to 6 egg yolks in a casserole with 2 loth butter and 3 loth ground sugar with lemon zest rubbed on beforehand pounding
3. Add 2 tablespoons cold milk, let the eggs cook until they begin to thicken
4. Stir in the porridge, let it draw a skin, arrange it on a plate, carefully lift the skin from the pan and place it on top of the porridge

title: Citronenbrod (Lemon Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- salt
- 4 Loth fine sugar
- 8 Loth melted butter
- 2 spoonfuls good beer yeast
- 1/2 lemon
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a firm dough in a bowl from 1 pound flour, a knife-tip of salt, 4 Loth fine sugar, 8 Loth melted butter, 2 spoonfuls of good beer yeast, the yolk of half a lemon, 2 eggs and 2 yolks
2. Work it for a quarter hour on a noodle board
3. Form a long stick from it and place it on a black sheet sprinkled with flour
4. Let it rise well at gentle warmth
5. When it is ripe, brush it with a beaten egg
6. Bake in the oven
7. The next day, cut the stick into thin slices
8. Toast them beautifully golden on a black sheet in the oven

title: Citronenbrod anderer Art (Lemon Bread, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sifted sugar
- 2 egg whites
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 lemon
- 8 Loth flour
- wafers (Oblaten)

## Directions

1. Add the snow from 2 egg whites and 2 egg yolks to 1/2 pound sifted sugar
2. Stir for a quarter hour
3. Stir in the yolk of 1 lemon (grated on a grater) and the juice from half a lemon
4. Mix in 8 Loth flour
5. Place long or round loaves from this mass on wafers on a sheet
6. Bake them

title: Citronenbrod mit Hefe (Lemon Bread with Yeast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of fine flour
- 2 spoons of good beer yeast
- half a schoppen of lukewarm milk
- 4 loths of melted butter
- 2 whole eggs and 2 egg yolks
- the grated yellow of one lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place one pound of fine flour in a bowl
2. Make a small dough in the center of the bowl with 2 spoons of good beer yeast and half a schoppen of lukewarm milk
3. Add 4 loths of melted butter, 2 whole eggs, 2 egg yolks, and the grated yellow of one lemon
4. Beat the dough for a quarter hour
5. Let it rise until ripe
6. On a pasta board, form 2 long sausage shapes
7. Place on a baking sheet dusted with flour
8. Set in a warm place and let rise again
9. Brush with beaten egg
10. Sprinkle with sugar
11. Bake in the oven until beautifully golden yellow
12. Do not cut until the next day
13. Can be served with tea and for dessert

title: Citronencrême (Lemon Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound sugar
- 2 lemons
- 1/2 maas wine
- juice of the lemons
- 6 egg yolks
- a little flour
- 2 tablespoons water
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Rub the sugar with the zest of 2 lemons and cook with half a maas of wine and the juice of the lemons for 4 minutes.
2. Stir 6 egg yolks smooth with a little flour and 2 tablespoons of water.
3. Slowly stir the cooked wine into the eggs.
4. Let everything come together in the pan once more until it becomes smooth and thick.
5. Beat the 6 egg whites into stiff snow.
6. Fold the snow under the cream.
7. Once cooled, brown it with a glowing spatula dusted with sugar.

title: Citronencrême (Lemon Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar (quarter pound)
- lemons (2)
- wine (half a maas)
- egg yolks (6)
- flour
- water (2 tablespoons)

## Directions

1. Cook a quarter pound sugar rubbed with 2 lemons with half a maas wine and the juice of the lemons for 4 minutes
2. Stir 6 egg yolks smooth with a little flour and 2 tablespoons water

title: Citroneneis (Lemon Icing)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 lemon
- one quarter pound sugar
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of one lemon on one quarter pound sugar
2. Finely pound and sieve the remaining sugar
3. Press the juice of the lemon into it
4. Beat one egg white to snow
5. Stir it together for a quarter hour and use as desired

title: Citronenkuchen auf besondere Art (Lemon Cake Special Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lemons
- quarter-pound almonds
- rose water
- 8 eggs
- 8 Loth finely ground sugar
- 2 grated Kreuzer breads
- butter
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Grate the zest of 8 lemons on a grater
2. Remove the white skin from the zest
3. Cut the pulp into pieces
4. Squeeze out the seeds
5. Finely grind a quarter-pound of almonds with rose water
6. Mix with the lemon pulp and the zest from the grated lemons
7. Add 8 eggs
8. Stir in 8 Loth of finely ground sugar and 2 grated Kreuzer breads
9. Mix everything together for half an hour
10. Butter a springform pan
11. Fill the mixture into it
12. Bake the cake beautifully golden in the oven
13. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Citronenkuchen auf besondere Art (Lemon Cake, Special Preparation)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 lemons
- 1/4 pound almonds
- rose water
- 8 eggs
- 8 loths fine ground sugar
- 2 grated Kreuzerbrode
- butter
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Rub 8 lemons on a grater and remove the white skin
2. Cut the pulp into pieces and press out the pits
3. Mix 1/4 pound almonds with rose water, finely ground, together with the lemon pulp and the yellow from the grated lemons
4. Beat 8 eggs and mix in
5. Stir 8 loths fine ground sugar and 2 grated Kreuzerbrode into the mixture and beat everything together for half an hour
6. Coat a plafond with butter, fill the mass into it
7. Bake the cake in the oven until beautiful yellow
8. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Citronenkuchen auf englische Art (Lemon Cake, English Preparation)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 lemon peels
- lemon pulp
- 1 orange
- orange juice
- 3/4 pound ground sugar
- 1/2 pound blanched, roughly ground almonds
- 2 handfuls flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 spoon ground sugar
- butter
- rose water
- 6 eggs

## Directions

1. Finely chop the peels of 6 lemons
2. Mix the pulp with an orange rubbed on sugar and the orange juice
3. Add 3/4 pound ground sugar and 1/2 pound blanched, roughly ground almonds
4. Let stand for one hour
5. Make a dough with 2 handfuls flour, 2 egg yolks, 1 spoon ground sugar, butter the size of a chicken egg, and rose water on a pastry board
6. Roll out the dough and place it in a springform pan coated with butter
7. Beat 6 eggs and add to the mass described above
8. Fill into the pan and bake the cake
9. After baking, it can be glazed or topped with lemon ice

title: Citronenpudding (Lemon Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound blanched almonds
- egg white or rose water
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 7 egg yolks
- zest of 1 whole lemon, grated
- juice of 1 lemon
- 2 loth Geigenmehl
- 2 knife-points fine flour
- snow from 5 egg whites
- butter dough
- 1 schoppen wine
- 4 loth sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 knife-points fine flour
- cold water
- 1/2 glass Arak

## Directions

1. Grind the blanched almonds very fine with egg white or rose water
2. Lightly stir the butter in a bowl
3. Add the ground sugar and almonds
4. Beat in 7 egg yolks
5. Stir together for a quarter hour
6. Add the grated lemon zest and juice, plus Geigenmehl and fine flour
7. Beat the egg whites from 5 eggs to stiff peaks and fold in
8. Roll out butter dough knife-back thick
9. Cut finger-wide strips with a pastry wheel
10. Line a casserole so the strips hang over the edge and are woven together
11. Fill with the mass
12. Trim the strips around the edges and bake in the oven until golden (takes 1-1/2 hours)
13. Turn onto a plate
14. For the sauce: put 1 schoppen wine in a brass pan with 4 loth sugar and cook
15. Beat 2 egg yolks in a pot, add 2 knife-points fine flour, stir smooth with cold water
16. Add half a glass Arak
17. Stir in the boiling wine, put back in the pan and let it set
18. Pass through a sieve and serve separately

title: Citronenpudding in Aprikosensauce (Lemon Pudding in Apricot Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen water
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound sugar
- zest of 2 lemons
- juice of 1 lemon
- 4 loth fine flour
- 2 loth bitter almonds
- 2 loth sweet almonds
- 6 egg yolks
- snow from egg whites
- 8 pitted apricots
- 1 schoppen wine
- 1 schoppen water
- 6 loth sugar

## Directions

1. Cook one schoppen water with one quarter pound butter in a brass pan for a quarter hour
2. Add the sugar with grated lemon zest, lemon juice, fine flour, and the ground almonds
3. Cook until the mass separates from the pan
4. Transfer to a bowl
5. Beat in the egg yolks gradually
6. Fold in the beaten egg whites slowly
7. Coat a metal capsule with butter and dust with sugar
8. Fill in the mass and seal the capsule
9. Place in a covered casserole with simmering water
10. Cook the pudding for 1-1/2 hours
11. For the sauce: cook 8 pitted apricots in a small casserole with 1 schoppen wine, 1 schoppen water, and 6 loth sugar for a quarter hour
12. Pass through a sieve
13. Heat again and pour over the arranged pudding

title: Citronensuppe für Kranke (Lemon Soup for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 fresh egg yolks
- A piece of sugar rubbed on a lemon rind
- Juice from 2 lemons
- 1 schoppen of borage water
- 1 schoppen of black cherry water
- Wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir the egg yolks in a pot
2. Add the sugar rubbed on lemon rind and the juice from 2 lemons
3. Add 1 schoppen of borage water and 1 schoppen of black cherry water
4. Heat on a coal fire until it begins to boil
5. Wine may be added if the illness permits

title: Citronentorte (Lemon Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- zest of 2 lemons, minced
- pulp of 6 lemons, seeds removed
- 1/4 pound citron peel
- 1/4 pound orange peel
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1 cup water
- butter dough
- 1 egg yolk

## Directions

1. Finely cut the blanched almonds
2. Mix together the lemon zest, lemon pulp, citron peel, and orange peel
3. Cook 1/2 pound sugar with 1 cup water in a brass pan
4. Skim the sugar carefully
5. Cook the almond and fruit mixture in the sugar together
6. Let the mass cool
7. Line a springform with good butter dough
8. Fill the mass into it
9. Make a thin lid of butter dough over it
10. Brush the lid with egg yolk
11. Bake the torte in a not-too-hot oven

title: Cold Herring Sauce (Kalte Häringsauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 beautiful herring, male fish
- small piece butter
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- herring roe
- 2 spoons Provençal oil
- vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and bone a beautiful male herring carefully
2. Pound it with a small piece of butter and 6 hard-boiled egg yolks very finely in a mortar
3. Strain everything through a sieve
4. Take the roe from the herring and stir it with 2 spoons of Provençal oil, some vinegar and pepper
5. Stir everything together and serve the sauce with beef

title: Consommé (Concentrated Meat Broth)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 to 12 pounds beef
- 4 to 5 pounds veal bones
- 6 veal feet
- 3 old cleaned chickens
- water

## Directions

1. Chop all the meat and bones into pieces and put in a large kettle
2. Pour enough water over it so it rises 3 fingers above the contents
3. Let it cook for 10 to 12 hours, skimming diligently
4. Do not salt it; use few herbs and roots to prevent souring
5. Refill with water often during cooking
6. Let it reduce to half its volume, then strain through a hair sieve
7. Let it stand overnight if time permits
8. The next day, remove the fat from the top and put the remainder in a casserole
9. Let it cook until a sample on a plate remains in drops
10. Skim continuously during cooking
11. Pour through linen into a flat dish and cut into small tablets like chocolate when firm
12. It is especially good for poultry and for glazing, and can be used to prepare the strongest meat broths and cleanest soups

title: Cotelettes papillotées (Parchment-Wrapped Cutlets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful white cutlets
- lemon zest
- onion
- parsley
- pork forcemeat
- 2 egg whites
- salt
- bacon
- butter
- parchment paper

## Directions

1. Pound beautiful white cutlets well, no thicker than a knife, and shape them somewhat lengthily
2. Chop the zest of half a lemon finely with some onion and parsley
3. Mix this finely with pork forcemeat, combine with 2 egg whites, and salt to taste
4. Spread this mixture on the cutlets above and below, knife-back thick
5. Cut a piece of bacon as thin as possible and lay it above and below the cutlet
6. Brush a sheet of parchment paper with butter, wrap the cutlet in it so the bone sticks out at the top
7. Cut off the remaining paper, seal it tightly with your hand so nothing can leak out
8. Place on a grate and turn it; the cutlets are done in a quarter hour
9. Serve them with the paper on the table

title: Coulis (Brown Meat Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds old beef or cow meat
- 2 veal knuckles
- 3 veal feet
- 1 old hen
- various roots
- onion
- nutmeg
- 6 measures of water
- 1/2 pound butter
- 2 handfuls of fine flour
- 1/2 glass of cold water

## Directions

1. Chop all the meat, bones, roots, onion, and nutmeg into small pieces
2. Cook in a covered casserole or kettle with 6 measures of water for 4 to 6 hours
3. In a separate casserole, melt 1/2 pound butter
4. Stir in 2 handfuls of fine flour and let it cook together for two minutes
5. Pour in 1/2 glass of cold water and stir the flour until smooth
6. Strain the broth through a hair sieve
7. Remove the fat from the top and pour the broth into the casserole with the flour, stirring constantly
8. Let it cook with the flour for 2 more minutes
9. Pour this coulis into an earthen dish and use it for all white sauces

title: Croquette
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- veal
- onions
- lemons
- fat
- a penny roll
- water
- 2 to 3 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- a roll
- lard

## Directions

1. Weigh veal with onions and lemons, braise it in a piece of fat
2. Soak a penny roll in water, press it out firmly, and add it to the above
3. Braise everything together for another quarter hour
4. Add 2 to 3 eggs, salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste
5. Cut a roll (with the bottom crust removed) into pieces
6. Spread the mass onto the pieces and fry them golden yellow in hot lard

title: Crême für Kranke (Cream for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 schoppen Rhine wine
- 1/2 schoppen black cherry water
- 3 finger-widths flour
- a little water
- 3 egg yolks
- a little pounded cinnamon
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir 3 finger-widths of flour smooth with a little water.
2. In a brass pan, cook together 1/2 schoppen Rhine wine, 1/2 schoppen black cherry water, the flour mixture, 3 egg yolks, a little pounded cinnamon, and 1/4 pound sugar rubbed with lemon zest for a short time.
3. Press through a hair sieve into any desired bowl.
4. Serve cold or warm.

title: Crême mit Eis (Cream with Ice)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- 1/8 pound sugar
- 9 egg yolks
- a little flour
- 6 egg whites
- 6 drops orange blossom water

## Directions

1. Cook half a maas of milk, a piece of whole cinnamon, and 1/8 pound sugar together in a brass pan.
2. Stir 9 egg yolks smooth with a little flour.
3. Stir the cooked milk into it.
4. Let everything come together once more in the pan.
5. Pour the cream through a hair sieve into a bowl and let it cool.
6. Beat 6 egg whites into stiff snow.
7. Stir 6 drops of orange blossom water and sugar into the snow until the ice is thick.
8. Pour the ice around the cream.
9. Make a spatula glowing and hold it over the cream until it is dry.
10. Serve cold.

title: Crême von Reis (Rice Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound rice
- boiling water
- half Maas milk
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- 4 Loth pounded sugar
- 2 Loth diced citron

## Directions

1. Scald one-quarter pound rice with boiling water and let it stand for a quarter hour
2. Strain through a hair sieve
3. Cook it with half a Maas milk until very soft
4. Add 1 schoppen sweet cream, 6 egg yolks, 1 lemon rubbed with sugar, 4 Loth pounded sugar, and 2 Loth diced citron
5. Let everything come together slightly in a pan
6. Place a bowl in hot water
7. Fill the mass into it
8. Cover with a lid topped with coals and let the cream become firm
9. Serve cold or warm

title: Crême von Roiaille (Almond and Biscuit Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one-eighth pound biscuit
- 4 Loth bitter and sweet almond bread
- 3 schoppen milk
- 10 Loth sugar rubbed with 1 lemon
- 2 teaspoons pounded cinnamon
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 to 4 spoonfuls raspberry jelly

## Directions

1. Cook one-eighth pound biscuit and 4 Loth bitter and sweet almond bread in a brass pan with 3 schoppen milk, 10 Loth sugar rubbed with 1 lemon, and 2 teaspoons pounded cinnamon for 4 minutes while stirring constantly
2. Meanwhile beat 6 egg yolks in a bowl
3. When the cooked mixture has cooled slightly, stir it into the eggs and pass through a hair sieve
4. Dip a coffee cup or proper cream cup in cold water
5. Fill the cream into it
6. Place the cup on the fire and cover with a lid topped with weak coals until the cream is firm, which takes about half an hour with ordinary cups
7. Turn the cream out onto a plate and pour 3 to 4 spoonfuls of raspberry jelly over it

title: Crême von saurem Rahm (Sour Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- thick sour cream
- 1 lemon
- one-eighth pound sugar
- grated biscuit

## Directions

1. Beat thick sour cream until very foamy
2. Rub 1 lemon with one-eighth pound sugar
3. Pound the sugar and mix with the rubbed zest under the cream
4. Arrange the cream and sprinkle grated biscuit over it

title: Crême von Schaum (Foam Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- half Maas milk
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 4 Loth sugar rubbed with 1 lemon
- 8 eggs
- 1 pound refined sugar
- half glass water
- raspberry juice
- chocolate ice (for winter)

## Directions

1. Beat 12 egg whites into a snow
2. Cook half a Maas milk in a wide brass pan with 1 piece whole cinnamon and 4 Loth sugar rubbed with 1 lemon for 6 minutes
3. Slowly stir into 8 eggs in a pot
4. Let the milk with eggs come together once more in the pan
5. Strain through a hair sieve into a wide dish for the table
6. Make milk boil again
7. When it rises, place 4 quenelles made with a spoon from the egg snow into the boiling milk
8. Let them cook briefly
9. Place them on a hair sieve to drain
10. Lay them on the cold cream
11. Let 1 pound refined sugar cook with half a glass water in a brass pan until it threads
12. Using a spoon, make 2 lattices of the syrup over the quenelles
13. Color one quenelle red by pouring raspberry juice over it and another yellow
14. In winter, chocolate ice can be made over it

title: Crême von süßem Rahm (Sweet Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half Maas cream
- biscuit slices
- roasted almonds
- 2 Loth vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Beat half a Maas cream in a bowl with a white beater like egg snow
2. Lay biscuit slices on a plate
3. Sprinkle roasted almonds on top
4. Mix 2 Loth vanilla sugar under the snow
5. Pour this mixture around the slices

title: Crême von Vanille (Vanilla Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a maas milk
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 1 piece whole vanilla
- 4 loth sugar
- 3 fingers full flour
- 8 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Let half a maas milk, one piece whole cinnamon, one piece whole vanilla, and 4 loth sugar cook together for a while
2. Mix 3 fingers full flour and 8 egg yolks together, stir slowly into the cooked milk
3. Let everything come to a boil once more in the pan
4. Press the cream through a fine sieve into a dish, put a cover with weak coals on it
5. Place the dish in boiling water and let the cream become firm
6. Burn it with a glowing shovel, sprinkle sugar on it and serve warm

title: Crême à la coquin (Rascal's Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen cream
- 1 schoppen milk
- orange blossom water
- 1 lemon
- 4 loth sugar
- 6 crushed macaroons
- 6 egg yolks
- 3 finger-widths flour

## Directions

1. Cook the cream, milk, orange blossom water (value of one kreuzer), water, lemon zest rubbed in sugar, 4 loth sugar, and 6 crushed macaroons together for a strong quarter hour.
2. Beat 6 egg yolks in a bowl, add 3 finger-widths of flour.
3. Slowly pour the cooked milk onto the mixture.
4. Stir everything for one minute over the fire.
5. Press through a hair sieve into the serving bowl.
