6. Place the cream in boiling water, cover with a lid topped with coals and let it set.
7. Sprinkle sugar on top and brown it with a glowing spatula.

title: Crême à la Sultan (Sultan's Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter pound sugar
- half a glass water
- half a maas milk
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 6 loth sugar rubbed on a lemon
- 3 fingers full flour
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook the quarter pound sugar and half glass water on the fire until the sugar is dark yellow
2. Pour half a maas milk to it, add one piece whole cinnamon, and let it cook together
3. Take 6 loth sugar which has been rubbed on a lemon, 3 fingers full flour, and 8 egg yolks, and stir slowly into the cooked milk
4. Let everything on the fire come to a boil once more
5. Press the cream through a fine sieve onto a dish that will be served at table
6. Beat the 8 egg whites into stiff snow, make a wreath of it around the cream, place a round ball in the middle
7. Sprinkle coarsely pounded sugar over it, place it in an oven, and let it become a nice yellow
8. Serve the cream warm

title: Crêmeauflauf (Cream Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 cooking spoonfuls flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 schoppen milk
- 4 loth finely ground sugar
- 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir 4 egg yolks into 4 cooking spoonfuls flour in a brass pan, pour 1/2 schoppen milk into it, and cook to a thick cream.
2. Put the cream into a bowl and add 4 loth finely ground sugar and 1/2 lemon grated on a grater.

title: Crêmeauflauf (Cream Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons flour
- 4 egg yolks
- half a Schoppen milk
- 4 Loth finely ground sugar
- half a lemon
- 4 more egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir 4 egg yolks into 4 tablespoons of flour in a brass pan
2. Pour in half a Schoppen of milk and cook this into a thick cream
3. Place this cream in a bowl and add 4 Loth of finely ground sugar and the zest of half a lemon, grated on a grater
4. Stir in 4 more egg yolks, one after another
5. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold slowly into the mass
6. Butter a baking sheet and spread the mixture on it
7. Let it rise beautifully in the oven
8. The soufflé is done in a quarter hour

title: Crêmetorte (Cream Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- raw butter dough
- raw potatoes
- preserved currants, raspberries, or cherries
- 1/4 pound sugar
- zest of 2 lemons
- 1 tablespoon flour
- sweet milk
- 1 schoppen milk
- 6-7 egg yolks
- snow of 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Form a torte of raw butter dough
2. Lay raw potatoes on it
3. Bake it so that it does not shrink
4. When it has cooled, brush it with preserved currants, raspberries, or cherries
5. For the cream: grate 1/4 pound sugar with 2 lemons onto paper and dry it
6. Stir 1 tablespoon flour smooth in a pan with sweet milk
7. Pour in another schoppen of milk
8. Add 6-7 egg yolks to it
9. Cook this like a porridge and stir until it is cold
10. Carefully spread it on the preserved fruit
11. Beat the snow stiffly from 6 egg whites
12. Mix the dried lemon sugar with it
13. Put the snow on the cream
14. Place the torte in the oven at low heat

title: Cucumber Sauce (Gurkensauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 cucumbers without seeds
- 2 onions
- handful of parsley
- butter
- flour
- cold water
- meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- 2 tablespoons herb vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut 2 to 3 seedless cucumbers into salad
2. Chop 2 onions with a handful of parsley
3. Steam all of this in a casserole with a piece of butter for a quarter hour
4. Sprinkle 3 finger widths of flour on it
5. Add a little cold water, one ladle of meat broth, salt, pepper and 2 tablespoons of herb vinegar
6. Let everything boil together

title: Cured Meat (Pökelfleisch)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef from the brisket or tail piece
- Saltpeter (2 loth)
- Tarragon
- Thyme
- Basil
- Bay leaves
- Lemon balm
- Celery herb
- Rosemary
- Marjoram
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Juniper berries
- Salt (1 pound)
- Water (1½ maas)
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Choose a piece of beef from the brisket for serving warm, or from the tail for serving cold.
2. Rub the meat well with 2 loth of saltpeter.
3. Lay it in a stone pot or tub.
4. Crush the following herbs: tarragon, thyme, basil, bay leaves, lemon balm, celery herb, rosemary, marjoram, cloves, pepper, nutmeg, and juniper berries, and add all to the meat.
5. Cover it and place it in the cellar for 2 to 3 days.
6. On the fourth day, cook the spices in a pan with 1 pound of salt and 1½ maas of water until no salt is visible.
7. Let it cool completely.
8. Pour it again over the meat, cover it firmly, lay 2 stones on top.
9. Let it stand for 3 days, turn it on the third day, add the pickle brine again, and continue this for 3 to 4 weeks.
10. To cook, place it in a not very large pot with water; it must not be salted.
11. Serve it warm with horseradish; it is also very good with sauerkraut, winter cabbage, and beans.

title: Dried Beans (Bohnen zu trocknen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Young beans
- Water

## Directions

1. Remove the strings from young beans.
2. Tie them to a strong thread.
3. Heat water in a kettle or pan.
4. Put the beans in, let boiling water pass over them.
5. Remove them and hang them in a dry place where they have air.
6. Preserve them for winter; they can also be dried or smoked.

title: Dried Green Peas (Grüne Erbsen zu dörren)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Green peas, not very old
- Warm water

## Directions

1. Shell the peas.
2. Let boiling water pass over them twice in a pan.
3. Pour them into a strainer.
4. Lay them on paper and let them dry.
5. Store them for winter.
6. To cook, pour lukewarm water over them.
7. Let them stand overnight.
8. In the morning, drain them.
9. Pour fresh lukewarm water over them and cook them like other green peas.

title: Dried Plums (Prünellen zu dörren)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful ripe plums
- Boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the plums.
2. Remove the skin.
3. Take out the pits.
4. Lay them on paper, or better yet on a wire mesh grate.
5. Place this in a baking oven that is no longer hot.
6. When well dried, preserve them in a wooden box.

title: Dukatennudeln (Ducat Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 2 spoonfuls of good thick beer yeast
- 1/2 schoppen of lukewarm sweet cream
- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 loth of sugar
- 1 knife-tip of salt
- 1/2 quarter-pound of butter
- milk
- 1/2 glass of milk

## Directions

1. With 1 pound fine flour, 2 spoonfuls of good thick beer yeast, and 1/2 schoppen of lukewarm sweet cream make a dough and let it rise well
2. Cut in 1/4 pound butter and add 4 egg yolks; if the dough is still too stiff, add a little more sweet cream
3. Beat it for half an hour, add 1 loth of sugar and a knife-tip of salt, let it rise again
4. When half-risen, scoop out little cakes with a spoon the size of a walnut, place them on a floured sheet and let them rise completely
5. Melt 1/2 quarter-pound of butter in a frying pan, pour in milk to nail-height, place the noodles in it, cover the dish
6. Place hot ashes on the lid and coals under the dish, let the noodles boil up, pour in 1/2 glass of milk and place them on a platter with the help of a baking shovel
7. Can be served with compote or lemon sauce

title: Eier Geruehrt (Scrambled Eggs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 2 tablespoons whole milk
- 4 g salt
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 50 g butter

## Directions

1. Beat the eggs with pepper, salt, and milk in a pot
2. Melt the butter in a clean cooking pot over low heat
3. Pour in the egg mixture and push it back and forth on the bottom of the pot with a spoon until a chunky but still creamy mass forms
4. Serve the scrambled eggs on a hot dish

title: Eier in brauner Sauce (Eggs in Brown Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- lard
- handful of grated black bread
- 2 loth brown gingerbread
- glass of wine
- half glass vinegar
- meat broth or water
- lemon juice
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Fry as many eggs as needed in hot lard, but keep the yolk soft
2. For the sauce: take a handful of grated black bread and 2 loth brown gingerbread together in a casserole
3. Pour in a glass of wine, half a glass of vinegar, a ladle-full of meat broth or water on fasting days
4. Add the juice of half a lemon, salt, pepper, and nutmeg
5. Let it boil together for a quarter hour
6. Strain through a hair sieve
7. Place the eggs on a plate, pour the sauce over them, and let everything become hot together again

title: Eier in der Pfanne (Fried Eggs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 15 g margarine
- 1 pinch white pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Melt the butter on a plate or enameled dish
2. Carefully open the fresh eggs, pour them onto the plate, and sprinkle with salt and pepper
3. Let them coagulate slowly
4. The egg white around the yolk should be white, but still translucent over the yolk
5. Use a glass to remove the irregular egg white parts
6. Serve the fried eggs

title: Eier in Mostrichsauce (Eggs in Mustard Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 1/2 liter water
- Mustard sauce

## Directions

1. Prepare soft-boiled eggs
2. Immediately shock the eggs with lukewarm water
3. Gently loosen and remove the shells by tapping on the table
4. Cut the eggs lengthwise on a plate with a sharp knife, or leave them whole
5. Pour cooled or warm mustard sauce over the eggs

title: Eier in Sole (Pickled Eggs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 9 tablespoons salt
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Cook the eggs in boiling water with the salt for 10 minutes
2. Crack the tips of the eggshells
3. Place the eggs in cooled salt water where they will keep for a long time
4. After 2-3 days they can be used
5. Optional: place the pickled eggs in cooled ham or curing brine for 1 to 3 days in winter

title: Eierbier (Warm Beer)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 90 g sugar
- 1 teaspoon lemon sugar
- 3/4 l white or brown beer

## Directions

1. The whole eggs are beaten with the sugar. The beer is heated and then beaten with the egg foam and lemon sugar over gentle heat with the whisk and arranged for serving immediately.

title: Eierbrei (Egg Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- thick porridge
- butter
- 2 to 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Cook a thick porridge, then let a piece of butter melt in a dish, beat 2 to 3 eggs into it
2. Stir them on the fire until the eggs begin to thicken, then stir them with the porridge and let a crust cook on it

title: Eiercrêmetorte (Egg Cream Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely ground sugar
- 20 egg yolks
- snow of 16 egg whites
- zest of 1 lemon, grated
- juice of 1 lemon
- 16 loth starch flour
- butter and sugar for the pan
- 1-1/2 loth fish glue
- 1 schoppen water
- zest of 1 orange
- 6 loth sugar
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- juice of 2 oranges
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 schoppen sweet cream for whipping
- citron or chocolate icing

## Directions

1. Stir 1 pound finely ground sugar with 20 egg yolks and the snow of 16 egg whites until the mass is thick
2. Add the grated zest and juice of 1 lemon
3. Mix in 16 loth starch flour
4. Brush a plafond 3 fingers high and not very wide with butter, sprinkle with sugar, and bake the torte in it
5. For the cream: dissolve 1-1/2 loth fish glue in 1 schoppen water
6. Pour the water through a hair sieve
7. Grate the zest of 1 orange with 6 loth sugar
8. Pour the fish glue water into a brass pan
9. Take the sugar, 1 schoppen sweet cream, juice of 2 oranges, and 6 egg yolks
10. Cook everything on the fire into a cream
11. Pour it through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl and let it cool
12. Beat 1 schoppen sweet cream in a bowl like snow
13. Let it drain in a hair sieve and mix it into the cold cream
14. Place the torte on a plate and remove the lid from the top
15. Cut a 2-inch deep opening into the torte and hollow it out
16. Pour the cream into it
17. Replace the lid
18. Make a lemon or chocolate icing on the lid as has been described before
19. Decorate the torte with citron peel and preserved fruits

title: Eierkuchen (Egg Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound peeled, finely ground almonds
- 6 to 8 bitter almonds
- 1 lemon
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- 1/4 pound beef marrow
- 2 knife points ground cinnamon
- some rose water
- 1 knife point salt
- 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Weigh 1/4 pound peeled, finely ground almonds with 6 to 8 bitter almonds among them, and the yellow of 1 lemon very fine, pour 1 schoppen sweet cream to it
2. Cut 1/4 pound beef marrow very fine, add 2 knife points ground cinnamon, some rose water, 1 knife point salt, 8 eggs
3. Stir everything together, butter a cake pan, fill the mass into it and bake the cake in the oven until golden

title: Eierkuchen (Pancake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 105 g flour
- 5 g salt
- 1 pinch baking powder
- 1/4 liter skim milk
- 40 g cooking fat
- 10 g sugar

## Directions

1. Crack the egg, separating yolk and white into different pots
2. Beat the yolk with milk and salt, then gradually stir it into the center of the flour along with the baking powder
3. Beat the egg white until stiff and slowly fold into the mixture, which should not sit
4. Wipe a handled frying pan dry with paper
5. Heat 1 spoon of fat in the pan until hot
6. Slowly pour in the batter and distribute evenly by moving the pan back and forth
7. Brown the first side of the pancake nicely over frequent shaking, slide it onto a lid, add about 10 g fat to the unbaked side, cover the pan with the lid, flip, let the pancake steam covered for a few moments, then remove the lid
8. The cake should have risen nicely; bake to a nice color, serve, and sprinkle with sugar
9. Pancakes should not sit; if necessary, keep them only on a pot of hot water

title: Eierkuchen mit Aepfeln (Egg Cake with Apples)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- rose apples
- butter
- 4 to 6 apricots
- 4 loth sugar
- 2 knife points mace
- 6 whole eggs
- 1/2 schoppen sour cream
- 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Peel rose apples and cut them into small pieces, braise them soft in a small piece of butter, place them in a bowl
2. Pit 4 to 6 apricots, add 4 loth sugar, 2 knife points mace, 6 whole eggs and 1/2 schoppen sour cream
3. Grate 1/2 lemon on a grater, mix everything well together, butter a cake pan, fill the mass into it and bake the cake on charcoal fire until well done
4. Make a lemon ice on top as previously described

title: Eierkuchen mit Aepfeln (Pancake with Apples)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Pancake batter
- 1/2 peeled apple, cut into pieces
- Fat

## Directions

1. Fry the apple pieces in fat on the pan for a while
2. Pour pancake batter over them and bake the cake as described

title: Eierkuchen mit Ochsenmark (Egg Cake with Beef Marrow)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound sweet almonds
- 6 to 8 bitter almonds
- rose water
- 2 lemon peels
- 3 dried apricots
- 3 peeled, sliced apples
- 1/4 pound beef marrow
- 10 eggs
- 4 loths ground sugar
- 1 schoppen sweet cream

## Directions

1. Grind 1/4 pound sweet almonds with 6 to 8 bitter almonds finely with rose water in a mortar
2. Finely chop the peels of 2 lemons
3. Add 3 sliced dried apricots and 3 peeled, sliced apples
4. Mix in 1/4 pound beef marrow, 10 eggs, 4 loths ground sugar, and 1 schoppen sweet cream
5. Line a cake pan with oblaten
6. Spread the mass on it
7. Bake in the oven

title: Eierpunsch (Egg Punch)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Schoppen egg punch
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 large tablespoons sugar
- boiling water
- Arak

## Directions

1. To 1 Schoppen egg punch take 2 egg yolks and 2 large tablespoons of sugar
2. Stir everything together in a glass with a silver spoon until it is like porridge
3. Then pour boiling water to it with constant stirring until the glass is almost full
4. Finally fill it up completely with Arak

title: Ein altes Weib (An Old Woman)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen water and butter
- fine flour
- 3 eggs
- white bread
- hot lard
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- cold water
- 1 schoppen red wine
- sugar
- cinnamon
- 1/4 pound raisins

## Directions

1. Heat 1 schoppen water and butter the size of a hen's egg in a brass pan.
2. Stir in fine flour until very thick, cook until the batter pulls away from the pan, and smooth it with 3 eggs (the batter must be such that it runs off a spoon).
3. Cut a small piece of white bread, shape it round, dip it in the batter and fry golden in a deep pan with hot lard.
4. Stick it on a strong fork, turn it in the batter again, and repeat this 12 to 15 times.
5. Once the bread is large enough, place it on bread slices to drain well.
6. Make an English sauce: stir 1 small cooking spoon flour with cold water, add 1 schoppen red wine, sugar to taste, a knife-tip of ground cinnamon, and 1/4 pound raisins, and bring to a boil.
7. In a small pan, place a piece of sugar, add 1 tablespoon water, cook until it spins threads, add to the sauce, and bring everything to a boil once more.
8. Place the old woman on the plate and pour the sauce over her.

title: Eingesezter Salat (Molded Salad)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- sour aspic
- endive salad
- green beans
- bean kernels
- potatoes
- 4 tablespoons of fine Provençal oil
- 2 tablespoons of mustard
- yolk of 2 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 tablespoons of vinegar

## Directions

1. In a beautiful aspic mold, let sour aspic run around knife-back thick and then set.
2. Line the mold beautifully with flowers, with endive salad that should be neither too large nor too small, with green beans, bean kernels, potatoes, press everything firmly in.
3. Stir together 4 tablespoons of fine Provençal oil, 2 tablespoons of mustard, the yolk of 2 hard-boiled eggs with 2 tablespoons of vinegar.
4. Pour it over the salad, let it stand for a while and turn it out onto the intended plate when serving.

title: Endiviensalat mit rothen Rüben gefärbt (Endive Salad Dyed with Red Beets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful endive
- potatoes
- red beet broth

## Directions

1. Chop the endive finely, place it in lukewarm water.
2. Peel the potatoes and lay them in red beet broth for half an hour.
3. Dress the endive, place it in the bowl, cut the potatoes into wheels and garnish the salad with them as desired.

title: Endiviensuppe (Endive Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- endive, finely cut and cleaned
- flour
- meat broth
- Mundbrode bread, cut lengthwise
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place a piece of butter in a pot and braise finely cut, carefully washed, beautiful yellow endive until tender
2. Sprinkle as much flour on top as one can grasp between three fingers
3. Pour meat broth over it
4. Let it come to a boil again
5. Serve over 2 pieces of Mundbrode bread cut lengthwise and roasted golden on the grill; add salt and nutmeg

title: Englischer Braten (English Roast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Piece of loin roast with fat from a fat ox slaughtered 8 days prior
- Salt
- Pepper
- Pure clear butter

## Directions

1. Season a piece of loin roast with fat with salt and pepper
2. Put it on a spit and wrap it double with 4 sheets of paper
3. Roast it slowly over weak fire for 4 hours, basting constantly with pure clear butter
4. Half an hour before serving, remove the paper so the jus runs into the roasting pan
5. Leave the roast on the fire and baste several more times with melted pure butter
6. Place it on the platter, strain the jus through a sieve, remove the fat, and pour the jus over the roast

title: Englischer Pudding (English Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef kidney fat, cleaned and finely chopped
- 1/2 pound large raisins
- 1/2 pound small raisins
- 1/2 pound ground sugar
- zest of 1 lemon, chopped
- 8 loth grated rolls
- 1 cooking spoon fine flour
- 1/2 glass Arak
- 5 eggs
- flour for dusting
- preserved fruits of all kinds
- approximately 8 peeled apples, sliced

## Directions

1. On a noodle board dusted with flour, make a well-worked dough from the kidney fat, raisins, sugar, lemon zest, grated rolls, flour, Arak, and eggs
2. Roll it out as well as possible
3. Lay a clean cloth on a pillow and stretch it out as finely as possible on it
4. Let it dry slightly
5. Put it back on the noodle board
6. Spread half of the mixture over it
7. Add all kinds of preserved fruits and the sliced apples
8. Spread the other half of the mixture on top
9. Roll up the pudding
10. Form it into a spiral shape
11. Bind it tightly in a damp cloth, leaving a finger-width empty at the top
12. Cook in boiling water for 4 hours

title: Englischer Pudding anderer Art (English Pudding Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound blanched almonds
- egg white
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 white roll, soaked and firmly squeezed out
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from 6 eggs, beaten to snow
- 1/4 pound currants
- 1/4 pound raisins
- 1 apricot half, pitted
- large currants
- salt

## Directions

1. Beat 1/4 pound butter in a bowl until very foamy.
2. Pound 1/4 pound blanched almonds with egg white very finely.
3. Mix together 1/4 pound sugar with lemon zest grated on it, the squeezed-out soaked white roll, 6 egg yolks, the beaten egg white snow, 1/4 pound currants and raisins with the pounded almonds, beating very foamy (the snow must be added last).
4. Spread out a damp cloth, place half a pitted apricot in the center, arrange large currants around it, fill the mass onto it.
5. Tie the cloth closed leaving a finger's width of space, cook it one hour in lightly salted water.
6. For the lemon sauce: rub 2 lemons over sugar, mix with a cooking spoon of flour and cold water, add 1 pint of wine, the lemon sugar, and the juice, and pour this sauce over the pudding.

title: Englischer Pudding anderer Art (English Pudding Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 loth beef kidney fat
- 20 loth flour
- 8 loth finely pounded sugar
- 1/2 lemon
- 8 loth citron and orange peel
- 8 loth small raisins
- 8 loth large raisins
- 4 whole eggs

## Directions

1. Chop the beef kidney fat very finely.
2. Rub 20 loth flour with the hand and mix into the chopped fat.
3. Add 8 loth finely pounded sugar, zest of half a lemon grated on the grater, 8 loth citron and orange peel finely chopped, 8 loth small raisins, 8 loth large raisins washed clean.
4. Beat 4 whole eggs and mix everything with a cooking spoon together like a sparrow dough.
5. Tie the mass firmly in a cloth dipped in cold water and put it in boiling water.
6. Cook for three hours.
7. Serve with flaming Arak or any desired sauce.

title: Ente, Gebratene, für 4 Personen (Roast Duck for 4 People)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 duck
- 1 sprig mugwort
- 8 apples
- 5 g sugar
- 10 g salt
- 5 g potato starch
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Prepare the duck according to the poultry preparation instructions.
2. Remove the neck, wings and feet, wash the duck, rub it inside and out with salt and fill it with whole apples that have been quartered, peeled and mixed with sugar and mugwort.
3. Tie it into a good shape, place it in a roasting pan, pour some boiling water over it and roast it, basting frequently, for 1-1 1/2 hours until done.
4. Arrange the duck and place the apples around it.
5. Thicken the sauce with potato starch stirred in cold water.

title: Erbsen (Peas)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- peas
- butter or lard
- flour

## Directions

1. Sort and rinse the peas
2. Soak overnight in cold water
3. Cook with cold water until soft
4. Press through a strainer
5. Lightly toast the flour in butter or lard
6. Add to the peas and bring to a boil again

title: Erbsen, Dicke (Thick Peas)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g yellow peas
- 1/2 liter ham or bacon broth
- 10 g onions
- 15 g lard
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the peas as for pea soup, skim and drain (after removing the husks that float on top), cook with ham or bacon broth, and when the liquid is mostly absorbed, stir to a puree with a wooden pestle; optionally add some marjoram or onions. A finely cut onion is sweated until light brown in lard and poured over the peas seasoned with pepper and salt.

title: Erdbeerengefrorenes (Strawberry Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 measures of strawberries
- sugar
- 2 knife points of ground cinnamon
- 1 glass of nutmeg wine

## Directions

1. Press the strawberries through a sieve
2. Add sugar, ground cinnamon, and nutmeg wine
3. Mix everything together
4. Let it freeze in the freezing tin
5. Do not cook it over fire and do not work it during freezing

title: Erolzheimer Torte (Erolzheim Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 5 egg whites
- 1 pound blanched almonds, cut lengthwise into fine pieces
- a knife tip of fine cinnamon
- 2 lemons, grated on a grater
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 pound almonds grated on a grater
- 1/2 pound almonds cut into pieces
- wafers for lining

## Directions

1. Whip together the sugar and egg whites into a thick meringue
2. Add the cut almonds, cinnamon, grated lemons, and lemon juice
3. Mix everything together
4. Line a springform with wafers
5. Fill the mixture in
6. Bake the torte in the oven
7. Note that 1/2 pound of almonds should be grated on a grater and the other 1/2 pound cut into pieces

title: Estragonessig auf französische Art (Tarragon Vinegar, French Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- a strong handful of tender tarragon leaves
- good wine vinegar
- 6 pieces pimpernelle
- 6 shallot onions
- 8 cloves
- 8 white peppercorns
- a piece of whole ginger
- a piece of whole cinnamon
- some lemon

## Directions

1. Take a strong handful of tender tarragon leaves and pour good wine vinegar over them together with 6 pieces pimpernelle, 6 shallot onions, 8 cloves, 8 white peppercorns, a piece of whole ginger, a piece of whole cinnamon and some lemon in a clean container
2. Bind the container firmly shut
3. Set it in the sun for 14 days or 3 weeks, or otherwise in a warm place
4. After 3 weeks the vinegar is excellently good
5. Fill it into bottles, seal these firmly and the vinegar can be used for sauces and for salad

title: Falsches Mandelbrod (False Almond Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 Viertelpfund Mehl
- ein Viertelpfund Zucker
- ein Viertelpfund Butter
- ein Stück Zucker, woran eine Citrone abgerieben
- 2 bis 3 Löffel voll Wein
- 1 Ei zum Bestreichen
- Zucker und Zimmt zum Bestreuen

## Directions

1. 1-1/2 Viertelpfund Mehl, ein Viertelpfund Zucker, ein Viertelpfund Butter und ein Stück Zucker, woran eine Citrone abgerieben, zusammen auf dem Nudelbrett zu einem Teig machen.
2. 2 bis 3 Löffel voll Wein dazugeben.
3. Den Teig noch einmal arbeiten.
4. Stark messerrückendick auswalken.
5. Mit einem viereckigen Mödelchen Küchlein ausstechen.
6. Diese auf einem mit Mehl bestreuten Blech mit verklopftem Ei bestreichen.
7. Viel Zucker und Zimmt darüber streuen.
8. Im Ofen backen.

title: Farcirte Aepfel (Stuffed Apples)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Borsdorf apples
- 1 schoppen wine
- 1 schoppen water
- 10 loth sugar
- yolks of 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 1 loth candied citron
- 1 loth blanched almonds
- 1 loth sugar
- 2 spoonfuls raspberry or currant juice

## Directions

1. Peel and halve the apples, remove the cores, and hollow them out slightly without making holes
2. Cook the apples in 1 schoppen wine, 1 schoppen water, and 10 loth sugar until soft
3. Drain on a hair sieve
4. Press the yolks of 4 hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve
5. Finely weigh 1 loth candied citron and 1 loth blanched almonds
6. Mix them with the eggs and 1 loth sugar
7. Place the apples on a plate with the openings facing up
8. Fill half of each apple with the egg mixture and cover with the other half
9. Cook 2 spoonfuls raspberry or currant juice with the syrup until it bubbles
10. Pour the syrup over the apples to give them a nice glaze

title: Farcirte Eier (Stuffed Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- white bread (Kreuzerbrode)
- butter
- currants
- one egg
- sour cream
- salt
- nutmeg
- sweet cream

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, peel them, and place them in cold water.
2. Cut them lengthwise in half and carefully remove the yolk.
3. Cut the hard crust from white bread, soak it in water, and press it firmly out.
4. Let a piece of butter melt in a casserole, add the bread and hard-boiled egg yolks, along with a handful of cleaned small currants.
5. Cook this all together over the fire, beat in one egg, 2 spoonfuls of sour cream, salt and nutmeg.
6. Stir it once more over the fire and fill the hard-boiled egg whites with this mixture.
7. Beat 3 egg yolks in half a pint of sweet cream on the plate intended for serving, pour it over the eggs, and let them become nicely golden in a baking oven.
8. Beat 3 egg yolks in a brass pan, add a small piece of butter and half a pint of sweet cream, let this come together over the fire, and pour the sauce over the eggs.

title: Farcirte Feldhühner (Stuffed Partridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 partridges
- 1/4 pound bacon
- onions
- lemons
- truffles
- salt
- mace
- 1 egg
- white bread (for 1 kreuzer), grated
- milk
- bacon for binding
- fat for braising
- juniper berries
- yellow turnips

## Directions

1. Pluck and singe one partridge, cut off the head (unplucked), remove the crop, split the back, and remove the breast so all meat remains attached to the skin.
2. Carefully remove all bones from the body (keeping only the leg bone).
3. Cut the feet at the second joint.
4. From a second partridge, remove all meat and mince finely with 1/4 pound bacon, adding onions and lemons.
5. Pound in a mortar or on a rubbing stone until very fine.
6. Add truffles, salt, and mace.
7. Mix in a bowl with 1 egg.
8. Add grated white bread (for 1 kreuzer) that has been soaked in milk and firmly pressed out, and mix well again.
9. Fill the first bird with this mixture.
10. Sew the back closed.
11. Bind with bacon.
12. In a casserole with a piece of fat, onions, juniper berries, and yellow turnips, braise slowly and beautifully.
13. Serve hot or cold, best with mayonnaise sauce.

title: Farcirter Hecht (Stuffed Pike)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- 1/2 pound pork fat
- 1/4 pound bacon
- 4 anchovies
- bread soaked in milk
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- onions, finely chopped and steamed
- 2 eggs
- sliced onions
- 1 bay leaf
- lemons
- meat broth
- hot butter
- consommé

## Directions

1. Clean the pike carefully, cut out the gills, make a careful cut at the opening below, and cut the skin on the back; make a stitch at the fins but not completely through, then remove the skin
2. Lard the pike nicely with a mixture made from 1/2 pound pork fat, 1/4 pound bacon, 4 anchovies, bread that has been soaked in milk and squeezed out and finely ground, salt, nutmeg, pepper, and finely chopped steamed onions, all mixed together with 2 eggs and thoroughly pounded in a mortar
3. Fill the pike with this mixture and sew it shut
4. Place it in a roasting pan with a rack at the bottom, add sliced onions, 1 bay leaf, lemons, and a little meat broth
5. Roast in the oven until golden, frequently basting with hot butter
6. When serving, let consommé melt over it, coat the pike with it, and pour the sauce through a hair sieve over it

title: Farcirter Kapaun auf russische Art (Stuffed Capon Russian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One beautiful fat capon
- Half pound lean ham
- Three eggs
- One schoppen sweet cream
- One nutmeg, grated
- Butter
- One lemon
- Paper
- One lemon wheel

## Directions

1. Pierce the capon and let it bleed thoroughly.
2. Pluck it while warm, carefully to avoid holes.
3. Singe off remaining hairs over a flame.
4. Remove innards carefully, drawing out the crop with the intestines from below, but leave the heart and lungs.
5. Loosen the breast, back, and thighs as you would a young dove.
6. Prepare the filling: mince the lean ham very finely, add three eggs, pour in one schoppen sweet cream, add one grated nutmeg.
7. Fill a syringe with a tube the thickness of a small finger with this mixture.
8. Inject and fill the capon with the mass.
9. Sew the opening closed again.
10. Place the head with beak on the breast, arrange the feet nicely, place a lemon wheel on the breast.
11. Wrap in a sheet of paper thickly brushed with butter.
12. Roast slowly in a braising pan, basting frequently.
13. Can be served as a roast or ragout. For ragout, prepare sauce: melt a piece of butter in a casserole and braise finely cut onions and a small piece of sugar for a good quarter hour.
14. Sprinkle in one cooking spoon of flour, mix, add one large spoon of meat broth and one glass of wine.
15. Let simmer for half an hour.
16. Force the sauce through a hair sieve back into the casserole.
17. Add the yolk and marrow of one finely minced lemon.
18. Let come to a boil once more and rub in one nutmeg.
19. When ready to serve, place the capon carefully on a platter and pour the sauce directly onto the platter, not over the capon.

title: Fasan in Butter (Pheasant in Butter)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant, meat portions only
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- 1/8 pound butter
- 1 handful parsley, washed
- 2 shallots
- 1/2 lemon, zest and juice

## Directions

1. Rub the pheasant pieces with salt, pepper, and cloves.
2. Brown them in 1/8 pound butter in a casserole until golden.
3. Finely mince the parsley, shallots, and lemon zest.
4. Let this mixture braise in the casserole for a while.
5. Add the juice of 1/2 lemon.

title: Fasan mit Trüffeln (Pheasant with Truffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant, prepared
- butter
- 2 truffles, cleaned
- raw ham
- 1/2 schoppen meat broth
- 1/2 lemon, juice and zest

## Directions

1. In a casserole, braise the pheasant in butter until half-done.
2. Remove and cut the meat from the breast and legs into neat round pieces.
3. Slice the cleaned truffles into leaves.
4. Place butter in a casserole with the meat pieces and truffle slices.
5. Cut in some raw ham.
6. Pour 1/2 schoppen meat broth around.
7. Add the juice and zest of 1/2 lemon.
8. Cover and braise slowly for another half hour.
9. Arrange on a platter.

title: Felchen auf dem Rost gebraten (Whitefish Grilled on the Rack)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- whitefish
- pepper
- salt
- lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Scale the fish and clean it out
2. Dry it with a cloth
3. Rub with pepper and salt
4. Make a cut across the back of the fish
5. Insert a lemon slice into the cut
6. Place the fish on the rack
7. Pour butter over it
8. Grill it nicely
9. When it is on the platter, pour lemon juice over it

title: Feldhühner (Partridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- vinegar with assorted roots
- salt
- pepper
- onions
- cloves
- bacon
- lemons
- yellow turnips
- bay leaves
- liver and heart

## Directions

1. Pluck the partridges and cut off the head (save it) and claws.
2. Place the birds in vinegar with assorted roots.
3. Rub with salt and pepper.
4. Stud with onions stuck with cloves and wrap in bacon and lemons.
5. Cover with a piece of paper.
6. Braise in a casserole with onions, yellow turnips, lemons, and bay leaves.
7. When serving, reattach the head (bacon and lemons may remain on the breast).
8. Remove fat from the sauce.
9. Mince the liver and heart with a little lemon but do not cook them so they do not become tough.
10. Pour the sauce onto the platter and lay the partridges on top.

title: Feldhühner auf englische Art (Partridge English Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges, plucked and cleaned
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- melted butter
- lemons

## Directions

1. Lard the partridge breasts.
2. Mix salt, pepper, and cloves well together and rub into the birds.
3. On a grill, braise half-done with melted butter.
4. Cool, then cut out the breasts and legs neatly.
5. Turn in melted butter.
6. Braise again on the grill until golden.
7. Top with lemons.
8. Serve with sauerkraut, winter cabbage, or blue cabbage.

title: Feldhühner in Salmisauce (Partridges in Salmi Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- partridges
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- onion
- lemon
- bacon
- yellow turnips
- bay leaves
- juniper berries

## Directions

1. Clean the partridges thoroughly, pluck them, singe them
2. Cut off the wings at the second joint and the heads, then draw them
3. If the partridges are fresh, do not wash them but rub them inside with salt and pepper
4. Insert an onion studded with cloves and a piece of lemon in the cavity
5. Wrap them with bacon, place them in a casserole with sliced onion, yellow turnips, bay leaves, and juniper berries

title: Feldhühner in Salmisauce (Partridges in Salmi Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Partridges
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Onion
- Lemon
- Bacon
- Carrots
- Bay leaves
- Juniper berries
- Fat
- Bread crust
- Lard
- Red wine
- Jus
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean and pluck the partridges, singe them, cut off the wings at the second joint and remove the heads and innards.
2. If the partridges are fresh, do not wash them but rub them inside with salt and pepper.
3. Insert an onion studded with cloves and a piece of lemon into the opening.
4. Wrap the partridges with bacon and place them in a casserole with chopped onion, carrots, bay leaves, juniper berries, a piece of fat, and the heads, wings, stomach and liver.
5. Cover the casserole and let it brown well.
6. When cooked, place the partridges on a platter to keep warm.
7. Remove the other ingredients from the casserole and pound them finely in a mortar.
8. Pour the pounded mixture back into the casserole.
9. Add a bread crust fried in lard.
10. Pour in a schoppen of red wine and a spoonful of jus.
11. Let it boil for a quarter hour, strain through a fine sieve and pour back into the casserole.
12. Add the partridges and chopped lemon, cloves, nutmeg, pepper and salt to taste.

title: Feldhühnerpastete (Partridge Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 partridges
- half pound pork sausage meat
- Kreuzerbrod (bread roll)
- 2 eggs
- 3 Loth cleaned anchovies
- 3 Loth butter
- zest of 1 lemon
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- mace
- bacon
- onions
- juniper berries
- carrots
- celery
- capers
- 2 to 3 cleaned truffles
- butter pastry
- eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the breasts from 6 partridges and remove bones from 2 breasts
2. Pound the meat finely in a mortar
3. Rub half a pound pork sausage meat, a soaked and firmly pressed out Kreuzerbrod and 2 eggs on a grater until foamy
4. Put the grated mixture in a bowl
5. Pound 3 Loth cleaned anchovies with 3 Loth butter, the minced zest of 1 lemon, salt, pepper, nutmeg and mace and add it
6. Lard 4 chicken breasts and the legs, place them with cut bacon, butter, onions, juniper berries, carrots and celery in a casserole
7. Cover and braise them for half an hour
8. Chop the bodies of the partridges together and braise them as well
9. Line a melon mold with butter pastry
10. Place half of the stirred mass in it
11. Place the breasts and legs on top
12. Place a spoonful of capers and 2 to 3 cleaned truffles over it
13. Cover with the other half of the stirred mixture
14. Pour the fat in which they were braised over it
15. Make a lid of butter pastry on top, brush it with egg, pinch it well together and bake the pastry in the oven

title: Filets von jungen Hühnern mit süßem Rahm (Fillets of Young Hens with Sweet Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Young hens (Poulards)
- Béchamel sauce
- Glace
- Veal from the shank
- Butter
- Onions
- Parsley
- Flour
- Egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Roast the young hens half-done.
2. When cold, remove the skin.
3. Cut the white meat into fine fillets and cook with Béchamel sauce and some glace.
4. For the Béchamel: cut one pound of veal shank into small pieces.
5. In a covered casserole, gently braise the veal with one pound of butter, onions and parsley.
6. Sprinkle two handfuls of flour over it, stir a few times, cover and braise for a few more minutes.
7. Pass everything through a fine sieve using a vegetable mill.
8. Place a piece of butter and the Béchamel in a casserole.
9. Stir in 3 egg yolks, salt and nutmeg, and stir several times.
10. Pour the Béchamel onto the serving platter.
11. Place the young hens on top and squeeze the juice of half a lemon over them.

title: Fisch mit Sauerkohl (Fish with Sauerkraut)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g cooked or fried fish (deboned)
- 375 g ready-cooked sauerkraut
- 20 g margarine
- 1/8 liter cream
- 5 g salt
- 10 g grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Layer the sauerkraut on a buttered dish or in a form
2. Over it place a layer of the deboned fish, broken into small pieces
3. Continue alternating layers, ending with sauerkraut
4. Pour sweet cream mixed with salt over the dish
5. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and drizzle with margarine
6. Bake for 1/2 hour in the oven until browned

title: Fisch mit Schoten (Fish with Pods)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 250 g can of pods
- 125 g shredded cooked fish (firm variety)
- 15 g margarine
- 1-1/2 milk rolls
- 20 g margarine
- 1/4 teaspoon grated onion
- 10 g margarine
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Lightly fry the fish pieces in 15 g margarine
2. Dice the peeled rolls
3. Toast the bread crumbs in 20 g margarine until crispy
4. In 10 g margarine sautéed with onion, add the drained pods and toss quickly with pepper and salt until hot
5. Mix fish, bread crumbs and pods together
6. Arrange on a hot dish

title: Fisch, Gruener (Green Fish)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg fish
- 40 g fat
- 1 medium onion
- 2 tablespoons soup greens
- 10 g salt
- 40 g flour
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley
- 1 pinch pepper
- 3/4 liter water

## Directions

1. Scale the fish, wash it, cut it, clean it and salt it
2. Cut the soup greens
3. Cook the soup greens covered in 3/4 liter salt water until soft
4. Add the fish pieces and cook them until done in a few minutes
5. In a small pot, cook the finely chopped onion in fat until done but not brown
6. Add the flour and thin with a few spoonfuls of fish water
7. When the flour mixture bubbles, add it to the fish dish
8. Add pepper and chopped parsley and arrange, after everything has cooked into an even slimy consistency, the fish with sauce

title: Fisch-Cotelettes (Fish Cutlets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- inferior fish forcemeat
- milk
- 3 eggs
- onion
- half a vierling of butter
- lemon
- salt
- grated bread
- fat for frying

## Directions

1. Make forcemeat from inferior fish and lay it in milk for half an hour
2. Chop it finely, stir it strongly with a schoppen of milk, beat in 3 eggs and some chopped onion
3. Braise onion and lemon in half a vierling of butter, which also goes into the forcemeat
4. Add salt to taste
5. Stir everything together once more, form into beautiful small cutlets
6. Turn them in eggs, then in grated bread
7. Fry them in an iron pan with plenty of fat until golden yellow
8. Do not let them stand long or they will become tough

title: Fischen den moosigen Geruch zu nehmen (Removing Musty Smell from Fish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- fresh water
- wine
- vinegar
- various herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the ears out of the fish and gut it
2. Salt it and lay it in fresh water for one hour
3. Salt it again and lay it in water once more
4. Make a good sauce of wine, vinegar and all kinds of herbs and add to it

title: Fischkoteletten (Fish Cutlets)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 150 g cooked fish or fish scraps (deboned)
- 15 g chopped onions
- 15 g margarine
- 15 g flour
- 1/8 liter skim milk
- 1 pinch salt and pepper
- 1 egg yolk
- 5 g flour
- 1 egg white
- 20 g grated bread crumbs
- frying fat for temporary use
- 1/2 sheet blotting paper

## Directions

1. Sauté the onions in margarine until soft
2. Add the flour and stir; when done, stir in milk, salt and pepper to make a thick sauce
3. Remove from heat with the beaten egg yolk
4. Add the fish in small pieces and optionally some finely cut mushrooms; everything must become hot but not boil
5. Cool the mixture and form small cutlets on a floured board with a floured wooden palette when it is stiff
6. Coat with beaten egg white and bread crumbs
7. Fry in steaming fat to a beautiful even color
8. Degrease on blotting paper and arrange neatly

title: Fischotter (Otter Fish Stew)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- otter fish
- onions
- cloves
- mace
- lemon
- herbs and spices
- port wine or stone wine
- fat
- veal
- bread crust
- flour
- truffles
- citron yellow
- nutmeg
- white pepper
- kernels of cubeb peppers
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Skin the otter fish and cut into pieces
2. Soak in cold water for 5 to 6 days, changing the water daily
3. On the sixth day, remove and soak for 2 days in good wine with onions, cloves, mace, lemon, and various herbs and spices
4. Place with a bottle of Port or stone wine over the fire and let simmer for one hour
5. For the truffle sauce: line a casserole thickly with onions
6. Cook a piece of fat and 1-1/2 pounds of finely cut veal until golden
7. Add half a ladle of the stock in which the otter was cooked, bread crust, and some flour
8. Cook together and strain through a fine sieve
9. Weigh 2 to 3 truffels with their rough skin removed, with the citron yellow and juice
10. Add a pinch of nutmace, some white pepper, and 3 cubeb kernels
11. Place the otter fish in the sauce
12. Simmer for a while and add sliced mushrooms and truffles
13. Cook everything a bit longer

title: Fischpudding (Fish Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fish forcemeat
- 1 soaked and pressed roll
- 1 large finely minced onion
- 1/2 quarter pound butter
- 3 eggs
- 1/2 quart milk
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 1 thymus gland
- 1 onion
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 1 half cooking spoon of flour
- Cold water
- 1 spoonful good meat broth
- Juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Mince the onion and steam it in 1/2 quarter pound butter
2. Mix the fish forcemeat, soaked roll, steamed onion and 3 eggs together for a quarter hour
3. Add 1/2 quart milk, salt and nutmeg and mix thoroughly
4. Bind the mass tightly in a wet cloth
5. Let it cook in boiling water for one hour
6. Clean the thymus gland and mince it finely with an onion and lemon zest
7. Steam the minced thymus with a piece of butter in a casserole
8. Sprinkle half a cooking spoon of flour over it
9. Stir smooth with cold water
10. Bring to a boil with one spoonful of good meat broth
11. Add salt and juice of half a lemon
12. Place the pudding on a plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l fish broth
- 30 g flour
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 1 teaspoon diced onion
- 1/8 l skim milk
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley
- Fish scraps or fried bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Fish broth or cooking water from good fresh fish is well suited for this soup. To the diced onions and flour sweated in an earthenware pot with the fat, gradually add the fish broth while stirring through a sieve, let simmer for 1 hour, add the milk to balance the salt of the broth, bring to a boil again and season with pepper and parsley, place clean fish scraps in it or serve bread crumbs alongside.
2. Optionally, the soup can be finished with an egg yolk mixed into 2 tablespoons milk or white wine.

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish
- Butter
- Onion
- Fish bones
- Parsley

## Directions

1. Scrape the meat from the bones of a fish (usually white fish, pike, or barbel, or eel)
2. Place a piece of butter in a casserole
3. Cut onion into pieces worth 2 Kreuzer of wax
4. Chop the fish bones and add some parsley

title: Fischsuppe (Fish Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Fish (usually whitefish, pike perch, or barbel, or also eel)
- Butter
- Onion for 2 kreuzer worth
- Fish bones
- Parsley
- Water or meat broth (on fast days, water)

## Directions

1. Scrape the flesh from a fish of any kind, removing it from the bones
2. Place a piece of butter in a casserole, cut onion, chop the fish bones, add some parsley
3. Steam everything together in the casserole for one hour
4. On fast days pour water over it, let it cook for one hour, then strain through a hair sieve
5. Return it to the casserole and let it come to a boil again
6. Make small dumplings from the scraped fish meat, as has been described before, and arrange the cooked mixture over them

title: Fischwürste (Fish Sausage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fish (whitefish or barbel)
- milk
- onions
- parsley
- lemons
- butter
- 2 to 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scale and clean the fish, cutting the skin open along the back and peeling it off
2. Scrape the flesh from the bones and soak it for half an hour in milk to make it white
3. Weigh onions, parsley, and lemons and braise them in a piece of butter
4. Mince the fish meat as finely as possible
5. Stir it in a bowl with milk until it is completely white
6. Add 2 to 3 eggs and stir for another quarter hour
7. Add the braised vegetables with salt and nutmeg
8. Press the mass into casings and place the sausages in warm water
9. Fry them on a grill or in a pan

title: Fleisch, Eingeschnittenes (Diced Meat Stew)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g meat scraps
- 1/2 sour cucumber
- 20 g bacon or fat
- 20 g onions
- 20 g flour
- 1 spice mixture, ground
- 1/2 liter water
- 1 drop food coloring
- 5 g sugar
- 5 g salt
- 1 teaspoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into clean uniform pieces, dice the cucumber, and grate the onion.
2. In an earthenware pot, sauté bacon or fat with onion and flour until yellow, gradually add the water colored with food coloring while stirring, as well as the spice mixture, let the sauce cook thick, season it with salt, sugar, and vinegar, braise the cucumber dice in it, add the meat which should only be heated and not boiled, and serve immediately.

title: Fleischbrühe für einen Kranken oder einen Säugling (Meat Broth for a Sick Person or Infant)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g lean beef or veal
- 1/4 l water
- 3 g salt

## Directions

1. The washed meat is finely chopped, better still minced, cold water is poured over it, salt is added.
2. This mixture is cooked covered slowly for 3/4 hour and poured through a sieve. The broth can be served in the nursing bottle alone or with milk, and is also used for softening rusks or old rolls for a small child.

title: Fleischextraktbrühe (Meat Extract Broth)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 teaspoon meat extract
- 1/4 liter boiling water or light broth
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Dissolve the meat extract in the boiling water or light broth
2. Season with salt to taste

title: Fleischhache mit Reis (Meat and Rice Hash)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g cooked meat
- 50 g rice
- 1/4 liter broth
- 15 g margarine
- 1/8 liter meat extract broth
- 10 g onions
- salt to taste
- 5 g Swiss or Parmesan cheese
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 3 g flour

## Directions

1. Prepare a rice porridge from washed, blanched rice and rich meat broth (or 1 teaspoon fat and meat extract broth), 1 onion which is cooked whole and later removed, the necessary salt, and finally the grated cheese.
2. Let the coarsely chopped meat, to which one can also use cooked ham or cured meat, simmer for a few minutes in hot brown margarine along with 1/8 liter meat extract broth, the pepper, and flour.
3. Combine the meat and rice.
4. Alternatively: Layer rice and meat, each prepared separately, in alternating layers so that rice forms the top layer on a butter-brushed dish from which the dish can be brought both to the oven and to table, brush one beaten egg over it, sprinkle the surface with grated cheese, and let the hash brown in the oven.
5. Alternatively: Press both in layers into a well-buttered mold dusted with breadcrumbs and bring the dish turned out, with a piquant brown or Dutch sauce to table. The meat can also be cut into thin slices, sprinkled with margarine pieces and grated cheese, and prepared with the rice according to the various instructions above. (Requires more fat and cheese.)

title: Fleischklösse, Gebratene (Fried Meat Balls)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 70 g scraped beef
- 50 g scraped pork
- 1 peeled bread roll
- 1/8 liter water for soaking
- 1/2 egg
- 1 pinch pepper, 5 g salt
- 1 knife point grated onions
- 10 g grated bread roll
- 25 g fat or margarine for frying

## Directions

1. Peel the bread roll, cut it into pieces, and soak it.
2. Mix beef, pork, egg, squeezed-out bread roll, pepper, salt, and onion thoroughly together in a bowl; form this mixture into 4 uniform balls, press them smooth, roll them in grated bread roll, and fry them in steaming fat for 7 minutes slowly with frequent turning.
3. Alternatively, form the entire mixture into an oblong shape and fry it in a pan with 1 tablespoon fat for 1/2 hour under frequent basting; gradually pour a few spoons of water under the roast and thicken the sauce after removing it with 3 g potato flour mixed in 2 tablespoons water.
4. Alternatively (Fleischpudding): In a greased, breadcrumb-lined sealed mold, the well-pressed mixture is cooked for 1 1/2 hours in a water bath or in a pressure cooker, turned out, and served with sauce of choice. The frying fat is then omitted.

title: Fleischsalat (Meat Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g cooked lean meat
- 1/16 l cold broth
- 1/2 tsp mustard
- 1/4 tsp grated onion
- 1 tbsp wine vinegar
- 1 tsp chopped herbs
- 1 tsp salt and pepper
- 1 tbsp oil
- 1 pickled gherkin

## Directions

1. Cut the meat into cubes or small fillets. Slice the gherkin.
2. Mix the meat with the broth and then with mustard, onions, vinegar, and herbs. Season with pepper, salt, and optionally 4 drops of Maggi seasoning. Cover and let stand for 1-2 hours.
3. Arrange the salad mixed with oil and taste again for seasoning, arranging it in a mound. Garnish with the gherkin sliced into uniform slices, arranging them in an overlapping circle, and sprinkle with the gherkin ends cut into small cubes.
4. This salad can be refined by using pearl onions, capers, etc. as ingredients. Fish or poultry salad are prepared the same way, only the pieces are irregular and of arbitrary size.

title: Flugpasteten (Flying Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- grated chocolate

## Directions

1. Whip egg whites into stiff peaks
2. Stir in grated chocolate
3. Make sleeves from white paper and place on a black baking sheet
4. Fill with the mixture
5. Dry in the oven for 4 minutes

title: Flugpasteten (Flying Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 1 tablet grated chocolate
- white paper

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites to a stiff snow
2. Stir one tablet grated chocolate into it
3. Make small paper cuffs
4. Place them on a black baking sheet
5. Fill them with the mixture and dry in the oven for 4 minutes

title: Forellen mit saurer Sulz (Trout in Sour Jelly)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- 2 calf's feet, cleaned
- 1 Loth isinglass
- fish broth
- 1 egg
- 1 sheet blotting paper
- clove leaves
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the trout blue and place on a dish, cover tightly so the blue color does not fade
2. Chop 2 cleaned calf's feet together with 1 Loth isinglass, add fish broth so it makes 1-1.5 Maas
3. Cook for 2 to 3 hours, strain the jelly through a fine sieve and let it stand in a bowl for a quarter hour so the sediment remains on the bottom
4. Draw a sheet of blotting paper through the jelly so the fat adheres to it, beat a whole egg in a casserole and pour the jelly slowly into it
5. Set over fire and stir constantly with a new cooking spoon until it boils, then remove it, pour into a felt hat
6. Place the trout on a large plate, pour some of the clear jelly over it, let it stand and color part of the jelly green
7. Take fat clove leaves, pound them in a mortar, press the juice through a cloth and make the jelly green with it
8. When the jelly is half set, pour the green jelly over the trout, let it set completely and pour clear jelly over once more
9. Garnish the fish to taste

title: Forellen mit saurer Sulz (Trout in Sour Jelly) - Variant
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- 2 calf's feet, cleaned
- 1 Loth isinglass
- fish stock
- 1 whole egg
- 1 sheet blotting paper
- clove leaves
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil the trout blue and place on a dish, cover tightly so the blue does not disappear
2. Make the following sauce: Chop 2 cleaned calf's feet together with 1 Loth isinglass, add fish stock so it makes 1-1.5 Maas
3. Cook for 2 to 3 hours, strain the jelly through a fine sieve and let it stand in a bowl for a quarter hour so the sediment remains on the bottom
4. Draw a sheet of blotting paper through the jelly so the fat adheres to it, beat a whole egg in a casserole and pour the jelly slowly into it
5. Set over fire and stir constantly with a new cooking spoon until it boils, then remove it and pour into a felt hat
6. Place the trout on a large plate, pour some of the clear jelly over it and let it stand
7. Color part of the jelly green in a dish: take fat clove leaves, pound them in a mortar, press the juice through a cloth and make the jelly green with it
8. When it is half set, pour the green jelly over the trout, let it set completely and pour clear jelly over once more
9. The fish can now be garnished to taste

title: Forellen, blau gesotten (Blue Trout, Boiled)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- trout
- wine
- vinegar
- lemon
- whole pepper
- herbs
- salt
- oil

## Directions

1. Make a court bouillon from equal parts wine and vinegar
2. Add lemon, whole pepper, all kinds of herbs, and a handful of salt
3. Let everything simmer together for a quarter hour
4. Cut open the trout at the throat just enough to remove the gallbladder
5. Place the trout in the court bouillon
6. Remove from the fire
7. Place the fish on a platter and pour vinegar and oil over it
8. Note: all types of trout must not be handled or washed extensively to preserve their beautiful blue color; only wipe away the blood with a cloth

title: Frankfurter Brodknöpflein (Frankfurt Bread Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 rolls
- butter
- onions and parsley
- 6 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls fine flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- bread for braising

## Directions

1. Cut 4 rolls into cubes and roast half of them in butter
2. Steam finely cut onions and parsley also in butter
3. Beat 6 eggs into them, add 2 spoonfuls fine flour, the unroasted roll cubes, salt, and nutmeg
4. Mix everything together
5. Make dumplings the size of a hen's egg from this mixture
6. Let them cook for a quarter hour in salt water
7. Braise them with butter and bread
8. Serve them with game

title: Frankfurter Kugelhopf (Frankfurt Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- 2 spoons of beer yeast
- half a schoppen of lukewarm milk
- 10 loths of butter
- one quarter pound of clean small raisins
- 2 loths of large pitted raisins, cut in half
- one loth of ground sugar
- one knife point of salt
- 4 blanched bitter almonds, coarsely ground
- 4 whole eggs

## Directions

1. In a bowl, make a small dough with one pound of flour, 2 spoons of beer yeast, and half a schoppen of lukewarm milk
2. Let it rise in gentle warmth
3. Cut in 10 loths of butter
4. Add one quarter pound of clean small raisins, 2 loths of large pitted raisins cut in half, one loth of ground sugar, one knife point of salt, 4 blanched bitter almonds coarsely ground, and 4 whole eggs
5. Work the dough together with all ingredients
6. Beat for a quarter hour
7. Brush a kugelhopf mold with butter
8. Sprinkle a little sugar into it
9. Fill in the mass
10. Let the kugelhopf rise a little more in gentle warmth
11. Bake in the oven until golden yellow

title: Frankfurter Speise (Frankfurt Dish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 to 18 peeled Borsdorf apples
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 schoppen white wine
- 3 schoppen milk
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- 1/2 glass Maraschino
- Pastry dough
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Steam 16 to 18 peeled Borsdorf apples, from which the core has been removed, in a casserolle with 4 loth sugar and 1 schoppen white wine
2. Make a crème with Maraschino as follows: let 3 schoppen milk, 4 loth sugar and 1 piece cinnamon cook together for a time
3. Smooth 8 egg yolks and 1 cooking spoon flour together, pour the boiling milk over it along with 1/2 glass Maraschino
4. Let everything come to the boil once more in the pan, spurt it vigorously in a pot
5. Place the apples on a plate, make a firm water pastry dough and make a ring of it around the plate
6. Brush with an egg, pour the crème over the apples and let them bake golden in a baking oven

title: Frankfurter Wildbretpastete (Frankfurt Venison Pie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- thick muscles of a roe deer leg
- bacon
- onions
- bay leaves
- pepper
- salt
- 1 pound pork forcemeat
- half pound tender veal
- half pound lean bacon
- 2 cleaned herrings
- yellow of 1 lemon
- 4 shallot onions
- 2 ordinary chopped and braised onions
- 1 peeled cross roll
- fine spices
- 2 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls chopped capers
- 12 peeled olives
- quarter pound cleaned truffles

## Directions

1. Make beautiful pieces from the thick muscles of a roe deer leg.
2. Beat them and lard them with bacon.
3. Braise them in a covered saucepan with finely cut onions, bay leaves, pepper, and salt.
4. When the meat is golden, let it cool on a plate.
5. Skin the remaining meat from the roe deer leg and pound it with 1 pound pork forcemeat, half pound tender veal, half pound lean bacon, 2 cleaned herrings, and the yellow of 1 lemon into a dough.
6. Add 4 shallot onions and 2 ordinary chopped and braised onions.
7. Soak 1 peeled cross roll, press it out, and add it along with salt and all kinds of fine spices to the meat.
8. Rub everything together on a grater to a foam.
9. Mix 2 eggs, 2 spoonfuls chopped capers, 12 peeled olives, and quarter pound cleaned truffles cut into pieces.

title: Franzosensuppe (French Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head of white cabbage
- Savoy cabbage
- 2 kohlrabi
- 2 celery stalks
- 2 yellow turnips
- 1 white turnip
- 1 handful of parsley
- cold water
- meat broth
- liver, stomach and heart of poultry (optional)
- finely cut bread

## Directions

1. Cut all vegetables as finely as noodles
2. Place in cold water and set on fire, letting it cook until half soft
3. Pour through a hair sieve and rinse with fresh cold water, letting it drain well
4. Cook it in a pot in meat broth until tender
5. If available, liver, stomach and heart of poultry cut into pieces can be added and fine cut bread cooked with it
6. Alternatively, everything can be cooked so soft that it can be pushed through a soup strainer, but in this case omit the cabbage and liver

title: Französische Jussuppe (French Meat Broth Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter or other fat
- Onions
- Yellow turnips
- Celery
- 2 pounds beef
- Bones (optional)
- Water
- Meat broth
- Black bread
- Eggs (optional)

## Directions

1. Put a piece of butter or fat in a casserole
2. Line the bottom with onions, yellow turnips and celery
3. Cut 2 pounds of beef into small pieces and place in the casserole
4. Add any leftover bones if available
5. Cover the casserole and let it steam for one hour
6. Uncover and let the steamed mixture brown well
7. When it is brown, add a ladle full of water
8. Fill the casserole with meat broth and let it cook 2 hours
9. Cut slices from black bread and add to the casserole
10. After everything has cooked together for half an hour, pass it through a hair sieve
11. Let it come to a boil once more in the casserole and serve
12. If desired, eggs can be beaten into the soup bowl

title: Freimaurerbrod (Freemason Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 quarter-pound peeled almonds with rose water
- 1-1/2 quarter-pound sugar
- 4 Loth finely chopped citron
- 2 Loth finely chopped orange peel
- 1/2 Quint pounded cinnamon
- 1 Quint cloves
- 2 to 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together in a bowl for a quarter hour: 1-1/2 quarter-pound peeled almonds pounded with rose water, 1-1/2 quarter-pound sugar, 4 Loth finely chopped citron, 2 Loth finely chopped orange peel, 1/2 Quint pounded cinnamon, 1 Quint cloves, and the snow from 2 to 3 egg whites
2. Form finger-long sticks from the mixture
3. Place them on a sheet brushed with bacon or wax
4. Bake in the oven

title: Freimaurerbrod anderer Art (Freemason Bread, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon
- flour
- bacon
- citron

## Directions

1. Beat 2 whole eggs and 2 yolks into 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar and stir well until it becomes thick
2. Grate the yolk of half a lemon on a grater and mix in
3. Knead in flour until it can be worked well on a noodle board
4. Form finger-long small sticks from it
5. Place them on a sheet brushed with bacon
6. Make 2 cuts with the back of a knife into each stick
7. Cut citron as long and as wide as the cuts
8. Place it in the cuts
9. Let them stand another quarter hour so the sugar rises up
10. Then bake them

title: Fricandeau von Kalbfleisch (Fricandeau of Veal)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal shank
- Butter
- Onions
- Carrots
- Celery
- Bay leaves
- Lemon
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Beef broth
- Flour
- Jus

## Directions

1. Take a piece of veal shank, cut it along the grain, pound it, remove the skin and lard it.
2. Place it in lukewarm water so it stays white.
3. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole and add chopped onions, carrots, celery, bay leaves and lemon.
4. Place the meat in the casserole.
5. Pour in a glass of wine, some vinegar and beef broth.
6. Braise for one hour.
7. Remove the meat and let the sauce turn dark yellow.
8. Sprinkle a little flour and pour in a little broth or jus.
9. Let it cook together for another quarter hour.
10. Strain the broth through a fine sieve, pour it back into the casserole and add the meat.
11. Let it boil a little longer and add the zest and juice of half a lemon.
12. When serving, add lemon slices.

title: Frische Heringe zu Braten (Fresh Herrings for Frying)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 fresh herrings
- 40 g salt
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 75 g flour
- 250 g frying fat
- 1 l vinegar
- 50 g onion slices
- 1 spice portion
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Scale the herrings, gut them, wash them, make a few light cuts along the back and salt them for 1/2 hour.
2. Dry them off and roll them evenly in flour mixed with pepper and salt.
3. Fry or bake the herrings in steaming fat until cooked through and golden.
4. Once cooled, pour over the herrings a sauce made from vinegar, water, salt, spices and onion slices that has been brought to a boil, seasoned to taste, then cooled.
5. Pack them in a stone pot, tie it shut, and use as needed.
6. Note: herrings should only be purchased when the head looks entirely pale; once it has turned red, the herrings are not fresh. The fat should be clarified.

title: Froschschenkel in Citronensauce (Frog Legs in Lemon Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Frogs
- Lemon
- Onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the frogs thoroughly and cut off the paws.
2. Draw one foot over the other.
3. Chop half a lemon with its pulp and onions.
4. Put butter in a casserole, add the chopped mixture and frog legs.
5. Cover and braise for a quarter hour.
6. Add as much flour as you can hold between two fingers.
7. Pour in a spoonful of water and salt.
8. Make sure no lumps form.

title: Fruchtsauce (Fruit Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g Johannisbeeren oder Blaubeeren, oder 1/2 l beliebiger auch gemischter Fruchtsaft
- 1 Stueck Apfelsinenschale
- 1/4 l Wasser
- 30 g Zucker
- 5 g Staerkemehl
- 1 Essl. Wasser

## Directions

1. Die abgebeerten Beeren werden gewaschen und mit Apfelsinenschale und Wasser auf den Herd gestellt; das Staerkemehl wird mit Wasser ausgeloest.
2. Die Beeren werden zerkocht, durch ein Sieb gestrichen und mit Zucker abgeschmeckt. Der Saft wird wieder in den Topf zurueckgegossen, mit dem in Wasser geloesten Staerkemehl aufgekocht, in einen Napf gegossen und kaltgestellt. Wird Fruchtsaft verwendet, so kann der Zucker fehlen, die Sauce stellt sich dann aber meistens teurer. Man gibt die Sauce zu gelben oben weissen Flammeris oder Puddings und auch zu Mehl- oder Griesskloessen.
3. Es koennen auch Pflaumen (Pflaumensauce) oder Kirschen (Kirschsauce) oder Fruchtsuppenreste verwendet werden. Pflaumenmus in derselben Weise verduennt ergibt Pflaumenmussauce, Kirschmus: Kirschsauce. Man kann die Fruchtfluessigkeit nach Geschmack beliebig verduennen und kalt als Fruchtkaltschale geben oder auch mit 30 g Staerkemehl verkocht zu Fruchtflammerl benutzen.
4. Die durchgestrichenen Reste kann man noch einmal etwas mit aufkochen, durchgiessen und zur Sauce mit verwenden.

title: Fülle zu Pastetchen auf französische Art (Filling for Pastries in the French Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound of skinned veal
- 1/2 pound of beef kidney fat
- zest of 1 lemon
- juice of 1 lemon
- steamed onions
- 1 soaked and squeezed roll
- 4 eggs
- 1 knife point of ground pepper
- 1 knife point of nutmeg
- 1 knife point of cinnamon
- 2 tablespoons of cold water
- puff pastry
- bacon
- flour

## Directions

1. Chop together the veal, kidney fat, and lemon zest like a dough
2. Add the lemon juice, steamed onions, and the soaked and squeezed roll
3. Put everything in a mortar and stir with the eggs until frothy, adding the pepper, nutmeg, cinnamon, and cold water
4. Make puff pastry and roll it out two knife backs thick
5. Place on a floured black baking sheet and form a round base
6. Cover with bacon and spread the mixture on top
7. Cover again with bacon, leaving a 2-finger wide border empty
8. Cover with a puff pastry lid brushed with cold water
9. Frame the lid with a 2-finger wide strip of puff pastry
10. Cut various flowers into it with a hot knife
11. Brush with egg and bake
12. Serve cold

title: Galanterieküchlein (Fancy Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound fine flour
- 1 pound butter

## Directions

1. Rub together one pound fine flour and one pound butter by hand until no butter is visible anymore

title: Galanterieküchlein (Fancy Little Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1 pound butter
- 1/2 pound sifted sugar
- 2 eggs
- coarsely ground cinnamon and sugar

## Directions

1. Rub flour and butter together by hand until no butter is visible
2. Add sifted sugar and eggs
3. Roll out the dough and fold it over itself
4. Roll out finger-thick
5. Cut out little cakes with a tin mold
6. Brush with egg white
7. Sprinkle with coarsely ground cinnamon and sugar
8. Bake until golden yellow

title: Gansleber in Hammelsnetz auf dem Rost gebraten (Goose Liver Roasted in Caul on the Grate)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- salt
- pepper
- wheat meal
- caul

## Directions

1. Wash the goose liver well
2. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and grated wheat meal
3. Spread out the caul
4. Wrap the liver in the caul
5. Place on the grate
6. Put some coals underneath
7. Roast slowly until nicely browned
8. When serving, add lemon juice

title: Gansleber mit Citronensauce (Goose Liver with Lemon Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose liver
- Butter
- Onion
- Cloves
- Shallots
- Lemon
- Flour
- Wine
- Beef broth

## Directions

1. Soak the goose liver in water beforehand and let it drain on a cloth.
2. Place 4 lots of butter, a cleaned onion studded with 3 cloves, 3 shallots and half a lemon cut into slices in a casserole.
3. Place the liver on top and braise slowly.
4. If the liver is large it needs half an hour.
5. Place the liver on the serving dish.
6. For the sauce: take as much flour as you can grasp firmly with 3 fingers and add to the casserole.
7. Add a glass of wine, juice of a lemon and some beef broth.
8. Let it cook together for a quarter hour.
9. Strain through a fine sieve and pour over the liver.
10. Sprinkle the finely chopped zest of a lemon on top.

title: Gansleber mit Sardellen (Goose Liver with Anchovies)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Goose liver
- Butter
- Lemon
- Nutmeg
- Shallots
- Anchovies
- Flour
- Beef broth
- Bouillon
- Wine
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. In a casserole, braise a goose liver with 3 lots of butter, half a lemon cut into slices, some whole nutmeg and 4 shallots.
2. Clean and remove the insides from 4 anchovies.
3. Pound them finely in a mortar with a little butter.
4. Knead 3 finger-widths of flour into the anchovy butter.
5. Add a little beef broth or preferably strong bouillon and 2 to 3 spoonfuls of wine.
6. Let everything cook together.
7. Add salt, pepper and juice of half a lemon.
8. Place the liver on the serving platter.
9. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve and pour over.
10. Sprinkle some chopped lemon zest on top.

title: Gansleberwürste (Goose Liver Sausage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 large goose livers (not soaked or only slightly soaked)
- 3 quarter-pounds of very finely minced ham
- onions
- one handful of parsley
- bacon
- the zest of 2 lemons, finely minced
- the pith of 2 lemons without seeds, finely minced
- white bread for 2 kreuzer, soaked in milk and pressed out
- 3 knife tips of white pepper
- 2 knife tips of nutmeg
- half a pound of clean, peeled, and finely pounded truffles

## Directions

1. Mince 2 of the goose livers finely, cut 2 into cubes
2. Mix all the livers together in a bowl with the finely minced ham, onions, and parsley
3. Braise everything in bacon
4. Add the finely minced lemon zest, the finely minced lemon pith, the soaked and pressed bread, white pepper, and nutmeg
5. Mix in the finely pounded truffles
6. Combine everything with the liver mixture and salt strongly
7. Fill into beef casings of the type usually used for lung sausage
8. Do not tie the casing firmly at the bottom, and fill it with the mass using a tablespoon, not pressing too firmly

title: Gebackene Aepfelschnitten (Fried Apple Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 5 pieces of Mundbroden (sweet bread)
- milk
- 1 schoppen lukewarm wine
- flour
- 6 to 8 apples
- 1 piece of butter
- 2 loth sugar
- 1/8 pound small raisins
- cinnamon
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut off the hard rind from the bottom of 3 to 5 pieces of Mundbroden
2. Dip 3 to 4 slices at a time in milk, lay them on top of each other, and continue until all are used
3. Make a thin dough (Flädchenteig) from 1 schoppen lukewarm wine and flour
4. Peel 6 to 8 apples, put a piece of butter in a casserole with the apples, 2 loth sugar, and 1/8 pound small raisins
5. Cook them soft, add 2 knife points of cinnamon, stir all together like a porridge, and let it cool on a plate
6. Heat lard, take half a spoonful of the apple mixture and stir it into the dough
7. Take 2 of the bread slices, spread a tablespoonful of the cooked apples on one, cover with another slice, dip in the dough
8. Fry until golden and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Gebackene Bratwürste (Fried Bratwurst)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk
- eggs
- lard
- bratwurst sausages

## Directions

1. Make a batter from flour, milk, and eggs
2. Pour 2 spoonfuls of hot lard into it
3. Put the bratwurst sausages in hot water
4. Cut them into finger-long pieces
5. Mix them into the batter
6. Fry the batter in lard until golden

title: Gebackene Chokoladecrême (Baked Chocolate Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- chocolate (quarter pound)
- water (1 glass)
- milk (1 maas)
- sugar (4 loth)
- cinnamon (1 knife point)
- egg yolks (8)
- flour (half a cooking spoon)
- cold milk
- egg whites (8)
- almonds (2 loth, finely cut lengthwise)
- green granulated sugar

## Directions

1. Dissolve a quarter pound chocolate in a brass pan with one glass of water
2. Cook it with one maas milk, 4 loth sugar and a knife point of cinnamon
3. Stir 8 egg yolks smooth with half a cooking spoon of flour and cold milk
4. Stir the chocolate to it
5. Let everything draw once more in the pan together
6. Spoon it into a pot until it cools slightly
7. Pour the cream through a hair sieve onto the plate
8. Whip a stiff foam from the 8 egg whites
9. Cover the entire cream with it
10. Decorate with 2 loth almonds finely cut lengthwise
11. Sprinkle green granulated sugar over it
12. Place the cream in the roasting oven a quarter hour before serving so it becomes hot again

title: Gebackene Finger von Mandelnteig (Fried Almond Fingers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- one quarter pound butter
- one quarter pound ground almonds
- half a glass wine
- cinnamon and cloves
- zest of one lemon
- some orange peel
- 2 eggs
- hot lard

## Directions

1. Work a smooth dough from one pound flour, one quarter pound butter, one quarter pound ground almonds, half a glass wine, cinnamon and cloves (as desired), zest of one lemon, some orange peel and 2 eggs
2. Make finger-like shapes from it and fry in hot lard

title: Gebackene Flädlein mit Mandelfülle (Fried Pancakes with Almond Filling)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk or water for pancakes
- sugar
- 1/4 pound blanched almonds, finely pounded with egg white
- 6 loth finely pounded sugar
- 1/2 lemon, zested on a grater
- 3 whole eggs
- 1 egg, beaten
- lard
- wine or milk
- arak

## Directions

1. Prepare pancakes (Flädlein) as usual, but add some sugar to the batter
2. Mix 1/4 pound blanched almonds with egg white and pound finely
3. Beat together with 6 loth finely pounded sugar, 1/2 lemon zest, and 3 whole eggs until thick
4. Separate the pancakes and spread the filling on each one
5. Roll them together
6. Cut each roll in half
7. Turn in beaten egg
8. Fry well in lard
9. Make a wine or milk sauce with arak to serve alongside

title: Gebackene Froschschenkel (Fried Frog Legs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- frog legs
- salt
- flour
- eggs
- breadcrumbs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Wash the frog legs clean
2. Cut off the front joints
3. Salt the remaining legs
4. Clean them well so they lie nicely on the plate
5. Dip them in flour, then in eggs, then in grated bread
6. Fry them in lard
7. Do not put too many in the pan at once, otherwise they will not roast properly
