title: Gebackene Froschschenkel (Fried Frog Legs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Frog legs
- Salt
- Flour
- Eggs
- Grated bread
- Lard

## Directions

1. Wash the frog legs clean and cut off the front joints
2. Salt the remaining legs, clean them well so they lie nicely on the plate
3. Turn them in flour, then in eggs, and in grated bread
4. Fry them in lard
5. Do not put too many in the pan at once, otherwise they will not be roasted properly

title: Gebackene Griesknöpflein (Fried Semolina Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 to 5 handfuls semolina
- 1/2 Maas milk
- 4 eggs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Cook 4 to 5 handfuls of semolina in 1/2 Maas milk for a quarter hour until very thick.
2. Stir 4 eggs into it in a bowl (adding gradually).
3. Fry small dumplings in hot lard.

title: Gebackene Griesschnitten (Baked Semolina Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 4 to 5 handfuls semolina flour
- 6 eggs
- Chives
- Nutmeg
- Salt
- Butter for the form

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 maas milk with 4 to 5 handfuls semolina flour to a thick porridge
2. Gradually stir in 6 eggs in a bowl
3. Add considerable chives, nutmeg and salt
4. Bake the dough in a butter-brushed form
5. Cut into small slices and serve in soup - you can serve them in meat soups or in soups à la Reine

title: Gebackene Kalbsfüße (Fried Calf's Feet)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- salt water
- 1 Schoppen water
- butter (hen egg-sized)
- flour
- 3 to 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the feet
2. Cook them soft in salt water
3. Cut them into small pieces
4. Make a batter: let 1 schoppen water and hen egg-sized butter cook together
5. Add flour until it becomes a thick porridge
6. Let it cook until it separates from the pan
7. Remove from fire
8. Stir in 3 to 4 eggs
9. Dip the pieces one after another into the batter
10. Fry them golden brown

title: Gebackene Kalbsleber (Fried Calf's Liver)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- flour
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from a calf's liver
2. Cut out the veins
3. Cut the liver into slices
4. Turn them in flour
5. Fry in butter
6. Season with salt only just before serving

title: Gebackene Kartoffeln (Fried Potatoes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt water
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Using a cutter, make small round potatoes of even size
2. Boil them in salted water until half-cooked
3. Pour into a colander
4. Fry in a pan with hot lard until golden yellow, but do not put too many in the pan at once
5. These potatoes are used to garnish vegetables, such as cabbage and spinach

title: Gebackene Kartoffeln anderer Art (Fried Potatoes, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- salt
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Peel nice white potatoes well
2. Cut them into slices the thickness of a knife back
3. Salt them lightly
4. Fry them in hot lard until cooked through

title: Gebackene Küchlein von Kalbsbrieschen (Fried Cakes of Calf's Sweetbreads)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 rolls (Wecken)
- water
- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 calf's sweetbreads
- 2 to 3 shallot onions, chopped
- chives or parsley
- 5 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- lard

## Directions

1. Soak 2 grated rolls in water and press out firmly
2. Stir 1/4 pound butter lightly in a bowl and add the rolls
3. Simmer 2 calf's sweetbreads in water
4. Cut them, after removing the windpipes and veins, into small pieces
5. Braise in a casserole with a piece of butter 2 to 3 chopped shallot onions, some chives or parsley, and the sweetbreads
6. Let cool, then stir in 5 eggs one after another into the butter-stirred rolls
7. Add the sweetbreads along with salt and nutmeg
8. Form round cakes from the mixture
9. Fry beautifully golden in lard
10. Can be served as a side dish

title: Gebackene Mandeln (Fried Almond Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Eidotter
- 1 Ei
- ein Viertelpfund Zucker
- 1 Citrone
- welschnußgroße Butter
- 5 Loth Mehl
- heißes Schmalz zum Ausbacken

## Directions

1. 3 Eidotter und 1 Ei an ein Viertelpfund Zucker rühren.
2. In eine Schüssel nehmen.
3. Eine Citrone am Reibeisen abreiben und dazugeben.
4. Welschnußgroße Butter zergehen lassen und alles untereinander rühren.
5. 5 Loth Mehl darunter mischen.
6. Teig auf dem Nudelbrett fingersdick auswalken.
7. Mit einem blechernen Mödelchen in Mandelform ausstechen.
8. Die Küchelchen im heißen Schmalz gelb ausbacken.
9. Mit Zucker bestreuen.
10. Beim Ausstechen nicht zu viel Mehl hineinarbeiten.

title: Gebackene Mandeln anderer Art (Fried Almond Cakes, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein Viertelpfund abgezogene Mandeln
- Rosenwasser
- 2 Eier
- 4 Loth fein gestoßener Zucker
- eine halbe Citrone
- eine Messerspitze voll Zimmt
- 12 Loth feines Mehl
- heißes Schmalz zum Ausbacken

## Directions

1. Ein Viertelpfund abgezogene Mandeln mit Rosenwasser ganz fein stoßen.
2. In eine Schüssel bringen.
3. 2 Eier darunter schlagen.
4. 4 Loth fein gestoßenen Zucker hinzufügen.
5. Die Masse eine Viertelstunde lang rühren.
6. Die am Reibeisen abgeriebene Schale einer halben Citrone und eine Messerspitze voll Zimmt dazugeben.
7. Mit 12 Loth feinem Mehl gut rühren.
8. Auf einem mit Mehl bestreuten Brett kleinf ingerdick auswalken.
9. Mit einem Mödelchen Mandeln ausstechen.
10. Im heißen Schmalz schön gelb ausbacken.
11. Mit Zucker bestreuen.

title: Gebackene Mandeln anderer Art (Fried Almond Cakes, Third Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Eidotter
- 1 Löffel voll Kirschengeist
- 2 Löffel voll Zucker
- Mehl
- Zimmt
- Zucker zum Bestreuen

## Directions

1. 3 Eidotter, 1 Löffel voll Kirschengeist und 2 Löffel voll Zucker untereinander machen.
2. So viel Mehl darunter rühren, bis man den Teig würgen kann.
3. Im Uebrigen wie die andern gebackenen Mandeln machen.
4. Mit Zimmt und Zucker bestreuen.

title: Gebackene Milch (Baked Milk)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 whole eggs
- 5 egg yolks
- 1/2 measures milk
- sugar
- 1 lemon
- 3 spoonfuls flour
- hot lard
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir together 5 whole eggs, 5 egg yolks, and 1/2 measures milk well
2. Take sugar on which a lemon has been grated
3. Roast 3 spoonfuls flour in hot lard until light yellow
4. Pour the milk with the eggs to it
5. Cook everything until it becomes thick, stirring constantly
6. Should the lard cook out, it must be skimmed off
7. When the porridge is on the plate, sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on it

title: Gebackene Rosen (Fried Roses)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 eggs
- 4 lots butter

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 3 to 4 eggs
2. Work 4 lots butter into it
3. Roll out strongly to the thickness of a knife back
4. Cut out 4 pieces with a tin rose mold
5. Brush each piece in the middle
6. Lay the 4 pieces on top of each other
7. Press them firmly together in the middle
8. Bake them in hot lard
9. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Gebackene Schwarzwurzeln (Fried Salsify)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Salt water
- Flour
- 1 schoppen wheat beer or wine
- Lard
- Butter sauce or spinach

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and cut the salsify into finger-length pieces, and cook soft in salted water.
2. Make the following batter: stir 2 handfuls of flour smooth with 1 schoppen of wheat beer or wine, dip the salsify in this batter, fry them in a pan with hot lard until beautiful yellow, and either make a butter sauce to go with them or serve them dry with spinach.

title: Gebackene Vanillecrême (Baked Vanilla Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 finger-length piece vanilla
- 1 schoppen milk
- 4 spoonfuls flour
- cold milk
- 4 loth sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Cook 1 finger-length vanilla piece with 1 schoppen milk for a quarter hour
2. In a brass pan, stir 4 spoonfuls flour smooth with cold milk
3. Add 4 loth sugar, 6 egg yolks and 1 more schoppen milk, as well as the vanilla milk
4. Cook everything to a thick paste on the fire
5. Slowly fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Butter a dish, fill with the cream and bake until golden yellow

title: Gebackene Weincrême (Baked Wine Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a maas wine
- 1 lemon peel
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 1 quarter pound sugar
- a little flour
- cold water
- 8 egg yolks
- 8 egg whites
- finely cut almonds
- coarsely pounded sugar

## Directions

1. Let half a maas wine cook with the peel of a lemon, one piece whole cinnamon, and a quarter pound sugar
2. Stir a little flour smooth with cold water, beat 8 egg yolks into it
3. Stir this into the boiling wine and let everything cook once more in the pan
4. Pour the cream through a fine sieve onto the plate
5. Beat the 8 egg whites into snow and form round balls the size of a hen's egg
6. Sprinkle them with finely cut almonds and coarsely pounded sugar
7. Place the cream in the oven for a quarter hour before serving

title: Gebackenes Kalbshirn (Fried Calf's Brain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brain
- water
- vinegar
- salt
- eggs
- bread crumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the brain
2. Place it on the fire with half water, half vinegar, and salt
3. Cook for one hour
4. Remove it and let it drain
5. Turn it in beaten eggs
6. Then turn it in grated bread
7. Fry it golden brown in hot lard

title: Gebackenes Lammfleisch (Fried Lamb)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb meat
- salt water
- eggs
- flour
- breadcrumbs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and simmer it in salted water until tender
2. Cut into pieces of desired size
3. Dip in beaten egg, then in flour mixed with breadcrumbs
4. Fry in hot lard until golden yellow

title: Gebratene Brieschen (Fried Calf Sweetbreads)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf sweetbreads
- salt water
- butter
- onion
- lemon zest
- salt

## Directions

1. Blanch the sweetbreads in salt water, clean them, and lard them
2. In a casserole, fry them in butter and sliced onion until golden yellow
3. When serving, pour the jus through a fine sieve over them, sprinkle finely chopped lemon zest over them, and salt to taste

title: Gebratene Ochsenzunge (Braised Ox Tongue)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ox tongue
- onions
- lemons
- bay leaves
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- 6 Loth butter
- 1 glass wine

## Directions

1. Clean an ox tongue and simmer it until the skin can be removed
2. Lard it
3. Place it in a casserole
4. Add onions, lemons, bay leaves, salt, pepper and mace
5. Add 6 loth butter and a glass of wine
6. Cover the casserole
7. Let it braise
8. You can also braise this tongue in the oven or on a spit
9. When serving add lemons

title: Gebratene Schnepfe (Roasted Woodcock)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- woodcock
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- onion
- black bread
- fat
- yellow carrots
- parsnip roots
- jus
- giblets
- half a lemon shell
- parsley
- young hen liver
- nutmeg
- white pepper
- flour
- butter
- red wine
- roasting fat
- white bread without crust
- lard

## Directions

1. Pluck the woodcock with its head, though it is better if the head is not marinated.
2. Turn the feet back onto the thighs and remove the entrails.
3. Rub the bird well with salt, pepper, and some cloves.
4. Place an onion and a piece of black bread inside it, tuck the head onto the breast, and press out the eyes.
5. Roast it in a casserole with a piece of fat.
6. Lay onions, yellow carrots, and parsnip roots beside it, covering the casserole so the bird stays white.
7. Pour a little jus over it.
8. For the woodcock farce: Take the giblets (but not the stomach), clean the intestines, and mince them very finely with the shell of half a lemon, parsley, and the liver of a young hen.
9. Add nutmeg, white pepper, and 3 finger-widths of flour.
10. In a small casserole, stir together in melted butter 2 tablespoonfuls of flour, some red wine, and a spoonful of the fat the woodcocks were roasted in, but do not cook it.
11. Cut white bread without crust into slices, fry them in lard, spread the woodcock farce on them, and place the slices on the platter.
12. Put the woodcocks in the center, garnish with parsley and lemon, and serve the sauce separately.

title: Gebratener Aal (Fried Eel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eel
- pepper
- salt
- sage
- lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Skin the eel and remove the hair, head, and tail
2. Remove the gallbladder
3. Cut the eel into pieces two fingers wide
4. Rub with pepper and salt
5. Wrap each piece with sage and lemon slices
6. Fry in a frying pan with a piece of butter
7. Before serving, remove the thread that bound the sage and lemon, and arrange lemon slices alongside

title: Gebratener Auerhahn (Roasted Capercaillie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Capercaillie
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Mace
- 1 Onion
- Half a lemon
- 1 Bay leaf
- Butter
- Pork skin (from half the side of a young pig)
- Wine (1 Schoppen)
- Parsley
- Lemon

## Directions

1. The bird can be kept dead for 6 days; pluck it, singe it, and clean it carefully with a cloth without washing if possible
2. Remove the entrails carefully so the gall is not crushed
3. Rub it with salt, pepper, cloves, and mace
4. Cut into the breastbone and remove the claws but leave the feet on the body
5. Dress it nicely and fill it with an onion, half a lemon, a bay leaf, and a piece of butter
6. Bind it firmly together and cut off the head and neck so the skin remains on the body
7. Wrap it in the skin of half the side of a young pig and sew it firmly with a strong trussing needle
8. Roast it slowly and yellowly in the oven; if young it takes 2 hours, if old 3 hours
9. Pour a Schoppen of wine over it and baste it frequently
10. Serve it with the skin and garnish with parsley and lemon
11. Treat the sauce as for roasted turkey

title: Gebratener Fasan (Roasted Pheasant)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Pheasant
- Butter paper
- Wine or vinegar
- Onions
- Lemon
- Celery
- Carrots
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- White pepper
- Salt
- Pepper
- Bacon
- Paper
- Green ribbon

## Directions

1. Pluck the pheasant but not the head; wrap the head immediately and firmly in butter paper
2. Remove the entrails and rinse inside with wine or vinegar
3. Soak it for a day in wine with onions, lemon, celery, carrots, bay leaves, cloves, and white pepper, turning it frequently
4. The next day, rub it with pepper and salt
5. Bind it well in bacon and wrap it in a sheet of paper
6. Braise it slowly and yellowly in a casserole with the marinade wine, basting frequently
7. Before serving, remove the paper and place the head on the breast, leaving only the bacon on the breast
8. Remove the fat from the roast and pour lemon juice over it
9. Strain the sauce through a hair sieve
10. When the pheasant is on the platter, tie a green ribbon around its neck where the feathers begin

title: Gebratener Kapaun (Roasted Capon)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Capon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onion
- Butter
- Paper

## Directions

1. Dry pluck the capon and, if time permits, it can remain undrawn until the next day.
2. Before roasting, draw it, wash it clean, rub it well with salt and pepper.
3. Stuff an onion inside, cut off the neck and head but leave the skin on the body.
4. Spread a sheet of paper with butter, wrap the capon in it and roast it in the oven until golden.
5. The feet must remain on, only the claws are cut off.

title: Gebratener welscher Hahn (Roasted Turkey)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turkey
- cherry spirit (Kirschengeist)
- salt
- pepper
- turkey liver and gizzard
- onion
- parsley
- butter
- 1 fresh roll (Mundbrod)
- 1/2 schoppen thick sour cream
- 3 eggs
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Before slaughter, pour a glass of cherry spirit into the bird so it runs well into the body.
2. After bleeding out, let it twitch on the ground.
3. Pluck warm but do not eviscerate.
4. Wrap in cloth and set aside until the next day.
5. Carefully remove the crop with innards, loosen the breastbone, and cut the legs from both sides at the joint, loosening carefully at the breast.
6. Wash clean and half-chop the feet.
7. Salt and pepper well.
8. Using a trussing needle, truss so the breast stands up nicely.
9. Prepare the stuffing for the crop: cut liver and gizzard (removing the tough skin) into small square pieces.
10. Braise these with onion and parsley in a piece of butter in a casserole for half an hour.
11. Meanwhile, cut a fresh roll (removing the crust).
12. Mix the braised mixture with the roll, add 1/2 schoppen thick sour cream, 3 beaten eggs, and nutmeg to taste.
13. Mix well and fill the crop with this stuffing.

title: Gebratenes Haselhuhn (Roasted Hazelhen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 hazelhen
- wine
- pepper
- salt
- 1 onion
- lemon
- basil
- bacon
- goose liver
- onions
- bay leaves
- celery
- yellow turnips

## Directions

1. It is always better if the hazelhen, after being plucked dry, soaks in wine for a time
2. Cut off the head and neck
3. Rub the hen well with pepper and salt
4. Stuff the hen with an onion, some lemon, and basil
5. Dress it nicely, bind it thickly with bacon, and roast it until golden yellow in the oven or on the spit
6. The claws must be cut off
7. Put onions, bay leaves, celery, and yellow turnips into the casserole with the roast
8. Pound together goose liver and a little lemon, add them as well
9. Remove the fat from the sauce, press it through a fine sieve, and pour it with a little jus over the roast

title: Gebratenes Ochsenfleisch (Roasted Beef)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A rib piece or a piece from the breast, or generally a good, juicy piece of beef, preferably the thick flap
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Lemons
- Bay leaves
- Cloves
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Meat broth or water
- Butter paper (if no spit is available)
- Madeira wine (optional)

## Directions

1. Rub the meat with salt and pepper
2. If using a spit, roast it on the spit, or if not available, wrap it in butter paper
3. Place the meat in a roasting pan with onions, lemons, bay leaves, cloves, and pepper
4. Pour in a glass of wine, half a glass of vinegar, and half a ladle-full of meat broth or water
5. Roast until beautifully browned, frequently basting with the sauce
6. Half an hour before serving, drain the broth and skim off the fat, then return it to the roasting pan
7. Optionally, add a glass of Madeira wine
8. Let it come to a boil once more and arrange on a platter
9. Serve the sauce separately

title: Gebrühter Eierhaber (Steamed Egg Pancake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one schoppen of milk
- 4 loth of butter
- flour
- 6 eggs
- lard
- salt
- sugar

## Directions

1. Let a schoppen of milk and 4 loth of butter cook together in a brass pan
2. Stir in flour until it becomes a thick paste
3. Put the batter in a bowl and stir in 6 eggs, one after the other
4. Heat lard in an iron pan, let the batter run in slowly, cover it for a while
5. Turn it over, let it set again, pour a little hot lard over it
6. Break or cut it up, but not too fine, and let it finish baking
7. You can salt it or sweeten it

title: Gebrühter Teig (Poured Dough)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schopen water
- 1 schopen milk
- 3 lots butter
- Flour
- 6 to 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Let 1 schopen water, 1 schopen milk and 3 lots butter cook in a brass pan
2. Stir in flour until as thick as dough
3. Dry it well over the fire
4. Transfer to a bowl and beat in 6 to 8 eggs

title: Geduldzeltchen (Patience Tents)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 8 loth flour
- yolk of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Whip egg whites into stiff peaks
2. Stir in sugar, flour, and lemon yolk until very thick
3. Coat a black baking sheet with wax
4. Using a funnel dipped in water, let the mixture drip onto the sheet
5. Dry in the oven, keeping them white

title: Gedämpfte Felchen (Steamed Whitefish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- whitefish
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- lemon
- parsley
- onion
- flour
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Scale the fish and clean it out
2. Dry it thoroughly with a cloth
3. Cut it into pieces
4. Rub well with salt and pepper
5. Place it in a casserole with a piece of butter
6. Add chopped lemon, parsley, and onion
7. Cover the casserole and place it on low coals
8. Let the fish simmer for half an hour
9. Sprinkle 3 finger-widths of flour over it
10. Add a little meat broth
11. Let it come to a boil again
12. Place the fish on a platter and pour the sauce over it

title: Gedämpfte Kohlrabi (Steamed Kohlrabi)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- salt
- boiling water
- cold water
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- 3 finger-lengths of flour
- white bouillon
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean and peel the kohlrabi, cut finely, salt them, cover, and let stand for one hour.
2. Add boiling water and cook until tender.
3. Strain and pour cold water over them so they remain white.
4. In a casserole, steam finely minced onions and parsley in a piece of butter.
5. Let the kohlrabi cook in this for one hour.
6. Sprinkle 3 finger-lengths of flour over them, stir, and pour 1 spoonful of white bouillon over them.
7. Add pepper and nutmeg.

title: Gedämpfte Nieren (Steamed Kidneys)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- kidneys
- onions
- butter
- flour
- half a glass of Burgundy wine
- meat broth
- zest and juice of half a lemon
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut the kidneys very finely
2. Cut a good amount of onions
3. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan with the onions and kidneys
4. Cover the saucepan and let it steam for half a quarter hour
5. Sprinkle 3 strong finger-fulls of flour over it, turn it a couple of times
6. Pour in half a glass of Burgundy wine and some meat broth
7. Add the finely chopped zest and juice of half a lemon, salt, and nutmeg
8. Let it cook together once more

title: Gedämpfter Fasan (Steamed Pheasant)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant, cleaned and prepared
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- mace
- 1/2 schoppen wine
- 1/2 schoppen meat broth
- 3 shallots, minced
- butter
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 1 lemon
- chopped lemon peel

## Directions

1. Lard the pheasant breast, truss it, and rub with salt, pepper, cloves, and mace.
2. Place in a casserole and pour 1/2 schoppen wine and 1/2 schoppen meat broth around it.
3. Cover the casserole tightly so no steam escapes and braise slowly for 15 minutes.
4. Remove the pheasant and keep it warm in a covered dish.
5. In a brass pan, braise the minced shallots in a piece of butter until golden.
6. Let 1 tablespoon flour cook briefly in the pan, then add the mixture from the casserole in which the pheasant braised.
7. Cook together for 15 minutes.
8. Add the juice of 1 lemon.
9. Place the pheasant on a platter, pass the sauce through a fine sieve over it, and sprinkle with chopped lemon peel.
10. The sauce should be somewhat thick.

title: Gedämpftes weißes Kraut (Steamed White Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white cabbage
- butter
- sugar
- flour
- meat broth or jus

## Directions

1. Cut the cabbage finely and remove the ribs.
2. Put a piece of butter and a piece of sugar, as large as a hen's egg, in a casserole.
3. When the sugar and butter are nicely golden, put in the cabbage.
4. Let it steam until soft, sprinkle flour over it.
5. Pour half a ladle of meat broth or jus over it and finish cooking.

title: Gefrorenes (Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure of water
- ice
- salt

## Directions

1. Crush the ice as finely as possible
2. Place a hand's height of ice in the freezing bucket and mix in a portion of salt
3. Set the covered tin or tin plate with the mixture into the bucket
4. Crush the remaining ice very finely, mix it with salt, and completely cover the tin with it
5. Sprinkle several handfuls of salt on top to accelerate freezing
6. Let the tin stand in the ice for only a quarter hour and shake it several times by the handle, ensuring it remains in the ice
7. Open the lid and insert the stirring spoon with one hand while driving the tin around the spoon with the other hand as quickly as possible, all while keeping it in the ice, as this is very important so that the mass not only freezes faster but also becomes fine and tender
8. Take care that no ice falls into the tin; wrap a clean cloth around the tin instead
9. During the stirring, work the bottom and sides of the tin well so that any frozen deposits continually detach
10. If the frozen mass adheres too strongly, loosen it with the stirring spoon and press it upward in small pieces
11. Then let the tin stand for a quarter hour, stir and work the mass until the frozen substance is stiff but not lumpy, rather such that it can be cut like butter
12. Finally close the tin, cover it, let it stand in the bucket, and set it in the cellar
13. When the mass is firmly frozen, this ice will keep for 3 to 4 hours
14. When serving, turn it out onto a plate

title: Gefrorenes von bittern Mandeln (Bitter Almond Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound blanched bitter almonds
- cream
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 measure of water
- 10 to 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pound the blanched bitter almonds finely in a mortar with some cream
2. Place them in a dish and pour enough cream over them to make 1/2 measure total
3. Cover and let stand for 1/2 hour
4. Cook 1/2 pound sugar in a brass pan with 1/2 measure water
5. Remove the dark scum completely
6. Let it cook until it forms threads
7. Press the almonds through linen cloth so they are completely dry
8. Pour the cream into the sugar
9. Let everything cook together for 2 minutes
10. Beat 10 to 12 egg yolks in a pot and slowly stir in the cream
11. Put it in the freezing tin
12. Once it is slightly cold, proceed according to the method given in No. 767

title: Gefrorenes von Citronen (Lemon Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- 4 lemons
- 1/2 measure of water
- 1 schoppen of wine

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 4 lemons into the sugar
2. Cook the zest together with the lemon juice and 1/2 measure of water thoroughly
3. Add 1 schoppen of wine
4. Strain through a cloth
5. Freeze in the freezing tin according to the method given in No. 767

title: Gefrorenes von Schlagrahm (Frozen Whipped Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Maas cream
- 8 Loth sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Simmer 1 Maas cream with 8 Loth sugar
2. Stir 10 egg yolks smooth in a pot with sour cream
3. Pour the boiling cream slowly to the eggs
4. Let it draw once more in the pan
5. Force it through a hair sieve into another dish
6. Let it cool
7. Place it in the freezing bucket and proceed according to the rule given in No. 767

title: Gefüllte Eier anderer Art (Stuffed Eggs Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- a roll of bread (Wecken)
- milk
- butter
- one whole egg
- soft bread
- sugar
- anchovies
- ham
- sweet cream
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, peel them, and place them in cold water.
2. Cut the crust from a roll and soak it in milk, then press it out.
3. Let a piece of butter melt in a casserole and braise the roll and egg yolks in it for a quarter hour.
4. Beat in one whole egg and fill the egg whites with this mixture.
5. Cut soft bread into slices, pour a little milk on them so they become soft.
6. Butter a baking sheet, sprinkle it with sugar, and lay the soft bread slices on it.
7. Place the eggs on the bread slices.
8. Cut 6 cleaned and deboned anchovies and some ham into small pieces and lay them around the eggs.
9. Beat 3 whole eggs with one pint of sweet cream, salt it, grate in 4 lots of Parmesan cheese, stir everything together, and pour it over the eggs.
10. Place the baking sheet in a baking oven and let them rise nicely.

title: Gefüllte Eier mit Hirn (Stuffed Eggs with Brain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- hard-boiled eggs
- sour cream, 2 spoonfuls
- calf's brain
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- fat broth

## Directions

1. Shell as many hard-boiled eggs as needed, cut in half and remove the yolks
2. Press the yolks through a hair sieve
3. Add 2 spoonfuls sour cream to the yolks
4. Skin a calf's brain, melt a piece of butter in a saucepan and braise the brain in it
5. Mix this with salt and pepper into the egg yolks
6. Fill the egg whites with the mixture
7. Place in a roasting pan, pour a little fat broth over, place in the roasting oven for a quarter hour and bake golden yellow

title: Gefüllte Eier mit Spinat (Stuffed Eggs with Spinach)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- spinach
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- beaten eggs
- breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, shell them and place in cold water
2. Cook spinach as for a vegetable
3. Press the egg yolks into the spinach
4. In a saucepan, melt a piece of butter and braise the spinach and egg yolks in it with salt and nutmeg
5. Fill the egg whites with this, press them together so they look like whole eggs
6. Turn the eggs in beaten eggs and then in grated breadcrumbs and bake

title: Gefüllte Eier mit Spinat (Stuffed Eggs with Spinach)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- spinach
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- beaten eggs
- breadcrumbs
- lard

## Directions

1. Boil the eggs hard, peel them, and place them in cold water.
2. Cook some spinach as you would for a vegetable.
3. Press the egg yolks into the spinach.
4. Let a piece of butter melt in a casserole, braise the spinach and egg yolks in it, add salt and nutmeg.
5. Fill the egg whites with this mixture, press them together so they look like whole eggs.
6. Roll the eggs in beaten eggs and then in grated breadcrumbs.
7. Bake them in hot lard until nicely golden.

title: Gefüllte Flädlein in Vanillecrême (Filled Pancakes in Vanilla Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 handful of fine flour
- cold milk
- 4 eggs
- bacon for coating the pan
- 1 pint milk
- 1 piece vanilla
- 2 loth sugar
- 3 fingers full of flour
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls sweet cream
- sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 1 handful of fine flour, stir it smooth with cold milk, and beat 4 eggs into it
2. Coat a medium Flädleinspfanne (thin pancake pan) with bacon and bake the Flädlein in it
3. Lay them out separately on a noodle board
4. For the Vanillecrême: cook together 1 pint milk, 1 piece vanilla, 2 loth sugar, and 3 fingers full of flour
5. Beat 4 egg yolks and stir them into the boiling milk
6. Let everything draw once more on the fire
7. Pour the cream through a hair sieve into a dish and let it cool
8. Spread 2 spoonfuls of it around on the Flädlein
9. Roll them together
10. Lay them in a dish coated with butter crosswise on top of each other
11. Pour 2 spoonfuls of sweet cream over them
12. Draw them up in the oven and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Gefüllte Flädlein mit saurem Rahm und Rosinen (Filled Pancakes with Sour Cream and Raisins)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ordinary Flädlein (thin pancakes)
- thick sour cream
- washed raisins and Zibeben (dried grapes)
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter
- 2 spoonfuls of milk

## Directions

1. Make ordinary Flädlein (thin pancakes)
2. Coat them with thick sour cream
3. Sprinkle with washed raisins and Zibeben (dried grapes)
4. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over them
5. Roll them together
6. Coat a dish for serving with butter and lay the filled Flädlein in it crosswise
7. Add 2 spoonfuls of milk
8. Let them rise in the oven

title: Gefüllte Gans (Stuffed Goose)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose
- 2 rolls (Wecken)
- water
- goose liver, heart, and gizzard
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/4 pound bacon, finely sliced
- parsley
- 1 lemon, minced

## Directions

1. Pluck, wash, and clean out the goose.
2. Soak 2 rolls in water and press out.
3. Mince the liver, heart, and gizzard finely.
4. Mix with salt, pepper, nutmeg, 2 eggs, 1/4 pound finely sliced bacon, parsley, and 1 minced lemon.
5. Fill the mixture into the goose.
6. Rub with pepper and salt.
7. Braise until golden.

title: Gefüllte Kalbsbrust auf Schweizer Art (Stuffed Veal Breast, Swiss Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- Cleaned veal liver (or goose liver)
- One quarter pound beef kidney fat
- 6 shallot onions
- Zest of one lemon
- Basil
- Thyme
- Parsley
- Tarragon
- Butter
- 2 white bread rolls
- 3 to 4 eggs
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Bacon or pork fat
- Onions
- Bay leaf
- Pepper
- Jus

## Directions

1. Remove the breast bones and soak the breast in lukewarm water for an hour until it turns white.
2. Mince the cleaned veal liver and one quarter pound of beef kidney fat as finely as possible and put them in a bowl.
3. Mince 6 shallot onions, the zest of one lemon, basil, thyme, parsley, and tarragon very finely and steam them in a piece of butter.
4. Grate 2 white bread rolls, soak them, and squeeze them out again; mix with 3 to 4 eggs, salt, nutmeg, and the steamed mixture, stir for a quarter hour, add the minced liver and stir again.
5. Fill the breast with the mixture, sew it firmly shut, cook it in boiling water for a quarter hour, place it in a casserole with bacon or pork fat, onions, bay leaf, pepper and salt in the oven, having first tied some bacon pieces on top of the breast.
6. Pour a little jus over it, place the breast on a platter, and pour the sauce (passed through a fine sieve) over it.

title: Gefüllte Kalbsmilz (Stuffed Veal Spleen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal spleen
- Half a pound of fatty pork
- Salt
- Pepper
- Fat
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Juniper berries

## Directions

1. Do not cut the spleen completely open.
2. Cut half a pound of fatty pork into pieces and mix it with salt and pepper.
3. Stuff the spleen with this mixture, sew it closed again.
4. Let it braise beautifully golden in a covered casserole with a piece of fat, onions, bay leaves, juniper berries and salt.

title: Gefüllte Kartoffeln (Stuffed Potatoes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- medium-sized potatoes
- half a quarter pound of cut bacon
- onions
- parsley
- one soaked and squeezed bread roll, grated
- half pound finely chopped leftover meat
- two to three eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- lemon
- lemon juice
- butter

## Directions

1. Select beautiful, equal-sized potatoes of medium size and peel them
2. Cut off a small lid from the top and hollow them out
3. In a small pot, melt the bacon and braise finely chopped onions and parsley in it
4. Add one grated, soaked, and firmly squeezed bread roll
5. Add half pound of finely chopped leftover meat, two to three eggs, salt, nutmeg, pepper, lemon, and lemon juice
6. Fill the potatoes with this filling
7. Cover with the small lid and cook them soft in a roasting pan with a piece of butter
8. It is even better if one places a lid with coals on the roasting pan

title: Gefüllte Kohlrabi (Stuffed Kohlrabi)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful, half-grown, equal-sized kohlrabi
- cold roast
- 1 soaked and squeezed bread roll
- 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- onions
- parsley
- butter
- meat broth
- butter sauce

## Directions

1. Peel the kohlrabi, cut away the heart leaf with the lid at the top, and cook until tender.
2. Pour through a sieve and pour cold water over them.
3. Hollow out the kohlrabi.
4. For the filling: mince the cold roast with the soaked and squeezed bread roll, 3 eggs, salt, nutmeg, finely minced onions and parsley, mix together.
5. In a casserole, steam this mixture in a piece of butter.
6. Fill the kohlrabi heads with this mixture.
7. Place in a roasting pan with a little meat broth on coals.
8. Place the cut-off lids back on top.
9. Steam a little longer and make a butter sauce to serve with it.

title: Gefüllte Ochsenzunge (Stuffed Ox Tongue)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 ox tongue
- salt water
- quarter pound of bacon
- some raw ham
- 1 kreuzers worth of bread
- water
- half a lemon
- 1 onion
- fat
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- 1 egg
- string
- lard slices
- bay leaf
- lemon juice
- jus

## Directions

1. Wash the tongue and cook it in salt water until not too soft
2. Remove the skin, cut it open at the base of the throat, and remove the meat such that the tongue does not get a hole
3. Mince the removed meat very finely with a quarter pound of bacon and some raw ham
4. Grate a kreuzers worth of bread, soak it in water and press it out firmly, add it to the minced meat
5. Mince half a lemon, steam an onion with fat, and add both to the mixture along with salt, nutmeg, and pepper to taste
6. Work everything together with one egg until well combined
7. Fill the tongue with this mixture and tie it together with string
8. Place it in a saucepan with some lard slices, onions, a bay leaf, and a piece of lemon, cover, and steam for half an hour
9. If serving as a roast, add lemon juice and jus
10. As a ragout it is best with a white lemon sauce: peel the zest from one large or two small lemons and mince it finely together with the pulp of the lemons (without the white skin and seeds) and small onions
11. In a saucepan with a piece of butter, let as much flour as can be held between 3 fingers brown slightly, extinguish it with meat broth, add the lemon mixture and some of the sauce in which the tongue was braised, let everything cook together for half an hour
12. Arrange the sauce on a plate and place the tongue on top

title: Gefüllte Omelette (Filled Omelet)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 strong spoonfuls pounded sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- half a lemon
- 4 egg whites
- 3 fingers full flour
- lard, size of a hen's egg

## Directions

1. Stir 4 strong spoonfuls pounded sugar, 4 egg yolks and half a lemon grated on a grater together until very thick.
2. Mix in the snow from 4 egg whites and 3 fingers full flour.
3. Make a piece of lard the size of a hen's egg hot in a pancake pan and let it cool a little.
4. Pour the mass slowly into it.
5. Cover the pan and let the omelet bake slowly on hot ashes.
6. Turn it and bake it on the other side also and fold it over.
7. When all are baked, place them for a while on a long plate in the baking oven and finish them.

title: Gefüllte Schnecken (Stuffed Snails)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Snails
- Ash
- Salt
- Anchovies (6 loth cleaned)
- 3 Shallot onions
- Lemon
- Parsley
- 1 spoonful of capers
- Butter
- Breadcrumbs

## Directions

1. Boil the snails with a little ash for a quarter hour
2. Pour them into a strainer and pull the snails out of their shells with a fork
3. Clean them well and remove the black skin from the back
4. Boil the shells once more and rub off the slime from the snails with a handful of salt
5. Finely mince 6 loth of cleaned anchovies, 3 shallot onions, some lemon, parsley, and 1 spoonful of capers
6. Knead the minced mixture with butter
7. Fill the snail shells halfway with this mixture
8. Press 2 snails into each shell and cover with more of the mixture on top
9. Sprinkle a little breadcrumbs over it
10. Place them on a snail plate or in a baking vessel, put this in an oven, and leave it in only as long as until the butter has melted on the snails

title: Gefüllte Waffeln (Filled Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- short butter dough
- red currants or raspberries
- egg
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Roll out a short butter dough knife-back thick and lay a piece in the waffle iron.
2. Lay a piece of paper on this dough and bake the dough on one side.
3. In the same way, bake another piece of dough on one side.
4. Coat both pieces on the baked side with egg.
5. Lay a tablespoon of red currants or raspberries on the unbaked side of the first piece.
6. Fit the unbaked side of the other piece on top; bring both pieces together with their filling into the waffle iron once more, bake a little more, and then sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Gefüllte Äpfel (Stuffed Apples)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples of equal size
- butter
- sugar
- 2 beaten eggs
- finely grated bread
- 10 loth almonds in their shells
- 1/4 pound ground sugar
- 1 nutmeg
- a knife-tip of ground cloves
- 4 loth grated black bread
- 10 egg yolks
- snow from 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel apples of equal size and remove the core.
2. Stew the apples in a piece of butter and a piece of sugar until half-soft.
3. When cold, turn them in 2 beaten eggs, then in finely grated bread.
4. Place on a baking sheet and bake until golden in the oven.
5. Pound 10 loth almonds in their shells very fine, 1/4 pound ground sugar, one nutmeg, a knife-tip of ground cloves, and 4 loth grated black bread together.
6. Beat 10 egg yolks into this mixture and stir everything together strongly like a cake batter.
7. Finally fold in the snow from 10 egg whites.
8. Place the apples on a plate, fill the mixture on top, and let it bake golden in the oven.

title: Gefüllter Hammelsbug in Form einer Gans (Stuffed Lamb Shoulder in the Form of a Goose)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb shoulder
- pork
- bread
- pepper
- salt
- onions
- lemons
- 1 egg
- flour
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut out a large shoulder nicely, loosen the blade along with the first and second bones, and cut off the bones except for the outer knob
2. Make a filling of pork, some bread, pepper, salt, onions, and lemons; mix this together with an egg
3. Fill the shoulder with this mixture, roll it together, sew it shut
4. Turn the collar with a wooden peg onto the breast
5. Make a drawn sauce in a casserole with it, adding onions and yellow turnips
6. Let the sediment in which the shoulder cooked reduce completely, lift out the meat, sprinkle as much flour as can be held with 3 fingers, pour 1 ladleful of meat broth over it, add the juice of half a lemon, let it cook together for half a quarter hour
7. Pour it through a fine sieve, place the meat back in, and serve with lemons

title: Gefüllter Hammelsmagen (Stuffed Lamb Stomach)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Lamb stomach
- 2 milk rolls
- Water
- Onions
- Parsley
- Piece of lard
- 2 lamb kidneys
- Salt
- Chives
- 3 eggs
- Butter

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape the stomach thoroughly and wash it
2. Grate 2 milk rolls on a grater, soak them in water, press them out firmly, and put them in a bowl
3. Chop onions and parsley, braise them in a piece of lard, and add to the bread
4. Chop 2 lamb kidneys and add them
5. Mix everything together with salt, chives, and 3 eggs
6. Fill the mixture into the stomach and tie it closed at the top
7. Simmer it in salted water for half an hour
8. Bake it in butter until golden brown

title: Gefüllter Kalbsschlägel (Stuffed Veal Leg)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A nice white and tender veal leg
- Pork
- Lemon
- Onion
- Lard
- Bread
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mace
- One egg
- One egg yolk
- Goose liver
- Smoked tongue
- Half a pound of truffles
- Carrots
- Oil or fat
- Wine
- Tarragon vinegar
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Remove the shinbone and the marrow bone from a nice white and tender veal leg (best from a cow calf).
2. Cut a hole in the leg and chop the cut-out flesh together with a piece of pork.
3. Grate the rind and marrow of a lemon, an onion browned golden in lard and a piece of soaked bread on a grater until foamy.
4. Mix this in a bowl with the chopped meat, season with salt, pepper and mace and mix it with one egg and one egg yolk to make a forcemeat.
5. Spread half of the forcemeat on the opened-out leg, place a nice large white goose liver, cut into knife-back-thick pieces, on the forcemeat.
6. Lay smoked tongue cut into long pieces on the goose liver, then half a pound of washed, skinned truffles cut into leaves, and on these the rest of the forcemeat.
7. Roll together the leg, sew it closed and place it in a casserole with cut onions, lemons, carrots and a little fat.
8. Pour a glass of wine, a little tarragon vinegar and a little meat broth over it.
9. Let it cook slowly covered for 2 hours.
10. It can be served warm or cold; cold is better however.

title: Gefüllter Kohl (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- salt water
- meat
- onions
- parsley
- two bread rolls, grated and squeezed dry
- three to four eggs
- butter sauce or jus

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the cabbage and cook it in salted water
2. Chop the meat, onions, and parsley finely
3. Add two grated, soaked, and firmly squeezed bread rolls
4. Stir in three to four eggs
5. Dip a cloth in cold water and lay the cabbage leaves one after another on it
6. Spread one spoonful of the filling on each leaf
7. Tie the cloth tightly together and cook the cabbage soft in salted water
8. Serve with either a butter sauce or jus

title: Gefüllter Kohl (Stuffed Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- meat
- onions
- parsley
- 2 soaked and squeezed bread rolls (Wecken)
- 3 to 4 eggs
- salt water
- butter sauce or jus

## Directions

1. Pick over, wash, and cook the cabbage in salt water.
2. For the filling: finely mince the meat with onions and parsley, add 2 grated, soaked, and firmly squeezed bread rolls, stir in 3 to 4 eggs.
3. Dip a cloth in cold water and lay the cabbage leaves one after another upon it.
4. Spread one spoonful of the filling on each leaf, bind the cloth firmly together, and cook the cabbage so in salt water until tender.
5. Serve with either butter sauce or jus.

title: Gefüllter Reisauflauf (Filled Rice Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice
- boiling water
- half a Maas milk
- 4 Loth butter
- 4 Loth butter
- 1 Vierling sugar
- 1 lemon
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter
- 2-3 tablespoons preserved currants

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/4 pound of rice in boiling water 2-3 times and let it drain through a hair sieve
2. Cook it with half a Maas of milk and 4 Loth of butter in a casserolle
3. Stir 4 Loth of butter in a bowl and add the rice until it cools
4. Fold in 1 Vierling of sugar grated on a lemon, finely ground, 6 egg yolks and the snow from the 6 egg whites
5. Butter a baking sheet and fill in half the mass
6. Top with 2-3 tablespoons of preserved currants and cover with the remaining half of the mass
7. Bake the soufflé in the oven

title: Gefüllter Reispudding (Filled Rice Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice
- boiling water
- 1/2 quarter pound butter
- 1/4 pound ground sugar
- 1/2 quarter pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- half a lemon, zest grated
- egg whites from 6 eggs
- 1 wafer
- preserved fruits
- 3 loth blanched, finely ground almonds
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- 1 schoppen milk
- 1 lemon peel
- a piece of sugar
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Scald the rice with boiling water, let it stand for a quarter-hour, cook it soft with 1/2 quarter pound butter
2. Stir it with 1/4 pound ground sugar and another 1/2 quarter pound butter until it is cold
3. Beat 6 egg yolks to it, half a lemon with zest grated on a rasp, the snow from the 6 egg whites
4. Stir everything together, fill half of the mass into a lift-pan, lay a wafer on top, on this something preserved, on this the other half of the mass
5. Bake the pudding in the oven
6. For the sauce: let 3 loth blanched, finely ground almonds come to boil with 1 schoppen sweet cream, 1 schoppen milk, 1 lemon peel and a piece of sugar
7. Stir together 3 egg yolks, pour the boiling milk to it, let everything come to a boil once more and pour this sauce over the pudding

title: Gefüllter welscher Hahn auf italienische Art (Stuffed Guinea Fowl in the Italian Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 guinea fowl
- salt
- pepper
- 1 pound macaroni
- half pound grated Parmesan cheese
- half pound finely chopped ham
- quarter pound butter
- 2 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- bacon
- lemons
- 1 ladleful meat broth

## Directions

1. Prepare the aspic as described for the pickled guinea fowl
2. Immediately after killing, strike the breast, pluck while still warm, clean carefully over flame, gut it, remove the crop so there is no hole, remove the breastbone and ribs, and rub the body with salt and pepper
3. Cook 1 pound macaroni in salted water until half-soft, drain well through a sieve
4. Mix together the macaroni with half pound grated Parmesan cheese, half pound finely chopped ham, quarter pound butter, 2 eggs, and 2 spoonfuls sour cream, mixing well in a bowl
5. Fill the guinea fowl and its crop firmly with this mixture, sew it shut, dress it, wrap in bacon and lemons, place in a casserole
6. Pour 1 ladleful of meat broth over it, cover, and braise slowly over coals for 2 hours, taking care not to lay it on its breast
7. Let it cool on a plate; once cool, place it on the serving plate, pour clear aspic over it, let it set, then pour half-set aspic over it again
8. Once this has completely set, garnish the guinea fowl as desired

title: Gefüllter Wildschweinskopf (Stuffed Wild Boar Head)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar head
- lean wild boar meat
- fresh bacon
- white bread
- eggs
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- mace
- nutmeg
- truffles
- calf's feet
- pig's feet
- old hen
- beef or cow meat
- roots and herbs
- bay leaves
- wine
- water
- vinegar
- spinach
- lemon
- butter
- lard

## Directions

1. Cut the head not too short and wash it 2 to 3 times in warm water.
2. Brush it clean until it becomes a nice yellow color.
3. Clean the ears thoroughly and cut open the bottom of the head.
4. Carefully remove the skull so that no holes are made in the skin.
5. Chop 2 pounds of lean wild boar meat and the meat removed from the head with 1 pound of fresh bacon and mix to a paste.
6. Soak white bread (roll or semmel) with the crust removed in water, press it firmly out, and work it together with the pork forcemeat, 2 eggs, salt, pepper, cloves, mace, and nutmeg in a mortar until foamy.
7. Fill half of this mixture into the head, lay some thinly sliced truffles on top, then add the other half of the forcemeat.
8. Place a piece of bacon rind on top of the stuffing before the opening to keep the filling secure.
9. Sew the opening closed and wrap the head in cloth.
10. For the aspic: put 8 cleaned calf's feet, 2 pig's feet, an old hen, 2 pounds of beef or cow meat, all sorts of roots and herbs, cloves, pepper, mace, whole cinnamon, onions, bay leaves, and a generous handful of salt into a well-tinned copper pot.
11. Pour in 3 measures of wine, 4 measures of water, and 1.5 measures of vinegar.
12. Place everything together with the pig's head on the fire, cover the pot, cook the head slowly, and turn it often.
13. A large pig's head often takes 3 to 3.5 hours to cook through.
14. Place the cooked head on a dish to drain; pour the drained liquid back into the aspic and let it stand overnight if time allows.
15. Beat 4 eggs into a casserole, carefully remove the fat from the aspic, pour the settled part into the casserole, put it on the fire, and whisk with a whisk until it begins to boil.
16. Remove it from heat, squeeze the juice of a lemon into it, and strain it through a felt hat; if it does not become clear on the first straining, filter it through the felt until it is clear enough.
17. Pour some of this clear aspic onto the plate on which the head is to be served and let it set.
18. Make a green aspic: crush 2 to 3 handfuls of cleaned spinach in a mortar, press the juice through a cloth, let it coagulate in a small vessel on the fire, and pour it through a fine sieve.
19. Stir this spinach cheese into half-set aspic, pour it onto the plate so that it forms a mound, and let it set.
20. Clean the pig's head of all fat and lay it on the mound of green aspic.
21. Cover it with clear aspic until the head is completely embedded in the aspic.
22. Before serving, garnish it with lemons and cut-out decorative herbs.
23. For grand tables, the pig's head can also be prepared as follows: 5 pounds of butter and 5 pounds of lard are stirred together for half an hour until they become like pomade.
24. Make a kind of altar on a large long plate from most of this mass and decorate it with columns formed from the rest of the mass, which can be colored brown with chocolate, yellow with saffron, or green with spinach cheese.
25. A hunter, pilgrim, or other figure formed from the same mass can be placed between two such columns.
26. The pig's head is then placed on this altar.

title: Gefülltes Kalbsnetz (Stuffed Calf's Caul)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat of any kind
- onions
- parsley
- bread, 2 pieces
- fat
- eggs, 4
- salt
- nutmeg
- calf's caul

## Directions

1. Chop leftover meat with onions and parsley.
2. Soak bread in water, wring out, and add to the meat mixture.
3. Braise everything in a casserole with a piece of fat for fifteen minutes.
4. Add eggs, salt, and nutmeg to the mixture.
5. Wrap the mixture in calf's caul, sew it closed, and roast in the oven until golden brown.

title: Gefülltes Ochsenfleisch (Stuffed Beef)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece from the thin breast or flap of beef
- White bread for 1 Kreuzer
- Meat broth
- Half a pound of ground pork
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Half a Vierling of cleaned anchovies
- Capers
- Olives
- Mushrooms
- Lemon peel
- Water
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Cream or truffle sauce

## Directions

1. Cut a piece of meat from the thin breast or flap open in the middle
2. Grate white bread on a grater and cut it into fine strips
3. Pour hot meat broth over the strips so they become moist and soft
4. Mix this with half a pound of well-worked ground pork and season with pepper and nutmeg
5. Spread half of this mixture on the meat
6. Place half a Vierling of cleaned anchovies on the meat and mixture
7. Mix the other half of the pork mixture with minced capers, olives, mushrooms, and lemon peel
8. Spread this over the meat, roll it up, wrap in a white cloth, and sew it closed
9. Cook in a casserole with two parts water, one part wine, and one part vinegar for two hours
10. The meat should remain beautifully white when done
11. Serve with a cream or truffle sauce

title: Gefülltes Reis (Stuffed Rice)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound rice
- 2 ladle-fuls of fat or 1 piece of butter
- 1 old hen (or young chickens)
- Parmesan cheese
- 1 piece of butter

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 pound rice in boiling water two to three times
2. Wash it clean and put it in a casserolle
3. Add 2 ladle-fuls of fat; if you don't have that, take a piece of butter
4. Place on low coals and let it only come to a boil
5. Take an old hen, cook it until half done, insert it into the rice and let it cook until very tender
6. When serving, carve the hen by the joint
7. Put 1 spoonful of rice on the plate, sprinkle 1 handful of Parmesan cheese over it, place some of the hen on the rice, then rice again and hen again, until it is finished
8. Let a piece of butter become hot in a small pan and brown the rice when serving; you can also add more Parmesan cheese if you wish

title: Gefülltes weißes Kraut (Stuffed White Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 head white cabbage
- salt water
- 1 pound pork forcemeat
- 1 soaked and squeezed bread roll
- onions
- parsley
- 3-4 eggs
- meat broth
- butter sauce or jus

## Directions

1. Cook 1 head of white cabbage half tender in salt water.
2. Prepare a filling of: 1 pound pork forcemeat mixed with 1 soaked and squeezed bread roll, finely minced onions and parsley, and 3-4 eggs.
3. Remove leaves from the head down to the heart leaf.
4. Spread one spoonful of filling on each leaf.
5. Reassemble the head and bind firmly with string.
6. Place in a roasting pan, pour a little meat broth over it, and bake in the oven.
7. Serve with either butter sauce or jus.

title: Gekochter Meerrettig (Cooked Horseradish Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 loths of butter
- 1 tablespoon of flour
- horseradish
- 3 tablespoons of sweet cream
- beef broth

## Directions

1. Melt the butter in a small pan
2. Add the flour
3. Add the horseradish and steam everything together for 3 minutes
4. Stir smooth with the sweet cream
5. Adjust the consistency with beef broth

title: Gelbe Bodenkohlrabi (Yellow Kohlrabi)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- kohlrabi
- butter
- flour
- meat broth
- sugar

## Directions

1. Peel and wash them, cut into leaves or square pieces.
2. In a piece of butter, steam them soft.
3. Sprinkle flour over them, pour meat broth over them, and cook a while longer.
4. If desired, add a little sugar.

title: Gelbe Rüben (Yellow Turnips)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow turnips
- Butter
- Finely minced onions
- Small peeled potatoes
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Scrape the yellow turnips, cut them long and fine, and rub them in a bowl of lukewarm water by hand until all impurities are removed.
2. Braise them with a piece of butter in a casserole together with finely minced onions until soft.
3. Add small peeled potatoes and cook everything together until soft.
4. Pour some meat broth over and arrange the yellow turnips in the middle of the plate with the potatoes arranged around it.

title: Gelbe Rüben (Yellow Turnips)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- yellow turnips
- butter
- onions
- small peeled potatoes
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Scrape the yellow turnips, cut them long and finely.
2. Rub them in a bowl of lukewarm water by hand until all impurities are removed.
3. In a casserole with a piece of butter and finely minced onions, steam them until tender.
4. Add small peeled potatoes and cook everything together until soft.
5. Pour some meat broth over them and serve.

title: Gelbe Rüben anderer Art (Yellow Turnips, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Yellow turnips
- Finely minced onions
- Parsley
- Butter
- Half a schoppen of good sweet cream
- Flour
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare the turnips the same way as the previous recipe.
2. Braise them in a casserole with finely minced onions and parsley in butter until soft.
3. Pour half a schoppen of good sweet cream over them, dust with a little flour and sugar, and cook for another quarter hour.

title: Gelbes Eis (Yellow Icing)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- a small knife point of saffron
- 1 spoonful boiling water
- one quarter pound finely grated sugar
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Let a small knife point of saffron and one spoonful boiling water stand for a moment
2. Add one quarter pound finely grated sugar to it
3. Beat one egg white into it
4. Stir together for a quarter hour
5. Add the infused saffron drop by drop until it is a beautiful yellow and use as desired

title: Geläuterte Chokoladetorte (Refined Chocolate Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound almonds
- egg white
- 4 eggs and yolks
- 1/2 glass water
- 1/2 pound fine sugar
- 6 loth finely grated chocolate
- 1/2 loth cinnamon
- butter and sugar for the pan

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/2 pound almonds and grate them very finely with egg white
2. Stir them in a bowl with 4 eggs and yolks for half an hour
3. Pour 1/2 glass water and 1/2 pound fine sugar into a brass pan
4. Cook until the sugar spins thick threads
5. Mix 6 loth finely grated chocolate with the cooked sugar and cinnamon
6. Add this to the stirred almond mass
7. Stir everything well together
8. Brush a cake mold with butter and sprinkle with sugar
9. Fill the mass into it
10. Bake the torte in the oven

title: Gelée für Kranke (Jelly for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Loth of isinglass
- half a Maas of water
- 1 Schoppen of good sea wine
- 2 to 3 handfuls of watercress
- 2 egg whites
- 2 to 3 drops of Prussian blue

## Directions

1. Let the isinglass melt in a pan with half a Maas of water over gentle heat.
2. When nothing of the isinglass remains visible, pour in 1 Schoppen of good sea wine.
3. Crush 2 to 3 handfuls of watercress in a mortar, press the juice through a cloth, put it in the pan, let everything cook together, beat 2 egg whites into it.
4. Draw it through a felt hat into a porcelain bowl, add 2 to 3 drops of Prussian blue, let it stand and give the patient one spoonful at a time; it is very good for the chest.

title: Gemüsegraupenuppe (Vegetable Barley Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g peeled potatoes
- 125 g mixed vegetables
- 20 g medium barley
- 20 g lean bacon
- 8 g salt
- 1 l water
- 1 pinch salt
- optionally 1 teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into cubes. Rinse the barley twice with cold water and drain.
2. Sauté the barley with bacon cubes and salt in an earthenware pot, but the bacon must not brown. Add the water and cleaned vegetables cut into pieces, as well as raw potatoes cut into cubes and well washed, cover and cook slowly for 2 hours. Season the soup with salt and serve. Optionally add 1 teaspoon chopped parsley.

title: Geriebene Gerstensuppe (Grated Barley Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 to 2 eggs
- flour
- meat broth
- chives

## Directions

1. Make a firm dough from 1 to 2 eggs and flour
2. Grate this on a grater
3. As soon as the grated dough has dried a little, sprinkle it into simmering meat broth while constantly stirring
4. When serving, sprinkle chives on top

title: Geringe Art von Lebkuchen (Simple Gingerbread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas honey
- 3 spoonfuls cherry spirit
- ground fennel
- anise
- ginger
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 pinch potash
- flour
- egg for brushing
- blanched almonds

## Directions

1. Let 1/2 maas honey cook in a brass pan for a quarter hour
2. Transfer to a bowl and add 3 spoonfuls cherry spirit
3. Add ground fennel, anise, ginger, 1 pinch pepper, and 1 pinch potash
4. Add enough flour to make a workable dough
5. Transfer to a pastry board and press into the molds
6. Place the gingerbread on a black baking sheet dusted with flour
7. Brush with egg and place a blanched almond on each of the four corners
8. Bake in the oven

title: Gerstenwasser für Brustkranke (Barley Water for Those with Chest Illness)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Loth washed and picked malt barley
- 4 Loth washed raisins
- peel of 1/2 lemon
- 3 Maas water
- juice of 1 lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 8 Loth washed and picked malt barley, 4 Loth washed raisins and the peel of 1/2 lemon with 3 Maas water for one hour
2. Fill the pot again with water
3. Simmer a little longer
4. Add the juice of 1 lemon and some sugar
5. When it has cooled, give it to the patient to drink

title: Geräucherte Speckschwarten (Smoked Pork Rind)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Speckschwarten (smoked pork rind)
- water

## Directions

1. Brush the rind clean with water or rub together in water.
2. Soak in cold water for one night.
3. Cook the rinds, using 250 g per 1 liter of water, covered, until soft.
4. Use the broth for legume soups; cut the soft rind into small pieces and add to the soup.

title: Geräucherter Schinken am Spieß gebraten mit Champagnerwein (Smoked Ham Roasted on a Spit with Champagne)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- smoked ham
- shallot onions
- bay leaves
- basil
- thyme
- cloves
- garlic
- mace
- champagne wine, one bottle
- butter
- coulis
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Place the ham on a grill and clean it thoroughly, removing any blackened parts.
2. Place it in a long dish and add sliced shallot onions, bay leaves, basil, thyme, several cloves, garlic, and mace.
3. Pour a bottle of champagne over it, cover the dish tightly, and let it stand until the next day.
4. 3 hours before serving, place it on a spit.
5. Baste it frequently with the sauce it marinated in, to which a piece of butter has been added, until it has absorbed all the liquid.
6. Before serving, bring a couple spoonfuls of coulis and the juice of a lemon to a boil and pour this over the haunch.

title: Geröstete Kalbsleber (Roasted Calf's Liver)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- onions
- butter
- flour
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Cut the calf's liver into small pieces and cut 2 to 3 onions into it
2. Heat butter in an iron pan with onions for a while
3. Add the liver
4. Turn it often
5. Add 3 finger-widths of flour
6. Turn a couple more times
7. Season with salt and pepper
8. Arrange on a plate
9. It is ready in a quarter hour

title: Geröstete oder gebackene Mandeln (Roasted or Baked Almonds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein halbes Pfund Zucker
- ein halbes Pfund Mandeln
- ein Tuch

## Directions

1. Ein halbes Pfund Zucker läutern.
2. Ein halbes Pfund Mandeln mit einem Tuch abreiben.
3. In dem Zucker kochen, bis sie krachen.
4. Vom Feuer nehmen.
5. So lange umrühren, bis sie kalt sind.
6. Wiederum kochen, bis sie glänzen.
7. Auf einem Brett verzupfen.
8. Aufbewahren.

title: Gerührte Brieschenpastete (Stirred Sweetbread Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 sweetbreads
- handful of chopped onions
- parsley
- lemon peel
- piece of butter
- 2 Mundbrod (bread rolls)
- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut 2 to 3 sweetbreads into square pieces
2. Braise them with a handful of chopped onions, parsley, lemon peel and a piece of butter for a quarter hour
3. Grate 2 Mundbrod, soak them, press them out firmly
4. Stir 1/4 pound butter lightly, add the bread, 4 eggs, salt and nutmeg
5. Stir everything together until quite foamy
6. Brush pastry molds with butter, fill the mass into them
7. Place a spoonful of the braised sweetbreads on each mold and bake them until golden

title: Gerührte Eier auf Pariser Art (Scrambled Eggs Parisian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- good jus
- salt
- pepper
- ham
- parsley

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs into a bowl (for 6 persons use 8 eggs), salt them as desired.
2. Let half a pound of butter melt in a casserole, stir the eggs into it slowly.
3. While cooking, stir in 2 spoonfuls of good jus.
4. When the eggs are done, place them on the plate for serving, make a wreath of them, and beat the yolks of 6 eggs onto the center of the plate.
5. Sprinkle a little salt and pepper over it and place it on boiling water so the eggs set.
6. Meanwhile, cut a piece of ham into small pieces, lay these on the egg wreath and place some beautiful parsley between them.

title: Gerührte Eier mit Sardellen (Stirred Eggs with Anchovies)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 eggs
- 4 loth anchovies
- fresh water

## Directions

1. To 8 eggs take 4 loth anchovies, wash them, remove the bones, place them for a quarter hour in fresh water

title: Gerührte Krebspastete (Stirred Crayfish Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 24 crayfish
- salt water
- crayfish butter
- 2 soaked, squeezed and trimmed breadcrumbs
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Boil the crayfish in salted water
2. Remove the tails and claws, make crayfish butter
3. Lightly stir one part of the crayfish butter in a bowl
4. Stir in 2 soaked, squeezed and trimmed breadcrumbs and 5 eggs
5. Coat small pastry molds with the remaining crayfish butter
6. Fill the mixture into the molds
7. Neatly place the crayfish tails and claws on top of the pastries
8. Bake until golden

title: Gerührte Krebspastete anderer Art (Stirred Crayfish Pastry, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 to 40 crayfish
- salt water
- butter
- water
- 6 eggs
- sour cream
- fine flour
- salt
- egg whites
- 3 calf sweetbreads
- soft-boiled ox palate
- lemon zest
- 1 onion
- crayfish tails and claws
- flour
- cold water
- meat broth
- salt
- nutmeg
- juice of half a lemon
- 2 egg yolks
- soft-boiled morels

## Directions

1. Boil 30 to 40 crayfish in salted water until like a hard egg
2. Break out the claws and tails, remove the bile, scrape the body clean
3. Remove the shells from the tails and claws, pound them fine, braise them with a piece of butter until foaming
4. Cook with a little water, remove from fire, skim the crayfish butter into cold water
5. Lightly stir half of it in a bowl, beat in 6 eggs with 1 spoonful of sour cream per egg
6. Add 6 spoonfuls of fine flour, 2 knife-tips of salt and the snow of 4 egg whites
7. Coat a baking sheet with butter, fill the mixture and bake in the oven until golden
8. Once slightly cooled, cut off a lid, remove some of the soft interior
9. Clean 3 calf sweetbreads, cut into pieces, peel and finely chop a soft-boiled ox palate
10. Braise the sweetbreads and palate with chopped lemon zest and 1 onion in the second half of the crayfish butter
11. Add the crayfish tails and claws, 3 spoonfuls of flour, a little cold water, some meat broth, salt, nutmeg and the juice of half a lemon, also 2 egg yolks and soft-boiled morels
12. Let everything cook together, fill this ragout into the pastry, cover with the lid and serve

title: Gerührte Rahmpastetchen (Stirred Cream Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 loths of butter
- 1 schoppen of sour cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 nutmeg
- 1 knife point of salt
- 6 egg whites
- 6 loths of flour

## Directions

1. Stir together the butter and sour cream lightly
2. Stir in the egg yolks
3. Add the nutmeg and salt
4. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
5. Mix the flour into the mass
6. Brush small pastry molds with butter
7. Fill with the mixture and bake until golden
8. Serve with venison or braised veal

title: Gerührte Waffeln (Stirred Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/4 pound blanched and finely pounded almonds
- 3 loth finely pounded sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- fine flour
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 schopp thick sour cream
- 8 egg whites
- bacon

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter in a bowl until foamy.
2. Mix in 1/4 pound blanched and finely pounded almonds and 3 loth finely pounded sugar.
3. Stir everything together, add 8 egg yolks, one after another, and with each yolk a tablespoon of fine flour, the grated zest of 1/2 lemon, and 1/2 schopp of thick sour cream.
4. Beat the whites of 8 eggs to a stiff white snow and very slowly mix under the batter.
5. Bake well in a hot waffle iron coated inside with bacon and sprinkle the waffles with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Gerührter Chokoladeauflauf (Stirred Chocolate Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large spoonfuls sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 knife point of cinnamon
- 1/4 pound grated vanilla chocolate
- 1 loth fine flour
- 6 egg whites
- butter for the dish
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Stir 6 large spoonfuls sugar with 6 egg yolks until the mass is quite thick.
2. Add a knife point of cinnamon, 1/4 pound grated vanilla chocolate, and 1 loth fine flour.
3. Mix everything together, brush a bowl with butter, and fill the mass into it.
4. Bake in the oven until nicely golden and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Gerührter Citronenauflauf (Stirred Lemon Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 spoonfuls sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- salt
- 2 lemons
- 6 egg whites
- 2 loth flour
- butter for the dish
- sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Stir 6 spoonfuls sugar, 6 egg yolks, salt, 2 lemons grated on a grater, and the juice of these lemons in a bowl until the mass is thick.
2. Beat a stiff snow from the 6 egg whites and mix 2 loth flour into it.
3. Brush a bowl with butter and fill the mass into it.
4. Bake the soufflé in a not very hot oven until nicely golden and sprinkle sugar on top.

title: Geschlungene Filets von Kalbfleisch (Braided Veal Fillets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal fillets
- Farce (forcemeat)
- Egg white
- Bacon strips
- Paper
- Lemon or lime sauce

## Directions

1. Cut the fillets two knife-back thicknesses thick and marinate them as previously described.
2. Line the bottom of the serving dish with a fine forcemeat and coat with egg white.
3. Lay the fillets on top in a chain-like braided pattern.
4. Remove any forcemeat not covered by fillets.
5. Cover the fillets with bacon strips and paper.
6. Bake in a not-too-hot oven.
7. Before serving, remove the bacon and fat as best as possible.
8. Pour a lemon or lime sauce over top.

title: Geschmälzter Hecht (Braised Pike)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- pike
- salt
- pepper
- wine
- vinegar
- water
- herbs
- cloves
- parsley
- butter
- lemon
- small potatoes

## Directions

1. Cut off the pike's ears and make a small opening
2. Remove the innards and wash thoroughly
3. Rub strongly with salt and pepper
4. Prepare a broth from 1 part wine, 1 part vinegar, and 1 part water
5. Add all kinds of herbs, whole cloves, and pepper
6. If the pike is large, simmer it in the broth for 1 hour (the broth should not completely cover the fish or it will not be flavorful)
7. Place the pike on a long platter
8. Braise a handful of parsley in a piece of butter
9. Pour this over the pike
10. Squeeze the juice of half a lemon over it
11. Garnish the platter with small roasted potatoes or serve potatoes in their skins with butter

title: Geschmälzter Karpfen (Braised Carp)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- good broth
- salt
- pepper
- 1 half-boiled egg, yolk and white separated
- hot butter
- 3 tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Make a good broth for the carp
2. Scale the carp, cut out the ears (gills), remove the entrails
3. Rub with salt and pepper
4. Place in the broth
5. Cook thoroughly for half an hour
6. Place on a serving plate
7. Pound 1 whole half-boiled egg, keeping yolk and white separate
8. Lay this in strips over the fish
9. Pour hot butter and 3 tablespoons vinegar over it

title: Geschnittene Gurken (Sliced Cucumbers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Gurken (Cucumbers without seeds)
- Salz (Salt)
- Schalottenzwiebeln (Shallot onions)
- Pfeffer (Peppercorns)
- Gelber Senf (Yellow mustard)
- Weinessig (Wine vinegar)
- Schmalz (Lard)

## Directions

1. Select cucumbers that have no seeds yet and peel them clean
2. Slice them as for salad and salt them well in a bowl
3. After one hour, press them out firmly
4. Slice shallot onions finely
5. Combine with coarsely crushed peppercorns and one tablespoon of yellow mustard
6. Mix this with the cucumbers
7. Press them firmly into a stone pot
8. Pour enough good wine vinegar over them so that it covers them
9. Seal with melted lard and store

title: Geschnittene weiße Mandeltorte (Cut White Almond Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely ground sugar
- 10 egg whites
- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- 1/2 pound unblanced almonds
- zest of 2 lemons
- 1/4 pound citron
- 1/4 pound orange peel
- butter
- wafers

## Directions

1. Beat together 1 pound finely ground sugar and 10 egg whites into a thick icing
2. Cut 1/2 pound blanched almonds lengthwise as finely as possible
3. Grate 1/2 pound unblanced almonds together with the zest of 2 lemons on a grater or nutmeg grater
4. Cut 1/4 pound citron and orange peel finely
5. Slowly fold all ingredients into the icing
6. Butter a springform or ring and line it with wafers
7. Fill the mixture into the form and bake the torte at low heat until beautifully done

title: Geschälte Zwetschgen in Zucker (Peeled Plums in Sugar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful large plums
- boiling water
- 1.5 pounds sugar per 2 pounds of plums
- cherry schnapps

## Directions

1. Place beautiful large plums in a bowl and pour boiling water over them
2. Peel off the blue skin
3. For 2 pounds of plums, take 1.5 pounds of sugar
4. Clarify the sugar with water and cook it until it spins threads
5. Add the plums and cook them as long as a soft-boiled egg
6. Place them in a container, cover it and let it stand overnight
7. The next day cook them again for the same length of time, and on the third day do so once more
8. Place them in a container, lay paper on top treated with cherry schnapps
9. Bind it and poke holes in the paper
10. Store them

title: Gespickte Kalbsleber (Larded Calf's Liver)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver
- bacon, sliced
- onions
- lemons
- bay leaf
- stock (Jus)
- gingerbread
- wine, half a glass
- salt
- cloves
- mace

## Directions

1. Skin the liver and lard it beautifully.
2. Place it in a casserole with sliced bacon, onions, lemons, and bay leaf, and lay more bacon on top.
3. Pour a little stock over it, cover the casserole with a lid topped with coals, and braise for one hour.
4. Remove the fat, add a piece of gingerbread to the liver, pour in half a glass of wine, add salt, cloves, and mace.
5. Bring to a boil and strain the sauce through a fine sieve before serving.

title: Gestürzte Caffeecrême (Turned Out Coffee Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee (quarter pound)
- milk (3 schoppen)
- sugar (8 loth)
- veal stock (1 schoppen, thickly cooked)
- cream (1 schoppen)
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Roast a quarter pound coffee light yellow
2. Crush it in a mortar
3. Let it cook in a brass pan with 3 schoppen milk
4. Let 8 loth sugar and one schoppen thickly cooked veal stock make a decoction with the coffee
5. Strain everything through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl
6. Let it cool
7. Whip one schoppen cream stiff in a bowl
8. When the cream is cold, slowly mix it into the whipped cream
9. Pour this mass into an aspic mold brushed with almond oil
10. Let it stand on ice
11. Garnish to taste

title: Gestürzte Chokoladecrême (Turned Out Chocolate Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet (4, cleaned, bones removed)
- water (2 maas)
- chocolate (12 loth)
- water (half schoppen)
- sugar (12 loth)
- vanilla (1 piece)
- sweet cream (1 schoppen)
- eggs (8)
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Set 4 cleaned calf's feet with bones removed with 2 maas water on the fire
2. Reduce to half a maas
3. Pour the aspic through a woolen cloth onto a porcelain bowl
4. Let it sit for a quarter hour
5. Dissolve 12 loth chocolate in a brass pan with half a schoppen water (if still too thick, another half glass of water can be added)
6. Add 12 loth sugar and one piece of vanilla
7. Carefully add the aspic
8. Stir smooth with one schoppen sweet cream
9. Stir the cooked chocolate into 8 beaten eggs
10. Let it draw once more in the pan, stirring constantly
11. Strain the cream through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl
12. Let it cool
13. Brush an aspic mold with almond oil, turn it over and let it run out well
14. Pour the cold cream into it
15. Before serving at table, arrange it beautifully on a plate

title: Gestürzte Crême von Thee (Turned-Out Tea Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a maas milk
- 1 piece whole cinnamon or vanilla
- 1 loth tea
- 1-1/2 schoppen calf's jelly
- 8 loth sugar
- cinnamon oil

## Directions

1. In half a maas milk with one piece whole cinnamon or vanilla, boil 1 loth tea in a brass pan for one minute
2. Then put 1-1/2 schoppen calf's jelly on the fire until it is melted
3. Add 8 loth sugar to it
4. Place the calf's jelly in a mold brushed with cinnamon oil on ice until firm
