5. Pour the above-described cream over it

title: Gestürzte Erdbeerencrême (Turned-out Strawberry Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 schoppen strawberries
- 2 loth isinglass
- 1/2 maas cream
- sugar
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Press 3 schoppen strawberries through a hair sieve.
2. Dissolve 2 loth isinglass in water.
3. Beat half a maas of cream as stiff as egg snow.
4. Let it drain on a hair sieve.
5. Pour the isinglass into the strawberry pulp.
6. Add as many spoonfuls of sugar as you have pulp.
7. Stir the cream on ice until it begins to become gelatinous.
8. Brush a jelly mold with almond oil.
9. Slowly fold the whipped cream and snow from 2 egg whites into the mixture.
10. Pour the cream into the mold.
11. Place it in the cellar or on ice.
12. Carefully turn the cream out onto a plate and garnish beautifully with strawberries.

title: Gestürzte Marasquinocrême (Turned-out Maraschino Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- a strong 1/2 maas milk
- 6 loth sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- 2 knife points fine flour
- 1 glass maraschino
- 1 strong schoppen pure calf meat
- calf meat
- anise oil
- chocolate
- cochineal dye
- green clove leaves

## Directions

1. Let a strong 1/2 maas milk and 6 loth sugar boil together, then let cool slightly.
2. Beat 8 egg yolks in a bowl, add 2 knife points of fine flour and stir smooth.
3. Stir the cooked milk into the eggs along with 1 glass of maraschino and 1 strong schoppen of pure calf meat.
4. Let it boil together.
5. Press it through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl.
6. Brush a jelly mold with anise oil and make three differently colored jellies: one from chocolate, one from cochineal dye, the third from green clove leaves, using calf meat for all.
7. Pour out the decorations nicely and let set.
8. Pour the cooked cream into it and place on ice.
9. When serving, turn it out onto a plate and garnish to taste.

title: Gestürzte Pomeranzencrême (Turned-out Orange Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 calf's feet
- 2 Maas water
- 3 oranges
- half pound sugar
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 schoppen cream
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 schoppen cream (for whipping)
- citron oil

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from 4 calf's feet and place the meat with 2 Maas water on the fire
2. Let it reduce to 1-1/2 Maas and strain through a woolen cloth
3. Let it stand for a time
4. Rub 3 oranges with half a pound of sugar
5. Press the juice of the oranges into a brass pan
6. Add the rubbed sugar, juice of 1 lemon, the calf stock, and 1 schoppen cream
7. Let everything cook together on the fire
8. Beat 8 egg yolks smooth with a little water and slowly stir the cooked mixture into the eggs
9. Let everything come to a boil once more in the pan
10. Strain through a woolen cloth into a porcelain dish
11. Whip 1 schoppen cream until very stiff, let it drain on a hair sieve, and slowly mix it under the cream
12. Coat a mold with lemon oil
13. Pour the cream in, place the mold on ice, and when the cream is completely set, turn it out onto a plate

title: Gesulzte Feldhühner (Aspic-Glazed Partridges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's feet
- water

## Directions

1. Chop together 4 calf's feet and mix with 1 measure of water and half a measure of vinegar...

title: Gesulzte Feldhühner (Jellied Partridges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 calf's feet
- 1 Maas water
- 0.5 Maas wine
- 1 Schoppen vinegar
- parsley
- celery
- onions
- bay leaves
- cloves
- mace
- salt
- 2 eggs with shells
- juice of 2 lemons
- 1 partridge breast and meat
- 0.25 pound lard with filling
- bread for 1 Kreuzer
- 4 truffles, skinned
- mace
- cloves
- pepper
- 3 onions
- 2 eggs
- 1 second partridge
- butter
- lemon
- parsley

## Directions

1. Chop 4 calf's feet together and set to fire with 1 Maas water, half Maas wine, and 1 Schoppen vinegar
2. Add parsley, celery, onions, bay leaves, cloves, mace, and a handful of salt
3. Cook for 2 to 3 hours, then strain through a fine sieve and let stand overnight
4. The next day, remove the fat, place the jelly in a brass pan, crush 2 eggs with their shells into it
5. Beat over the fire until it begins to boil, then remove from heat and squeeze in the juice of 2 lemons
6. Strain the jelly through a sugar loaf until it is quite clear
7. From a partridge, cut off the breast and all meat, mince with a quarter pound of filled lard on the chopping block
8. Soak bread (for 1 Kreuzer, de-crusted) in water and press out firmly
9. Add 4 skinned and finely ground truffles, mace, cloves, pepper, and 3 finely minced onions braised in butter
10. Beat in 2 eggs and add jelly as desired
11. Work this all through a wide sieve by hand so all skins remain behind
12. Split a second partridge down the back, loosen the body so all meat remains on the skin
13. Remove head and neck, keep the leg bone attached, cut off only the claws
14. Fill the hollow of the partridge properly with the farce and sew it closed
15. Bind the bird with lard and lemon and braise in a casserole with butter and onions over coals for 1 hour
16. Once the partridge is removed and cooled, let some light jelly set in a melon mold, line it with lemon and parsley
17. Place the partridge on it, pour remaining jelly around it and let it set firm
18. When serving, turn the whole onto a plate and garnish with parsley and lemon

title: Gesulzte Himbeeren (Jellied Raspberries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 maas pure raspberries
- 2 loth isinglass
- water
- half a maas raspberry juice
- 1 schoppen white Burgundy wine
- half a pound sugar
- 2 egg whites

## Directions

1. From 1 maas pure raspberries select 30 to 40 very large pieces and set aside
2. Put the others in a pot, tie it with cloth, place in a pan in boiling water and let cook for one hour
3. Take out and let the juice run through cloth, which should yield half a maas juice
4. Dissolve 2 loth isinglass in water, press through a fine sieve, put in a brass pan
5. Pour the raspberry juice to it along with 1 schoppen white Burgundy wine, half a pound sugar, and 2 egg whites
6. Beat with a whisk until it begins to boil, remove from fire and let stand a little
7. Run the jelly through a felt hat; if not clear enough the first time, it must be poured over 2 to 3 times
8. Take the designated mold and let a little run in, let it set
9. Lay some of the selected raspberries around in the mold as desired, pour some jelly to it and let it set again
10. Continue until the mold is full, place the jelly on ice and let it set there

title: Gesulzte Kirschen (Aspic of Cherries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of sour cherries
- juice of the cherries
- one bottle of Roussillon wine
- 3 Schoppen of veal aspic
- 12 Loth of sugar
- 3 egg whites
- 8 cloves
- whole cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stone the cherries, press them through a hair sieve, crush the stones and cook them with the cherry juice, a bottle of Roussillon wine, and 3 Schoppen of veal aspic together with 12 Loth of sugar, 3 egg whites, 8 cloves and some whole cinnamon.
2. Beat with a snow fork in the pan until the contents begin to boil (but it must only boil as long as a soft-boiled egg).
3. Remove the pan from the fire, pour the cherry aspic through a filtering hat, and if it is not a beautiful red, help it along with cochineal color.
4. Remove the stems from another half pound of sour cherries, let them sweat a little in a dish on the embers, then place them in a hair sieve to drain.
5. Pour some of the aspic into a desired mold, let it stand a little, place 6 to 8 cherries in the form, then pour aspic over them, fill the mold halfway with it, let it stand again, place cherries in again, and so a few more times until the mold is full.
6. Let the jelly set on ice and turn it out onto a plate.

title: Gesulzte Lachsforelle mit Schuppen (Jellied Salmon Trout with Scales)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon trout
- salt
- 3 calf's feet
- 2 pork feet
- 6 Schoppen water
- 3 Schoppen wine
- 1 Schoppen vinegar
- onions
- bay leaves
- mace
- good herbs
- 1 Schoppen wine for cooking
- 2 Schoppen water for cooking
- boiling milk
- lemon juice
- 6 hard-boiled eggs
- 6 fresh egg yolks
- Provence oil
- jelly

## Directions

1. Scale a beautiful salmon trout, cut open the belly and remove the entrails
2. Wash thoroughly, rub with salt inside and outside, wrap in cloth and let stand for 2 hours
3. Cook 3 calf's feet, 2 pork feet, 6 Schoppen water, 3 Schoppen wine, and 1 Schoppen vinegar together with onions, bay leaves, mace, and salt for 3 hours
4. Strain through a fine sieve into a bowl and let stand for half an hour
5. Wipe the slime from the trout and braise in a covered kettle with 1 Schoppen wine, 2 Schoppen water, all kinds of good herbs, and a small handful of salt, braising rather than boiling so it does not burst
6. Allow 1 hour for it to become tender, then let it cool
7. Place the jelly in a casserole, remove the fat from the top and leave the sediment behind
8. Cook the jelly on the fire with 1 Schoppen boiling milk, add the juice of 1 lemon and cook a while longer
9. Remove from heat and let stand for a quarter hour, then strain through a suspended felt hat
10. When properly made, it should be as clear as bright spring water
11. Pour it nail-thick onto the plate to be served, let it set firm
12. For the sauce, press the yolks from 6 hard-boiled eggs through a fine sieve into a bowl, beat in 6 fresh egg yolks
13. Add the juice of half a lemon and stir constantly for half an hour (it is best to stir over ice) with 3 drops Provence oil until quite white
14. Oil must be added drop by drop, along with some jelly, stirring for 1.5 to 2 hours
15. Pour onto the plate with the light jelly so that 2 fingers width of light jelly remains visible all around
16. Let it set firm, carefully pour light jelly over again, make some green jelly and pour it so the fish lies just on the green jelly
17. Then add some clear jelly and let it set

title: Gesulzte Pomeranzen (Aspic-Glazed Bitter Oranges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- large bitter oranges
- pulp from the oranges
- 2 schoppen of white wine
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 egg
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Make a shallow crosscut at the top of each orange so that the pulp can be extracted with a finger; remove all the pulp.
2. Press the extracted pulp through a fine sieve into a brass pan. Add 2 schoppen of white wine and bring to a boil with 1/4 pound of sugar.
3. Add 1 egg and whisk with a whisk fork until it begins to boil.
4. Remove the aspic from the heat, add the juice of 2 lemons, and strain through a felt hat.

title: Gesulzte Pomeranzen (Jellied Oranges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- large oranges
- 2 schoppen white wine
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 egg
- juice of 2 lemons
- 2 loth almonds
- 3 to 4 drops cochineal

## Directions

1. Make a shallow cross-cut at the top of each orange so the flesh can be removed with a finger, leaving nothing inside
2. Press the removed flesh through a hair sieve into a brass pan
3. Pour 2 schoppen white wine into the pan and bring to a boil with 1/4 pound sugar
4. Add 1 egg and beat it with a whisk until it begins to boil
5. Remove the jelly from the heat and add the juice of 2 lemons
6. Strain through a felt hat; if not clear enough the first time, boil again
7. Stand the oranges upright in a dish so they cannot fall over
8. Pour some of the clear jelly over them and let it set
9. Make a thick milk from 2 loth almonds and mix it with some of the clear jelly
10. When the oranges have set, pour the almond milk over them and let it set again
11. Color some clear jelly with 3 to 4 drops of cochineal
12. When the almond milk has set, pour the red jelly over it and let it set

title: Gesulzter Karpfen (Aspic-Glazed Carp)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp weighing 2 to 3 pounds
- salt and pepper
- 1 measure of water
- 1/2 pound grated hartshorn
- 1 onion
- 3 bay leaves
- whole cloves
- whole peppercorns
- 1/2 lemon, cut into leaves
- 1/2 schoppen of wine, water, or meat broth (if needed)
- 3 to 4 drops of violet juice

## Directions

1. Scale the carp cleanly, wash it, cut open the belly (not too wide), wipe the inside with a cloth, and rub well with salt and pepper.
2. Wrap the carp well in cloth and let it stand for 2 hours.
3. In a pan, slowly simmer 1/2 pound of grated hartshorn in 1 measure of water for 2 to 3 hours (do not turn it or it will become cloudy).
4. Place the fish in a cooking vessel where it will remain straight. Add 1 onion, 3 bay leaves, whole cloves, whole peppercorns, and 1/2 lemon cut into leaves.
5. Pour the clear hartshorn broth through a cloth over the fish. If the broth is insufficient, add 1/2 schoppen of wine, water, or meat broth. Cover and cook until the fish separates from the bones.
6. Let the fish cool on a platter. Strain the aspic through a cloth and divide into 2 parts. Place one part on the serving platter, let it set, then carefully place the carp on it.
7. Add 3 to 4 drops of violet juice to the remaining aspic and pour it over the carp.

title: Gesulzter welscher Hahn (Aspic-Glazed Guinea Fowl)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 guinea fowl
- 3 to 4 pounds pork forcemeat
- 1 pound veal forcemeat
- white bread (2 kreuzer worth, crusts removed), soaked in milk and squeezed
- 3 large onions, peeled and chopped
- 1/4 pound bacon, cut into small pieces
- a handful of salt
- 2 knife tips of cloves
- 3 knife tips of mace
- 1 whole nutmeg
- zest of 2 lemons, finely chopped, plus the pulp without seeds
- 1/4 pound anchovies, cleaned and boned
- 1/2 pound truffles, cleaned and skinned
- 2 eggs and 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound ham, cut into quill-feather-thick pieces
- 1/2 pound bacon, cut into quill-feather-thick pieces
- 8 chopped veal feet
- 1 measure of wine
- 2 measures of water
- 3 schoppen of vinegar
- 1 loth of mace
- 1 whole lemon, cut into leaves, without seeds
- 2 yellow turnips, cut into pieces
- 4 bay leaves
- 4 onions
- 1 celery, cut into pieces
- cloves and pepper, together 1 loth
- 3 whole eggs
- juice of 3 lemons
- bay leaves and lemons for garnish

## Directions

1. Clean the guinea fowl, wash it, remove the eyes, and scald the head to make it white; do not gut it. Cut off the feet at the knuckle, cut the fowl open along the back, and remove the body in such a way that all the meat remains attached to the skin. Cut off the wings at the second joint and cut through the neck so that the head remains attached to the skin. Remove the wing bones.
2. For the forcemeat: cut the bacon into small pieces and render it in a pan with the chopped onions until they are slightly golden. Mix the pork and veal forcemeat with the bread soaked in milk and squeezed, the cooked onions and bacon, salt, cloves, mace, nutmeg, lemon zest and pulp, anchovies, and truffles. Grind everything together in a mortar with 2 eggs and 2 egg yolks until it forms a foam.
3. Fill one quarter of the forcemeat into the guinea fowl, then layer half a pound of ham and half a pound of bacon cut into quill-feather-thick pieces with truffle slices. Add another layer of forcemeat, then another layer of ham, bacon, and truffle, finishing with a layer of forcemeat. Make sure to fill the neck and thighs well with forcemeat.
4. Sew up the fowl, tuck the head under the wing, and wrap it tightly in clean cloth to maintain its shape.
5. Place the prepared fowl in a large pan with 8 chopped veal feet, add 1 measure of wine, 2 measures of water, and 3 schoppen of vinegar. Add 1 loth of mace, 1 whole lemon cut into leaves without seeds, 2 yellow turnips cut into pieces, 4 bay leaves, 4 onions, 1 celery cut into pieces, and cloves and pepper totaling 1 loth.
6. Cook everything for 2 to 3 hours. If the broth does not cover the fowl, add more water and wine. After 2 hours, remove the fowl and let it cool in the cloth.
7. Continue cooking the remaining ingredients for another hour. Strain through a fine sieve into a clean vessel and let stand overnight.
8. On the day of serving, remove the fat from the aspic, bring the aspic to a boil in a pan, crush 3 whole eggs into it, and stir until it boils. Remove from heat, add the juice of 3 lemons, and strain through a felt hat.
9. Remove the cloth from the fowl, clean it with a clean cloth, place it on a platter, and cover with clear aspic. Let it cool, then cover again, repeating until the fowl is completely covered in aspic.
10. Garnish with bay leaves and lemons.

title: Gesulztes Kalbsnetz (Aspic-Glazed Calf's Caul)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork forcemeat
- 1 pound veal forcemeat
- 2 white breads, soaked in milk and squeezed dry
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- a handful of steamed onions
- 2 cleaned herring
- 1 large calf's caul or large intestine

## Directions

1. Mix the pork and veal forcemeat, white bread, salt, pepper, and mace together. Add the steamed onions and 2 cleaned herring, grinding everything together until fine and foamy.
2. Sew the mixture into a large calf's caul or large intestine. If using intestine, it must be poked (to prevent bursting).
3. Cook slowly in a pan with a handful of salt for 1 hour. Place on a platter and cover with clear aspic to set.

title: Getränk von Borsdorferäpfeln (Drink of Borsdorf Apples)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Borsdorf apples
- 1 Maas water
- peel of 1 lemon (the yellow)
- a handful of large and small raisins

## Directions

1. Peel and cut 4 Borsdorf apples into 4 parts
2. Pour 1 Maas water over them
3. Let this simmer with the yellow of 1 lemon and a handful of large and small raisins for one hour
4. Pour everything through a linen cloth
5. Let it cool and give it to drink

title: Gewickeltes Kalbfleisch in Sauce (Rolled Veal in Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal (from the knuckle, cut in strips 3 fingers wide)
- Onions
- Parsley
- Lemons
- White bread (a little, grated)
- Butter
- Bay leaf
- Lemon herb
- Flour (1 spoon)
- Cold water
- Light meat broth (1 ladle)
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Lemon juice (from half a lemon)

## Directions

1. Cut strips from the knuckle 3 fingers wide and pound them a little.
2. Mince onions, parsley and lemons, grate in a little white bread, lay out the strips and place some of the minced mixture on each strip.
3. Roll them together, bind them with thread, place a piece of butter in a casserole, add the meat, a chopped onion, a bay leaf and lemon herb and let them steam covered for half an hour.
4. Sprinkle one spoon of flour over them, pour a little cold water and one ladle of light meat broth, add salt, nutmeg, pepper to taste and the juice of half a lemon.

title: Gewickeltes Kalbsgekrös (Wrapped Calf's Mesentery)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's mesentery
- salt water
- eggs
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the calf's mesentery carefully
2. Cook it in salt water
3. Remove the glands
4. Cut it into small pieces
5. Turn them in beaten eggs
6. Then turn them in grated bread
7. Fry them golden brown

title: Gewöhnliche Fleischbrühe (Common Meat Broth)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef or ox meat
- Water
- Salt

## Directions

1. Prepare a meat broth from beef or ox meat, water, and a little salt by cooking for several hours. This is called simple or common meat broth.
2. In larger kitchens, at courts, in inns, and in larger families, much poultry trimmings fall away, which one cleans thoroughly, binds together with thread, and either cooks with the meat or in a separate pot into which simple broth has been poured; one may also add a piece of ham, a veal knuckle, and other clean bones left over from roasting, and cook this always with the ox meat.

title: Gewöhnliche Mandeltorte (Ordinary Almond Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1 pound blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 16 egg yolks
- 16 egg whites
- 1 whole lemon, grated on a grater
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- a handful of potato flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pound finely sifted sugar, 1 pound blanched, finely pounded almonds, 16 egg yolks until thick
2. Add the snow from 16 egg whites (this is added only after the mixture is already thick)
3. Add a whole lemon grated on a grater, the juice of 1/2 lemon, and a handful of potato flour
4. When the mixture is together, brush a metal form with butter and bake the torte in it in the oven

title: Gewöhnliche Spätzlein (Common Spätzle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- 4 eggs
- water or milk
- salt

## Directions

1. In a bowl, beat 4 eggs into 1/4 pound flour and mix with water or milk (do not beat the dough as it will become tough)
2. In a pan, bring water to boil and add a handful of salt
3. Dip a sieve into the water and press the dough through it with a cooking spoon
4. Place the spätzle in a sieve and pour cold water over them
5. If serving with soup, pour broth instead of cold water
6. If serving roasted, fry them in hot lard in a pan on both sides until golden yellow
7. Mix 2 eggs with 3 spoonfuls of sour cream, pour over the spätzle, let set, turn once more and serve

title: Gewöhnliche Spätzlein (Ordinary Spätzle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- 4 eggs
- water or milk

## Directions

1. Beat 4 eggs into 1/4 pound flour in a bowl
2. Mix it together with water or milk (the dough must not be beaten)

title: Gewöhnlicher Eierhaber (Ordinary Egg Pancake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- milk
- 4 eggs
- sour cream
- lard
- salt

## Directions

1. Put 2 spoonfuls of flour in a bowl (for one person, use one cooking spoon of flour and 2 eggs)
2. Stir it smooth with lukewarm milk, beat in 4 eggs and 1 spoonful of sour cream
3. Stir everything well together with milk
4. Heat lard in a pan, let the batter run into it, cover it
5. Let it set a little, turn it over, let it set again, tear it apart
6. Pour more lard over and let it bake until done

title: Gewöhnliches Hutzelnbrod (Common Fruit Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- medlars and plums as needed
- raisins to taste
- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- 50 to 60 pieces walnuts
- cinnamon, cloves, anise, figs, 1 glass cherry spirit
- wheat flour and some beer yeast
- medlar broth
- salt

## Directions

1. Boil medlars and plums
2. Add raisins as desired
3. Cut blanched almonds lengthwise
4. Break walnuts into 50-60 pieces
5. Combine with spices and cherry spirit
6. Mix wheat flour and beer yeast with cooked medlar broth
7. Make a starter dough and let it rise in warmth
8. When ripe, work it together thoroughly and salt it
9. Let it rise again until half-risen
10. Form into loaves
11. Work in the spiced medlars
12. Brush loaves with medlar broth
13. Let them rise once more
14. Bake in the oven

title: Gezupfte Knöpflein (Pulled Bread Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- soft crumb from 3 white rolls, torn into small pieces
- 1/2 quarter pound melted butter
- 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- equal parts water and meat broth for cooking

## Directions

1. Tear the soft crumb from 3 white rolls into small pieces.
2. Pour 1/2 quarter pound melted butter over it.
3. Stir in 3 eggs, salt, and nutmeg.
4. Let it stand for half an hour.
5. Place small dumplings, the size of a walnut, made from the dough into half water and half meat broth.
6. Add them to the soup.

title: Glacesuppe von Kalbsfüßen (Glazed Veal Foot Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 veal feet
- Celery
- Carrots
- Parsley
- Fish meat dumplings (optional)
- Egg yolks
- Nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Clean the veal feet, remove the skin, place in a casserole or pot
2. Cook with celery, carrots, and parsley until tender
3. Cut into small pieces and return to the casserole or pot
4. Strain the broth through a sieve back over the pieces and bring to a boil again
5. Small dumplings can be made from fish meat if desired
6. Place 2 egg yolks in the soup bowl and arrange the soup over them
7. Season with nutmeg and salt

title: Glacesuppe von Tauben (Glazed Pigeon Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Pigeons
- Butter
- Meat broth or water
- Hard-boiled eggs
- Chives
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean the pigeons but do not wash them; cut out the breast
2. Chop the remaining body with liver, heart, and cleaned stomach, along with beef bones
3. Steam with butter as desired for one hour and add meat broth, or water if broth is unavailable
4. Boil the breast, cut it into small pieces, place in a bowl with 3 finely chopped hard-boiled eggs and some chives
5. Strain the mixture through a hair sieve over the bowl and sprinkle nutmeg on top

title: Glacierte Kalbsbrust (Glazed Veal Breast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast (beautiful, fat, white)
- Pork fat
- Onions (chopped)
- Bay leaves
- Lemons
- Yellow turnips
- Meat broth or water (half a ladle)
- Flour (1 cooking spoon)
- Tarragon or raspberry vinegar (half a glass)
- Wine (1 glass)
- Jus (1 ladle)
- Lemon (yellow part and juice)
- Nutmeg (grated)
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Bone the veal breast and cut it in two parts so they become nicely round.
2. Place them in a casserole with boiling water and let it come to a boil.
3. Place them in cold water, lard them well and set them in a casserole with a piece of pork fat, add chopped onions, bay leaves, lemons and yellow turnips.
4. Pour in half a ladle of meat broth or water and let them steam for an hour.
5. If the meat does not have enough color, place it in a roasting pan and put it in the oven.
6. When the sauce has set, stir in one cooking spoon of flour, pour in half a glass of tarragon or raspberry vinegar, a glass of wine and one ladle of jus, let it cook for half an hour.
7. Press it through a hair sieve, add the meat back and season it with the minced yellow part and juice of a lemon, grated nutmeg, and salt and pepper to taste.

title: Glühwein (Mulled Wine)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 bottle red wine, preferably Burgundy
- 2 g cinnamon
- 4 cloves
- 80 g sugar
- 1/2 finely peeled lemon peel

## Directions

1. All ingredients are mixed, heated on the fire until simmering, poured through a hair sieve and served hot.

title: Glühwein (Mulled Wine)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Maas wine (red wine is best)
- 1/2 pound sugar
- whole cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Calculate 1/2 pound sugar to 1/2 Maas wine
2. Let it become very hot together in a brass pan, but it must not boil
3. Place whole cinnamon and cloves in a hair sieve
4. Pour the hot wine over them and cover immediately

title: Glühwein auf Münchner Art (Mulled Wine, Munich Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Maas good wine (Affenthaler is best)
- 1-1/2 pounds sugar
- finest cinnamon
- several cloves

## Directions

1. Cook 2 Maas good wine, 1-1/2 pounds sugar, finest cinnamon and several cloves together well covered in a brass pan until 1 Maas of it has cooked down
2. Strain the wine through a hair sieve into the serving dish
3. Cover it very tightly

title: Graupen mit Pflaumen (Pearl Barley with Prunes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g medium or strong pearl barley
- 5 g butter
- 3/4 l water
- 5 g salt
- 90 g dried prunes
- 25 g sugar
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 1 tsp lemon sugar
- 1/4 l water
- 25 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Prepare the pearl barley as indicated (see Pearl Barley Soup). Sauté the barley with butter while stirring and after adding 3/4 l water, allow it to swell completely and slowly with salt in an earthenware pot.
2. Prepare the prunes by adding them with sugar, cinnamon, lemon sugar, and the soaking water and allow them to swell slowly to full size.
3. Mix the prunes with the barley and arrange with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Graupenuppe (Barley Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 g barley
- 1 l mutton broth or water
- 15 g beef fat or margarine
- 1/2 egg
- 8 g salt
- 1 tablespoon celery and onion pieces

## Directions

1. Rinse the barley in an earthenware pot with water and put it on the stove with water and salt to soften. Clean celery and onion and cut into small pieces; finely chop the beef fat.
2. Drain the barley and brown it with celery, onion pieces and the fat; gradually pour in broth or water and salt while stirring and cook the barley slowly until soft covered. When the barley is done, mix the egg with a few tablespoons of soup and pour it into the whole liquid. Adding a few potatoes cooked together and 1 teaspoon parsley makes the soup tastier. For children and the sick it can also be pressed through a fine sieve to make 'barley slime soup,' but then butter is preferable.

title: Griessflammeri (Semolina Pudding)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Semolina
- Milk
- Sugar
- Orange peel
- Salt
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. Rinse molds or cups with water
2. Rinse and drain the semolina
3. Bring milk, sugar, orange peel, and a pinch of salt to a boil
4. Mix the semolina with some of the liquid, then stir into the milk and cook for 15 minutes
5. Beat the egg white until stiff and gently fold in after the semolina is cooked
6. Pour the pudding into the rinsed molds to cool
7. Once cooled, unmold the pudding and serve with braised fruit or fruit sauce
8. Optional: refine with grated almonds or vanilla and 1 egg yolk
9. Variation: use cornstarch instead of semolina, stirred cold (Cornstarch Pudding); use red fruit juice with water instead of milk and omit the egg white (Red Fruit Pudding); or use very thin apple puree instead of milk (Apple Pudding)

title: Griessklösse (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g semolina
- 1/4 liter skim milk
- 20 g margarine
- 1 pinch salt
- 30 g sugar
- 1 egg
- Water and salt for cooking the dumplings

## Directions

1. Bring milk, fat, salt, and sugar to a boil
2. Add the semolina and stir constantly to form a shiny mass

title: Griessuppe (Semolina Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- meat broth
- 4 handfuls of semolina flour
- butter (hen-egg size)
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls of sour cream
- chives
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. In a pot of simmering meat broth, stir in 4 handfuls of semolina flour
2. Add butter the size of a hen's egg
3. Let this cook together for a while
4. Beat 2 egg yolks in a soup bowl
5. Stir them smooth with 2 spoonfuls of sour cream
6. Stir the soup into them and sprinkle chives and nutmeg on top

title: Grießklöße (Semolina Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g semolina
- 1/4 l skim milk
- 20 g margarine
- 1 pinch salt
- 30 g sugar
- 1 egg
- water and salt for cooking the dumplings

## Directions

1. Bring milk, fat, salt, and sugar to a boil
2. Pour in the semolina while stirring constantly and cook until a shiny mass forms
3. While still hot, stir in the egg yolk
4. Beat the egg white to stiff peaks and fold into the dough
5. Using a damp spoon, drop uniform dumplings into salted boiling water just before serving
6. Cook uncovered in salted water until done
7. Remove with a skimmer, let drain, place on a warmed dish and serve with stewed fruit, fruit sauce or similar

title: Grillirter Kalbskopf (Grilled Calf's Head)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- salt water
- butter
- bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Clean the calf's head carefully
2. Break out the teeth
3. Cook the head soft in salt water
4. Remove it
5. Brush it with butter
6. Sprinkle it with grated bread
7. Place it on the grill
8. Let it fry golden brown

title: Grog
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/8 l cognac, arrack or rum
- 80 g sugar
- 3/4 l boiling water

## Directions

1. Sugar and cognac are quickly mixed with the boiling water and served immediately. The strength and sweetness of the drink are to be changed according to taste.

title: Grog
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 Loth sugar
- 1 glass boiling water
- 3 to 4 spoonfuls Arak

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water and 3 to 4 spoonfuls of Arak over 2 to 3 Loth sugar

title: Große italienische Würste (Large Italian Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- all intestines from one large fat pig
- salt
- pepper
- cloves (half-crushed)
- mace
- sweet caraway seed

## Directions

1. Take all intestines from a large fat pig; set aside the large fat intestine
2. Cut the remaining intestines into small pieces and salt them well
3. Let them lie in salt and drain the liquid they release themselves
4. Remove them from the salt water
5. Add pepper, half-crushed cloves, a little mace and sweet caraway seed
6. Fill the mixture very firmly into the fat intestine
7. Tie it off and salt it again
8. Hang it in a dry place with air circulation until quite dry
9. Afterwards these sausages can be simmered with green vegetables or alone, or also grilled

title: Grüne Kartoffeln (Green Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tbsp finely chopped soup vegetables (plenty of parsley root)
- 1 small onion
- 1/2 spice mixture
- 1 pinch pepper
- 30 g fat or margarine
- 30 g flour
- 1/2 tbsp chopped dill
- 1 tbsp chopped parsley
- 2 tbsp skim milk
- 15 g salt
- 1/2 kg potatoes
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Clean, wash and cut the soup vegetables; peel the onion and cut into cubes; wash the parsley and dill and chop them; wash the potatoes and put them in a pot, add water and salt.
2. Cook the soup vegetables, spice mixture, salt and water in a covered pot for 20 minutes to 1/2 hour. Cook the potatoes until done, drain, peel and cut into slices.
3. Brown the onion cubes in the fat until golden, add the flour and stir until cooked, pour in the vegetable water, cook for 1/4 hour, strain through a sieve, season with parsley, dill, pepper, salt and milk, mix with the potatoes, and serve the dish in a bowl.
4. The same sauce can be prepared for green fish; the fish is cooked first in the vegetable water and later placed in the sauce. Double the amount of liquid poured over the roux makes a good soup, fish soup, which can be made even more delicious with any chopped herbs. An egg yolk beaten into the finished soup makes it richer.

title: Grüne Würste (Green Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ingredients as in recipe 369
- White fresh butter
- Spinach cheese

## Directions

1. Make the sausages as in recipe 369, but instead of crayfish butter use only some white fresh butter
2. To color the sausages green, add the spinach cheese already described to the dough
3. Boil them in milk without grilling them strongly
4. Serve them warm

title: Grünkernsuppe (Green Spelt Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 g green spelt flour
- 30 g fat or margarine
- 1 l water
- 10 g onions
- 1/2 egg
- 8 g salt

## Directions

1. Dice the onion.
2. Braise the onion cubes and fat, add the green spelt flour, toast it with the onion fat while stirring, gradually add the water. After the soup has cooked for 1-1/2 hours, mix a few spoonfuls of it with the egg, though this can also be omitted, add it to the mass, season with salt, press the soup through a sieve and serve it reheated.

title: Grünkohl oder Braunkohl (Kale)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg kale leaves
- 20 g salt
- 3 l water
- 40 g pork, goose, or duck fat
- 20 g onions
- 3/8 l broth
- 5 g sugar
- 5 g oat groats
- 1/2 spice mixture
- 3 g salt

## Directions

1. Dice the onion. Remove the large leaf ribs from the kale and wash it.
2. Place the kale in boiling salted water and bring it to a boil several times, drain it, pour cold water over it, carefully squeeze it with a spoon and chop it lightly; in many places it is also enjoyed unchopped.
3. Heat the fat; soften the onions in it; add the kale, pour in the boiling broth; sprinkle oat groats, spice, salt and sugar between; toss the kale, add more broth and braise the vegetable slowly covered.
4. Instead of oat groats, one can also use 10 g semolina. Brussels sprouts or sprouting kale are cooked the same way but without oat groats.
5. Leftovers can be reheated several times.

title: Guirlandentorte (Garland Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds, finely cut
- 1/2 pound almonds, pounded
- 1/2 pound pounded sugar
- 12 egg yolks
- zest of 1/2 lemon, grated
- 8 egg whites
- a handful of potato flour
- butter
- 12 egg whites
- 12 loth pounded sugar
- 1/2 pound cut almonds
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound cut almonds

## Directions

1. Cut the blanched almonds very finely and pound 1/2 pound of them
2. Stir the pounded almonds with 1/2 pound pounded sugar and 12 egg yolks until the mixture is thick
3. Mix in the grated lemon zest, the snow from 8 egg whites, and a handful of potato flour
4. Fill the mixture into a springform brushed with butter and bake the torte
5. Whip a stiff snow from 12 egg whites, mix in 12 loth pounded sugar and 1/2 pound cut almonds
6. Spread this frosting evenly on the baked torte and dry it for another quarter hour in the oven
7. In a brass pan, continuously stir together 1/2 pound sugar and 1/2 pound cut almonds until the sugar becomes liquid and yellow
8. Remove the springform from the torte, make a beautiful garland from the roasted almonds around it and a crown in the middle

title: Gulasch (Hungarian Goulash)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 g beef without fat and bones
- 1 g caraway
- 10 g fat
- 1/2 teaspoon white pepper
- 5 g flour
- 10 g onions
- 5 g salt
- 1/8 liter hot water

## Directions

1. Cut the clean washed beef into 1-1/2 cm cubes, dice the onion, and soften it slowly in fat in an earthenware pot.
2. Add the meat to the softened onion dice, fry it slowly until it is somewhat browned, dust flour over it, and add 1 pinch salt.
3. When the flour is cooked, add hot water, add the prepared caraway, cover the pot, and let the dish simmer slowly for 3 hours under frequent shaking and addition of some water, skim fat if necessary, add pepper, check the consistency of the sauce, and serve the dish.
4. Also use leftovers, especially dry pieces and trimmings from braised or beef roasts for this flavorful and hearty dish.

title: Gurken in Essig (Cucumbers in Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Kleine Gurken (Small cucumbers)
- Salz (Salt)
- Meerrettig (Horseradish)
- Schalottenzwiebeln (Shallot onions)
- Lorbeerblätter (Bay leaves)
- Pfeffer (Peppercorns)
- Nelken (Cloves)
- Estragon (Tarragon)
- Basilikum (Basil)
- Thymian (Thyme)
- Senfmehl (Mustard flour)

## Directions

1. Rub small cucumbers with a cloth
2. Place them in a large dish and cover with strong salt water
3. Let them stand for 48 hours, stirring often (pump water is better than well water because it is harder)
4. When ready to preserve, lay them on a cloth and let them dry well
5. Cut horseradish into small pieces and peel shallot onions
6. Gather together bay leaves, whole peppercorns, some whole cloves, tarragon, basil, and thyme
7. In the designated dish, layer cucumbers alternately with the mixture of spices
8. Place a long sachet containing some mustard flour on top of the cucumbers
9. Tie the dish shut and store it for the winter

title: Gurkensalat (Cucumber Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cucumber
- 1 tbsp oil
- 5 g salt
- 1 strong pinch white pepper
- 1/2 tbsp vinegar or lemon juice
- 1/2 tsp chives
- 1 pinch sugar

## Directions

1. Wipe the cucumber and peel it from the tip to the stem end until all the green is removed, taste it, and if it is bitter, discard it wholly or partially. Shortly before serving, slice it thinly. Chop the chives.
2. Mix the cucumber slices with oil, salt, pepper, chives, sugar and acid and, because they quickly draw juice, serve immediately to the table.
3. Salad of pickled cucumbers is made exactly the same way, especially mixed with potato slices.
4. Radish salad is prepared the same way.
5. Cucumber salad with sour cream: 4 tbsp thick sour cream beaten clear with 1 tbsp vinegar, 1 tsp salt, 1 pinch pepper (optionally also 1 tsp sugar) and 1 tsp chives are mixed with the cucumber slices.

title: Gute schwarze Tinte (Good Black Ink)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound logwood
- 2 maas water
- 1 maas vinegar
- 3/4 pound coarsely pounded gall nuts
- 4 loth alum
- 12 loth dried iron vitriol
- 8 loth gum arabic

## Directions

1. Cook 1 pound logwood with 2 maas water until only 1 maas remains
2. When the logwood has settled to the bottom, pour this off
3. In an earthenware container, stir together 1 maas vinegar, 3/4 pound coarsely pounded gall nuts, 4 loth alum, 12 loth dried iron vitriol and 8 loth gum arabic together with the decocted liquid using a wooden stick thoroughly
4. Let it stand for 14 days and the ink is then ready for use
5. The liquid ink poured off and the residue consisting of the above ingredients, boiled again, gives again good ink which, mixed with the first ink, further enhances its quality

title: Gute, brauchbare Bierhefe (Good, Usable Beer Yeast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 to 12 pounds malt (wheat malt is best)
- 1 pound hops
- 40 to 50 maas water
- 1 pound good yeast
- 3 pounds good wheat flour
- half a schoppen brandy

## Directions

1. Bring 10 to 12 pounds malt (wheat malt is best) together with 1 pound hops into a vat or barrel with a double bottom
2. Let 40 to 50 maas water boil in a kettle and brew the malt with it
3. Let it stand 24 hours, noting that the second bottom must have holes, and draw off the liquid from below
4. Stir 1 pound good yeast under the drawn-off liquid
5. Mix 3 pounds good wheat flour and half a schoppen brandy together in an earthenware pot and combine this with the above
6. This yeast keeps well for 4 to 5 months

title: Gänsebraten, Fetter (Rich Roast Goose)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 goose of 4 kg
- 1 kg apples
- 40 g sugar
- 30 g salt
- 1 bunch mugwort
- 30 g onions
- spice mixture
- 8 g potato starch
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Prepare the goose according to instructions; remove wings, neck, head and feet.
2. Carefully separate the intestinal fat from the other fat and soak both separately.
3. After the goose is washed and dried, rub it with salt.
4. Peel or leave unpeeled the apples as desired, cut them or if small leave them whole, toss with sugar and salt and fill the goose with them along with the rinsed mugwort; sew it closed and rub salt on the outside.
5. Keep the oven not too hot in the first hour, pour 1/2 l boiling water over the goose; add onions and spice mixture.
6. Baste the goose with water for 1 hour in the oven. In the first hour you should notice neither color nor roasting aroma; if the oven temperature rises, cover the goose with parchment paper.
7. After about 1 hour, the goose will have rendered enough of its own fat to baste with; fill with spoonfuls of water from time to time so the fat doesn't brown.
8. After 1-1/2 hours, occasionally skim fat from the sauce and roast the goose to a nice brown color.
9. When tender, sprinkle it with 1 spoonful of cold water so the skin becomes crispy, arrange the goose and cook the sauce in the pan with some water and potato starch stirred in water to sufficient consistency, and can also color with food coloring.
10. Pour the sauce through a fine sieve.

title: Gänseklein (Goose Giblets Stew)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Heart, stomach, head, wings, feet and neck of goose, cut into pieces
- 30 g onion cubes
- 30 g skimmed goose fat
- 1 spice measure
- 30 g flour
- 10 g salt
- 2 tablespoons parsley
- 56 g soup greens
- 1 pinch pepper
- 3/4 l water

## Directions

1. Clean and singe the goose giblets and parts. Cook them covered in water with soup greens and spices until tender, then clean each piece individually.
2. Sauté the onion cubes in goose fat skimmed from the broth until soft, add the flour and cook until done.
3. Pour the goose broth onto the roux and let it reduce by half with the lid off.
4. Season the sauce with salt and pepper, pass it through a sieve, and let the meat become hot in it.
5. Finally add chopped parsley or, if not available, 1/2 teaspoon dried marjoram and serve the goose giblets in a deep bowl.

title: Götterspeise (God's Food)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 spoon porridge
- egg yolks from the porridge
- 4 bitter almonds
- 3 loth ground sugar with lemon zest
- egg whites from the porridge
- raspberry or apricot jelly for serving

## Directions

1. Cook a thick porridge from 1/2 maas milk, stir 1/4 pound butter lightly, add 1 spoon of porridge to the butter, beat an egg yolk into it, stir together and continue this way until the porridge is finished
2. Peel 4 bitter almonds, pound them fine in a mortar, stir under the mass
3. Add 3 loth ground sugar with lemon zest grated on it, beat the whites of the eggs used for the porridge to snow, mix everything together with the above
4. Butter a tin mold, sprinkle with sugar, fill the mass in, seal the mold tightly and cook 1 hour in boiling water
5. Turn this Götterspeise onto a plate and serve raspberry or apricot jelly with it

title: Habergrützsuppe (Oat Groats Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls of carefully cleaned oat groats
- half a measure of water
- butter
- 2 egg yolks
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Let 2 handfuls of carefully cleaned oat groats cook down halfway with half a measure of water
2. Stir in a piece of butter
3. Let it come to a boil again
4. Push it through a hair sieve into another dish
5. Stir in 2 egg yolks, salt and nutmeg

title: Hachispastete (Hash Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound roasted meat
- 2 spoonfuls capers
- 1 cleaned herring
- zest of 1 lemon
- marrow of 2 lemons
- Mundbrod (bread roll)
- 2 eggs
- salt
- pepper
- mace
- short pastry
- cold water
- butter pastry strip
- eggs

## Directions

1. Weigh 1 pound roasted meat, 2 spoonfuls capers, 1 cleaned herring, the zest of 1 lemon and the marrow of 2 lemons not very finely
2. Cut the crust from a Mundbrod, soak it in water, press it out firmly, add it to the minced meat
3. Add 2 eggs, salt, pepper and mace and mix everything together
4. Make a short pastry, roll it out knife-back thick to a round cake
5. Place the mass on it, spread it smooth, leaving 2 fingers width empty
6. Make another round cake, pull it over, cut it smooth with a warm knife
7. Brush the pastry with cold water, fasten it with a butter pastry strip, brush it with eggs and bake it in the oven
8. Serve it warm at table

title: Hafergrütze- oder Haferflockensuppe (Oat Grits or Oatflakes Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g oat grits or 40 g oatflakes
- 1 piece of orange peel
- 10 g fat
- 10 g sugar
- 5 g salt
- 1 tablespoon currants
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Rinse the oat grits or oatflakes three times with cold water in an earthenware pot; soak the currants according to instructions.
2. Put the rinsed oat grits with salt, orange peel and water in an earthenware pot and cook slowly until soft covered. When the oat grits are soft, press them through a fine sieve and season the soup with fat, sugar and salt, reheat once more and serve mixed with the currants.

title: Hafergrütze- oder Haferflockensuppe oder -trank für einen Kranken (Oat Groats or Oatflakes Soup or Drink for the Ill)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 g oatflakes or 40 g common oat groats
- 1 pinch salt
- 1 slice lemon
- 5 g sugar
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Preparation: Oat groats or oatflakes are washed three times in cold water, stirring them thoroughly.
2. Add the lemon slice without pit and skin and the water to the oat groats, let them swell slowly under frequent stirring while covered, press through a hair sieve and warm them, seasoned with salt and sugar, while stirring.
3. These soups can be diluted with boiling water to make a drink, which can be made more nourishing, like the soups, by whisking with an egg yolk.
4. Optional: Hafergrützsuppe with almond flavor: Instead of lemon, add 5 blanched, finely grated sweet almonds to the oat groats, cook them slowly for 1 hour and complete the soup as above.

title: Haferschleim zum Trinken (Oat Gruel for Drinking)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 g oat groats
- 1 pinch of salt
- 1-1/2 l water

## Directions

1. The oat groats are stirred thoroughly, swelled with cold water and drained off.
2. In an earthenware pot, 1/4 l water is filled in while frequently beating with the wooden spoon, this is allowed to evaporate and the process continues until the groats are quite soft and the liquid is slimy. Then the oat gruel is rubbed through a hair sieve, or in the absence of one through a hot-washed cloth, and heated with a small amount of salt. Sugar is added according to taste and the gruel is diluted according to use with boiling water. For the sick, a little raspberry juice or red wine is gladly added.

title: Hagenbuttencrême (Rosehip Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 loth isinglass
- 1 schoppen water
- 4 to 6 spoonfuls rosehip pulp
- 6 eggs
- 1 schoppen good wine
- 1/2 schoppen water
- 1/2 pound sugar
- grated zest of 1 lemon
- almond oil

## Directions

1. Dissolve 1-1/2 loth isinglass in 1 schoppen water.
2. Place 4 to 6 spoonfuls of rosehip pulp in a brass pan.
3. Beat 6 eggs into it.
4. Add 1 schoppen good wine, 1/2 schoppen water, 1/2 pound sugar, and the grated zest of 1 lemon.
5. Cook everything together with constant stirring.
6. Fill the cream into a jelly mold brushed with almond oil.
7. Place it in the cellar and let the cream set in it.

title: Hagenbuttenlatwerge (Rose Hip Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 to 12 Maas of rose hips
- 20 Maas water per 10 Maas rose hips

## Directions

1. Remove the seeds and stems from 10 to 12 Maas of rose hips
2. Place them together in a cleaned pot with 20 Maas of water (for 10 Maas rose hips)
3. Let them cook together for 2 to 3 hours, always adding more water as needed
4. Remove the rose hips and press them well
5. Let the juice simmer down (20 Maas of water must reduce to one)
6. Skim it frequently
7. Store it in a porcelain container
8. It is very good for coughs

title: Hamburger Pudding (Hamburg Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pint water
- 1/4 pound butter
- flour
- 1/2 quarter pound small raisins
- 3 loth pounded sugar
- cooked fruit
- prunes
- cherries
- apricots

## Directions

1. Boil 1 pint water and 1/4 pound butter together in a brass pan.
2. Stir it in a bowl with a little flour loosely so that it gets no lumps.
3. Add 1/2 quarter pound small raisins and 3 loth pounded sugar, mix everything together.
4. Tie it in a damp cloth, cook it half an hour in boiling water.
5. Serve with cooked fruit such as prunes, cherries, or apricots.

title: Hammel- oder Kalbsgekröse (Mutton or Veal Offal)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 offal (stomach, net, and tripe)
- 1 spice packet
- 100 g salt
- 50 g onions
- 40 g fat or margarine
- 40 g flour
- 1 pinch saffron
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- 1 pinch pepper

## Directions

1. The offal consists of stomach, net, and tripe. It should only be used from freshly slaughtered animals. Pour warm water over it repeatedly and rinse in cold water. Starting from one side, rub it vigorously with salt, let soak for 24 hours, and cut into 4 cm pieces. Slice the onions.
2. Add to the offal 1 spice packet, half the onions, salt and 1-1/2 l water and cook slowly until tender in 2-3 hours. Make a gelatinous sauce from fat with the remaining onions sweated in it, flour, and the strained broth. Color the sauce yellow with saffron or stirred egg yolk and season with chopped parsley, pepper, and salt. Drain the offal on a sieve, heat it in the sauce and serve.

title: Hammel-, Rinder-, Schweine-, Kalbs-, oder Gänseleber, gebratene (Fried Mutton, Beef, Pork, Veal, or Goose Liver)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g mutton or beef liver
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 2 tablespoons grated breadcrumbs
- 1/2 tablespoon vinegar
- 40 g fat or margarine
- 5 g salt and pepper
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 4 tablespoons water or broth

## Directions

1. Wash the liver, skin it, and cut diagonally into 4-5 slices. Mix breadcrumbs and flour together (breading). Melt the fat for frying.
2. Salt and pepper the sliced liver, coat in breading, and fry in steaming fat, turning four times, for 4-6 minutes until a beautiful brown color. Lay the slices on a dish side by side, not on top of each other.
3. Add 1 teaspoon flour to the fat in the pan, stir until cooked, pour in several spoonfuls of water or broth and 1/2 tablespoon vinegar, taste and adjust seasoning with salt, and serve the sauce with the liver.

title: Hammelfleisch (Boiled Mutton)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) mutton (shoulder)
- 1 liter water
- 8 g or 1 scant tablespoon salt

## Directions

1. Set the water with salt on the stove. Quickly wash and pound the mutton.
2. When the salt water boils, put the meat in and cook it slowly covered until foam forms. After the meat has cooked for a while with the foam, remove the foam and cook the meat covered until tender in 1-1/4 to 1-1/2 hours.
3. Remove it with a spoon, pour a few spoons of the mutton fat from the broth over it, and keep it covered and hot.
4. The broth can be used for cooking beans. You can also cook beans that have been blanched in salt water and cooled in cold water with the mutton, but add them 3/4 hours before the meat is tender, otherwise treat as described. If you want to use the meat broth as soup, add 1 tablespoon finely cut soup vegetables as seasoning to the meat.

title: Hammelfleisch mit Kohlrüben und Kartoffeln (Mutton with Rutabagas and Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g mutton
- 3/4 l water
- 8 g salt
- 3/4 kg rutabagas
- 1-1/2 l water for blanching
- 250 g peeled potatoes
- water and salt
- 1 spice packet
- 10 g fat or margarine
- 10 g flour
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Place 3/4 l water with 8 g salt on the stove. Wash and pound the meat quickly. Brush rutabagas clean in water, peel them, and cut into uniform strips. Cut potatoes in half or quarters depending on size.
2. Blanch the rutabagas in boiling water uncovered and drain on a sieve. Treat the potatoes the same way but in salted water. When the salted water comes to a boil with the meat, add the meat and cook slowly until foam forms and cook for several more minutes. Remove the foam, add potatoes, rutabagas, and spice packet to the meat and cook together for another 1-1/2 hours. Sweat the fat and flour together, cook with some broth, add to the dish and shake thoroughly to combine. Before serving, taste and adjust seasoning with pepper and salt.

title: Hammelfleisch, geschnitten, mit Reis (Mutton Pieces with Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 65 g lean mutton
- 20 g onions
- 15 g fat
- 15 g celery
- 125 g rice
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Wash the meat and cut into uniform 2 cm pieces. Weigh the onion, clean the celery, and wash the rice thoroughly.
2. Brown the meat cubes and onion in fat until yellow, season with pepper and salt. Add celery, rice, and water and let everything cook together slowly until tender. Taste and adjust seasoning with salt before serving.

title: Hammelkeule, geschmorte (Braised Leg of Mutton)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small leg of mutton, cut short, 2 kg
- 10 g salt
- 20 g fat
- 10 g flour
- 50 g onions
- 1 spice packet
- 1/4 l water or wheat beer

## Directions

1. Wash the leg, pound it, and rub with salt.
2. Heat the fat strongly, turn the leg in flour, and brown on all sides in the fat. Add onions and spices. Slowly add 1/8 l boiling water, cover the pot, and braise the leg very slowly over gentle heat for 3 hours, basting frequently and adding water as needed. After this time it will be very tender. Remove fat from the sauce and strain through a sieve, coat the leg with it and serve. You can also braise some tomatoes with it and strain through the sauce, or add sour cream or 1 tablespoon cognac.

title: Hammelniere in Scheiben (Sliced Mutton Kidneys)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 raw mutton kidneys
- 1 pinch salt and pepper
- 1/4 lemon
- 15 g margarine
- 5 g flour
- 1 tablespoon chopped herbs
- 4 tablespoons gravy leftovers or water with 1 knife tip of meat extract
- 1 tablespoon red wine
- 1 pinch sugar

## Directions

1. Slice the kidneys thinly and marinate with a little salt, pepper, and a few drops of lemon juice for 1/4 hour.
2. Brown the margarine in a pan. Dry the kidneys with a cloth and toss them quickly in the pan for 1-2 minutes. Remove the slices, place them in a bowl and set in a water bath. Roast 5 g flour in the cooking fat, add herbs and, if desired, sliced mushrooms, gravy sauce or meat extract broth, red wine, lemon juice, and sugar. Let everything cook through and pour the sauce over the kidneys.

title: Hammels-Cotelettes (Mutton Chops)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful young mutton ribs
- onion
- parsley
- salt
- pepper
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the sinew from the bottom of beautiful young mutton ribs, cut off the fatty skin from the top, strip the bone, remove the membrane
2. Pound the ribs a little and sprinkle them with a mixture of onion, parsley, salt, and pepper
3. Lay them in a roasting pan, pour a little water over them, cover them, and let them fry quickly
4. They must be done in half a quarter hour
5. Serve them with savoy cabbage or kohlrabi

title: Hammelsschlägel in Gurkensauce (Leg of Mutton in Cucumber Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- meat broth
- vinegar
- onions
- basil
- lemon herb
- parsnip root
- 2 cucumbers
- butter
- parsley
- 1 lemon
- capers
- 2 anchovies
- flour
- wine
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove fat and end bone from the leg of mutton and cut off the lower bone
2. Pound the leg well and braise it nicely golden in a covered casserole with 1 spoonful of meat broth, some vinegar, onions, basil, lemon herb, and parsnip root
3. Peel 2 cucumbers, cut them as for a salad, remove the seeds, and braise them soft in a casserole with butter
4. Pound together a handful of clean washed parsley, 1 whole lemon with its yellow zest and pulp (but not the white skin), 1 spoonful of capers, and 2 clean washed anchovies
5. Add them to the cucumbers and let everything braise together for another quarter hour
6. Thicken with 1 spoonful of flour, add 1 spoonful of jus and some wine, pepper, and salt
7. Lift out the meat, place it on a dish, strain the sauce through a fine sieve, return it to a casserole with the cucumbers, and let it cook a while longer

title: Hammelsschlägel mit einer Sauce von Weintrauben (Leg of Mutton with Grape Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leg of mutton
- butter
- verjus or meat broth

## Directions

1. Roast the leg of mutton 1 to 1.5 hours before serving, basting frequently with butter
2. Place it warm on a dish
3. Boil a little verjus (juice of sour unripe grapes) or a little meat broth in a casserole and pour hot over the leg
4. Alternatively, the leg of mutton can be treated like a quarter of lamb, larded with parsley and served with a sauce hachée, or larded with bacon and served with another sauce

title: Haselnußküchlein (Hazelnut Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound sugar
- 6 egg whites
- grated zest of 1 lemon
- 8 loth butter
- 4 loth flour
- 2 handfuls ground hazelnuts

## Directions

1. Work all ingredients together thoroughly
2. Bake in a waffle iron until golden
3. Sprinkle with sugar

title: Hasenbraten (Roast Hare)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- hare
- onions
- lemons
- bay leaves
- juniper berries
- meat broth
- salt
- spices

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and feet of the hare
2. Cut the breast not too close to the saddle so it gets a nice shape
3. Skin the roast, lard it, and roast it nicely golden in a roasting pan with cut onions, lemons, bay leaves, and juniper berries along with 1 spoonful of meat broth
4. Add salt and spices as desired
5. Note: if the game has not been marinated, it must be poured over with boiling vinegar in which onions are

title: Hasenbraten (Roast Hare)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare head and feet
- Hare breast
- Onions, sliced
- Lemons
- Bay leaves
- Juniper berries
- Meat broth
- Salt and spices to taste
- Boiling vinegar with onions (if hare has not been marinated)

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and feet of the hare; cut the breast not too close to the saddle so it has a nice shape.
2. Skin the roast, lard it, and place it in a roasting pan with sliced onions, lemons, bay leaves, and juniper berries along with a spoonful of meat broth.
3. Roast until beautifully golden brown; add salt and spices to taste.
4. Note: If the game has not been marinated, it must be poured over with boiling vinegar containing onions.

title: Hasenbraten für 6 Personen (Roasted Hare for 6 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 full-grown young hare
- 65 g bacon
- 15 g salt
- 200 g margarine
- 1/4 liter sour cream or milk, or milk with 1 drop of vinegar
- 6 g cornstarch
- 1/4 liter hot water

## Directions

1. Hang the hare by its hind legs on 2 hooks or strong nails; if not yet eviscerated, cut open the body lengthwise from the tail, remove the entrails with the rectum, separate the gallbladder from the liver, keep the liver, heart and lungs, discard the rest, loosen the fur on the belly from the skin, cut around the paws with a sharp knife, pull the paws out of the skin, pull the entire pelt over the back and hindquarters, remove it at the front legs, cut off the ears and pull the skin over the head. Cut off the head, neck, and back; remove the belly skin and front legs, skin the legs and braise them later. Leave the kidneys and fat in the hare, then wash it thoroughly in cold water without soaking. Cut off the hind legs at the joint, break the tailbone so the hindquarters lie well, beat the hare everywhere with a flat mallet, skin it, first the hindquarters from the underside likewise the back, either remove the fillets or place 1 slice of bacon underneath and lard the hare and legs.
2. Once everything is prepared, first place the hindquarters in the roasting pan and, so the roast does not dry out, 15 minutes later add the back and the bent-in legs, sprinkle the hare with salt and pour hot margarine over it. After the hare has browned, gradually pour in the sour cream or milk and some hot water alternately and let it finish roasting with frequent basting. Meanwhile, stir cornstarch and a few spoonfuls of sour cream or milk into the sauce, coat the hare with it after a few minutes and arrange it on a long platter after it has been cut into appropriate pieces (one can also bring the whole hare to table and separate the meat in clean pieces to still use the bone framework for a soup); deglaze the roasting pan with sour cream or milk until the sauce becomes thick and glossy and strain it through a fine sieve into a gravy boat, pouring a few spoonfuls over the arranged hare. The length of roasting time and heat naturally depends on whether the hare is young or old; a young full-grown hare would become tender at medium heat in 35 minutes, the back in 25 minutes, an old hare at lower temperature only in 1-1/2 hours. In the latter case, cover it with butcher paper.

title: Hasenpfeffer (Peppered Hare Stew)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g hare meat: the split head, breast, neck, lungs and heart
- 50 g bacon cubes
- 20 g onions
- 20 g flour
- 1 spice measure
- 1/4 liter meat extract broth
- 4 tablespoons red or malt wine
- 15 g sugar
- 8 g salt and pepper
- 2 drops food coloring
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. The small hare pieces are minced and washed thoroughly.
2. Cook them in 1/2 liter water, then cut into cubes and remove the bones. The bacon is browned in an earthenware pot; the meat and onion cubes are added. After everything is browned, dust flour over it, add spices, broth, wine, sugar, salt and pepper and braise the dish slowly until done. The appearance is enhanced by food coloring; the wine can be omitted.

title: Hasenöhrchen (Rabbit Ears Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls of sugar
- 2 spoonfuls of thick sour cream
- 6 loth of fine flour
- hot lard for frying
- sugar and cinnamon for topping

## Directions

1. Mix the egg yolks, sugar, and sour cream in a bowl with the fine flour to make a dough
2. Roll out the dough, fold it over itself, then roll it out again to the thickness of a knife back
3. Cut square pieces from the dough
4. Make a slit in each piece
5. Fry them in hot lard until golden yellow
6. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Hasenöhrchen (Rabbit Ears)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls of sugar
- 2 spoonfuls of thick sour cream
- 6 loth of fine flour

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 3 egg yolks, 3 spoonfuls of sugar, 2 spoonfuls of thick sour cream, and 6 loth of fine flour in a bowl
2. Roll it out, fold it over, and roll it out again knife-back thick
3. Cut square pieces from it

title: Hecht am Spieß gebraten (Pike Roasted on a Spit)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike
- salt
- pepper
- larding fat
- melted lemon butter
- lemon juice
- jus

## Directions

1. Clean the pike carefully, cut away the gills, and remove the skin
2. Rub it with salt and pepper
3. Lard it nicely and tie it onto the spit
4. Frequently baste it with melted lemon butter
5. After half an hour, remove it from the spit and place it on a serving plate
6. Pour lemon juice and jus over it

title: Hecht auf dem Rost gebraten (Pike Roasted on a Grill)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pike, cleaned and gutted
- salt
- pepper
- melted butter
- lemon butter
- 1 handful parsley
- 1 lemon
- lemon slices

## Directions

1. Rub the cleaned and gutted pike with salt and pepper
2. Let it rest for one hour
3. Make 3 to 4 diagonal cuts in it, then sew the fish shut again and cut out the ears
4. Place it on a grill and frequently baste with melted butter, also pouring hot butter into the ears
5. Turn the fish and baste the other side
6. Roast very carefully so the skin does not stick to the grill
7. For serving, make lemon butter as follows: finely chop 1 handful of parsley, let a piece of butter become hot in a casserole, steam the parsley in it, squeeze the juice of 1 lemon into it, let it come to a boil together, and pour this over the fish
8. Lemon slices can be placed in the cuts

title: Hecht à la tourne (Pike à la Tourne)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white pike
- lemon juice
- butter
- lemon
- fish forcemeat
- eggs
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Scale a fine white pike thoroughly and wash it well
2. Clean it out and cut off the head and tail
3. Split lengthwise and remove the backbone
4. Clean thoroughly with a cloth and cut into 4 pieces
5. Place the pieces in lemon juice, turning them frequently, and if time permits, let them stand overnight
6. The next day, place the pike on a platter with a large piece of butter and lemon juice
7. Put a lemon slice on each piece
8. Place paper thickly buttered over the pike
9. Finely chop 1 pound of fish forcemeat
10. Let 1 quarter-pound of butter melt in a bowl
11. Add the fish forcemeat to it and beat in 3 eggs
12. Add the yellow of half a lemon grated on a grater, nutmeg, and salt
13. Stir until very foamy
14. Place 2 buttered springforms on the platter where the pike lies
15. Fill with the fish forcemeat

title: Hefeklöße (Yeast Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 g yeast
- 250 g flour
- 1/8 l lukewarm whole milk
- 1 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon melted fat
- 1 tablespoon salt
- 3 l water

## Directions

1. Prepare a yeast mixture with yeast, one quarter of the flour, and milk; let rise
2. Beat the risen mixture with remaining ingredients until it becomes airy and bubbly
3. Form apple-sized dumplings and place on a flour-dusted board to rise again in a warm place
4. Cook the dumplings in steam or in boiling salted water by placing them in a colander set over boiling water or on a light cloth stretched over the pot
5. Test with a wooden stick to see if dumplings are cooked
6. Remove with a skimmer, tear slightly open with 2 forks, pour brown butter or fried bacon and onions over them as desired, and serve with stewed fruit

title: Hefepudding (Yeast Pudding)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g flour
- 15 g yeast
- 1/8 l whole milk
- 20 g beef fat or margarine
- 1 egg
- 2 cloves
- 40 g sugar
- 8 g salt
- 40 g large and small raisins
- 5 g margarine for the mould
- 5 g sugar for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Warm the milk and sift the flour; warm the flour as well
2. Crumble the yeast into the milk and stir
3. Sort and soak the raisins; crush the cloves; grease the mould or cloth and prepare a water pot for cooking
4. Melt 20 g fat
5. For the yeast piece: place 125 g flour in a bowl, make a well in the center, gradually stir in the yeast mixed with milk until a thick smooth batter forms; dust with flour, cover with a cloth and set in a warm place to rise to twice its height
6. Pour the melted but not hot fat into a bowl and mix with the other prepared ingredients except flour and yeast piece
7. When the yeast piece reaches the proper height, add it along with the remaining flour and mix well
8. Place in the mould or loosely bind in cloth and let rise another 1/4 hour
9. Place the sealed mould or cloth pudding in strongly boiling water and cook; place an inverted plate under the cloth
10. Maintain constant water level by adding boiling water as needed
11. After 1 hour, remove the pudding mould, dry it, let stand a moment, open it, place a plate on top, turn the mould over, lift it off and sprinkle the pudding with sugar; for cloth pudding, untie the cloth, place a dish on the pudding and turn over

title: Heilbronner Chokoladetorte (Heilbronn Chocolate Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1/2 pound blanched, finely ground almonds
- zest of 2 lemons, grated
- juice of 2 lemons
- 12 egg yolks
- snow of 8 egg whites
- 10 loth grated chocolate
- butter and sugar for the pan

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter until light and fluffy
2. Take 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar and an equal amount of blanched, finely ground almonds
3. Add the grated lemon zest and juice from 2 lemons
4. Beat 12 egg yolks into the mixture and stir everything together for half an hour
5. Beat the snow stiffly from 8 egg whites
6. Mix 10 loth grated chocolate with the snow
7. Fold this into the mass
8. Brush a cake pan with butter and sprinkle with sugar
9. Fill the mass into it
10. Bake the torte for one hour

title: Helle gebundene Suppe von Taube, Huhn oder Kalbfleisch (Light Bound Soup of Pigeon, Chicken or Veal)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 old pigeon or pigeon offal from 10 pigeons or chicken offal from 2-3 chickens or 375 g veal
- 1-1/2 l water
- 12 g salt
- 2 tablespoons soup vegetables
- 40 g flour
- 40 g fat (butter for the ill)
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon skim milk

## Directions

1. Preparation: The pigeon is prepared according to instructions and used whole or divided. It, as well as the pigeon offal, is cleaned, the stomachs are cleaned, their skin removed, the hearts and livers cleaned, everything is washed, briefly divided and put up with water and salt. The soup vegetables are cleaned, washed and cut up.
2. After skimming the meat, add the cleaned soup vegetables and let it cook slowly for 1-1/2 hours covered.
3. When the offal is soft, sweat flour with fat thoroughly, gradually pour the broth into the roux while stirring, let the soup cook until slimy, return the offal and soup vegetables, season with salt and add the egg yolk stirred with milk or 1 teaspoon chopped parsley.
4. Optionally, two cleaned asparagus pieces cut into small pieces can also be used.

title: Herb Sauce (Kräutersauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- chives
- chervil
- basil
- tarragon
- 2 handfuls dark grated bread
- 6 to 8 pounded juniper berries
- 1 loth sugar
- zest of 1 lemon, finely chopped
- 1 spoon French mustard
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop chives, chervil, basil and tarragon very finely together
2. Add 2 handfuls of dark grated bread, 6 to 8 pounded juniper berries, 1 loth sugar, finely chopped zest of 1 lemon, 1 spoon French mustard, salt and pepper

title: Heringe Zurechtzumachen und Einzulegen (Herrings Prepared and Preserved)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 herrings
- 1 l vinegar
- 100 g onion slices
- 4 spice portions
- 1 sour cucumber
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Soak the herrings. Boil the vinegar. Slice the onions and cucumber. Measure out the spices.
2. Wash the herrings and soak them in cold water for 12-24 hours depending on their firmness. Young Matjes herrings should not be soaked or at most for 10-15 minutes.
3. Place the herrings on a plate, break out the cheeks, cut a thin strip from the belly (which can be used for another herring dish and kept in vinegar until needed), remove the innards with a knife, pull off the back skin after making a straight cut along the back, and place the herrings in a pot.
4. Layer spice portions, onion slices and cucumber slices between the herrings.
5. Pour over the herrings a light vinegar that has been boiled and cooled, in which 5 g salt has been dissolved, and weight them lightly with a saucer so they are evenly covered with liquid.
6. Keep the roe of the herrings separately in a glass, pour vinegar over it and tie it shut; when serving, mix it with fresh vinegar and pour it over the herrings.

title: Heringe, Frische, zu Braten (Fresh Herring for Frying)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 fresh herrings
- 40 g salt
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 75 g flour
- 250 g frying fat
- 1 liter vinegar
- 50 g onion slices
- 1 spice dose
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. Scale the herrings, clean them, wash them and make light scratches a few times across the back
2. Salt them and let stand for 1/2 hour
3. Dry them and roll them evenly in flour

title: Heringskartoffeln (Herring Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg potatoes
- 1/2 herring or 5 herring bellies
- 25 g onions
- 25 g bacon, fat, or margarine
- 2 peppercorns
- 2 tbsp vinegar
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- 6 tbsp water

## Directions

1. Place the herring in cold water. Cut the bacon into small cubes. Cook the potatoes, peel them and cut into slices; peel the onions and cut into small cubes; mix water (in which you can previously boil out the herring bones), pepper and salt. Clean the herring, remove the bones, skin it and cut it into small cubes.
2. In a pot, if bacon is used, render it and shake the pot frequently; remove the cracklings with a spoon when they are golden brown. Pour the potato slices into the fat and let them fry quickly, turning them carefully frequently with a wide knife. When they are golden, add the onion and herring cubes and let everything cook for a few more minutes.

title: Heringskartoffeln (Herring Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Potatoes, sliced
- Herring, cleaned and diced
- Onions, peeled and diced
- Water (in which herring bones may be cooked)
- Pepper and salt
- Bacon (optional)

## Directions

1. If using bacon, render it in a pot and shake the pot frequently; remove the cracklings with a spoon when they turn golden brown.
2. Add potato slices to the fat and fry them quickly, turning them carefully with a broad knife frequently. When they turn yellow, add onion and herring pieces and let everything fry for a few more minutes while turning until golden brown.
3. Pour the potatoes into a bowl, pour the prepared liquid over them and let the dish covered rest in a warm place for 1/4 hour.
4. Sprinkle the hot bacon cracklings over the potatoes and serve.

title: Heringssalat (Herring Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg cooked potatoes
- 1 apple
- 1 herring
- 1 small onion
- 1/2 salt cucumber
- 3 tablespoons each vinegar and water
- 1 tablespoon oil or goose fat
- 1 pinch pepper
- 2-1/2 teaspoons sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Mix water, vinegar, sugar, oil, pepper, salt and one cooked, grated potato in a bowl to make a marinade.
2. Peel the onion and cut it into very small pieces or mince finely. Peel the apple, potatoes and salt cucumber and cut into uniform pieces; soak the herring, clean, skin, bone and cut finely; add everything to the marinade.
3. Mix the entire mass thoroughly with a wooden spoon and let it steep covered. Taste the salad and arrange it on a platter in a mound shape.

title: Heringssauce (Herring Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 herring
- 30 g flour
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 1/2 l meatball broth or broth
- 10 g onions
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Soak the herring, divide it, and finely chop one half; set the other half aside. Peel and chop the onion.
2. Heat the fat in an earthenware pot, add the onion and sweat it with the fat, then add the flour. Once the flour is cooked, pour in 1/2 l liquid and cook the sauce uncovered until thickened.
3. Add the chopped herring; the sauce should now only simmer.
4. Season with vinegar, pepper, and salt. If you want it very smooth, strain it through a sieve.
5. The sauce can be colored yellow with saffron or brown with food coloring, but use only a small knife point of either coloring agent.
6. The remaining herring half can be marinated or used for salad or herring potatoes.

title: Himbeer- oder Hohlbeerschaum (Raspberry or Bilberry Foam)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas raspberries
- sugar
- 12 egg whites
- blue sugar sand
- rosehips
- butter

## Directions

1. Press 1/2 maas of raspberries through a hair sieve using a pea-pusher.
2. Stir together as many spoonfuls of pulp with as many spoonfuls of sugar, mixing thoroughly.
3. Beat 12 egg whites into snow.
4. Mix the snow into the previous mixture.
5. Pour everything into a plate brushed with butter.
6. Shape it high and attractive.
7. Sprinkle blue sugar sand on top.
8. Garnish the plate with rosehips.
9. Treat the foam as indicated for apple foam.
10. It will be ready in a quarter hour.

title: Himbeeressig (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 kg (4 Pfd.) raspberries
- 1 l wine vinegar
- 1-1/2 kg (3 Pfd.) sugar
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Preparation: Crush the raspberries with a spoon, cover them for 12 hours with the wine vinegar, and strain through a cloth.
2. Preparation: Clarify the sugar, cook it into a syrup, cook it with the vinegar for another 20 minutes, and fill into bottles. Cork and seal the bottles, and the raspberry vinegar is mixed with fresh water or seltzer water for use.

title: Himbeeressig (Raspberry Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries (measured in Maas)
- authentic wine vinegar (equal measure to raspberries)
- sugar (1 pound per 1 Maas vinegar)

## Directions

1. Pour as many Maas of authentic wine vinegar as raspberries over the berries
2. Bind it up, place it in the cellar for 3 to 4 days and strain the clear liquid through a cloth
