3. Pour the vinegar with sugar into a pan (calculate 1 pound of pure sugar per 1 Maas vinegar)
4. Let it cook on the fire for a quarter hour
5. Skim the foam carefully
6. Pour it into a pot and let it stand overnight
7. The next day fill it into bottles and store it for use

title: Himbeergelée (Raspberry Jelly)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Carefully sort as many raspberries as you need to use
2. Tie them firmly in a pot and let it stand overnight
3. The next day place it in another pot or kettle with boiling water, cover it tightly so the steam cannot escape
4. Draw the clear juice through a fine sieve and mix sugar with it (for 1 schoppen juice count 3 quarter pounds sugar)
5. Let it cook together, skim diligently
6. To test if it is done, take a spoonful in a coffee cup and let it cool; if it is gelatinous, it is good
7. Store it in a sugar glass

title: Hippen mit Mandeln (Almond Wafers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- 1/4 pound sifted sugar
- 1/4 pound finely ground almonds
- 1 knife tip cinnamon
- grated zest of 1 lemon
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 3 loth flour

## Directions

1. Stir egg whites, sugar, almonds, and cinnamon together for a quarter hour
2. Mix in lemon zest, juice, and flour
3. Coat a black baking sheet with lard
4. Form wafers from the mixture
5. Bake until golden
6. While still warm, stretch them over a round piece of wood

title: Hippen ohne Mandeln (Wafers Without Almonds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loth flour
- wine or white beer
- 4 egg whites
- 3 loth ground sugar
- 1 quint cinnamon
- 1/2 nutmeg

## Directions

1. Stir flour smooth with wine or white beer
2. Add egg whites, sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg
3. Stir everything together thoroughly
4. Coat a wafer iron with lard and heat it
5. Pour a spoonful of batter into the iron
6. Bake until golden and immediately stretch over a round piece of wood while hot

title: Hirn (Brain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Brain
- Butter
- Onions
- Lemon
- Flour
- Jus
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Place the necessary amount of brain in lukewarm water and remove the membrane.
2. Make a jus: melt a piece of butter in a casserole.
3. Add chopped onions, lemon and lemon pulp and braise everything for a time.
4. Sprinkle flour, as much as you can hold between 3 fingers, and stir a few times.
5. Pour in a spoonful of jus.
6. Let it cook for a quarter hour.
7. Squeeze juice from half a lemon into it.
8. Strain through a fine sieve and pour back into the casserole.
9. Season with pepper and salt to taste.
10. Now add the brain to the sauce and let it cook in the sauce for a quarter hour.

title: Hirn in Kapernsauce (Brain in Caper Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Brain
- Water
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Onion
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Soak and clean the brain.
2. Let it set firm in equal parts water and vinegar.
3. For the sauce: place a piece of butter in a casserole.
4. Chop an onion with the zest of half a lemon and add to the butter.

title: Hirn in Kapernsauce (Brains in Caper Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Brains
- Water
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Onion
- Lemon (yellow part and juice)
- Flour (1 cooking spoon)
- Cold water
- Meat broth (1 ladle)
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Capers

## Directions

1. Soak and clean the brains.
2. Let them set firmly in equal parts water and vinegar.
3. In a casserole, add butter and place in the onion mixed with the yellow part of half a lemon; let it steam for a time.
4. Stir in one cooking spoon of flour, extinguish it with cold water, add one ladle of meat broth and let it cook for half an hour.
5. Add the brains, press in a little lemon juice and let it finish cooking.
6. Season to taste with salt, pepper, and nutmeg.

title: Hirnschnitten (Brain Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- brain
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- 2 eggs
- bread slices
- wheat meal
- egg white
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the brain
2. Steam the brain in a piece of butter with onions and parsley
3. Mix everything with 2 eggs
4. Spread the mixture on bread slices
5. Sprinkle wheat meal on top
6. Cover with egg white
7. Fry the slices in hot lard

title: Hirnsuppe (Brain Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 veal brains
- Onion
- Parsley
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place the veal brains in lukewarm water and remove the skin
2. Chop onion and parsley together and steam the brains with the chopped mixture in a casserole with butter for one quarter hour
3. Stir in half a tablespoon of flour to the brains without completely covering them
4. Pour in meat broth and bring to a boil
5. Season with salt and nutmeg

title: Hirschwildbret mit Sauerkraut (Venison with Sauerkraut)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Deer loin roast or another tender piece
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Lemons
- Juniper berries
- Wine
- Pork lard
- Sugar
- Sauerkraut
- Champagne or good wine
- Flour
- Sour cream
- Ground cinnamon
- Puff pastry
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Skin the deer meat, lard it, place it in an earthenware roasting dish, add sliced onions, bay leaves, lemons, and juniper berries.
2. Pour half a quart of wine over it, place the dish in an oven, and let the roast become half done.
3. In a casserole, make pork lard and 2 loths of sugar golden; let sauerkraut that has been firmly squeezed out steam in the casserole for half an hour, stirring often.
4. Pour a glass of champagne or other good wine over it, heat lard in a dish, let a handful of chopped onions become golden in it, sprinkle half a kitchen spoonful of flour into it and let it take color, pour this onto the sauerkraut along with 3 spoonfuls of sour cream.
5. Stir it together and let it cook until no more sauce is visible.
6. Place half of the sauerkraut on a platter, place the venison roast on it, cover with the remaining sauerkraut, sprinkle ground cinnamon on it.
7. Make a border around the platter with puff pastry, brush it with eggs, place the platter in the oven, and let the sauerkraut become beautifully golden brown.

title: Hirschziemer (Venison Saddle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison saddle
- Fat
- Roots of various kinds
- Wine
- Vinegar
- Jus
- Salt and pepper to taste
- 4 juniper berries
- Grated bread
- Sour cream
- Lemons

## Directions

1. Clean the saddle carefully and skin it beautifully; cut off the long bones and lard it nicely.
2. Place it in a roasting dish, heat a piece of fat in a pan, pour it over the saddle, add all kinds of roots, pour a glass of wine and a glass of vinegar with some jus, salt, and pepper to taste.
3. Place it in an oven and let it roast until beautifully golden.
4. Take 4 juniper berries and a handful of grated bread; each time the roast is basted, sprinkle some of this bread on it.
5. Finally, pour half a quart of sour cream over the saddle and let it become beautifully golden.
6. When serving, add finely chopped lemon on top, pass the sauce through a fine sieve, and serve it in a separate dish.

title: Hirse (Millet)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g millet
- 10 g butter
- 3/4 l skim milk
- 8 g salt

## Directions

1. Pick over the millet and rinse it three times with cold water, draining each time when hot.
2. Fry the millet with butter, pour the milk over it, salt the dish and let it swell while stirring frequently. Alternatively, you can swell the millet with water and 20 g butter and add 30 g brown butter.

title: Hirsebrei (Millet Porridge)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g millet
- water and salt
- 1/2 l skim milk
- 1 pinch salt
- 5 g fat
- 1 piece orange peel
- 20 g fat for pouring over
- 1 teaspoon sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Sort the millet and bring to a boil three times with cold water, pouring off the water each time when hot
2. After draining, brown the millet with 5 g fat, add the milk, salt and orange peel
3. Let the millet swell while stirring frequently; when done, serve in a bowl
4. Pour 20 g browned fat over it and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Hochwildbret mit englischer Sauce (High Game with English Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Game meat, preferably from the hind leg
- Vinegar for marinating
- Lard
- Onions
- Bacon
- Roots of various kinds
- Bay leaves
- 6 whole cloves
- Mace
- Whole white peppercorns
- Cubebs and cardamom totaling half a loth
- Wine
- Meat broth
- One quarter pound Nuremberg gingerbread
- Black bread
- Pork fat
- Sugar
- 6 to 8 mushrooms
- One spoonful capers
- One cleaned and finely ground truffle
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Marinate the game meat in vinegar for a few days beforehand.
2. Skin the meat well and lard it; pour hot lard over it.
3. Line the bottom of a casserole with onions, bacon, all kinds of roots, bay leaves, 6 whole cloves, mace, whole white peppercorns, and cubebs and cardamom (totaling half a loth).
4. Place all the game meat in the casserole, pour a glass of wine and a ladleful of meat broth over it, cover the casserole tightly (or better yet, seal it with paper), place it on coals, and let it steam slowly for 2 to 3 hours.
5. Do not salt it until serving time.
6. For the sauce: Rub one quarter pound Nuremberg gingerbread and the same amount of black bread on a grater.
7. Roast the grated mixture in a piece of pork fat along with a small piece of sugar until it looks brown like gingerbread.
8. Pour a quart of wine and 2 ladles full of good bouillon over it and let it cook together.
9. Place the game meat on a dish to keep warm, pass the juice through a fine sieve, and pour it into the sauce.
10. Put the meat and sauce into another casserole, cut 6 to 8 mushrooms into small pieces, add one spoonful of capers and one cleaned and finely ground truffle.
11. Let everything cook together for another quarter hour, and when serving, squeeze the juice of half a lemon over it and sprinkle the grated zest on the game.

title: Hochzeitkuchen (Wedding Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 1/2 pound butter
- 3 spoons beer yeast
- lukewarm milk
- 4 loths ground sugar
- 10 loths butter
- peeled apples
- raisins
- 1 loth blanched almonds
- 2 loths ground candied sugar
- eggs for brushing

## Directions

1. Stir 4 eggs and 1/2 pound butter until white
2. Stir 3 spoons beer yeast mixed with some lukewarm milk into the mixture
3. Add 4 loths ground sugar and work the dough together
4. Beat the dough for a quarter hour
5. Set aside to warm and let it half rise
6. Roll out the dough and cut 10 loths butter onto the cake
7. Fold it over like puff pastry, roll out 2 fingers thick
8. Arrange peeled apples and raisins on the edge of the cake
9. Fold the cake over 3 fingers wide
10. Coat a sheet of paper with butter, place the cake on it and let it rise
11. Brush with beaten eggs
12. Mix 1 loth blanched almonds with 2 loths ground candied sugar and scatter over the cake
13. Bake until beautiful yellow

title: Holländer Zwieback (Dutch Rusk)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 5 egg yolks
- coarsely powdered fennel for a kreuzer's worth
- 1/2 pound flour

## Directions

1. Stir 1 pound sugar and 5 egg yolks together for half an hour
2. Add fennel for a kreuzer's worth, coarsely powdered, and 1/2 pound flour
3. Mix everything together
4. Make finger-long little sticks from the dough on the noodle board and bake in the oven
5. Let them cool, cut them lengthwise in two, reheat them in the oven until golden, and serve with tea and coffee

title: Holländische Sauce (Dutch Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 g margarine
- 10 g flour
- 1/8 l vegetable or fish water
- 5 g sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon lemon juice
- salt to taste
- 1 egg yolk, beaten
- 1 tablespoon whole milk

## Directions

1. Make a white, smooth sauce from the margarine, flour, and liquid.
2. Beat in the sugar, lemon juice, salt, and a little nutmeg if desired, along with the beaten egg yolk, but do not let it boil.
3. The sauce can be mixed with mushrooms or capers as desired.
4. For sauce to chicken with rice or veal with rice, omit the lemon juice and sugar.
5. The sauce can be refined as desired by adding more eggs or butter beaten in at the end.

title: Hopfen mit Kalbshirn (Hop Shoots with Calf's Brain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- two to three handfuls hop shoots tied in bundles
- one handful salt
- four calf's brains
- half vinegar and half water
- parsley, one handful
- chervil, one handful
- chives
- shallot onions
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- meat broth
- juice of half a lemon
- three fingers-full flour

## Directions

1. Cook the hop shoots tied in bundles with one handful of salt until soft
2. Cook the four calf's brains in a pan with half vinegar and half water so that only a single broth covers them
3. Chop the parsley, chervil, chives, and shallot onions together
4. In a casserole with melted butter, braise the herbs and brain with salt, pepper, and nutmeg for a quarter hour
5. Pour in some meat broth and the juice of half a lemon
6. Sprinkle three fingers-full of flour over it and shake the whole together
7. When serving, place the brain in the middle of the plate, arrange the hop shoots around it, and pour the sauce over

title: Hosenbändel (Ribbon Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour (amount taken by 3 eggs)
- walnut-sized piece of butter
- salt
- hot lard
- chives, finely cut

## Directions

1. Take as much flour on the noodle board as 3 eggs will take, add walnut-sized piece of butter
2. Work the dough together well
3. Roll it out very thin
4. Cut half-finger-width noodles from it
5. Put part of them in a pan with boiling water in which a handful of salt is dissolved
6. Catch the noodles with a skimming spoon onto a plate
7. Let the remaining noodles become beautifully yellow in a small pan with hot lard
8. Pour them over the cooked noodles
9. Sprinkle finely cut chives on top

title: Huhn mit Reis (Chicken with Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 old chicken or 2 old partridges
- 125 g rice, washed several times
- 20 g butter
- 10 g flour
- 1 spice measure
- 1 onion
- 1 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Cut the raw chicken or partridges into pieces. Sauté the rice, butter and flour together, add the spice measure and onion, then cook in water until done.

title: Huhn, Gekochtes (Boiled Chicken)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 autumn chicken or 1 old chicken
- 56 g soup greens
- 15 g salt
- 1-1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Prepare the chicken and tie it with twine; place liver, heart and stomach inside.
2. Heat the water with soup greens and salt, add the chicken, cover and cook only until tender, which for a young chicken takes 1-1/4 to 1-1/2 hours.
3. Remove from the pot, let it drain and immediately place it briefly in cold water so it becomes white and cools. Then portion it as desired for various dishes.
4. The meat in smaller and larger pieces should immediately be marinated with broth, vinegar and oil or white wine depending on intended use, or serve the chicken as its own dish with sauce of choice.

title: Huhn, Junges, Geschmort (Braised Young Chicken)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 young chicken
- 10 g salt
- 50 g butter
- 1 bunch parsley
- 20 g bacon
- 1/8 l water or light broth
- 8 g potato starch
- 3 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Prepare, clean and dry the chicken. Rub the inside with salt, place parsley and 10 g butter inside, and tie the chicken into good shape. Tie a bacon strip over the breast or lard the chicken.
2. Heat butter in a braising pot, brown the chicken in it, cover and baste frequently, gradually adding 1/8 l water or light broth. Braise slowly for one hour.
3. Make the sauce creamy by cooking off the browned bits from the pot and stirring in potato starch mixed in cold water, then pass through a sieve.
4. Remove the bacon as desired, glaze the chicken with sauce and serve.

title: Häringsalat (Herring Salad)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large herrings
- 4 Borsdorfer apples
- 4 potatoes
- onions
- pepper
- milk from the herring
- Provençal oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the herrings, take out the bones, cut them into desired pieces.
2. Peel the apples, cut them into pieces the same size as the herring.
3. Peel the potatoes and cut them.
4. Stir the milk from the herring with Provençal oil, put all the cut ingredients in a salad bowl, pour the mixture over it, add some vinegar, mix it together and lay it on top of the salad.

title: Häringspastetchen (Herring Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- puff pastry
- cleaned herrings
- 1 handful of onions
- parsley
- 1 lemon (for juice and zest)
- butter
- beef broth
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Roll out the puff pastry the thickness of a knife back
2. Line small pastry molds with it (24 pieces for 12 tarts)
3. Brush 12 pieces with water and place the others upside down on top to form lids
4. Puncture lightly with a hot knife, brush with egg yolk and bake until golden
5. Chop the cleaned herrings not too finely
6. Chop together a handful of onions and parsley along with the juice and zest of a lemon
7. Steam the mixture with butter the size of a hen's egg in a casserole for some time
8. Add the herrings, turn several times and pour in a couple of tablespoons of beef broth
9. Fill each pastry with a teaspoon of the mixture and cover with the lid
10. Serve on a plate after the soup course

title: Im Dampf Aufgezogenes (Steamed Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 handfuls fine flour
- 1 schoppen milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons maraschino
- a handful of finely ground sugar
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 3 handfuls fine flour smooth with 1 schoppen milk and cook to a thick porridge, let it cool
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks, 4 tablespoons maraschino, a handful of finely ground sugar and the snow of 6 egg whites, stir the mass well together
3. Fill it into a dish suitable for table service, place this in a dish with boiling water, cover it tightly, place glowing coals on the lid and serve the dish after it has cooked for half an hour

title: Italienische Cotelettes (Italian Veal Cutlets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful white veal cutlets
- butter
- lemon juice
- 3 to 4 anchovies
- onion
- parsley
- lemon zest
- grated bread
- jus
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the sinew from the bottom of beautiful white veal cutlets, strip the bone clean, pound the meat, and tenderize it well
2. Shape it nicely, salt it lightly, and braise it in butter and lemon juice
3. Meanwhile, chop 3 to 4 anchovies finely with some onion and parsley and the zest of half a lemon
4. Toast a handful of grated bread with a piece of butter in a casserole until light golden
5. Mix the chopped ingredients into the bread
6. Spread this mixture knife-thick on the cutlets and pour jus over them

title: Italienischer Makaronipudding mit Schinken (Italian Macaroni Pudding with Ham)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound macaroni
- Salt water or meat broth
- Cold water
- 1/2 pound lean ham
- 1 handful grated Parmesan cheese
- 1/2 schoppen sour cream
- Grated nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cook 1/4 pound macaroni in salt water or meat broth
2. Draw it through cold water and let it drain
3. Mince 1/2 pound lean ham
4. Mix everything together in a bowl with 1 handful grated Parmesan cheese, 1/2 schoppen sour cream and grated nutmeg
5. Bind the mass in a wet cloth
6. Cook it for one hour in salt water
7. Arrange the pudding on a plate and brown it with wet butter

title: Italienischer Salat (Italian Salad)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 large herrings
- 6 to 8 potatoes
- 4 peeled Borsdorfer apples
- 2 large spoonfuls of capers
- 2 large spoonfuls of olives
- 4 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 clean, soft-boiled celery roots
- endive
- vinegar
- Provençal oil
- red beets

## Directions

1. Clean the herrings, remove the bones, cut them into pieces.
2. Cut 6 to 8 potatoes into square pieces and 4 peeled Borsdorfer apples into pieces.
3. Stone the olives.
4. Cut 2 clean, soft-boiled celery roots.
5. Combine all with endive in a bowl and dress with vinegar and Provençal oil.
6. Cut the hard-boiled eggs into long pieces, arrange the salad beautifully from the bowl with herring pieces and make a beautiful star of red beets in the middle; from hard-boiled eggs make the white and yolk into the star and from capers the green.

title: Johannisbeeren in Zucker (Currants in Sugar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Schöne reife Johannisbeeren (Beautiful ripe currants)
- Zucker (Sugar)
- Wasser (Water)

## Directions

1. Strip beautiful ripe currants from their stems (for 2 pounds of currants, use 1½ pounds of sugar by weight)
2. Place the sugar in a brass pan and add one bottle's measure of water
3. Cook until it gives off bubbles
4. Add the currants and let them cook, skimming off the foam carefully
5. Test a drop on a plate to see if they are done
6. If done, place them in a dish and store

title: Johannisbeergelée (Currant Jelly)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- currants
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make currant jelly in the same way as raspberry jelly

title: Johannisbeersaft (Currant Juice)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ripe red currants
- sugar (equal measure to currant juice in quarter-pounds)

## Directions

1. Pluck ripe red currants and place them in a firmly covered clean pot on hot ashes
2. Let it stand overnight and the next day pour off the clear liquid
3. For as many quarter-pounds of juice as you have, let the same amount of quarter-pounds of sugar cook together for a quarter hour
4. Let it cool, fill it into bottles and seal them tightly
5. This juice is very good for the sick to drink in water

title: Junge Hühner (Young Chickens)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- young chickens
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pierce the chickens and place them in cold water so they become nice and white
2. Dip one after another into boiling water
3. Pluck them clean so they do not get holes
4. Wash them once more in lukewarm water
5. Poke out the eyes and pull the skin away from the leg so it becomes nice and white
6. Loosen the breastbone
7. Rub them well with salt and pepper
8. Dress them nicely together
9. Cut the feet halfway off
10. Roast them on the spit or in an iron baking pan

title: Jungferncrême (Virgin Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas milk
- 6 to 8 bitter almond kernels
- a piece of cinnamon
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Put 1/2 maas of milk over the fire.
2. Pound 6 to 8 bitter almond kernels and add them along with a piece of cinnamon, 4 loth sugar with lemon zest rubbed in, into the milk.
3. Let everything cook together for a while.
4. Finally stir in the snow from 4 egg whites.
5. Once cooled, press the cream through a hair sieve into a bowl.
6. Place it on hot ashes.
7. Cover with a lid topped with weak coals and let the cream set.
8. Serve cold or warm.

title: Jägerschnitten (Hunter's Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 whole lemon, zested on a grater
- 4 loth blanched almonds, cut lengthwise
- 6 egg whites
- 6 loth fine flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Whisk together 1/2 pound sugar and 10 egg yolks in a bowl for half an hour
2. Add 1 whole lemon, grated on a box grater
3. Add 4 loth blanched almonds cut lengthwise
4. Fold in the stiffly beaten whites of 6 eggs and 6 loth fine flour
5. Butter a metal form and sprinkle a little sugar in it
6. Pour the mixture in and bake at gentle heat
7. Cut into slices only on the second day

title: Kachelmuß (Tile Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Schoppen milk
- 6 eggs
- 6 spoonfuls flour
- sugar
- cinnamon
- butter

## Directions

1. To 1 Schoppen milk take 6 eggs, 1 spoonful flour for each egg.
2. Stir smooth with the milk.
3. Add sugar and cinnamon as desired.
4. Stir everything well together.
5. Coat an earthenware tile with butter.
6. Fill the batter into it.
7. Place the tile in a baking oven or on coals.

title: Kachelmuß anderer Art (Tile Pudding, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Maas milk
- sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 spoonful flour
- cold milk
- 3 eggs
- butter
- a small handful raisins

## Directions

1. In a brass pan bring half a Maas milk with a piece of sugar rubbed with lemon peel to a boil.
2. Stir 1 spoonful flour smooth with cold milk.
3. Beat 3 eggs into it and stir into the milk.
4. Coat a tile with butter.
5. Pour the mixture with a small handful of raisins into it.
6. Bake in an oven or between coals.

title: Kaesekuchen (Cheese Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Cheese
- Currants
- Sugar
- Cinnamon
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Mix cheese with currants, sugar, cinnamon, and an egg, then distribute on the dough
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Kaffee (Coffee)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 15 g coffee beans
- 1 pinch of chicory
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. The coffee beans are ground, mixed with finely crumbled chicory (which can also be omitted) and poured into a porcelain pot.
2. Then 1/2 l boiling water is poured on. The coffee is allowed to steep covered for 20 minutes and is then poured through a coffee filter, which like the coffee pot is first rinsed with boiling water, into the prepared pot. If one wants to keep coffee hot or reheat it, it is placed covered in a porcelain vessel in hot water.

title: Kaffee brennen (Roasting Coffee)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- coffee beans
- 1 teaspoon powdered sugar per 1 kg (2 pounds) coffee

## Directions

1. Remove all impure beans from the coffee
2. Wash the coffee lukewarm
3. Drain on a sieve and dry on a cloth
4. Pour into the coffee roaster and roast for 5 minutes
5. Shake the roaster, then continue roasting while slowly turning
6. Every 5 minutes shake the coffee roaster, opening it out the window the second and third times so the loosening husks do not dirty the kitchen
7. Continue roasting until the coffee has a beautiful dark chestnut brown color, checking frequently
8. In the last minute, add 1 teaspoon powdered sugar per 1 kg (2 pounds) coffee, which coats the coffee with a light coating that holds the aroma in the bean
9. Pour out the coffee and stir frequently until it cools so it does not continue roasting and become too dark
10. Store the coffee in earthenware or tin containers on top, which should always be kept closed

title: Kaffeebereitung von Kathreiners Malzkaffee (Coffee Preparation with Kathreiners Malt Coffee)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 g Kathreiners malt coffee
- 1 l water
- 10 g coffee beans

## Directions

1. The malt coffee is ground and placed with cold water on the fire.
2. It must boil slowly covered for 5 minutes, then the ground bean coffee is poured over with the malt coffee decoction. The mixture must now stand for 15 minutes in a warm place and is then poured through a filter funnel with over-poured filter paper placed in it.

title: Kaiserkuchen (Emperor Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- four rolls (Mundbroten)
- water
- butter
- almonds
- sugar
- lemon
- egg yolks
- currants
- raisins
- egg whites
- cherries
- cherry stones
- wine
- flour
- cinnamon
- cloves

## Directions

1. Cut the crust from 4 rolls, soak the bread in water and press it out.
2. Braise it in a brass pan with a piece of butter until quite dry, place it in a bowl.
3. Stir in 10 lots of blanched, finely-pounded almonds, a quarter pound of sugar also finely-pounded, one lemon grated on a grater, and 6 egg yolks, mixing well together.
4. Add a quarter pound of currants and raisins along with the snow of egg whites to the mixture.
5. Bake the cake in a ring mold (Gogelhopfenmodel) brushed with butter and sprinkled with sugar until nicely golden.
6. For the cherry sauce: pound half a pound of cherries together with their stones in a mortar, let them cook for a quarter hour with a glass of water, pass them through a sieve with 1 pint of wine.
7. In another dish, stir 2 spoonfuls of flour smooth with wine, add the strained cherries, let all cook together with 4 lots of sugar, 2 knife-tips of cinnamon and the same amount of cloves.
8. Place the emperor cake on the plate and pour the sauce over it.

title: Kaiserpudding (Emperor Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound almonds
- egg white
- 1 lemon
- 1/4 pound citron and orange peel
- 1/2 pound butter
- 10 egg yolks
- 1 white Kreuzer roll
- 8 egg whites
- butter
- sugar
- 1-1/2 pints wine
- lemon peel
- cinnamon
- 3 egg yolks
- 3 fingers full flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Pound 1/2 pound almonds with egg white as finely as pap.
2. Chop the lemon peel, 1/4 pound citron and orange peel very finely.
3. Beat 1/2 pound butter very foamy, stir together the above with 10 egg yolks, a white Kreuzer roll rubbed on the grater and pressed through a sieve, and the snow beaten from 8 egg whites very well together, like an almond torte.
4. Brush a pudding mold with butter, sprinkle it with sugar, fill the mass into it, brush the mold's cover with butter, seal it tightly, place it in boiling water and cook it a good half hour.
5. For the sauce: cook 1-1/2 pints of wine with a little lemon peel and whole cinnamon, stir 3 egg yolks and 3 fingers full of flour smooth with cold water, pour the boiling wine to it, spurt everything with the chocolate spurtle until it rises over itself, arrange the pudding and pour the sauce through a sieve over it.

title: Kakao (Cocoa)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon cocoa
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon water
- 3/8 liter water or milk

## Directions

1. Mix 1 tablespoon cocoa and 1 teaspoon sugar with 1 tablespoon water
2. Boil with the entire liquid (3/8 liter water or milk per 1 tablespoon cocoa) for 5 minutes

title: Kakao (Cocoa)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon cocoa
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 tablespoon water
- 3/8 l water or milk

## Directions

1. 1 tablespoon cocoa and 1 teaspoon sugar are stirred with 1 tablespoon water and boiled with the whole amount of liquid (3/8 l water or milk per tablespoon cocoa) for 5 minutes.

title: Kalbenschnitzel, Paniert, Gebraten (Breaded and Fried Veal Cutlet)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 350 g veal loin
- 5 g salt
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 30 g grated bread roll
- 1 egg white
- 60 g butter or margarine

## Directions

1. Cut and strike small pieces of the knuckle until they are evenly sized and not too thin.
2. Lightly salt and pepper them and coat with bread crumbs.
3. Brown the fat in the pan and let it settle.
4. Lay the cutlets in the steaming fat.
5. Once they have a light brown crust on the bottom side, turn them and fry them slowly but quickly enough so the coating does not become soggy from the meat juices that develop.
6. Frying time: 6 minutes per 85 g.
7. The cutlets must be elastic and not become hard in the pan.
8. Do not cover the plated cutlets, otherwise the crust will become soft.

title: Kalbfleisch mit Trüffeln (Veal with Truffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A nice piece of veal from the leg
- Onions
- Lemon
- 3 truffles
- Flour

## Directions

1. Pound a nice piece of veal from the leg well and lard it.
2. Place it in a casserole, add finely cut onions and lemon, cover the casserole and let it braise for half an hour.
3. Cut the rough parts from 3 carefully cleaned truffles, pound one of them in a mortar, mix a little flour with it, cut the remaining 2 truffles into leaves.
4. Add everything to the meat and let it cook together for another quarter hour.

title: Kalbfleisch mit Trüffeln (Veal with Truffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful piece of veal from the hind leg
- Onions
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Pound a nice piece of veal from the hind leg well, lard it, and place it in a casserole.
2. Chop onions and lemon very finely and add to the casserole, cover it, and let it steam for half an hour.

title: Kalbfleischsuppe (Veal Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds veal, finely cut
- Butter
- Celery
- Parsnip
- Parsley
- Flour
- Meat broth
- Egg yolks
- Nutmeg
- Chives

## Directions

1. In a casserole, steam the finely cut veal very gently with a piece of butter, celery, parsnip, and parsley
2. Sprinkle 3 tablespoons of flour on top, pour 2 tablespoons of meat broth over it
3. Cook together for another half hour
4. Press through a hair sieve and arrange on 2 egg yolks in the soup tureen
5. Add some nutmeg and chives on top

title: Kalbsbrieschen mit Spargeln (Calf Thymus with Asparagus)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 calf thymus
- 50 asparagus
- salt
- lemon
- shallot onion
- butter
- nutmeg
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 cooking spoonful flour
- cold water
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean 2 to 3 thymus, blanch them slightly, place in cold water
2. Clean 50 asparagus, cook them soft in salt water, remove and cut into pieces
3. Finely chop half a lemon peel and a shallot onion and steam them with a piece of butter in a casserole
4. Cut the thymus, add them to the casserole, squeeze the juice of half a lemon over them
5. Add salt and nutmeg, let everything steam for another quarter hour
6. Mix 3 egg yolks with 1 cooking spoonful flour and cold water until smooth
7. Pour this with 1 spoonful of good meat broth to the thymus, cook a little longer
8. Arrange on a plate and serve the asparagus with it

title: Kalbsbrust, Geschmorte (Braised Calf's Breast)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g calf's breast
- 2 tablespoons beef fat
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 5 g salt
- 1/4 bay leaf
- 2 peppercorns
- 1/4 liter water

## Directions

1. The calf meat is washed, dried and beaten, and 1/4 liter water is brought to a boil.
2. The fat is rendered and heated to steaming; the prepared calf meat is rubbed with salt, dusted with flour, placed in the steaming fat and browned on all sides by turning; peppercorns and bay leaf are added; half of the boiling water is poured over it and the meat is covered and braised slowly. The meat is turned frequently and water is added at intervals. When the meat is tender, taste the sauce, remove the spices, arrange the meat and pour the sauce over it.

title: Kalbscarbonade auf Brabanter Art (Veal Chops Brabant Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- veal chop
- flour
- salt
- onion
- bay leaf
- thyme
- wine
- jus
- lemon

## Directions

1. Make a piece of butter golden in a casserole
2. Turn the chop in flour and lay it in the casserole with salt, an onion, bay leaf, and thyme
3. Fry golden on both sides
4. Pour a small glass of good wine and some jus over it
5. Let it cook together for a while
6. Before serving, squeeze the juice of half a lemon over the chop

title: Kalbsfrikassee (Calf's Fricassee)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g calf's breast
- 65 g pork, weighed
- 65 g beef, weighed
- 10 g salt
- 20 g flour
- 25 g margarine
- 1 spice measure
- 10 g soup greens
- 15 g onions
- 20 g dried mushrooms
- 12 g milk bread
- 1 knife point grated onions
- 1/2 egg
- 8 g salt and pepper
- 1 pinch white pepper
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1/2 liter water

## Directions

1. The calf's breast is beaten, cut into evenly-sized pieces (about 4 cm square; the cartilage remains in it) and poured over on a sieve. The dried mushrooms are soaked 2 hours beforehand in cold water.
2. Salted, dusted with flour, and covered with 20 g melted margarine or calf fat, the meat is braised until the flour is cooked through; then water is added to it, spices, soup greens and onions are tied in a cheesecloth bag, the bag is placed with the meat, everything is cooked until the meat is tender and it is skimmed once, then the mushrooms are added to the calf meat and allowed to swell slowly or use well-prepared fresh mushrooms such as champignons, porcini, or chanterelles. From the beef and pork, the soaked milk bread fried in 5 g margarine, the grated onions, 1/2 egg, pepper and salt, a dumpling dough is stirred together. With 2 ladles, evenly-shaped round dumplings are formed, which must cook in boiling salted water until done but not dry.

title: Kalbsgekröse in Meerrettigsauce (Veal Mesentery in Horseradish Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal mesentery (cleaned)
- Cold water
- Horseradish (half a root)
- Butter
- Flour (1 spoon)
- Sweet milk (half a schoppen)
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Set a cleaned veal mesentery with cold water and let it cook until soft.
2. Remove it and place it again in cold water, cut it into pieces of choice.
3. Grate half a root of horseradish on a grater.
4. Let a piece of butter in a casserole with one spoon of flour turn golden, add the horseradish, turn it a couple of times, smooth it with half a schoppen of sweet milk.
5. Add salt, nutmeg and the mesentery and let it come to a boil together.

title: Kalbshirn (Calf's Brain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's brain
- water
- vinegar
- onions
- lemon
- bay leaves
- chives
- butter
- salt

## Directions

1. Place the calf's brain in lukewarm water and remove the skin
2. Put it in a casserole with water, vinegar, onions, lemon, and bay leaves
3. Cook until as firm as hard-boiled eggs
4. Arrange on a plate
5. Chop chives very finely over it
6. Brown hot butter until light golden and pour over the brain
7. Add a little lemon juice
8. Salt to taste

title: Kalbshirnpastete (Calf Brain Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- short butter pastry
- butter

## Directions

1. Fold a short butter pastry 3 to 4 times on top of each other
2. Roll it out 2 fingers thick and place it on a black sheet
3. Turn a large plate onto the rolled out cake and cut around the plate with a hot knife
4. Cut a second cake around the plate that will be served at table
5. Poke the lid or the smaller cake with a hot knife and press the edge with a hot knife

title: Kalbshirnpastete (Calf's Brain Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- short pastry dough
- butter
- 4 calf brains
- water
- vinegar
- butter
- flour
- 3 shallot onions
- cold water
- light meat broth
- juice of half a lemon
- 1 glass white wine
- half a nutmeg
- nutmeg flower
- zest of half a lemon
- egg

## Directions

1. Fold the short pastry dough over itself 3 to 4 times, roll it out 2 fingers thick and place it on a black baking sheet
2. Place a large plate on the rolled-out dough and cut around it with a hot knife to remove the excess dough
3. Cut a second piece of dough around the plate that will be served at table
4. Pierce the lid or smaller piece of dough with a hot knife and press the edges together with a hot knife
5. Brush both pieces with egg and bake them on a black baking sheet in the oven until golden, then carefully remove the lid
6. Cook the 4 cleaned calf brains in a copper pan or casserole with half water and half vinegar until as firm as hard eggs
7. In a casserole, melt a piece of butter, sprinkle in a small spoonful of flour, add 3 finely chopped shallot onions, and braise this for a while in the butter
8. Stir it smooth with half a small ladle of cold water, pour in 1 ladle of light meat broth, add the juice of half a lemon, 1 glass of white wine, half a nutmeg, a knife-tip of nutmeg flower
9. Let everything cook together for a quarter hour, add the zest of half a lemon and let it cook a while longer
10. When ready to serve, put the pastry back in the oven to warm it, place it on a plate, fill it with the brain, cover with the lid and serve

title: Kalbskeule [Kalbsbraten] (Veal Knuckle Roast)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 kg (8 Pfd.) veal knuckle
- 125 g fat and margarine
- 50 g bacon
- 20 g salt
- 1/4 l milk, sour milk or water
- 6 g potato flour
- 3 tablespoons milk or water

## Directions

1. Remove the thick joint bone from the veal knuckle; cut off any loose pieces that would dry out in the oven and spoil the shape.
2. Strike the knuckle slowly on both sides with a damp mallet, then skin it and lard it, taking into account the direction of the muscle fibers so they lie properly on the larding needle.
3. Pour a ladle of boiling water over the meat, salt it on top and bottom, and in the pan cover with boiling brown margarine and fat.
4. Place some clean bacon rinds under the roast.
5. The oven should not be too hot for a veal knuckle, otherwise the roast will dry on the outside and remain raw inside.
6. Roasting time: for every half kilogram (1 Pfd.), allow 8 minutes for knuckles over 5 kg (10 Pfd.), and about 10 minutes per half kilogram for smaller ones.
7. During roasting, gradually pour water or milk into the pan, scraping the bottom, and every 5-10 minutes baste the roast with the liquid fat.
8. Complete the roast by mixing 3 tablespoons milk or water with potato flour, cook it out with the sauce, strain through a sieve, and coat the knuckle with the sauce.
9. If preferring well-done rather than pink meat, allow 10 minutes per half kilogram (1 Pfd.).

title: Kalbskopf in Madeirasauce (Calf's Head in Madeira Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's head
- bacon
- pork forcemeat
- truffles, finely crushed
- bread, soaked in water and pressed dry
- anchovies
- egg yolk
- salt
- nutmeg
- lard
- mushrooms
- morels
- consommé
- lemon juice
- red Burgundy wine, half a glass

## Directions

1. When the head is soft, cut it into square pieces the size of checkerboard squares.
2. Make small dumplings from bacon, pork forcemeat, finely crushed truffles, bread soaked in water and pressed dry, and anchovies; grind everything thoroughly together in a mortar or stone grinder.
3. Place this mixture in a bowl, beat in an egg yolk, add salt and nutmeg, knead everything together once more, and form into dumplings as small as possible.
4. Fry the dumplings in hot lard until golden.
5. On a platter, layer the calf's head pieces, then a layer of mushrooms, truffles, morels, and fried dumplings; continue layering until everything is arranged.
6. Heat consommé, add lemon juice, pour in half a glass of red Burgundy wine, and slowly pour this sauce over the calf's head using a brush.

title: Kalbskoteletten, Gehackt (Minced Veal Cutlets)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g veal
- 65 g beef kidney fat
- 8 g salt
- 1/2 egg
- 30 g grated bread roll
- 5 g flour
- 30 g margarine

## Directions

1. Finely chop or pass the meat and fat twice through the meat grinder.
2. Mix the mixture with 6 g salt and divide into 4 equal portions.
3. Using a damp knife on a plate, shape each portion into a cutlet form.
4. Coat with grated bread roll.
5. Fry in brown margarine over 7 minutes, turning three times, until slowly cooked through and nicely browned.
6. Cook a broth from the sinews, which can be mixed with a little meat extract to make a sauce if desired.
7. For the sauce: let the flour sweat gently in the frying fat, add the broth, season with salt, cook the sauce while stirring, strain through a sieve, and serve with the cutlets.
8. Do not cover the cutlets during sauce preparation, otherwise they will become soft.

title: Kalbsroulade (Veal Roulade)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal breast
- One pound of pork or sausage meat
- Bread crumbs (for one kreuzer)
- 2 to 3 onions
- Lard
- Half a lemon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Mace
- One egg
- Carrots
- Celery

## Directions

1. Remove all bones from the veal breast, cut away the cartilage at the bottom, break through the breast, open it out so it becomes nice and wide.
2. Wrap it in cloth, pound it firmly.
3. Make the following forcemeat: place one pound of pork or sausage meat in a bowl, rub in bread crumbs (for one kreuzer) on a grater, chop 2 to 3 onions, braise them in a piece of melted lard until light golden and add them to the meat.
4. Grate the zest of half a lemon and add it with salt, pepper, cloves and mace.
5. Mix everything with one egg, spread it on the breast, roll it together, sew it closed, bind it with twine.
6. Place it in a roasting pan or casserole with a piece of fat, onions, bay leaves, carrots and celery, cover the casserole and let it cook for one hour.
7. It can be served with jus or sauce.

title: Kalbsschlägel auf englische Art (Veal Leg English Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal leg
- Salt
- Pepper
- Thyme
- Basil
- Bay leaves
- Lime
- Shallot onions
- Vinegar
- Butter
- Flour
- Sour cream
- Capers
- Lime rind

## Directions

1. Place the veal leg in a large bowl, add salt, pepper, thyme, basil, bay leaves, the zest of a lime cut into leaves and a couple of shallot onions.
2. Pour vinegar over it and let it stand overnight.
3. The next day, place the leg with the vinegar in a roasting pan 2 hours before serving, add a piece of fresh butter and baste frequently with the roasting broth so it achieves a nice golden color.
4. Make the sauce as follows: in a casserole, brown a piece of butter with a spoonful of flour, add some chopped shallot onions, pour in a small ladle of jus and half a bottle of sour cream.
5. Let the sauce cook with capers, a little cut lime zest and the juice of a lime.
6. Place the leg on the plate and pour the sauce over it.

title: Kalbsschlägel mit Bechamel (Veal Leg with Béchamel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A nice white veal quarter
- Béchamel sauce
- Nutmeg
- Pepper
- Salt
- Glaze or consommé

## Directions

1. Treat a nice white veal quarter as in the previous recipe, roasting it 2 hours before serving.
2. Make a béchamel and place it in a casserole.
3. Before serving, remove the roast from the spit and place it on the dish in which it is to be served so the jus remains in it.
4. Cut a large round hole in the top of the leg, but not all the way through.
5. From the cut-out hole, remove the brown rind with small-finger-thick flesh so it fits exactly over the made opening.
6. Keep the béchamel warm over coals, cut out the white flesh from the hole into filets, removing the veins, and add them to the béchamel.
7. Add a little nutmeg, pepper, salt and a little glaze or consommé and stir this in a quick oven so it heats rapidly but does not boil.
8. Fill this into the hole made in the leg and cover it with the cut-off brown rind.
9. Then glaze the leg so that the made opening is visible.
10. This entire preparation must be done very quickly.

title: Kalbsschlägel mit sauerm Rahm (Veal Leg with Sour Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal leg
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Bay leaf
- Wine vinegar
- Bouillon
- Flour
- Half a measure of sour cream
- Capers
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Place the veal leg in a roasting pan with butter, salt, pepper, onions and bay leaf.
2. Pour a little wine vinegar over it, cook slowly, turning frequently.
3. Pour bouillon over it so it achieves a nice golden color.
4. Brown a little flour in butter and add it with half a measure of sour cream to the roast.
5. Let it cook slowly, add a little capers and the juice of a lemon.
6. Skim off the fat from the top and pour the sauce over it when the leg is on the plate.

title: Kalbsschmorbraten (Braised Veal Roast)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1 1/2 Pfd.) kidney piece
- 50 g fat or margarine
- 8 g salt
- 4 g potato flour
- 1/8 l water
- 4 tablespoons sour milk

## Directions

1. Brush the kidney piece clean in water, strike it, and salt it.
2. Pour boiling water over the meat, then drain it.
3. In browned fat, brown the meat and braise it gently in a braising pot, pouring and adding milk and water until tender.
4. Coat the meat with sauce, which is made silky after removing the roast by stirring potato flour mixed with a little water into the cooking liquid.

title: Kalbsvögel (Calf Birds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's leg meat
- butter
- onion, sliced
- bay leaves
- lemons
- gingerbread, brown
- tarragon or raspberry vinegar, half a glass
- sour cream, a few tablespoons
- salt

## Directions

1. Remove the bones from a calf's leg and cut the meat along the grain into pieces of desired size (usually 3 pieces per pound of meat).
2. Pound the pieces soft, skin and lard them, but not with pieces of bacon that are too long so they look attractive.
3. Place them in lukewarm water.
4. Put a piece of butter with sliced onion, bay leaves, and lemons in a casserole, add the birds, and braise for one hour.
5. Remove the birds, add a piece of brown gingerbread and half a glass of tarragon or raspberry vinegar to the sauce, return the birds, and cook for another quarter hour.
6. Pour in a few tablespoons of sour cream.
7. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve, return it to the casserole with the birds, bring to a boil again.
8. Add the finely chopped zest and juice of half a lemon to the sauce and salt to taste.
9. Note: When making this type of meat dish, it is good to add the skin and cut-out bones as they give the sauce a beautiful gloss.

title: Kalbszunge auf dem Rost gebraten (Calf's Tongue Grilled)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's tongues
- salt
- pepper
- egg
- bread crumbs
- melted butter

## Directions

1. Cook the tongues until soft
2. Remove the skin
3. Cut them in 2 parts
4. Rub them with salt and pepper
5. Turn them in beaten egg
6. Sprinkle with grated bread
7. Place on grill
8. Pour melted butter over them
9. Fry golden brown

title: Kalte Häringsauce (Cold Herring Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one fine herring, male
- a small piece of butter
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- the herring milk
- 2 spoonfuls of Provençal oil
- some vinegar
- pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the herring thoroughly, removing the bones
2. Pound the herring together with the small piece of butter and hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar until very fine
3. Pass everything through a hair sieve
4. Take the milk from the herring and stir it with 2 spoonfuls of Provençal oil, some vinegar, and pepper
5. Mix everything together and serve the sauce with beef

title: Kalte Kräutersauce (Cold Herb Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 handful of chives
- 1 handful of chervil
- equal amount of parsley
- 2 shallot onions
- 2 hard-boiled egg yolks
- vinegar
- oil
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Chop the chives very finely
2. Chop together the chervil, parsley, and shallot onions very finely
3. Stir the hard-boiled egg yolks smooth with vinegar and oil
4. Add salt and pepper to taste
5. Mix in the chopped herbs and onions
6. Place in a sauce bowl and serve with beef

title: Kalte Kräutersauce (Cold Herb Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One handful of chives
- One handful of chervil
- One handful of parsley
- Two shallot onions
- Two hard-boiled egg yolks
- Vinegar
- Oil
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut one handful of chives very finely
2. Mince one handful of chervil, one handful of parsley, and two shallot onions together finely
3. Mix two hard-boiled egg yolks with vinegar and oil until smooth
4. Season with salt and pepper
5. Add the minced herbs
6. Arrange in a sauce bowl and serve with beef

title: Kalte Senfsauce (Cold Mustard Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One handful of chervil
- One handful of parsley
- Some tarragon
- Six hard-boiled eggs
- Two tablespoons of mustard
- Salt
- Pepper
- Two tablespoons of Provençal oil
- Good herb vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash and clean one handful of chervil, one handful of parsley, and some tarragon
2. Mince everything together finely
3. Press the yolks of six hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve
4. Mix everything together in a bowl with two tablespoons of mustard
5. Add salt, pepper, two tablespoons of Provençal oil, and good herb vinegar
6. Blend everything together

title: Kaninchengericht (Rabbit Dish)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 rabbit
- 30 g margarine
- 3 tablespoons oil
- 20 g salt
- 1 teaspoon powdered marjoram and thyme
- 1 spice seasoning
- 20 g chopped onions
- 25 g flour
- 1 broth or meat extract broth
- 1/8 l apple wine
- 1 tablespoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Prepare the rabbit as directed for hare, then cut into 4 cm pieces.
2. Salt the rabbit meat and fry with the spice for 20 minutes in margarine and oil.
3. Remove the meat from the pan, sweat the onions with the flour in the butter until done, add broth and wine, season the sauce, let the meat warm through in it without boiling, and sprinkle parsley before serving.

title: Kapaun im Reis (Capon in Rice)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One capon
- Salt
- White pepper, pounded
- Bacon
- Lemon
- Half pound rice
- Good meat broth
- Butter
- Mace
- Lemon peel
- Four eggs
- One cooking spoon sour cream

## Directions

1. Prepare the capon for roasting as usual.
2. Place it in lukewarm water.
3. Rub with salt and pounded white pepper.
4. Bind it with bacon and lemon.
5. Braise it in a casserole until half done.
6. Cook half a pound rice with good meat broth.
7. Stir in a piece of butter, add mace and lemon peel.
8. Place half the rice in a bowl, add the capon, pour the other half over it.
9. Mix four eggs with one cooking spoon of sour cream, pour this over the rice.
10. Brush a ring mold with butter and place it over the dish.
11. Set in the oven until the rice has puffed up.

title: Kapaunenspargeln (Capon with Asparagus)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- capon
- bacon
- butter
- onions
- bay leaves
- carrots, yellow
- celery
- crayfish, 25
- asparagus
- salt
- flour
- egg yolks, 3
- water or meat stock
- mace

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the capon, cut off the head in such a way that the skin remains on the body, wrap it in bacon, place it with a piece of butter in a casserole.
2. Add onions, bay leaves, yellow carrots, and celery, cover the casserole, and braise the capon for half an hour.
3. Clean and cook the crayfish as usual, then pound them finely with a quarter pound of butter.
4. Braise them in a casserole until they foam, add 2 ladlefuls of water or meat stock, cook for a quarter hour, remove from heat, and skim off the crayfish butter into cold water.
5. Clean and bundle the asparagus, cook in salted water until tender.
6. For the sauce: melt half the crayfish butter, stir in one strong spoonful of flour, add 3 egg yolks, smooth with cold water, add capon broth and a pinch of mace.
7. Arrange the capon on a platter, lay the asparagus bundles around it, pour the crayfish sauce over it, brush the capon with the remaining crayfish butter, and place the crayfish tails on the capon's breast.

title: Kapuzinerauflauf (Capuchin Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 finely cut white rolls
- a glass of boiling wine
- 1 pound butter
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 4 rose apples
- 1/4 pound citrons and raisins
- all kinds of cooked fruits
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Moisten 2 finely cut white rolls with a glass of boiling wine
2. Stir 1 pound of butter until foamy and add the bread with 1/4 pound of sugar grated on a lemon, finely ground
3. Peel 4 rose apples, cut them into small thin slices
4. Add 1/4 pound of citrons and raisins and all kinds of cooked fruits
5. Add 6 egg yolks and finally fold in the snow from the egg whites
6. Mix the whole mass well together and bake the soufflé on a buttered baking sheet in the oven

title: Kapuzinerküchlein (Capuchin Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 3 spoonfuls beer yeast
- semi-sour cream
- 1/8 pound shelled almonds (some should be bitter)
- 1/4 pound butter
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 pound raisins (pitted)
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lard

## Directions

1. Make a pre-dough in a bowl with 2 pounds flour, 3 spoonfuls beer yeast, and semi-sour cream
2. Let it rise
3. Pound 1/8 pound shelled almonds (including some bitter ones) very finely
4. Cut 1/4 pound butter into the dough
5. Add the almonds, 3 eggs, and 1/4 pound pitted raisins
6. Work the dough with everything together and let it rise again in warmth
7. When ripe, roll it out to knife-back thickness
8. Cut triangular pieces with the pastry wheel
9. Fry the cakes in lard
10. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Karlsruher Muß (Karlsruhe Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 spoonfuls fine flour
- 8 Loth pounded sugar
- milk
- 7 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- half a Maas milk
- 7 egg whites
- coarsely pounded sugar
- finely chopped almonds

## Directions

1. In a brass pan stir 6 spoonfuls fine flour and 8 Loth pounded sugar smooth with milk.
2. Take 7 egg yolks and 1 lemon grated on a grater.
3. Pour half a Maas milk to it.
4. Cook a thick cream from it.
5. Remove from fire and stir until somewhat cooled.
6. Mix in the snow from 7 egg whites.
7. Pour all into a butter-coated plate.
8. Sprinkle coarsely pounded sugar and finely chopped almonds over it.
9. Let the pudding bake nicely. It is ready in half an hour.

title: Karlsruher Zwieback (Karlsruhe Rusk)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pounds flour
- 4 strong tablespoons beer yeast
- 3 schoppen warm milk
- 1 maaß warm milk
- 12 loth finely powdered sugar
- 4 loth melted butter
- 1 egg for brushing

## Directions

1. Take 4 pounds flour in a bowl with 4 strong tablespoons beer yeast and 3 schoppen warm milk and make a very strong dough
2. Set the dish in warmth and let the dough rise
3. Place it in another dish
4. Work together 1 maaß warm milk, 12 loth finely powdered sugar, and 4 loth melted butter into a stiff dough
5. Add the first-made, smaller dough to it
6. Work everything again until no flour is visible
7. Take it to the noodle board, work it another 3/4 hour or a whole hour
8. Set it in the bowl in warmth and let it rise beautifully (it needs 1.5 to 2 hours to be ripe)
9. Cover it so it does not form a skin
10. Take it to the noodle board again, make round balls with your hands
11. Roll them out finger-long, dust a black sheet with flour, place them side by side to the end, let the dough rise once more in warmth
12. Brush with beaten egg and bake in the oven until beautifully golden, but do not cut them until the third day
13. Toast the slices, made knife-back thick, on a black sheet in gentle warmth
14. The slices can be kept for a quarter year and are given with tea, coffee, and punch

title: Karmelitertorte (Carmelite Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds, cut lengthwise very finely
- 7 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound sifted sugar
- zest of 2 lemons
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1/4 pound flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Cut the blanched almonds lengthwise as finely as possible
2. Whip 7 egg yolks in a deep bowl into a stiff foam
3. Add 1/2 pound sifted sugar and stir vigorously for a quarter hour
4. Add the zest of 2 lemons, the juice of 1 lemon, 1/4 pound flour, and half of the almonds to the mixture
5. Immediately fill this into a torte form brushed with butter
6. Sprinkle the remaining almonds on top and bake until nicely golden yellow
7. Turn the torte out onto a plate

title: Karpfen in brauner Sauce (Carp in Brown Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- raspberry vinegar
- salt
- water
- 1 piece gingerbread
- a little black bread
- 2 chopped shallot onions
- whole cloves
- pepper
- brown jus
- 1/2 schoppen wine
- 1/2 lemon, yolk

## Directions

1. Scale the carp and carefully remove the entrails
2. Wash it with raspberry vinegar
3. Cut it into desired pieces
4. Boil it in salt water for half an hour
5. Make the sauce as follows: let gingerbread, a little black bread, 2 chopped shallot onions, whole cloves, and pepper cook together with some brown jus and 1/2 schoppen wine for half an hour
6. Push the sauce through a hair sieve, return it to the casserole
7. Add the carp with the juice and the pounded yolk of 1/2 lemon

title: Karpfen in eigener Sauce (Carp in Its Own Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 carp
- salt
- pepper
- 3 cleaned anchovies
- 1/2 lemon, yolk and marrow
- 1 tablespoon capers
- 1 piece butter
- grated bread
- 1/2 ladle jus

## Directions

1. Scale the carp, clean it, remove the entrails, cut it in 2 parts
2. Rub with salt and pepper (not very strong)
3. Pound 3 cleaned anchovies, the yolk and marrow of 1/2 lemon, and 1 tablespoon capers
4. Place the fish in a roasting pan with a piece of butter and the pounded mixture
5. Let it cook for half an hour
6. Sprinkle grated bread over it
7. Pour in 1/2 ladle of jus
8. Let everything cook together for another half hour
9. Place the fish on a plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Kartoffelbrei (Potato Puree)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white potatoes
- butter
- water
- milk

## Directions

1. Peel the white potatoes raw and place them in a casserole with a piece of butter
2. Add a ladle-full of water and let them cook soft
3. Rub them through a hair sieve
4. Put them back into a casserole, pour in half a measure of milk and a piece of butter
5. Stir the puree together for a quarter hour and place it back on coals so it becomes hot
6. When serving, let butter become hot in a small pan, arrange the potato puree on a plate and pour the butter over it

title: Kartoffelbrod (Potato Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of potatoes, cooked the day before
- butter
- a handful of onions
- chives
- parsley
- 6 eggs
- one quarter schoppen of sour cream
- salt
- nutmeg
- a handful of grated Parmesan, Emmental, or Swiss cheese
- two handfuls of grated bread

## Directions

1. Grate the potatoes that were cooked the day before
2. In a piece of butter, braise a handful of onions, chives, and parsley (finely cut)
3. Add to the grated potatoes with 6 eggs, one quarter schoppen of sour cream, salt, nutmeg, a handful of grated cheese, and two handfuls of grated bread
4. Stir everything together again
5. Bake on a baking sheet
6. Cut into slices and add to brown meat sauce soup or white glace soup

title: Kartoffelklösse (Potato Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) potatoes without skin, weighed
- 65 g flour
- 2 egg whites or 1 egg
- 1 knife point grated nutmeg
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 10 g salt
- 20 g lard
- 1 white roll
- 2 l water
- 20 g salt

## Directions

1. Peel and boil the potatoes in their skin, then remove the skin and grate them; if using leftover peeled potatoes, press them through a sieve. Cut the white roll into small cubes and fry in 20 g lard.
2. Whisk the egg and stir with salt, fat, and nutmeg; add the bread crumbs and 50 g flour and mix thoroughly. With floured hands, form equal dumplings, roll them in flour, and cook in boiling salted water for 15 minutes with the pot uncovered.
3. Lay them on a colander to drain and serve very hot, usually with stewed fruit or pears or with sauce.
4. Leftover dumplings can be sliced, fried in hot fat in a pan.

title: Kartoffelknopf mit Schinken (Potato Dumpling with Ham)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled cold potatoes
- 1/2 pound lean ham
- 1 soaked and pressed roll
- Finely minced onions
- 1/4 pound butter
- 5 to 6 eggs
- Chives
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Grate the boiled cold potatoes on a grater
2. Mince the lean ham finely
3. Soak and press the roll
4. Melt 1/4 pound butter in a bowl
5. Mix together the potatoes, ham, roll, finely minced onions, 5 to 6 eggs, chives, salt and nutmeg
6. Bind the mass tightly in a wet cloth
7. Cook it for one hour in salt water
8. Arrange the dumpling on a plate and brown it with butter and bread crumbs

title: Kartoffelküchlein (Potato Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes (boiled the day before)
- 1 measure flour
- good beer yeast
- milk
- 1 pound butter
- 1/2 schoppen thick sour cream
- chives
- salt
- nutmeg
- 3 egg yolks
- lard

## Directions

1. Grate potatoes that were boiled the day before
2. Make a pre-dough or yeast dough from 1 measure flour, good beer yeast, and milk, and let it stand in moderate warmth
3. Melt 1 pound butter in a bowl and add the potatoes
4. Pour in 1/2 schoppen thick sour cream and stir well together
5. Chives can be added; also salt and nutmeg
6. Knead everything together with the yeast dough and 3 egg yolks
7. When the dough is ripe, form cakes and fry in lard

title: Kartoffeln mit Häring (Potatoes with Herring)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- six hard-boiled eggs
- half a pint sour cream
- salt
- pepper
- herring
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook good potatoes until not very soft, peel them and cut them into small pieces
2. Press the yolks of six hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve into half a pint of sour cream
3. Add salt and pepper and stir everything together well
4. Butter a plate and layer it with the cut potatoes, then with some herring, then with some of the stirred eggs
5. Continue until all ingredients are on the plate
6. Place some butter on top and put the plate in a roasting pan so the dish becomes golden on top

title: Kartoffeln mit Schinken (Potatoes with Ham)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- butter
- milk
- half pound ham
- half a pint sour cream
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes raw and place them in a casserole with a piece of butter
2. Pour some milk over them and let them braise soft
3. Chop half a pound ham and add it to the potatoes
4. Add half a pint of sour cream, salt, and nutmeg
5. Shake everything together (but do not stir it) and let it finish cooking

title: Kartoffelpuffer (Potato Pancakes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) raw potatoes, peeled and weighed
- 5 g salt
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 teaspoon potato starch
- 1/8 l (scant) whole milk
- 1 egg white
- 50 g fat or 60 g margarine

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and place in water. Grate them very quickly on a grater into a bowl with some cold water to preserve the color. Pour the mass onto a cloth, press out the moisture lightly, and stir the paste with salt, potato starch, milk, and egg yolk. Fold in the stiff egg white foam.
2. Heat plenty of pork lard or rendered bacon with the cracklings removed, mixed fat, or margarine in the pan. Spoon the mass with a small ladle into the steaming fat and spread as thin as possible to about the size of a small plate. Turn the pancakes often, fry them brown and crispy, and arrange on a hot plate. The above ingredients should yield 6 pieces.

title: Kartoffelsalat (Potato Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg potatoes
- 1/8 l hot salt water
- 2 tablespoons oil or 25 g lard or goose fat
- 10 g onions
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- 3 tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Grate the onion and mince the parsley. Cook the potatoes, peel them and cut into slices.
2. Always dress the potato slices while lukewarm; for this purpose first pour hot salt water and vinegar over them and let rest covered on a warm spot for a while. Then mix them with oil and vinegar, salt, pepper, onion and parsley, which must be done carefully, and season well to taste.

title: Kartoffelsalat, Warmer (Warm Potato Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg potatoes
- 20 g flour
- 25 g lard
- 10 g onions
- 1/4 l skim milk or broth
- 5 g salt
- 1 pinch pepper
- 2 tablespoons vinegar

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes, peel them and cut into slices; mince the onions.
2. Cook the onions soft in the lard in an earthenware pot, sweat the flour in it, add milk or broth, stir frequently, season with pepper, salt and vinegar, cook slowly for 1/4 hour, add the warm potato slices in the sauce, bring to a boil and serve.

title: Kartoffelstückchen, Saure (Sour Potato Pieces)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg raw peeled potatoes
- 20 g bacon, fat or margarine
- 15 g onions
- 1/2 l broth or water
- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- 1/2 spice mixture
- 1/2 pinch pepper and salt
- 25 g sugar or syrup

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and cut them into quarters.
2. Bring them to a boil with cold water and drain. Render 20 g bacon or fat with the finely cut onions, cook with the heated liquid, vinegar, spices, salt and pepper, cook the potatoes through in it, season with sugar or syrup and, if necessary, as is the case with new potatoes, thicken with a flour roux made from 10 g fat and 10 g flour and bring to a boil.

title: Kartoffelsuppe (Potato Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g raw potatoes
- 2 tablespoons soup vegetables
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 5 g flour
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 seasoning portion
- 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley or celery or leek
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Preparation: The potatoes are peeled, the soup vegetables are cleaned and not cut too small.
2. Set the potatoes with cold water and when they boil, drain them. Then pour 1 l water over them, add spices, soup vegetables and salt; the whole must cook covered for 1 hour.
3. Meanwhile, in an earthenware pot, sweat fat with flour until it foams.
4. Remove the soup vegetables from the soup, press the remaining mass through a sieve and, while stirring with a wooden spoon, pour into the roux, with which it must cook in an open earthenware pot for at least 10 minutes.
5. Add chopped parsley (or celery greens) to the soup and season with a pinch of pepper.

title: Kartoffelsuppe (Potato Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- potatoes
- fat
- parsley
- celery
- chives
- onions
- butter (hen-egg size)
- 1 cooking spoonful of flour
- meat broth
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls of sour cream
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Peel and wash raw potatoes and braise them soft with a piece of fat in a pot
2. Braise parsley, celery and chives together soft and push through a sieve
3. Finely mince onion and parsley and braise with a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg in a pot
4. Add one cooking spoonful of flour and the potatoes
5. Pour meat broth over the whole thing and let it come to a boil again
6. Place two egg yolks with two spoonfuls of sour cream in the bowl and serve the soup; add nutmeg and salt

title: Kartoffelsuppe mit Fleisch (Potato Soup with Meat)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g beef
- 1/2 kg raw peeled potatoes
- 2 tablespoons soup vegetables
- 10 g salt
- 1 tablespoon parsley
- 1 pinch pepper
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Wash the beef, cut it into bite-sized cubes and place in an earthenware pot. Cut the potatoes into quarters, cut any cleaned vegetables such as beets, leeks, cabbage, celery into cubes and pour everything over with 1 l boiling water. Fill this into the pot with the meat using a skimming spoon, sprinkle salt over it and add 1 l water.
2. Cover the pot tightly; after 1/2 hour skim the soup and serve it, after 2-1/2 hours of slow cooking, mixed with fresh chopped parsley and seasoned with pepper.

title: Kartoffeltorte (Potato Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- good Gruber potatoes
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 3/4 pound potatoes
- 7 whole eggs
- 7 egg yolks
- 2 loth blanched, finely pounded bitter almonds
- 1 lemon, grated on a grater
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook good Gruber potatoes in steam the day before or roast them in the oven
2. Grate them (for 1/2 pound sugar, use 3/4 pound potatoes)
3. Stir them with the sugar, 7 whole eggs, and 7 egg yolks for half an hour
4. Add 2 loth blanched, finely pounded bitter almonds, a lemon grated on a grater, and the juice of 1/2 lemon
5. Brush a metal form with butter, dust with sugar, and bake the torte in it

title: Karviol (Cauliflower)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower florets
- salt water
- flour
- eggs
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Remove the florets from the stem, peel them and cook them soft in salted water
2. For the sauce: stir three spoonfuls of flour smooth in a casserole with cold water
3. Add three eggs and a small piece of butter, salt and nutmeg
4. Pour this sauce over the cauliflower

title: Karviol anderer Art (Cauliflower Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cauliflower
- salt water
- twenty-five crayfish
- butter
- flour
- water or meat broth
- eggs

## Directions

1. Prepare the cauliflower as in the previous recipe
2. Cook twenty-five crayfish, remove the tails and pound the rest in a mortar very fine with butter
3. Braise it in a casserole with a piece of butter until it throws up foam, then braise it with water or meat broth
4. Remove the crayfish butter, roast it in a casserole with two spoonfuls of flour
5. Extinguish it with cold water and let it cook up with meat broth
6. Beat two eggs in a bowl, pour some of the crayfish broth over them and pour everything over the cauliflower

title: Karviolsuppe (Cauliflower Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white cauliflower
- salt water
- flour
- butter
- cold water
- meat broth
- 6 eggs
- 1 cooking spoonful of flour
- 1.5 measures of milk
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut beautiful white cauliflower into florets, remove the green skin and cook it soft in salt water
2. Stir one cooking spoonful of flour into melted butter, turn it over a few times
3. Pour in first a glass of cold water, stir it smooth, then a cooking spoonful of meat broth
4. Add the cauliflower and let it cook slowly
5. For the Eierkäse: Beat 6 eggs into one cooking spoonful of flour in a small bowl and stir well together
6. Pour 1.5 measures of milk to it
7. Butter a dish, pour the mixture into it, and place it in boiling water or in the oven and let the mass become firm
8. When time to serve, place the cauliflower in the tureen, pour meat broth over it, use a spoon to cut dumplings from the Eierkäse and place them in the tureen; also add salt and nutmeg

title: Kindbetterinbrod (Childbed Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound finely ground almonds
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- 4 loth finely ground sugar
- 1/4 pound fine flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Work all ingredients together into a dough
2. Form thumb-sized sticks pointed at both ends
3. Make crosshatch marks with a knife
4. Place on a black baking sheet coated with lard
5. Brush with egg white
6. Sprinkle with coarsely ground sugar
7. Bake in the oven

title: Kirschen in Dunst eingekocht (Cherries Preserved in Steam)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful, ripe, not overripe cherries
- Pork or beef bladder

## Directions

1. Remove the stems from beautiful, ripe, not overripe cherries
2. Fill them into a pork or beef bladder that has been previously placed in lukewarm water, rubbed by hand, pulled through cold water, and pressed out firmly
3. Tie the bladder firmly at the top so no water can run in
4. Make a loop of twine for hanging
5. Push a stick through the loop and let the bladder cook very slowly in a pot or kettle filled with boiling water for a quarter hour
6. Leave the bladder in the kettle until the water has cooled
7. Let it drain and hang it in the air in a dry place
8. The cherries will keep well for 2 years and can be used for all baked goods and compotes

title: Kirschen in Essig (Cherries in Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Schöne zeitige Kirschen (Beautiful ripe cherries)
- Zucker (Sugar)
- Essig (Vinegar)
- Zimmt (Cinnamon)
- Nelken (Cloves)
- Kirschengeist (Cherry liqueur)

## Directions

1. Cut the stems of beautiful ripe cherries half off strongly
2. In a brass pan, cook 1/4 pound of sugar with as much vinegar as needed for the cherries, half a loth of crushed cinnamon, and some whole cloves together for a quarter hour
3. Remove from heat and let cool
4. When the vinegar is still lukewarm, pour it over the cherries, cover, and let stand for 2 days
5. On the third day, cook the vinegar again in the pan and pour it lukewarm over the cherries again
6. Draw a paper the size needed through cherry liqueur, place it on top of the dish, tie it shut, and store

title: Kirschen in Zucker (Cherries in Sugar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Nicht überreife Kirschen (Not overripe cherries)
- Zucker (Sugar)
- Wasser (Water)
- Kirschengeist or Arak (Cherry liqueur or arak)

## Directions

1. Cut the stems half off non-overripe cherries
2. In a brass pan, cook 3/4 pound of sugar with half a bottle's measure of water until it reaches the small thread stage
3. Place the cherries in the sugar and cook them as long as it takes to hard boil eggs
4. Lift the cherries out with a skimming spoon into the vessel in which they are to be stored
5. Reduce the syrup in the pan until thick
6. Pour the syrup over the cherries and let cool
7. Cover with stiff paper and pour some cherry liqueur or arak on top
8. Tie the dish shut, prick small holes in the cover with a fork, and store

title: Kirschen mit Salicyl (Cherries with Salicyl)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg (6 Pfd.) sour cherries
- 1-1/2 kg (3 Pfd.) granulated sugar
- 3 g salicyl

## Directions

1. Preparation: Pit very fleshy sour cherries. Mix them in a stone pot with the sugar and set aside covered and cool for one night.
2. The next day, dissolve the salicyl with 3 tablespoons of the juice, stir it into the cherries, and tie up the pot with parchment paper. Every other day the cherries must be stirred until the juice covers the fruit. Prepared this way, the compote keeps excellently.
3. More palatable and economical: take the necessary compote from the pot, dilute it slightly with water, poach the cherries, and cook the sauce with a little cornstarch to a thick consistency.

title: Kirschenkuchen (Cherry Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/2 pound ground sugar
- 1/2 pound ground almonds, unpeeled
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 1/4 pound citron and orange peel
- 2 rolls with crusts removed, soaked and pressed
- 1 loth ground cinnamon
- 1 quint ground cloves
- 4 egg whites for snow
- 1 pound pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Melt 1/2 pound butter in a bowl
2. Add 1/2 pound ground sugar, 1/2 pound ground unpeeled almonds, 12 egg yolks, and 6 egg whites
3. Stir everything for 3/4 hour
4. When thick, add 1/4 pound citron and orange peel, not too finely chopped
5. Add 2 rolls with crusts removed, soaked and pressed
6. Add 1 loth ground cinnamon and 1 quint ground cloves
7. Stir everything again
8. Beat snow from 4 egg whites and fold in
9. Press 1 pound pitted cherries gently into the cake with a spoon just before baking
10. Bake in a pan coated with butter until beautiful yellow

title: Kirschenkuchen anderer Art (Cherry Cake, Another Preparation)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 schoppen milk
- semolina flour
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound ground sugar
- 1/2 lemon
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 1 pound cherries

## Directions

1. Cook 3 schoppen milk and semolina flour together in a brass pan to make a thick paste
2. Pour into a bowl and cut in 1/4 pound butter
3. Add 1/4 pound ground sugar and 1/2 lemon rubbed on a grater
4. Add 6 egg yolks and stir everything strongly for half an hour
5. Mix in the snow from 6 egg whites
