6. Finally add 1 pound pitted cherries
7. Bake in a pan thickly coated with butter until beautiful yellow in the oven

title: Kirschenkuchen mit Hellenenmasse (Cherry Cake with Hellenenmasse)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 pound flour
- 9 egg whites
- 1 pound pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter until creamy
2. Stir in 9 egg yolks and the yellow of 1/2 lemon rubbed on sugar
3. Stir in 1/2 pound flour and the snow from 9 egg whites
4. Coat a plafond with butter and put the mass into it
5. Lay 1 pound pitted cherries on top and press gently into the mass with a finger
6. Bake in a roasting oven

title: Kirschenkuchen mit Schwarzbrod (Cherry Cake with Black Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 loths grated black bread
- 1 glass good, old wine
- 1 whole nutmeg
- 1 loth ground cinnamon
- 1 quint ground cloves
- 2 knife tips ground mace
- 1 lemon peel
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound ground almonds
- 12 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- 1 pound black pitted cherries

## Directions

1. Moisten 16 loths grated black bread with 1 glass good, old wine
2. Grate 1 whole nutmeg over it
3. Add 1 loth ground cinnamon, 1 quint ground cloves, 2 knife tips ground mace
4. Add the finely chopped peel of 1 lemon
5. Add 1/2 pound sugar and 1/2 pound ground almonds
6. Add 12 egg yolks and stir everything together for half an hour
7. Mix in the snow from 6 egg whites last
8. Add 1 pound black pitted cherries
9. Bake in a pan coated with butter in the oven

title: Kirschenlatwerge (Cherry Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds ripe cherries
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- half a Schoppen water
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Pit 2 pounds of ripe cherries
2. Let them sweat in a ceramic pot for a quarter hour
3. Force them through a hair sieve
4. Take 1.5 pounds of sugar in a casserole and pour half a Schoppen of water over it
5. Cook it until it reaches the largest thread stage
6. Pour the cherry pulp into it
7. Cook it with 3 knife-tips full of cinnamon until thick (it must be cooked very carefully as it burns easily)
8. Store the paste in a porcelain, bound pot

title: Kirschenpudding (Cherry Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 white rolls
- 1/4 pound blanched almonds
- egg white
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound pounded sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound pitted cherries
- 6 egg whites
- 1 orange
- 1 pint sweet wine
- 1 pint milk
- 1 small glass cherry brandy
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Cut the rind from 3 white rolls, soak them, press them out, pound 1/4 pound blanched almonds with egg white.
2. Beat 1/4 pound butter foamy, add 1/4 pound pounded sugar, the rolls, 6 egg yolks, 1/2 pound pitted cherries and the snow beaten from 6 egg whites, stir everything thoroughly together.
3. Fill the mass into a damp cloth, tie it closed firmly, cook the pudding one hour in boiling water.
4. For the orange sauce: rub an orange over sugar, peel away the white skin, cut the flesh into small pieces, let it cook in a casserole with 1 pint of sweet wine and the grated sugar for a time, then add 1 pint of milk, let it cook again, add 1 small glass full of cherry brandy and 2 egg yolks, press the sauce through a sieve and pour it over the pudding.

title: Kirschenwasser für Kinder (Cherry Water for Children)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Maas cherries
- 1 Maas water
- 1 piece cinnamon
- peel of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Pound 1/2 Maas cherries very well in the mortar
2. Let them cook with 1 Maas water, 1 piece cinnamon and the peel of 1/2 lemon for half an hour
3. Pour the water through a hair sieve and give it cold to drink

title: Kirschkompott (Cherry Compote)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g sour cherries
- 40 g sugar
- 2 g starch flour
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Wash the cherries, remove the stems, pit them with a new hairpin. Half of the pits are crushed, bound in a rinsed muslin bag and the cherries are tossed with sugar. Dissolve the starch flour.
2. Bring the prepared cherries to the heat with the pits and simmer slowly soft covered; then remove the muslin bag, make the sauce silky with the dissolved starch flour, shake the compote a little so that the cherries fill, and arrange it.

title: Kirschkuchen (Cherry Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Cherries
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Distribute cherries, sugar, and cinnamon on top
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Kleine Butterbiscuits (Small Butter Biscuits)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound fresh butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1/2 lemon
- 6 egg whites
- 6 spoonfuls fine flour

## Directions

1. Stir the fresh butter in a bowl into foam
2. Gradually stir in the 6 egg yolks, 1/4 pound finely sifted sugar, and the zest of half a lemon (grated on a grater)
3. Mix the stiff snow from 6 egg whites with 6 spoonfuls of fine flour and fold into the mixture
4. Mix everything well together
5. Fill the dough into finger-long and 2 finger-wide capsules made of stiff paper, moistened inside with melted butter
6. Bake in the oven

title: Kleine Butterbiscuits anderer Art (Small Butter Biscuits, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 6 eggs
- 1/2 pound finely pounded sugar
- raisins (optional)
- 6 Loth flour

## Directions

1. Stir the butter in a bowl until foamy
2. Stir in the eggs one after another
3. Stir in 1/2 pound finely pounded sugar
4. If desired, add a small handful of raisins
5. Stir in 6 Loth flour
6. Fill the mass into small greased metal molds
7. Bake in the oven

title: Kleine Tabaksrollen (Small Tobacco Rolls)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound flour
- 4 lots ground almonds
- 4 lots finely ground sugar
- 4 lots melted butter
- Half glass wine
- The cream from half a maa sour milk

## Directions

1. Make a dough from half pound flour, 4 lots ground almonds, 4 lots finely ground sugar, 4 lots melted butter, half glass wine and the cream from half a maa sour milk
2. Roll out to knife back thickness
3. Roll strips onto finger-thick and finger-long wooden sticks that are buttered
4. Tie with thread
5. Bake beautifully yellow in hot lard and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Klopse, Königsberger (Königsberg Meatballs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 g ground beef
- 60 g ground pork
- 1 teaspoon melted fat
- 1 egg white or egg yolk
- 1/2 roll
- 1 tablespoon water
- 1 pinch salt and pepper
- 1 knife point grated onions
- 1 l water
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Set water with salt to boil for cooking the meatballs.
2. Cut the roll into cubes, soak, and squeeze out.

title: Kofern (Kofern Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 loth butter
- 4 loth sugar
- 1/2 lemon grated on a grater
- 1 whole egg
- 8 loth fine flour
- 1 spoon sweet cream
- 1/2 loth bitter orange peel
- cinnamon and cloves to taste

## Directions

1. Stir all ingredients together until very smooth
2. Coat a kofern iron with lard and heat it
3. Pour a spoonful of batter into the iron
4. Close the iron tightly
5. Bake and press onto a round piece of wood while still hot

title: Kohl oder Wirsing (Cabbage or Savoy Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage or savoy cabbage
- salt water
- onions
- parsley
- butter

## Directions

1. Clean the cabbage, cut off the ribs
2. Cook it soft in salted water
3. Strain it in a colander so the water runs off
4. When on the plate, finish it with onions and parsley which have been braised with a piece of butter

title: Kohlrabi (Kohlrabi)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 8-15 kohlrabi, depending on size
- 10 g salt
- 1 l water for blanching
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 1/8 l water
- 1 pinch salt
- 8 g flour
- 1 pinch pepper
- Kohlrabi greens

## Directions

1. Set 1 l water with 10 g salt to boil; wash the young greens of the kohlrabi; peel the bulbs and cut into uniform slices.
2. Add the kohlrabi slices to the boiling salt water, blanch uncovered, drain, cook soft in a pot with 1/8 l water, salt and 3/4 of the fat. Blanch the young greens also, drain and mince finely. Sweat the remaining fat on a saucer with the flour, slowly stir three tablespoons kohlrabi broth to it; bring the flour roux, kohlrabi slices and greens back to a boil together and season with pepper before serving.

title: Kohlrüben (Rutabaga)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1-1/2 lbs) rutabaga
- 10 g salt
- 1-1/2 l water
- 1/4 l hot broth
- 1/2 spice dose
- 1 sprig marjoram
- 5 g sugar
- salt and pepper to taste
- 10 g fat or margarine
- 5 g flour

## Directions

1. Scrub the rutabagas in water, peel them, and cut into uniform strips.
2. Blanch in boiling salted water uncovered, then drain thoroughly.
3. Cook the rutabagas covered with broth, spice dose, marjoram, sugar, salt and pepper until soft.
4. Make a roux with the fat and flour, cook it with the vegetable broth, and thoroughly shake together with the vegetables.

title: Krachtorte (Crack Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound almonds
- 1 pound refined sugar
- almond oil
- 1 schoppen cream
- 3 loth vanilla sugar

## Directions

1. Blanch 1 pound almonds, peel them, cut them finely and lengthwise, and roast them with 1 pound refined sugar in a brass pan under constant stirring until the sugar becomes yellow
2. Brush a melon mold with almond oil
3. Press the seams well with some of the roasted almonds
4. Warm the rest and fill the mold completely with them
5. When the torte has cooled, turn it upright onto a plate so that the opening faces upward
6. Whip 1 schoppen cream in a bowl with a stiff whisk until it resembles egg snow
7. Let it drain a little on a hair sieve and mix in 3 loth vanilla sugar
8. Pour this sauce over the torte

title: Kraftsuppe für Kranke (Strengthening Broth for the Ill)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one old, clean chicken
- 2 Maas meat broth
- 1/4 pound blanched, finely ground almonds

## Directions

1. Take an old, carefully cleaned and washed chicken and braise it until half-cooked.
2. Pound it in a mortar together with the bones very finely.
3. Let it cook in a saucepan for one hour, gradually adding 2 Maas meat broth.
4. Press it through a clean cloth.
5. Let it come to a boil again with 1/4 pound blanched, finely ground almonds.
6. Serve it over toasted bread slices.

title: Krampfstillendes Getränk (Cramp-Relieving Drink)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- a handful of carefully picked and washed barley
- Scorzonera
- 2 Loth grated hartshorn
- 1 Loth chicory root
- 3 Maas water
- sugar (optional)

## Directions

1. Cook a handful of carefully picked and washed barley, Scorzonera, 2 Loth grated hartshorn and 1 Loth chicory root with 3 Maas water until 2 Maas remain
2. Sugar may be cooked in if desired
3. Give it cold to drink as thirst dictates

title: Krapfen von Lebkuchen (Cakes of Gingerbread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound grated brown gingerbread
- 2 loth finely pounded sugar
- 3 spoonfuls honey
- some fine spices
- 2 loth grated almonds
- 1 egg
- 1 egg white

## Directions

1. From 1/4 pound grated brown gingerbread, 2 loth finely pounded sugar, 3 spoonfuls honey, some fine spices, 2 loth grated almonds, 1 egg, and 1 egg white, make a firm dough on the noodle board
2. Roll it out
3. Cut it out with a jagged mold
4. Lay the cut pieces on a black sheet
5. Bake them at mild heat in the oven so that they remain still white, almost only dried

title: Krautsalat (Cabbage Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g white or red cabbage, cut
- 1-1/2 l water
- 5 g salt
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons oil
- 1 pinch sugar
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 5 g salt
- 1 pinch white pepper

## Directions

1. Blanch the finely cut cabbage in boiling water to which salt and 1 tablespoon vinegar has been added, using a colander, and let it drain.
2. Mix together oil, sugar, 1 tablespoon vinegar, salt and pepper.
3. Pour the sauce over the cabbage and mix thoroughly.
4. Serve cold with goose roast, etc.

title: Krautwürste (Cabbage Rolls)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage leaves
- butter
- 1 roll soaked and pressed out
- onion and parsley
- 1/2 pound finely chopped beef
- 3 to 4 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the ribs out of the cabbage leaves
2. Cover the leaves with boiling water and blanch them
3. Let them drain
4. In a piece of butter, steam together a grated, soaked, pressed-out roll, onion and parsley, along with 1/2 pound finely chopped beef for half an hour
5. Add 3 to 4 eggs, salt, and nutmeg
6. Coat the cabbage leaves with this filling
7. Roll them together like sausages
8. Fry them until golden in a frying pan with a piece of fat
9. Pour meat broth over them
10. Cook them up

title: Krebsdampfnudeln (Crayfish Steamed Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine flour
- 3 spoonfuls of good beer yeast
- 1 schoppen of lukewarm cream
- 1/2 pound crayfish butter
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 loth of ground sugar
- 1 knife-tip of salt
- 1 schoppen of milk
- 1/2 maas of milk
- zest of 1 lemon
- 1 piece of whole cinnamon
- 2 loth of sugar
- 3 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Make a dough with 2 pounds fine flour, 3 spoonfuls of good beer yeast, and 1 schoppen of lukewarm cream, and let it rise in warmth
2. Prepare 1/2 pound crayfish butter as previously described
3. Stir 1/4 pound of it in a bowl with 3 egg yolks, 2 loth of ground sugar, and 1 knife-tip of salt until light
4. Add the prepared dough with the flour to the crayfish butter and make a good dough
5. Beat it for a quarter hour, set it in warmth and let it rise
6. In a steamed noodle dish, melt the remaining 1/4 pound crayfish butter, pour in 1 schoppen of milk, place the noodles in it, cover and bake until done
7. For the sauce: cook together 1/2 maas of milk, zest of 1 lemon, 1 piece of whole cinnamon, and 2 loth of sugar
8. Beat 3 egg yolks in a pot and stir smooth with 3 finger-widths of flour and cold milk
9. Gradually pour the boiling milk to it and let everything boil together once more
10. Strain through a fine sieve and serve separately
11. Arrange the steamed noodles on a platter

title: Krebsküchlein (Crayfish Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- crayfish
- salt water
- butter
- water
- bread
- milk
- salt
- nutmeg flower
- 3 eggs
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Wash the crayfish and kill them by removing the middle of the 5 tail fins and the attached thread
2. Boil them in salted water
3. Break out the tail and remove the shells
4. Take out the gall from the top of the body
5. Pound the remainder with half a pound of butter in a mortar
6. Steam the pounded mixture in a casserole until it foams
7. Pour water on it and cook for a quarter hour
8. Pour the butter onto cold water and when firm, beat it foamy in a bowl
9. Grate 2 pieces of bread at the grater, soak in milk, press out firmly, and add to the butter
10. Add salt and nutmeg flower
11. Mince the tails and claws finely, add to the mass, and bind with 3 eggs
12. Make round cakes from this dough and fry them in lard

title: Krebspudding (Crayfish Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 crayfish
- 1/4 pound butter
- Water or meat broth
- 2 soaked and pressed rolls
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 grated nutmeg
- Salt
- Snow from 5 egg whites
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- Crayfish broth
- 1 knife tip of nutmeg flower
- 1 spoonful sour cream
- 2 egg yolks (for sauce)

## Directions

1. Remove the gall and tails from 25 crayfish
2. Pound the tails with butter very finely
3. Steam the pounded mixture with 1/4 pound butter in a casserole until it foams
4. Add water or meat broth
5. Remove the crayfish butter from the top
6. Pour it into cold water and stir until white in a bowl
7. Take half the crayfish butter and mix with 2 soaked and pressed rolls, 5 egg yolks, 1 grated nutmeg, salt and the snow from 5 egg whites
8. Place the crayfish tails on a wet cloth
9. Fill the mass on top
10. Bind the cloth and cook the pudding in boiling water for one hour
11. For the sauce: put the second half of the crayfish butter with 1 cooking spoon flour in a casserole
12. Turn it several times in the butter
13. Rinse with water and fill with meat broth
14. Add some crayfish broth, 1 knife tip of nutmeg flower, salt and 1 spoonful sour cream
15. Bring to a boil together
16. When ready to serve, stir in 2 egg yolks and pour the sauce over the pudding

title: Krebswürste (Crayfish Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Small cooked crayfish
- Crayfish shells
- Half a loaf of bread without crust
- Sweet cream
- Calf sweetbread
- 2 calf udders
- Basil
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Small onion
- Egg yolks
- Pepper
- Salt
- Sausage casings
- Milk

## Directions

1. Remove the tails from the boiled crayfish and make crayfish butter from the shells
2. Cut half a loaf of bread into small pieces and soak it in sweet cream for one hour
3. Blanch the crayfish tails, calf sweetbread, and calf udders finely together and add to the soaked bread
4. Add a little basil, thyme, marjoram, and a small finely chopped onion that has been cooked in crayfish butter
5. When the crayfish butter is completely cold, add some egg yolks, a little pepper, and salt
6. Mix everything well together
7. Fill the casings using a sausage maker
8. Slowly bring the sausages to a boil in hot milk in a pot
9. Half an hour before serving, brush paper with crayfish butter and fry the sausages on a grate

title: Kräftiges Muß für Schwache (Strengthening Pudding for the Weak)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- milk bread or roll slices, baked
- cinnamon
- rose water
- butter

## Directions

1. Cook baked milk bread or roll slices with cinnamon, rose water and a small piece of butter.
2. Stir smooth with rose water.
3. Sprinkle cinnamon over when the pudding is arranged.

title: Kräutersauce (Herb Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- chives
- chervil
- basil
- tarragon
- 2 handfuls of grated black bread
- 6 to 8 crushed juniper berries
- 1 loth of sugar
- the finely minced zest of one lemon
- one spoonful of French mustard
- salt
- pepper
- 2 spoonfuls of Provençal oil
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Mince chives, chervil, basil, and tarragon together very finely
2. Mix in the grated black bread, crushed juniper berries, sugar, minced lemon zest, mustard, salt, and pepper
3. Blend everything together with 2 spoonfuls of Provençal oil and vinegar
4. Serve with fish, pickled meat, or smoked beef

title: Kräutersuppe (Herb Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Chervil
- Parsley
- Sorrel
- Garden cress
- Chives
- A piece of butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Meat broth
- Toasted bread slices
- Three tablespoons of sour cream
- Two egg yolks

## Directions

1. Wash chervil, parsley, sorrel, garden cress, and chives thoroughly and finely chop after pressing out water
2. Braise everything together with a piece of butter in a casserole for a quarter hour
3. Add one tablespoon of flour and pour in meat broth
4. Let everything cook together for another quarter hour
5. Arrange over toasted bread slices
6. Stir together three tablespoons of sour cream with two egg yolks and add to the soup

title: Kräutersuppe (Herb Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- chervil
- parsley
- sorrel
- garden cress
- chives
- butter
- 1 cooking spoonful of flour
- meat broth
- fried bread rolls
- 3 spoonfuls of sour cream
- 2 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Wash chervil, parsley, sorrel, garden cress and chives clean and after squeezing out water, mince finely
2. Braise with a piece of butter in a pot for one quarter hour
3. Add one cooking spoonful of flour and pour on meat broth
4. Let everything cook together for another quarter hour
5. Serve over fried bread rolls
6. Stir together 3 spoonfuls of sour cream with 2 egg yolks and add on top

title: Kräutersuppe für Kranke (Herb Broth for the Ill)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- young leaves of purslane, 3 loth
- young leaves of endive, 3 loth
- young leaves of corn salad, 3 loth
- young leaves of sorrel, 3 loth
- chervil, 6 loth
- a small piece of butter
- 3 fingerfulls flour
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Wash the young leaves of purslane, endive, corn salad, and sorrel (each 3 loth) and chervil (6 loth) in lukewarm water.
2. Press out firmly and chop as finely as possible.
3. Braise it for half an hour with a small piece of butter in a saucepan.
4. Add 3 fingerfulls of flour, deglaze with meat broth, and serve to the patient.
5. Fine dumplings may also be added.

title: Kugelhopfen (Braided Yeast Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 3 spoonfuls good beer yeast
- half a schoppen cream
- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 loth powdered sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- warm milk as needed
- 4 loth melted butter
- 10 loth large and small raisins

## Directions

1. Take 1 pound flour in a bowl and make a small dough in the middle with 3 spoonfuls of good beer yeast and half a schoppen cream
2. Let it rise, add 1/4 pound butter, 4 egg yolks, 1 loth powdered sugar, and 1 knife point salt
3. Make a good, somewhat stiff dough from all ingredients, add a little warm milk if necessary
4. Work it for a quarter hour and let it rise beautifully
5. When it is ripe, roll it out somewhat broad on a noodle board
6. Brush the cake with 4 loth melted butter and scatter 10 loth large and small raisins on top
7. Roll it together like a sausage
8. Brush a Turkish mold with butter, place the sausage in it, let it rise well, and bake the Kugelhopfen in fairly strong heat until beautifully golden

title: Kuttelfuß (Tripe Foot)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One ox snout
- One ox foot
- Vinegar
- Salt
- One lemon peel, minced
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Simmer the ox snout and foot until very soft
2. Let them cool
3. Cut them into thin slices
4. Cook them further for some time with vinegar, one spoonful of the broth in which they were first cooked, salt, the minced lemon peel, and pepper
5. Cook until little broth remains
6. Pour everything into a mold
7. Let it set and then unmold onto a plate

title: Käseplätzchen (Cheese Cakes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 180 g white cheese
- 30 g flour
- 1 egg
- 30 g currants
- 1 pinch salt
- 20 g sugar
- 30 g fat

## Directions

1. Grate the cheese through a sieve and sift the flour. Prepare the currants.
2. Mix all ingredients except the fat together thoroughly. Form small cakes from the dough and fry them in the hot fat in a pan.

title: Käskuchen (Cheese Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast or puff pastry dough
- whey or non-sour curd cheese
- 1/2 schoppen thick, sour cream
- 4 whole eggs
- 2 spoons fine flour
- 1 handful chives
- salt

## Directions

1. Line a cake pan with yeast or puff pastry dough
2. Mix whey or curd cheese that is not too sour with 1/2 schoppen thick, sour cream
3. Add 4 whole eggs and 2 spoons fine flour
4. Add 1 handful chives and a knife tip of salt
5. Stir everything together for a quarter hour
6. Pour the mass onto the cake
7. Cut a little butter on top
8. Bake

title: Käsküchlein (Cheese Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- Parmesan cheese
- 3 spoonfuls sour cream
- 2 knife points salt
- 2 spoonfuls beer yeast
- 2 spoonfuls cherry spirits

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 1 pound flour, Parmesan cheese, 3 spoonfuls sour cream, 2 knife points salt, 2 spoonfuls beer yeast, and 2 spoonfuls cherry spirits
2. Work it for half an hour
3. Let it rise in warmth
4. Make small cakes with a spoon and bake them in hot lard

title: Königin von Brasilien (Queen of Brazil)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 10 eggs
- 1/2 schoppen thick, sweet, warm cream
- salt
- 1 loth sugar
- 10 tablespoons fine flour
- 3 tablespoons good white yeast
- 3/4 pound chocolate
- 1 schoppen water
- 24 egg yolks
- 1 maas milk
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1 glass water
- cherries
- apricots

## Directions

1. Prepare a fine Gugelhopf or Munich butter loaf the day before.
2. Beat 1/2 pound butter in a bowl for half an hour until foamy.
3. Place 10 eggs in warm water and stir into the butter one by one (each egg must be stirred for 3 minutes).
4. Stir in 1/2 schoppen thick, sweet, warm cream, a knife-tip of salt, and 1 loth sugar for another quarter hour.
5. Stir in 10 tablespoons fine flour until no flour is visible, then 3 tablespoons good white yeast.
6. Grease a tall vessel with butter, fill with the batter, place in gentle warmth, let it rise until it begins to sink again, and bake in gentle heat until golden.
7. Stand the baked loaf upright on a plate and when ready to serve, carefully cut off the lid level with the top.
8. Remove the interior with a spoon so no hole remains.
9. Dissolve 3/4 pound chocolate in a brass pan with 1 schoppen water.
10. Beat 24 egg yolks in a pot, heat the chocolate with 1 maas milk until boiling, pour onto the egg yolks, add 1 cooking spoon flour, cook again in the pan with constant stirring, pour through a hair sieve into another vessel, and let cool slightly.
11. Fill the cream into the hollowed Munich loaf and carefully replace the lid.
12. Place on a large round plate.
13. Beat the snow from 24 egg whites and cover the bread and plate up to the middle band with it, making the mixture as high as possible with a rough, mountain-like appearance, creating a cross-cut at the top to give it a crown shape.
14. Dissolve 3/4 pound chocolate again in a brass pan with 1 glass water, add 1/2 pound sugar, add 1 more glass water, and cook everything together for a quarter hour.
15. Brush the chocolate glaze over a brush onto the snow mountain until completely covered.
16. Place a beautiful cherry at each of the 4 corners of the crown and a beautiful apricot in the middle.
17. Before the snow goes onto the torte, place it in the oven for a quarter hour to warm it again, but not after.

title: Königsberger Klopse (Königsberg Meatballs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 140 g ground beef
- 60 g ground pork
- 1 teaspoon melted fat
- 1 egg white or egg yolk
- 1/2 roll
- 1 tablespoon water
- a pinch of salt and pepper
- 1 knife point grated onions
- 1 l water
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Set the water for cooking the meatballs to boil with salt. Cut the roll into cubes, soak and squeeze out.
2. Place ground beef and ground pork in a bowl; add egg white or egg yolk, salt, pepper, roll, onions and fat, mix everything thoroughly together, form 4 meatballs from the mixture, which must cook slowly in the salt water for 10-15 minutes in an open pot. Serve the meatballs with herring sauce. Remove the meatballs from the water, let them drain, place them in a bowl and keep warm until the sauce, which will then be poured over them, is ready.

title: Königscrême (King's Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 1/2 maas milk
- 2 eggs
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- 1 loth blanched, finely pounded bitter almonds

## Directions

1. Cook 1/4 pound sugar in a brass pan with 2 tablespoons water until the sugar becomes light yellow.
2. Pour it into any desired form and let it run all around.
3. Stir together 1/2 maas milk, 2 eggs, 1 cooking spoon flour, and 1 loth blanched, finely pounded bitter almonds.
4. Pour everything into the sugared form.
5. Place it in boiling water.
6. Cover with a lid topped with coals.
7. Let it cook for 2 to 3 hours and then turn it out onto a plate.

title: Königsnudeln (Royal Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen milk
- flour
- fresh butter
- 4 eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls good beer yeast
- sugar
- cinnamon
- lard

## Directions

1. Bring 1 schoppen milk to a boil
2. Stir in flour until it becomes a thick porridge
3. Add some fresh butter
4. Beat in 4 eggs one after another and 4 egg yolks
5. Cook until completely dry
6. Place in a bowl with 2 spoonfuls good beer yeast and let stand in warmth for a time
7. Form noodles from it
8. Fry well in hot lard
9. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Königsragout (Royal Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover calf roast, 1 pound
- lemon zest and marrow
- shallot onions, 6 pieces
- bread roll, 1
- butter, quarter pound
- eggs, 4
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice
- ox palates, 4 to 6
- flour
- red Burgundy wine, 1 glass
- good stock, half a ladleful
- mushrooms, 6 to 8 pieces
- truffles, 4 to 5
- pepper
- mace
- young chicken breasts, from 2 birds
- sweetbreads, 2
- morels
- pork forcemeat, quarter pound
- bread, small handful
- egg white
- lard
- consommé

## Directions

1. Grind leftover calf roast very finely with lemon zest and marrow.
2. Add 6 diced, braised shallot onions and a soaked, wrung-out bread roll.
3. Lightly cream quarter pound butter, add the ground meat mixture, and stir in 4 eggs, salt, nutmeg, and lemon juice.
4. Butter a suitable baking dish, pour in the mixture, make a hollow in the center, and bake in the oven until golden.
5. For the ragout: remove the white skin from ox palates, cook very tender, and cut into square pieces.
6. Make a mushroom sauce: brown one spoonful of flour in butter, deglaze with cold water, add one glass red Burgundy and half a ladleful of good stock, and cook the ox palates in this sauce.
7. Add 6 to 8 mushroom pieces, 4 to 5 cleaned, peeled, and sliced truffles, salt, nutmeg, pepper, mace, and lemon juice and zest.
8. Cut the breasts from 2 young chickens, place in lukewarm water until white, clean 2 sweetbreads, lard both beautifully, and braise in butter with half a sliced lemon for a quarter hour.
9. Add them to the ox palates with the braising fat.
10. Make a dumpling batter from cleaned morels, crushed truffles, quarter pound pork forcemeat, a small handful of bread, and an egg white; form small dumplings the size of hazelnuts and fry in lard.
11. Add to the ragout.
12. Place the baked cake on a platter, pour the ragout into the hollow, lay the chicken breasts and mushrooms on top, garnish with lemon, and pour melted consommé over the chicken breasts.

title: Königssuppe (Royal Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 cleaned and washed chickens, finely chopped
- 1 pound lean beef, cut into small pieces
- Butter
- Celery root
- Parsnip root
- Flour
- Meat broth or jus
- Egg yolks
- Thick sweet cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. In a casserole with a piece of butter the size of an egg, combine the chopped chickens and beef pieces with celery and parsnip root
2. Steam for 2 to 3 hours
3. Sprinkle a tablespoon of flour on top, pour in meat broth or jus, and cook for another half hour
4. Press through a hair sieve with meat broth and bring to a boil once more
5. Beat 4 egg yolks in the soup tureen, add half a schoppen of thick sweet cream
6. Arrange the soup over the yolks and cream
7. Season with salt and nutmeg

title: Körbelsuppe (Chervil Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls of carefully washed chervil
- parsley (optional)
- butter
- 1 small cooking spoonful of flour
- meat broth
- 2 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls of sweet milk
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Finely mince 2 handfuls of carefully washed chervil (parsley can also be added)
2. Braise it for 1/4 hour in a pot in butter
3. Add a small cooking spoonful of flour and let it come to a boil in meat broth
4. Beat 2 egg yolks in the soup bowl
5. Add 2 spoonfuls of sweet milk and serve the soup over it
6. Also add salt and nutmeg

title: Kürbis (Pickled Pumpkin)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 kg (3 lbs) prepared pumpkin
- 4 l vinegar
- 1-1/2 kg (3 lbs) sugar
- 3/4 l water
- Peel of 1/2 lemon, 1 piece ginger and 1 piece cinnamon, 4 cloves (in a muslin bag)
- White paper, 1 tablespoon rum

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkin, cut into strips, and weigh it.
2. Pour light vinegar over the prepared pumpkin so that it is completely covered.
3. The next day, cook the sugar with water until it reaches the second degree and let the spice bag cook along with it.
4. The pumpkin, which has meanwhile drained on a colander, cook in the sugar until it appears clear.
5. Remove it with a skimming spoon and cook the juice down thickly, pour it after cooling without the spices over the pumpkin pieces packed in glasses, cover these with rum paper and seal the glasses with parchment paper.
6. The vinegar can be used for preparing sour vegetables or meat or for scrubbing. Leftovers go into the vinegar pot.

title: Kürbis (Pumpkin)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g peeled pumpkin pieces
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 clove
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 g starch flour
- 2 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkin, cut it into pleasing pieces, wash and drain it. Dissolve the starch flour.
2. Simmer the pumpkin pieces soft covered with vinegar, clove and sugar, stir in the starch flour dissolved in cold water, bring to a boil and arrange it.

title: Kürbis (Stewed Pumpkin)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g peeled pumpkin pieces
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 clove
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 g cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. The pumpkin is peeled, cut into pleasing pieces, washed and drained. The cornstarch is dissolved.
2. The pumpkin pieces are stewed covered with vinegar, clove and sugar until soft, stirred with the cornstarch dissolved in cold water, brought to a boil and arranged for serving.

title: Kürbissuppe (Pumpkin Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 375 g pumpkin
- 1 l water
- 2 cloves
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 1 piece lemon zest
- 15 g flour
- 15 g margarine
- 5 g salt
- 4 tablespoons vinegar
- 50 g sugar

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkin, cut into small pieces and wash.
2. Cook the pumpkin with water and spices until soft and press through a hair sieve.
3. Sweat flour and margarine together until it foams.
4. Slowly add the pumpkin puree, let the soup cook through and season with salt, vinegar and sugar.
5. Optionally add white bread cut into small cubes and roasted in fat or margarine.
6. Alternatively, add 40 g of soaked rice to the soup, then preparation in a slow cooker is recommended.

title: Lachsforelle in Majonessauce (Salmon Trout in Mayonnaise Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- salmon trout
- salt
- wine
- vinegar
- lemon
- bay leaves
- cloves
- pepper
- 8 hard-boiled eggs
- 8 fresh egg yolks
- light jelly
- 2 tablespoons Provence oil
- 1 tablespoon jelly

## Directions

1. Wash the salmon trout, cut out the ears and gut it
2. Rub with salt and season with wine, vinegar, lemon, bay leaves, cloves, and pepper
3. Cook slowly so it does not burst, then place on the designated plate
4. For the sauce, press the yolks from 8 hard-boiled eggs through a fine sieve, beat in 8 fresh egg yolks
5. Add one spoon of light jelly and stir constantly to one side for half an hour
6. Add 2 tablespoons Provence oil drop by drop, stirring constantly for 2 hours
7. Add another tablespoon of jelly (if you have ice, you can stir the jelly over it)
8. The sauce must be as thick as pomade, then spread it on the fish, but not on its head

title: Lammbraten (Roasted Lamb)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One lamb
- Salt
- Pepper
- Onions
- Jus
- Lemon juice

## Directions

1. Skin the lamb and cut off the head, feet, and breast as you would with hare.
2. Skin it and lard it where it has no fat.
3. Rub with salt and pepper.
4. Arrange it nicely and let it roast golden in a braising pan with onions.
5. Pour a little jus over it, baste frequently with jus, and squeeze lemon juice over it.
6. Lamb roasts are most flavorful in spring, at Easter time.

title: Lammviertel am Spieß gebraten (Lamb Quarter Roasted on a Spit)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One beautiful lamb quarter
- Bacon for larding
- Salt
- Pepper
- Butter
- Paper
- Fat meat broth
- One glass wine
- Meat broth or butter
- Onions
- Bay leaves
- Sour cream
- Half lemon

## Directions

1. From a beautiful lamb quarter, pull back the skin on the shank slightly.
2. Lard it with bacon.
3. Rub with salt and pepper.
4. Put it on a spit.
5. Brush paper with butter and bind it around the meat.
6. Baste frequently with fat meat broth.
7. Pour one glass of wine, some meat broth or butter into the pan, add cut onions and bay leaves to make a good jus.
8. Half an hour before serving, remove the paper.
9. Baste the roast with sour cream (the roast must look quite glossy).
10. Squeeze the juice from half a lemon over it.
11. Force the jus through a hair sieve and serve separately with the roast.

title: Leberknöpflein (Liver Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf's liver
- 1 large handful onions
- fat
- 3 eggs
- 2 rolls
- butter
- onions for braising

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the calf's liver, cut out the veins, and chop as finely as possible
2. Steam 1 large handful onions in a piece of fat
3. Take 3 eggs and 2 rolls to the liver
4. Stir this all together for half an hour
5. Cook dumplings made from the mixture in salt water
6. Braise them with butter and onions

title: Leberpfauzel (Calf Liver Pâté)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 calf liver, cleaned and skinned
- half a pound of bacon
- 2 kreuzers worth of bread
- water
- 2 onions, finely chopped and steamed in fat
- zest of one lemon
- mace
- pepper
- salt
- 4 eggs

## Directions

1. Clean and skin the calf liver, place it on a chopping block and mince it together with half a pound of bacon very finely
2. Grate bread worth 2 kreuzers on a grater, soak it in water, then press it out
3. Place the minced liver in a bowl with the bread, add 2 finely chopped onions that have been steamed in fat, the zest of one lemon, mace, pepper, salt, and 4 eggs, stirring in one egg after another
4. Stir everything vigorously for a quarter hour
5. Put the mixture in a large calf's caul and bake it at gentle heat; it will be ready in half an hour
6. Can be served as a side dish or as a roast with jus
7. For sauce: roast a good amount of onions in a piece of fat and a little sugar in a saucepan until light yellow, dissolve as much flour as can be held between two fingers with a ladle full of meat broth, add a little raspberry vinegar, let it cook together for a quarter hour, season with salt, pepper, and nutmeg to taste
8. Place the baked liver on a plate, pour the sauce over it, and top with softly cooked morels

title: Leberpfauzel (Liver Pfauzel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- calf's liver, cleaned and skinned
- bacon, half pound
- bread
- water
- onions, 2, finely chopped and braised in fat

## Directions

1. Take a clean, skinned, and washed calf's liver, place it on a cutting board, and grind it very finely with half a pound of bacon.
2. Grate bread worth 2 kreuzer on a grater, soak in water, and wring out.
3. Put the ground liver in a bowl, add the bread, and 2 finely chopped onions braised in fat.

title: Leberschnitten (Liver Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's liver
- meat broth
- half a pound boiled rice
- a ladleful of meat broth

## Directions

1. Skin a calf's liver and remove the veins from it
2. Cook it in meat broth for one hour
3. When it has cooled, grate it on a grater
4. Cook it half soft in a casserole together with half a pound boiled rice and a ladleful of meat broth

title: Leberschnitten (Liver Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one calf's liver
- half a pound of cooked rice
- one ladle full of meat broth
- half a pound of beef kidney fat
- 6 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- mace

## Directions

1. Skin the calf's liver and remove the veins
2. Cook it for one hour in meat broth
3. When cooled, grate it on a grater
4. Cook the grated liver together with half a pound of cooked rice in a casserole and one ladle full of meat broth until half soft (it must not have broth)
5. Add half a pound of beef kidney fat, finely chopped, 6 eggs, salt, nutmeg, and mace
6. Stir everything together again
7. Bind the mixture in a damp cloth
8. Cook for 4 hours in water
9. When slightly cooled, cut into slices and add to soup à la Reine

title: Leberspickel (Liver Skewers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- liver
- fresh calf's mesentery
- lemon juice
- salt

## Directions

1. Treat the liver the same way as fried liver, with the difference that it is cut into triangular pieces
2. Take fresh calf's mesentery, place it in lukewarm water
3. Cut pieces from it
4. Wrap the liver in it
5. Place it on the grill
6. Let it fry well
7. When serving add lemon juice and salt

title: Leberspätzlein (Liver Spätzle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 calf's liver
- 2 loth butter or lard
- 2 whole eggs
- 1/2 mäßchen flour
- milk or water as needed
- salt, a handful

## Directions

1. Skin the calf's liver with a knife and remove the veins cleanly
2. Chop the liver very finely and place in a bowl
3. Heat 2 loth butter or lard and pour over the liver
4. Beat in 2 whole eggs and stir together for a while
5. Add 1/2 mäßchen flour and mix thoroughly
6. If the dough is too stiff, add a little milk or water
7. In a pan, bring water to boil with a handful of salt
8. Dip a sieve into the water and press the dough through with a cooking spoon
9. Place the spätzle in a sieve and pour a little water over them
10. Serve with soup or roasted

title: Lebersuppe (Liver Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal liver
- Onions
- Butter
- Bread for 2 kreuzer worth
- Meat broth or jus
- Egg yolks
- Sweet cream
- Toasted bread slices
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Chop the veal liver with onions
2. Place butter in a casserole and steam the liver in it
3. Cut bread into the mixture, steam together for half an hour
4. Pour in meat broth or jus and let it cook for one hour
5. Draw through a hair sieve, return to the casserole, and bring to a boil again
6. Beat 2 egg yolks in a soup bowl, add 2 tablespoons of sweet cream and toasted bread slices
7. Arrange the soup over this mixture
8. Season with salt and nutmeg

title: Leberwürste (Liver Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Pig lung
- Pig liver
- Pig mesentery
- Pig sweetbreads
- Some calf liver
- 4 to 6 onions
- Finely chopped lemons
- Pepper
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Fat in which the meat was cooked
- White bratwurst casings

## Directions

1. Scald the lung, liver, mesentery, sweetbreads, and some calf liver
2. Chop them very finely on a cutting block together with 4 to 6 onions
3. Mix finely chopped lemons, pepper, salt, nutmeg, and the fat in which the meat was cooked in a bowl
4. Mix everything together until it is not too thin
5. Fill the mixture into white bratwurst casings
6. Place in lukewarm water
7. Heat under the dish in which they lie until the sausages rise to the surface

title: Lemon Sauce for Game (Citronensauce zum Wildbret)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- dark bread crust
- wine
- lard
- jus
- lemon juice
- lemons, finely chopped

## Directions

1. Cook the top crust of dark bread soft in wine (but first the bread must be baked yellow in lard)
2. Strain through a sieve
3. Add one ladle of jus
4. Add some lemon juice and finely chopped lemons
5. Let everything boil together

title: Lendenbraten (Loin Roast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One piece loin roast
- Bacon for larding
- One large spoon meat broth
- Three finely cut celery roots

## Directions

1. Remove skin and fat from the piece.
2. Lard it with bacon.
3. Place in a braising pan and pour one large spoon of meat broth over it.
4. Roast in the oven.
5. When nearly done, add three finely cut celery roots and let them cook until tender.
6. Once soft, serve the roast.

title: Lendenbraten à la Robert (Loin Roast Robert Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One beautiful piece loin roast
- Half pound fresh bacon
- Four anchovies
- Half lemon
- One kreuzer worth of bread
- Water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mace
- Cloves
- Two eggs
- Cut onions
- Bay leaves
- Lemon
- Basil
- Tarragon
- One glass wine
- Half cooking spoon flour
- One glass Bordeaux wine
- One spoon meat broth

## Directions

1. From a beautiful piece of loin roast, remove fat and skin.
2. Cut it open at the side but not all the way through.
3. Cut out some flesh but leave no hole in the roast.
4. Mince the removed flesh with half a pound fresh bacon.
5. Mince four anchovies (which must be cleaned and boned beforehand) and half a lemon with its marrow very finely.
6. Grate one kreuzer worth of bread on a grater, soak in water, press out firmly.
7. Mix with the minced and chopped ingredients, salt, pepper, mace, cloves, and two beaten eggs.
8. Fill the entire mass into the loin roast, sew it closed again.
9. Place in a casserole with cut onions, bay leaves, lemon, basil, tarragon, and one glass of wine.
10. Let braise slowly over weak charcoal fire.
11. Half an hour before serving, remove the roast.
12. Add half a cooking spoon flour to the casserole, one glass Bordeaux wine and one spoon meat broth, plus juice from half a lemon.
13. Let simmer together for half a quarter hour.
14. Force through a hair sieve back into the casserole.
15. Return the roast and let finish cooking.

title: Likör oder Rossoli (Liqueur or Rossoli)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One Maas strong fruit brandy or first runoff
- One Schoppen rose water
- 12 Loth sugar
- 4 spoonfuls sweet milk
- One Loth cinnamon
- Half a Loth cloves
- Half a Loth anise oil

## Directions

1. Stir the anise oil with pounded sugar until no oil is visible
2. Pour the brandy to it
3. Mix in the remaining spices
4. Bind the mixture tightly in a wide glass
5. Leave it in the sun for 2 to 3 days
6. On the third day, strain through a felt hat
7. Pour into bottles
8. Cork the bottles and store

title: Limonade (Lemonade)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 lemon or orange peel
- 25 g sugar
- 1/2 liter boiling water
- 3 tablespoons lemon juice or 6 tablespoons orange juice

## Directions

1. Finely peel the lemon or orange peel
2. Add the sugar and pour boiling water over it
3. Once cooled, add lemon or orange juice through a sieve
4. Remove the peel and serve the lemonade cold or add a piece of artificial ice; it can also be served warm as a sweat-inducing remedy

title: Limonade (Lemonade)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One Schoppen measure
- One lemon, rubbed on sugar
- One lemon, juice pressed
- Fresh spring water
- 3 Loth sugar per Schoppen

## Directions

1. Rub one lemon on the sugar
2. Press the juice from one lemon
3. Fill with fresh spring water
4. Use 3 Loth sugar per Schoppen
5. For wine lemonade: use half wine and half water, juice from half a lemon, and rub the other half on sugar

title: Linsen mit Fasan (Lentils with Pheasant)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- pheasant
- one eighth good Bohemian lentils, cleaned and washed
- salt water
- one glass vinegar

## Directions

1. Roast the pheasant until half done
2. Cook one eighth of good Bohemian lentils, which have been carefully cleaned and washed, until soft in salted water
3. Press them through a sieve
4. Pour one glass of vinegar over them
5. Put half of the lentils into a bowl
6. Remove the breast along with the legs and thighs from the pheasant and lay it in the bowl
7. Add the remaining half of the lentils on top
8. Place it in a roasting pan and let it form a crust

title: Linsen mit Fasan (Lentils with Pheasant)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- 1/8 good Bohemian lentils
- salt
- vinegar

## Directions

1. Roast the pheasant until half-done
2. Cook the cleaned and washed lentils in salt water until soft
3. Press through a strainer
4. Add a glass of vinegar
5. Put half the lentils in a bowl, place the pheasant with the breast, shanks, and legs cut away on top, and cover with the remaining half of the lentils

title: Linzer Torte (Linz Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 1/2 pound pounded sugar
- 1/2 pound fine flour
- 1/2 pound unskinned, finely pounded almonds
- 4 loth grated chocolate
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 6 fresh egg yolks
- 1 loth pounded cinnamon
- 1/2 quint pounded cloves
- 1 egg white
- juice of 1/2 lemon
- 1/4 pound sugar
- preserved currants
- firm paper
- wafers

## Directions

1. Make a dough from butter, pounded sugar, flour, unskinned finely pounded almonds, grated chocolate, hard-boiled egg yolks (pressed through a hair sieve), fresh egg yolks, pounded cinnamon, and pounded cloves
2. Roll it out finger-thick
3. Make a rim of firm paper around the torte so it does not run
4. Cut strips from the remaining dough and make a lattice over the torte
5. Place on a dark baking sheet lined with wafers and bake at medium heat for half an hour in the oven
6. Make the following frosting: from 1 egg white, the juice of 1/2 lemon, and 1/4 pound sugar
7. Spread this frosting on the lattice of the torte, dry it in the oven with the frosting
8. Before serving, place preserved currants in the openings of the lattice

title: Luftnudeln (Air Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 Loth Butter
- 8 Loth Wasser
- 12 Loth Zucker
- 9 Loth feines Mehl
- 9 Kreuzer gestoßene Vanille
- 6 Eier
- Fett zum Bestreichen des Blechs

## Directions

1. Butter, Wasser und Zucker werden in einer messingenen Pfanne miteinander gekocht.
2. Feines Mehl und gestoßene Vanille dazu rühren.
3. Solange kochen, bis sich der Teig von der Pfanne losschält.
4. Den Teig in eine Schüssel nehmen.
5. 6 Eier, eines nach dem andern, darunter rühren.
6. Daumenlange Nudeln auf einem mit Fett bestrichenen schwarzen Bleche bei guter Hitze backen.

title: Luftnudeln (Air Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 loth butter
- 8 loth water
- 12 loth sugar
- 9 loth fine flour
- 9 kreuzer ground vanilla

## Directions

1. Cook butter, water, and sugar together in a brass pan
2. Stir in flour and ground vanilla

title: Lumsen oder Ulmerspeise (Lumsen or Ulm Dish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 or 5 eggs
- walnut-sized piece of butter
- 4 loth butter and milk
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from 4 or 5 eggs, add walnut-sized piece of butter
2. Roll it out like ordinary noodle dough
3. Let 4 loth butter and milk cook together as you see fit
4. Tear this noodle cake into pieces
5. Lay the pieces in the cooked milk
6. Put them in a baking oven so they get color on both sides
7. Then arrange them on a plate
8. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Lungen zu Kochen (Cooked Lungs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lung with heart
- 2 tablespoons soup greens
- 1 spice dose
- 15 g salt
- 2 l water

## Directions

1. Lung and heart are thoroughly washed and cut into hand-sized pieces. The soup greens are cleaned, washed and cut into pieces.
2. Water, salt, lung and heart pieces are placed in a pot and cooked covered. Once foam has formed and has cooked for a while, remove it, add the soup greens and spice dose and let the meat pieces continue cooking covered. To see if they are done, pierce the heart with a stick; if it is soft, remove all the meat and use the broth and lung according to the recipe for Lungenhache.

title: Lungenhache (Chopped Lung Hash)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 lung and heart (cooked)
- 20 g fat
- 10 g flour
- 20 g onions
- a pinch of pepper and salt
- 1 tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Free the meat pieces from skins, sinews and cartilage and chop not too finely. Peel the onions, cut into small cubes or chop. Heat 1/8 l of lung broth.
2. Heat the fat; soften the onions in it, braise the chopped lung and flour sprinkled over it in it and then add the broth and vinegar and cook it through. Season the hash with pepper and salt. Some chopped herbs and roast sauce, if you have them on hand, make the hash more palatable. It is advantageous to cook the lung the day before. The remaining lung broth can be used for lung soup. For hash one can also use all sorts of roasted or cooked meat scraps (meat hash). Meat hash is prepared exactly like lung hash.

title: Lungensuppe (Lung Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l lung broth
- 30 g flour
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Preparation: The broth of the lung is poured through a sieve.
2. Heat the fat in an earthenware pot, sweat the flour in it thoroughly, slowly stir in the hot broth and cook the soup open until slimy.
3. Season with salt, pepper and parsley and serve.
4. Optionally, the soup can be improved by finishing with 1 egg.

title: Lungensuppe für Schwindsüchtige (Lung Soup for the Consumptive)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 veal lung and some heart, cut into small pieces
- 6 crayfish
- 1 handful of chervil
- 1 handful of speedwell
- A few cow's parsley leaves
- Water
- Egg yolks (optional)
- Toasted bread slices (optional)

## Directions

1. Cut the veal lung and heart into small pieces; the lung should be taken fresh from the calf if possible
2. Kill 6 crayfish alive and add them to the lung
3. Place in a pot with 1 handful of chervil, 1 handful of speedwell, and several leaves of cow's parsley, all well washed
4. Pour 2 measures of water over it and let half of it reduce
5. Strain through a hair sieve
6. Can be served with toasted bread slices or finished with egg yolks

title: Magdalenenkuchen (Magdalene Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 2 pounds flour
- 9 egg whites
- preserved fruits
- almonds

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter until creamy
2. Add 9 egg yolks, 1/2 pound sugar, and 1 grated lemon
3. Stir in 2 pounds flour and the snow from 9 egg whites
4. Fill half the mass into a Potageblech
5. Add preserved fruits on top
6. Add the remaining half of the mass over the fruits
7. Sprinkle the cake with almonds
8. Bake at good heat

title: Maienmuß (May Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound blanched almonds
- rose water
- quarter pound sugar
- one-eighth pound butter
- almonds
- flowers

## Directions

1. Pound half a pound blanched almonds with rose water very fine.
2. Stir in a quarter pound sugar and one-eighth pound butter.
3. Stir everything together for half an hour.
4. Form a mountain on a not very large dish.
5. Stick almonds and flowers into it.
6. Serve at table; you can also sprinkle cinnamon over it.

title: Mailänder Bratwürste (Milanese Bratwurst)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- tame pork, half fat
- a little basil
- a little thyme
- a little rye bread
- fine pepper
- a little crushed cloves
- salt
- a small glass of water

## Directions

1. Mince the pork finely until it resembles dough
2. Mince basil and thyme very finely
3. Mix everything together with a little rye bread, fine pepper, a little crushed cloves, and salt
4. Add a small glass of water and mix very well
5. Fill into bratwurst casings
6. These sausages can be grilled or, as the Italians do, simmered in rice or other vegetables

title: Mailänder Kuchen (Milanese Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 5 eggs
- 3/4 pound flour
- 3 loths fine ground sugar
- milk

## Directions

1. Lightly stir 1/2 pound butter
2. Mix with 5 eggs, 3/4 pound flour, and 3 loths fine ground sugar into a dough
3. If too firm, add some milk
4. Roll out the dough on a pastry board and fold over several times
5. Roll out finger thick
6. Bake in a cake pan coated with butter
7. Can be topped with lemon or chocolate ice

title: Makronen (Macarons)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein halbes Pfund abgezogene Mandeln
- Eierweiß (4 große oder 5 kleine auf ein Pfund Mandeln)
- ein Pfund gesiebter Zucker
- 2 Eierweiß
- Oblaten

## Directions

1. Abgezogene Mandeln mit Eierweiß im Reibstein fein reiben.
2. Mit gesiebtem Zucker in einer Schüssel zusammenarbeiten.
3. 2 Eierweiß darunter rühren.
4. Runde oder längliche Makronen auf Oblaten auf einem schwarzen Bleche setzen.
5. Im abgekühlten Ofen backen.

title: Makronen (Macaroons - Pantry Recipe)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g sweet blanched grated almonds, 12 pieces bitter
- 280 g sifted sugar
- 7-8 egg whites
- 5 g baking powder
- 1 piece of bacon rind for the sheet

## Directions

1. Prepare the ingredients.
2. Stir the almonds, sugar, and 1 egg white over heat; fold the stiffly beaten snow of the remaining egg whites first into the stirred mixture, then add the baking powder. With a teaspoon, place small heaps on the greased baking sheet, slide into the oven immediately, and bake the macaroons pale yellow. Instead of almonds and sugar, one can use marzipan scraps; instead of grated almonds, use grated coconut, 1/2 kg (1 pound) available at the confectioner for M. 0.35. (Coconut Macaroons.)

title: Mandel-Fingerlein (Almond Fingers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound flour
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound ground almonds
- 1/2 glass wine
- cinnamon and cloves to taste
- zest of 1 lemon
- some bitter orange peel
- 2 eggs

## Directions

1. Work all ingredients into a smooth dough
2. Form into small finger-shaped pieces
3. Fry in hot lard until golden

title: Mandelauflauf (Almond Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound peeled almonds
- milk
- 3 tablespoons fine flour
- 4 Loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1 Schoppen milk
- 4 Loth butter
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pound 1/4 pound of peeled almonds very finely in a mortar with milk
2. Stir smooth in a pan with 3 tablespoons of fine flour
3. Add 4 Loth of sugar grated on a lemon
4. Pour in 1 Schoppen of milk and cook a not too thick porridge
5. Cut in 4 Loth of butter and stir in 6 egg yolks
6. Beat the egg whites to snow and mix in
7. Cook everything well together
8. Butter a bowl and fill the mass into it
9. Bake in a not very hot oven and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Mandelbögen (Almond Arches)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein Viertelpfund abgezogene Mandeln
- 1 Citrone
- 3 Eier
- 3 Loth gestoßener Zucker
- Wachs

## Directions

1. Ein Viertelpfund abgezogene Mandeln so fein wie möglich schneiden.
2. Das Gelbe von einer Citrone abreiben.
3. Von 3 Eiern einen steifen Schnee schlagen.
4. Die Mandeln mit 3 Loth gestoßenem Zucker darunter mengen.
5. Ein schwarzes Blech mit Wachs bestreichen.
6. Stark messerrückendicke, fingersbreite und fingerslange Stückchen von der Masse darauf schneiden.
7. Im Ofen gelb backen.
8. Das Gebackene gleich auf dem Wellholz krumm machen.

title: Mandelcrême (Almond Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 loth sweet almonds
- 1 loth bitter almonds
- cinnamon water
- 1/2 maas cream
- 8 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 3 to 4 spoonfuls cinnamon water
- 1 schoppen calf jelly
- 6 egg yolks
- a little milk
- orange oil

## Directions

1. Blanch 9 loth sweet and 1 loth bitter almonds.
2. Pound them very fine with a little cinnamon water.
3. Cook the almonds with 1/2 maas cream, 8 loth sugar rubbed with lemon zest, and 3 to 4 spoonfuls cinnamon water together with constant stirring.
4. Add 1 schoppen calf jelly and cook again with it.
5. Stir 6 egg yolks smooth with a little milk.
6. Finally pour this into the cream.
7. Let everything come together once more in the pan.
8. Pour the cream into a jelly mold brushed with orange oil.
9. Place it on ice or in the cellar.
10. Turn the cream out onto a plate and garnish to taste.

title: Mandelhäufchen (Almond Clusters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein halbes Pfund abgezogene Mandeln
- 4 Loth Citronat
- Pomeranzenschalen
- die Schale einer Citrone
- 4 Eierweiß
- ein halbes Pfund gesiebter Zucker
- Oblaten

## Directions

1. Ein halbes Pfund abgezogene Mandeln der Länge nach schneiden.
2. Mit 4 Loth Citronat, Pomeranzenschalen und der Schale einer Citrone (alles fein gewogen) rühren.
3. 4 Eierweiß zu Schnee schlagen und mit einem halben Pfund gesiebtem Zucker darunter rühren.
4. Eine starke Viertelstunde rühren.
5. Ein schwarzes Blech mit Oblaten belegen.
6. Häufchen von der Masse darauf setzen.
7. Diese im Ofen gelb backen.

title: Mandelhäufchen anderer Art (Almond Clusters, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Eierweiß
- ein Viertelpfund fein gesiebter Zucker
- Citronengelb
- ein Viertelpfund abgezogene, fein geschnittene Mandeln
- Oblaten

## Directions

1. Der steife Schnee von 2 Eierweiß und ein Viertelpfund fein gesiebter Zucker zu dickem Eis rühren.
2. Etwas Citronengelb daran reiben.
3. Mit einem Viertelpfund abgezogenen, möglichst fein geschnittenen Mandeln untereinander machen.
4. Oblaten auf ein schwarzes Blech legen.
5. Von der Masse Häufchen so groß wie eine Welschnuß darauf setzen.
6. In einem abgekühlten Ofen gelb backen.

title: Mandelkränzchen (Almond Wreaths)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 Loth Mehl
- ein Viertelpfund Butter
- 4 Loth Zucker
- 2 Löffel voll Rosenwasser
- 2 bis 3 Eierweiß
- 1 Ei zum Bestreichen
- grob gestoßener Zucker und Mandeln zum Bestreuen

## Directions

1. Aus 12 Loth Mehl, einem Viertelpfund Butter, 4 Loth Zucker, 2 Löffeln voll Rosenwasser und 2 bis 3 Eierweiß auf dem Nudelbrett einen Teig gut durcharbeiten.
2. Kränzchen davon machen.
3. Auf ein mit Mehl bestreutes Blech legen.
4. Mit Ei bestreichen.
5. Mit grob gestoßenem Zucker und Mandeln bestreuen.
6. Im Ofen gelb backen.

title: Mandelkuchen (Almond Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- egg whites
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound fine ground sugar
- 2 handfuls fine grated flour
- egg whites
- oblaten
- raisins and currants

## Directions

1. Grind 1/2 pound blanched almonds very finely in a mortar with egg white
2. Melt 1/4 pound butter in a bowl
3. Heat the mixture with 1/2 pound fine ground sugar for half an hour
4. Add 2 handfuls fine grated flour
5. Slowly mix in the snow from some egg whites
6. Line a baking sheet that has been coated with butter with oblaten
7. Spread the mass into it
8. Bake the cake in the oven
9. If desired, scatter raisins and currants on top

title: Mandelkuchen (Almond Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound blanched almonds
- egg white
- quarter pound butter
- half pound finely ground sugar
- 2 handfuls finely grated potato flour
- egg white snow from several eggs
- butter for coating baking sheet
- wafers
- raisins and dried plums (optional)

## Directions

1. Pound the blanched almonds very finely in a mortar with egg white
2. Let a quarter pound butter melt in a bowl
3. Boil the above with half a pound finely ground sugar for half an hour
4. Add 2 handfuls finely grated potato flour
5. Slowly mix in the snow from several egg whites
6. Line a baking sheet first coated with butter with wafers
7. Pour the mass onto it
8. Bake the cake in the oven
9. If desired, sprinkle raisins and dried plums on top

title: Mandelküchlein (Almond Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ein Viertelpfund Butter
- ein halbes Pfund Mehl
- 4 Loth Zucker
- 1 Eidotter
- 4 Loth abgezogene Mandeln
- Rosenwasser
- 2 Loth gestoßener Zucker
- 2 Messerspitzen voll Zimmt
- der Saft von einer Citrone

## Directions

1. Aus einem Viertelpfund Butter, einem halben Pfund Mehl, 4 Loth Zucker und einem Eidotter auf dem Nudelbrett einen Teig machen.
2. Diesen messerrückendick auswalken.
3. Mit einem Trinkglas Küchlein ausstechen.
4. Auf ein mit Mehl bestreutes Blech legen.
5. 4 Loth abgezogene Mandeln mit Rosenwasser stoßen.
6. Auf einen Teller schütten.
7. 2 Loth gestoßener Zucker, 2 Messerspitzen voll Zimmt und der Saft von einer Citrone darunter mengen.
8. Diese Masse auf die Küchlein verteilen.
9. Dieselben im Ofen gelb backen.

title: Mandelmilch (Almond Milk)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Loth blanched almonds
- Water

## Directions

1. Pound the blanched almonds very finely in a mortar with a little water
2. Gradually pour half a Maas of water while pounding
3. Press the almonds through a linen cloth
4. Pound the almonds again
5. Repeat this process several times
6. For an invalid, hemp milk, which cools greatly, can be added

title: Mandelschnecken (Almond Snails)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butterteig
- ein Viertelpfund abgezogene, nicht sehr fein gestoßene Mandeln
- das Gelbe, das Mark und der Saft einer Citrone
- 4 Loth Zucker

## Directions

1. Ein Butterteig messerrückendick auswalken.
2. Eine halbe Viertelelle lange Riemen daraus schneiden.
3. Ein Viertelpfund abgezogene, nicht sehr fein gestoßene Mandeln mit dem Gelben, dem Mark und dem Saft einer Citrone und 4 Loth Zucker vermischen.
4. Die Masse auf die Streifchen streichen.
5. Diese in Form von Schnecken zusammenrollen.
6. Auf einem mit Mehl bestreuten Blech gelb backen.

title: Mandeltorte (Almond Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 egg whites
- whole lemon
- half lemon
- handful of potato flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites until thick (this is added only when the mixture is already thick)
2. Grate a whole lemon on a grater and add the juice from half a lemon along with a handful of potato flour
3. Butter a tin form, fill the mixture in, and bake the torte in the oven

title: Mandeltorte anderer Art (Almond Torte of Another Kind)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 loth finely pounded almonds
- 12 loth finely pounded sugar
- 4 loth finely cut citron
- 4 loth finely cut orange peel
- zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Whip 8 egg whites into a stiff snow
2. Take 8 egg yolks with 12 loth finely pounded almonds, 12 loth finely pounded sugar, 4 loth finely cut citron, and 4 loth finely cut orange peel
3. Stir everything together very thick with the zest of 1/2 lemon
4. Fill into a metal form and prepare according to standard baking methods

title: Mandeltorte anderer Art (Almond Torte, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 12 loth finely ground almonds
- 12 loth finely ground sugar
- 4 loth finely cut citron
- 4 loth finely cut orange peel
- zest and juice of half a lemon
- butter

## Directions

1. Beat the 8 egg whites into stiff peaks
2. Mix the 8 egg yolks with the finely ground almonds, finely ground sugar, finely cut citron, finely cut orange peel, and zest and juice of half a lemon
3. Stir all together very thick, then fold in the egg white peaks
4. Butter a tin form
5. Fill the mixture into the form and bake in the oven at low heat until golden yellow

title: Marinierter Fasan (Marinated Pheasant)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pheasant
- onions
- lemons
- celery
- yellow turnips
- bay leaves
- cloves
- white pepper
- salt
- bacon
- wine from the marinade
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Turn the pheasant frequently in a marinade of onions, lemons, celery, yellow turnips, bay leaves, cloves, and white pepper.
2. The next day, rub with pepper and salt.
3. Bind well in bacon, wrap in a piece of paper.
4. In a casserole with the wine from the marinade, braise slowly until golden.
5. Baste frequently.
6. Before serving, remove the paper and tuck the head to the chest (only the bacon on the breast should remain visible).
7. Remove the fat from the braise, pour lemon juice over it.
8. Pass the sauce through a fine sieve.
9. When plated, tie a green ribbon around the pheasant's neck where the feathers begin.

title: Marinirte Fische (Marinated Fish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fish
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- wine vinegar
- shallot onions
- bay leaves
- lemons
- cloves

## Directions

1. Scale and wash the fish, rub them with salt and pepper, let them sit for 1 hour
2. Place them on a grill, baste them frequently with melted butter, and brown them nicely
3. Let them cool, place them in a stone pot
4. Pour wine vinegar over them, add shallot onions, bay leaves, lemons, pepper, and cloves
5. Seal the pot

title: Markknöpflein (Marrow Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound beef marrow
- 5 eggs
- grated breadcrumb flour
- meat broth for cooking

## Directions

1. Let 1/4 pound beef marrow render, pour it through a hair sieve onto cold water.
2. Stir it with the water for a quarter hour, drain the water, let the fat render.
3. Stir it again with water until it is completely white.
4. Beat 5 eggs into it gradually, adding with each egg a handful of grated breadcrumb flour.
5. Let everything stand for half an hour.
6. Place small dumplings made from the dough into boiling meat broth.
7. Let them cook for half a quarter hour and add them to the soup.

title: Meerrettichsauce (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 tablespoon grated horseradish
- 4 tablespoons broth
- 15 g margarine
- 10 g onions
- 10 g flour
- 1/4 l broth
- salt to taste

## Directions

1. Weigh the onions. Peel and grate the horseradish quickly.
2. Let the horseradish steep slowly with 4 tablespoons of broth in an earthenware pot. Make a smooth sauce from the fat, onions, flour, and broth.
3. Mix the horseradish into the sauce and season with salt. If it is very sharp, add 2 tablespoons of milk.
4. Note: Grated horseradish must not be allowed to dry out; it should be mixed with a little milk or vinegar so it does not turn black.

title: Meerrettig (Horseradish Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- grated horseradish
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour vinegar over the grated horseradish so it does not turn blue
2. Sweeten it with sugar
3. Serve with beef

title: Meerrettig (Horseradish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Grated horseradish
- Vinegar

## Directions

1. Pour vinegar over the grated horseradish so it does not turn blue

title: Meerrettig mit Mandeln (Horseradish Sauce with Almonds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- good grated horseradish
- 2 loths of blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 2 loths of butter
- 1 loth of sugar
- cream or milk

## Directions

1. Boil the grated horseradish and almonds in a casserole with the butter, sugar, and cream or milk

title: Meerspinne oder Hummer auf italienische Art (Lobster or Sea Spider in Italian Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 boiled lobster or sea spider
- several cleaned, boned anchovies
- 6 hard-boiled egg yolks
- 4 spoonfuls good Provence oil
- 3 tablespoons tarragon vinegar
- 2 spoonfuls good mustard
- 2 finely chopped shallot onions
- pepper
- salt
- chives, chopped

## Directions

1. After the lobster or sea spider is boiled, split it lengthwise
2. Place it in the serving dish
3. Make the sauce as follows: pound several cleaned, boned anchovies with 6 hard-boiled egg yolks in a mortar into a paste, stir in 4 spoonfuls good Provence oil, 3 tablespoons tarragon vinegar, 2 spoonfuls good mustard, 2 finely chopped shallot onions, pepper, salt, and chopped chives
4. Pour this sauce over the lobster or serve it separately on the table

title: Mehlklösse (Flour Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 g flour
- 10 g beef fat or margarine
- 2 g salt
- 1 tablespoon boiling water

## Directions

1. Bring fat and water to a boil and pour into the flour and salt while stirring rapidly. Using a teaspoon that has been held in boiling water beforehand, drop small dumplings into the mixture before the dough cools, cook them about 15 minutes slowly in the soup or salted water with the pot uncovered, and serve with them.
2. If the flour dumplings are to be served with brown butter or stewed fruit, use the ingredients threefold, drop with a tablespoon, and cook in salted water until done. Similarly for finer flour dumplings, which improve the above mixture by adding an egg and using 1-1/2 tablespoon of lean fried bacon cubes instead of the fat.

title: Meliertes Ragout von Hecht (Mixed Pike Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 half- or three-quarter pound pike
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onions
- lemons
- calf sweetbread
- 8 large boiled crayfish
- quarter pound of butter

## Directions

1. Clean and prepare 2 to 3 pike weighing half to three-quarters of a pound each
2. Cut them into finger-wide pieces and rub them well with salt and pepper
3. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan with the fish, some onions and lemons, cover and let it steam for half an hour
4. Meanwhile, take the throat from a piece of calf sweetbread, blanch it slightly, cut it into pieces and add it to the fish
5. From 8 large boiled crayfish, take the tails and claws, clean the bodies carefully so that the upper shell remains intact
6. Pound the remainder together with a quarter pound of butter
7. Put this in a saucepan, steam it until it foams
8. Pour it through a hair sieve

title: Melirtes Compote (Mixed Fruit Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Borsdorf apples
- Rose apples
- Calvil rouge apples
- Luiken apples
- Prunes
- small Muscat pears
- Quinces
- water (half a maas)
- wine (half a maas)
- large raisins (quarter pound)
- prunes (quarter pound)
- almonds (2 loth, peeled)
- raisins

## Directions

1. Peel Borsdorf apples, rose apples, Calvil rouge and Luiken apples and cut in half
2. Remove the skin from the prunes
3. Cook the small Muscat pears and quinces with half a maas water and the same amount of wine in a brass pan along with the skins and cores for a quarter hour
4. Pour into a hair sieve
5. Cook a quarter pound of large raisins a couple minutes in the syrup, together with a quarter pound of prunes
6. Arrange 2 layers of the cooked apples on a plate
7. Arrange each apple, prune, pear and dried plum next to each other
8. Make a pyramid from the fruits arranged so the pyramid looks nicely mottled
9. Cut 2 loth peeled almonds into 4 parts
10. Stick a raisin on each almond piece
11. Decorate the entire pyramid with these
12. Let the syrup reduce a bit more
13. Pour it over the pyramid

title: Melonen in Essig (Melons in Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Good, ripe melons
- Good wine vinegar
- Sugar (1 half pound per maas of vinegar)
- 1 quint coarsely ground cinnamon
- 8 to 10 whole cloves
- A piece of whole ginger

## Directions

1. Rub good, ripe melons with a cloth
2. Cut them into slices
3. Lay them in a bowl
4. Pour good wine vinegar over them
5. Let them stand for 3 days
6. Pour the vinegar into a brass pan (measuring 1 maas vinegar to 1 half pound sugar)
7. Cook 1 quint coarsely ground cinnamon, 8 to 10 whole cloves, and a piece of whole ginger together with the melons until soft
8. Let them stand overnight in a porcelain bowl
9. The next day put them on the fire again
10. Cook them completely through
11. Let them cool
12. Preserve them like other preserved goods and store in a dry place
13. Serve with roasted poultry

title: Meringuentorte (Meringue Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 12 egg whites
- 1 pound finely ground and sifted sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 12 egg whites into stiff dry peaks
2. Mix with 1 pound finely ground and sifted sugar
3. Fill part of the mixture into a piping bag with a finger-thick nozzle
4. Pipe 4 to 6 columns onto a very clean board
5. Let them set in the oven at gentle heat
6. Form the remaining mixture into a round the size and shape of a plate
7. Place it around the baked columns so they protrude above it
8. Bake the whole thing in the oven until beautifully and thoroughly baked

title: Milch, Saure (Sour Milk)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 l Whole milk
- 20 g Grated bread
- 30 g Sugar

## Directions

1. Distribute the milk among 2 deep soup bowls or small dishes, place them in a dust-free, warm room, and let them stand undisturbed for about 36 hours.
2. Serve sprinkled with bread and sugar, or serve the bread and sugar on the side.

title: Milchbrei (Milk Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 cooking spoon flour
- sweet cream
- 6 whole eggs
- sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 maas milk
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir 1 cooking spoon flour smooth with sweet cream, beat 6 whole eggs into it, some sugar grated from a lemon
2. Pour 1/2 maas milk to it, butter a bowl, fill the mass into it and bake it a nice yellow

title: Milchcrême mit einem Schneeberg (Milk Cream with a Meringue Mountain)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 Maas milk
- 4 Loth sugar
- half a lemon peel
- 8 egg whites
- 8 egg yolks
- 1 small spoonful flour
- red sanding sugar

## Directions

1. Let 1 Maas milk with 4 Loth sugar and half a lemon peel boil in a brass pan.
2. Beat 8 egg whites into stiff peaks by hand until very high.
3. Let the meringue cook 3 to 4 minutes in the boiled milk, turning carefully.
4. Let it cook that long again, then remove carefully and place in a hair sieve to drain.
5. Stir the 8 egg yolks with one small spoonful flour until completely smooth.
6. Pour the boiling milk onto it, let it draw up once more in the pan.
7. Pass half the milk through a hair sieve onto the plate.
8. Set the meringue mountain in the middle.
9. Pour the remaining milk over it.
10. Sprinkle red sanding sugar on top.
11. Serve cold in summer, warm in winter.

title: Milchflammeri (Milk Starch Pudding)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l cold whole milk
- 50 g starch flour
- 50 g sugar
- 1 pinch salt
- 1 piece orange peel

## Directions

1. Fill a mold or some teacups with water and immediately empty them just before pouring in the flammeri.
2. In an earthenware pot, whisk the starch flour with milk, sugar, orange peel, and salt, bring to a boil on the stove while stirring, cook for 3 minutes, and after removing the peel, pour into the rinsed molds, let cool, turn out, and serve with fruit sauce.

title: Milchgelee (Milk Jelly)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l whole milk
- 40 g semolina or rice flour
- 6 tablespoons whole milk
- 4 tablespoons sugar
- 1 piece lemon peel

## Directions

1. Whisk 6 tablespoons milk with the flour.
2. Boil 1/2 l milk with lemon peel and sugar, pour in the whisked flour, and let everything cook while stirring until it appears clear. Pour the jelly into a glass bowl and serve with fruit sauce when cooled.
3. Remark: Instead of rice flour, one can use 9 sheets of white gelatin.

title: Milchkaltschale (Milk Cold Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l whole milk
- 50 g sugar
- 2 g cinnamon
- 1 tablespoon lemon sugar or 1 piece orange peel
- 1 pinch salt
- 1 egg yolk
- 5 g cornstarch
- 6 tablespoons whole milk
- 2 rusks

## Directions

1. Cook milk, sugar, spices and salt.
2. Whisk the egg yolk mixed with cornstarch and 6 tablespoons of cold milk into the hot milk, stir the mixture on the heat until slightly slimy and let it cool.
3. Before serving, pour the soup through a sieve.
4. Add rusk crumbles.
5.
