Optional variations: Strawberry milk cold soup - add 125 g fresh strawberries mixed with 40 g sugar when serving; Elder milk cold soup - steep 1 flower cluster of elder or elderflower for 2 minutes in the milk; Peach milk cold soup - let 4 peach leaves steep for 2 minutes in the milk instead of lemon sugar; Vanilla cold soup - use 1 tablespoon vanilla sugar instead of cinnamon and lemon sugar.

title: Milchkartoffeln (Milk Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) potatoes
- water and salt
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 20 g flour
- 20 g onions
- 30 g ham or bacon scraps or bacon rind without smoke edges
- 1/2 l skim milk, pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cook the potatoes in salted water until done, then peel them. Dice the onions and ham, bacon rind, or bacon.
2. In an earthenware pot, sauté the bacon, rind, or ham and onion cubes, add the flour, then stir in milk, pepper, and salt while stirring. Let the sauce cook down slowly, pass it through a sieve, slice the warm potatoes into it, let them draw in the sauce covered for a while without boiling, and adjust the seasoning with pepper and salt before serving.
3. Remark: Instead of milk, water and 30 g white cheese pressed through and stirred in can be used. (Cheese Potatoes.)

title: Milchküchlein (Milk Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- thick curdled milk
- cream
- flour
- 2 whole eggs
- salt
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Let the thick curdled milk with cream run together in a pan
2. Pour into a colander and let the water drain well
3. In a bowl, stir the curds with 8 loth of flour, 2 whole eggs, and 2 knife points of salt
4. Make cakes as large as a big walnut
5. Fry them in a pan with hot lard until golden yellow
6. They can be served with spinach or other vegetables

title: Milchreis (Milk Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g rice
- 1/4 l whole milk
- 1/2 l water for soaking
- 1 pinch soda
- 1/4 l water
- 1 pinch salt
- 1 piece orange or lemon peel
- 5 g beef fat or margarine
- 5 g flour
- 20 g sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice well with cold water, drain it, bring to a boil in cold water in an earthenware pot, and drain again.
2. Sauté it with 5 g fat, sprinkle the flour over it, and cook briskly uncovered with 1/4 l water, 1/4 l milk, orange or lemon peel, and salt for a few minutes while shaking occasionally. Cover it and let it swell slowly while shaking occasionally, not stirring, until done. Remove the peel, arrange the rice, and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.
3. Leftover uses: Milk Rice Soup, Milk Rice Pudding.

title: Milchreis (Milk Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- 5 g Beef fat or margarine
- 5 g Flour
- 1/4 l Water
- 1/4 l Milk
- Orange or lemon zest
- Salt
- 20 g Sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Rinse the rice thoroughly with cold water, drain it, bring it to a boil with cold water in an earthenware pot, and drain again.
2. Sauté it with 5 g fat, sprinkle flour over it, and cook briskly for several minutes in an open pot with 1/4 l water, 1/4 l milk, orange or lemon zest, and salt, shaking frequently.
3. Cover it and let it simmer slowly, shaking frequently but not stirring, until the rice is tender. Remove the zest.
4. Plate the rice and sprinkle it with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Milchreispudding für 4 Personen (Milk Rice Pudding for 4 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g Rice
- 1 pinch Baking soda
- 1/2 l Water for parboiling
- 1/2 l Whole milk
- 1 pinch Salt
- 65 g Butter or margarine
- 125 g Sugar
- 3 Eggs
- 1 tablespoon Lemon sugar
- 5 g Margarine
- 1 tablespoon Grated breadcrumbs for the mold

## Directions

1. Wash the rice, parboil it, and drain it.
2. Cook the rice in the milk with salt slowly until soft and thick, then chill it.
3. Whip the fat into a cream, add sugar and egg yolks, then add the rice and lemon sugar. Finally, fold in the beaten egg whites.
4. Pour the mixture into a greased and breadcrumb-coated mold, cook it in boiling water for 1 hour, and turn it out onto a hot plate. Serve with fruit or wine foam sauce.
5. You can also use leftover milk rice for the pudding.

title: Milchsagosuppe (Milk Sago Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
- Milk
- 1/4 pound white sago
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Heat milk with a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
2. Cook 1/4 pound white sago in it until one measure of milk has cooked down
3. Add sugar and cinnamon
4. Arrange the soup

title: Milchsauce (Milk Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 l whole milk
- 15 g sugar
- 1 small pinch salt
- 5 g starch flour
- 2 egg yolks
- 4 tablespoons whole milk
- cocoa, almonds, or flavorings as desired

## Directions

1. Heat the sugar, salt, and 1/4 l milk with either 6 bitter almonds, 1/2 packet vanilla sugar, 15 g cocoa, or 1 tablespoon lemon sugar according to taste.
2. Mix the starch flour with the egg yolks and 4 tablespoons of milk, then stir this into the heated mixture while cooking until thickened.
3. Strain through a sieve and stir frequently as it cools to prevent a skin from forming.
4. Note: The egg yolks can be omitted if you increase the starch flour to 6 g instead.

title: Milchspätzlein (Milk Spätzle)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- flour, sufficient amount
- milk
- 4 loth butter
- salt or sugar

## Directions

1. Beat 3 eggs into sufficient flour in a bowl and make a spätzle dough with milk
2. In a pan, bring milk to boil
3. Dip a colander in cold water and press the dough through it into the boiling milk
4. Let it run slowly into the boiling milk, stirring the spätzle often to prevent lumps from forming
5. Add 4 loth butter and let cook well, but keep them moist
6. Season with salt or sugar as desired

title: Milchsuppe (Milk Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l whole milk
- 1 piece lemon or orange peel
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 6 g salt
- 30 g rice, 40 g semolina, flour or cornstarch

## Directions

1. Wash and drain the rice, wash the semolina, and mix the flour with liquid.
2. Bring the milk with spices, sugar and salt to a boil, add the rice, semolina, flour or cornstarch and let it swell under frequent stirring.
3. Before serving, strain the soup through an egg yolk if desired.

title: Milchsuppe mit Mandeln (Milk Soup with Almonds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 Schoppen milk
- One finger-long piece of vanilla
- 4 Loth blanched almonds
- 2 Loth sugar
- Bread

## Directions

1. Put 3 Schoppen milk on the fire
2. Add a finger-long piece of vanilla
3. Let it cook until 1 Schoppen has cooked down
4. Remove the vanilla with a skimming spoon
5. Add 4 Loth blanched and finely pounded almonds and 2 Loth sugar to the milk
6. Remove the hard crust from a piece of bread and cut it into fine soup slices
7. Serve the milk over the bread

title: Milchsuppe mit Reis, Griess, Mehl, Stärkemehl usw. (Milk Soup with Rice, Semolina, Flour, Cornstarch etc.)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l whole milk
- 1 piece lemon or orange peel
- 1 piece cinnamon
- 6 g salt
- 30 g rice, 40 g semolina, flour or cornstarch

## Directions

1. Preparation: The rice is washed and drained, the semolina is washed, the flour is mixed with liquid.
2. To the boiling milk with spices, sugar and salt, add rice, semolina, flour or cornstarch and let it swell under frequent stirring.
3. Before serving, optionally finish the soup with egg yolk.
4. Optional: Chocolate soup - soften 125 g chocolate in the milk, bring to a boil and finish with 20 g stirred cornstarch; the cinnamon can be omitted.

title: Milchsuppe mit Roggenmehl (Rye Flour Milk Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 g rye flour
- 30 g fat or margarine
- 1 l skim milk
- 1 pinch salt
- 10 g sugar

## Directions

1. Sweat the flour in an earthenware pot with the fat, slowly stir in the hot milk and cook for another 20 minutes.
2. Season the soup with sugar and salt.

title: Mostrichsauce, Braune (Brown Mustard Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 g fat
- 20 g onions
- 20 g flour
- 1 spice dose
- 1 l broth or water
- 2 teaspoons mustard
- 1 teaspoon vinegar
- 1/2 teaspoon sugar
- 1 pinch salt and pepper
- 2 drops food coloring

## Directions

1. Weigh the onions.
2. Brown the onions in fat, then sweat them with the flour until light brown. Add the spice and liquid and cook a smooth sauce.
3. Mix the mustard with sugar and vinegar along with a few spoonfuls of the finished sauce so it can blend, then stir it into the whole sauce where it comes to a boil.
4. Strain the sauce through a sieve and season with salt, pepper, and food coloring. Heat once more.
5. This mustard sauce is used for hard eggs (mustard eggs) and also for beef. It can be improved by adding leftover gravy sauce.

title: Muskazine (Nutmeg Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound almonds
- 2 whole eggs
- 1 egg white
- 1/2 pound sifted sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 3 loth finely chopped citron and orange peel
- 1 loth cinnamon
- 1 quint cloves

## Directions

1. Blanch and finely grind the almonds
2. Mix almonds with eggs, egg white, sifted sugar, lemon zest, citron and orange peel, cinnamon, and cloves
3. Stir the mixture together for half an hour
4. Place dough on a noodle board dusted with sugar and flour
5. Dust the molds with sugar and press dough firmly into them
6. Place wafers on a dark baking sheet
7. Turn the Muskazinen out from the molds onto the wafers
8. Bake

title: Mutschelpudding (Muschel Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 quarter pound butter
- 1 quarter pound blanched, finely pounded almonds
- 8 egg yolks
- 6 eggs
- 8 loth fiddle flour
- 1 lemon
- butter
- sugar
- 1/2 pint milk
- 1/2 lemon
- 3 loth sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 glass Maraschino

## Directions

1. Lightly beat 1 quarter pound butter in a bowl, add 1 quarter pound blanched, finely pounded almonds, stir 8 egg yolks in one after another, beat stiff snow from 6 eggs, mix 8 loth fiddle flour with the snow under the mass, grate 1 lemon on the grater and add it as well.
2. Brush a pudding mold with butter and sprinkle with sugar, fill the mass into it and leave it in boiling water 3 quarters of an hour.
3. For the sauce: let 1/2 pint of milk, the zest of half a lemon and 3 loth sugar cook together in a brass pan.
4. Beat 3 egg yolks in a dish, pour 1/2 glass of Maraschino and the boiling milk slowly to it, put it back in the pan and let it thicken, then turn the pudding onto a plate and serve the sauce with it.

title: Muß (Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- milk
- eggs
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Mix milk together with eggs, cook everything until it becomes thick, stirring constantly, and if lard cooks out it must be skimmed off.
2. When the pudding is on the plate, sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on it.

title: Muß von Eierweiß (Egg White Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg whites
- 1 spoonful sugar
- 1 Schoppen milk

## Directions

1. Beat 4 egg whites with 1 spoonful sugar into stiff peaks (you can rub the sugar on a lemon).
2. Cook in a brass pan with 1 Schoppen milk until it begins to thicken.

title: Möhrrüben (Carrots)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) carrots
- 3/4 l water
- 10 g flour
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 1 teaspoon parsley
- 5 g salt
- 5 g sugar

## Directions

1. Wash and clean the carrots and cut into uniform strips, rinse quickly again, and place in a pot with 3/4 l boiling water, salt and sugar.
2. Wash the parsley and chop finely.
3. Place the carrots on the stove and cook covered until soft.
4. Make a roux with the fat and flour, add one spoon of carrot broth while stirring and bring to a boil.
5. Pour the roux over the dish, let it come to a boil again, season to taste and add the parsley.

title: Möhrrüben, Brechbohnen und Kartoffeln (Carrots, Snap Beans and Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g carrots
- 125 g snap beans
- 250 g potatoes
- 1 l water
- 1 tablespoon lean bacon cubes
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 5 g salt
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the carrots as described above, but cut into cubes, as well as the peeled broken beans.
2. Cook the carrots, beans and potatoes in salted water until soft.
3. Mix the vegetables with the fried bacon and vinegar and season with pepper and salt.

title: Münchner Kugelhopf (Munich Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- 12 eggs (warmed in lukewarm water)
- 4 loths of finely ground sugar
- the yellow of one lemon, grated on the grater
- half a schoppen of thick sweet cream
- 12 tablespoons of very fine flour
- 3 loths of raisins
- 2 loths of finely cut citron
- 3 spoons of good beer yeast

## Directions

1. Let half a pound of butter melt in a bowl
2. Stir until very foamy
3. Gradually stir in 12 eggs (that have been warmed in lukewarm water) along with 4 loths of finely ground sugar
4. Add the yellow of one lemon grated on the grater
5. Stir in half a schoppen of thick sweet cream
6. Mix in 12 tablespoons of very fine flour, 3 loths of raisins, 2 loths of finely cut citron, and 3 spoons of good beer yeast
7. Mix everything well together
8. Brush a kugelhopf mold with butter
9. Fill in the mass
10. Let it rise well in gentle warmth
11. Bake the kugelhopf in the oven at not high heat

title: Münchner Springerlein (Munich Springerlein)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- rose water
- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1/8 pound starch flour
- flour and sugar for dusting
- wax
- lemon or cinnamon icing

## Directions

1. Grind 1/2 pound blanched almonds finely with rose water
2. Stir them together with 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar and 1/8 pound starch flour in a brass pan until the dough pulls away from the sides
3. Transfer to a pastry board and dust with flour and sugar
4. Dust the wooden molds with sifted sugar and press the dough into them
5. Turn out the molds and place the springerlein on a black baking sheet brushed with wax
6. Let stand overnight or for at least 2 to 3 hours
7. Bake at gentle heat and top with lemon or cinnamon icing if desired

title: Münchner Waffeln (Munich Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 16 loth flour
- lukewarm milk
- 6 eggs
- 2 knife-points of pounded cinnamon
- 1/2 lemon, minced
- 8 blanched bitter almonds
- 1 tablespoon sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter until foamy.
2. Stir 16 loth flour smooth in a bowl with lukewarm milk.
3. Add 6 eggs, one after another, 2 knife-points of pounded cinnamon, half a minced lemon, and 8 blanched bitter almonds.
4. Stir all this together with 1 tablespoon sugar under the butter until nothing of the butter is visible.
5. Bake golden in a waffle iron and sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top.

title: Mürbeteig für Obstkuchen und kleine Kuchen (Shortbread Dough for Fruit Cake and Small Cakes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 90 g Sugar
- 4 Egg yolks
- 1/2 Grated lemon
- 2 tablespoons Skim milk
- 220 g Margarine
- 1/2 kg (1 pound) Flour
- 1 Egg for brushing
- 1 Piece of bacon rind for the baking sheet

## Directions

1. Combine the sugar, lemon, milk, and egg yolks, then rub them into the margarine beaten to a foam. Add the flour last.
2. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a knife back, cut out small cakes or tartlets (these must be provided with a rim), brush them with egg, and sprinkle them, depending on how you wish to use them, with lengthwise-cut almonds, currants, or sugar and cinnamon.
3. Bake them on a greased baking sheet to a good color.

title: Nachgeahmte Makaroni (Imitation Macaroni)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- flour
- leftover veal roast
- white bread
- spinach
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Take 3 eggs and as much flour as they can take and work them together
2. Roll it out very nicely
3. For the filling: take leftover veal roast and pound it as fine as possible
4. Cut the crust off white bread (worth 2 kreuzer) and soak it in water
5. Scald a handful of cleanly washed spinach and pound it as fine as possible
6. Take it together with the meat and also the yolk of 3 hard-boiled eggs
7. Let a piece of butter melt in a casserole, put everything together in the casserole, press the bread out firmly and take it too, add 2 whole eggs, salt and nutmeg
8. Cook everything together on the fire for 6 minutes
9. Spread it on the cakes very thinly, roll them together but not thicker than a quill, cut them into finger-length pieces (they must look just like Italian macaroni)
10. Simmer them in meat broth, pour the broth into a soup bowl, add the macaroni to it and serve it at table

title: Napfkuchen (Bowl Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 175 g Flour
- 15 g Yeast
- 6 tablespoons Skim milk
- 30 g Beef fat or margarine
- 1 Egg
- 40 g Sugar
- 1 teaspoon Lemon sugar
- 8 g Salt
- 40 g Large and small raisins

## Directions

1. Prepare the Napfkuchen the same way as Hefepudding, but in an earthenware bowl greased with fat, dip beaten egg white and guide it around the edges of the rounds. Fold over the other side of the dough.
2. Using a glass, cut out small round cakes over the marked mounds and let them rise once more on a floured board, then bake them carefully in steaming fat. A wooden stick inserted will show when they are done.
3. The pancakes must not brown too quickly so they bake through completely. When finished baking, place them on a sieve lined with blotting paper and turn them in sugar (and cinnamon if desired).
4. Always bake only a few pancakes at a time. The fillings can be made from any fruit paste. This must not be thin, as it will run out in the heat.
5. Instead of 30 g yeast, 20 g baking powder (1 packet) can also be used.

title: Neunlothpudding (Nine-Loth Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 weak schoppen of water
- 6 loth butter
- 6 loth sugar
- half a lemon, zest grated
- 6 loth finely ground almonds
- 6 loth fine flour
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from 6 eggs

## Directions

1. In a brass pan, cook together the water, butter, sugar with grated lemon zest, ground almonds, and flour into a thick batter
2. Place the mass in a bowl and add the 6 egg yolks one after another
3. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in last
4. Brush a pudding mold with butter and sprinkle with sugar
5. Fill the mass into the mold and cook in boiling water for one hour
6. For the hagebutten sauce: stir together 2 spoonfuls of hagebutten puree and 1 spoonful of fine flour in a brass pan
7. Pour 1 schoppen of good wine to it, add sugar and let it come to boil
8. When the pudding is on the plate, pour the sauce alongside it

title: Nierenbraten (Kidney Roast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One kidney roast
- Cloth
- Pepper
- Salt
- String
- Paper
- Onions
- Yellow turnips
- Lemon
- Fat
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. From a kidney roast, remove the lobe from the front and the streak from the back.
2. Rub clean with a cloth.
3. Season well with pepper and salt.
4. Let sit for one hour.
5. Roll it together and bind tightly with string.
6. Roast on a spit or in a roasting oven.
7. Wrap in paper.
8. Add cut onions, yellow turnips, and some lemon.
9. Add considerable fat and roast slowly.
10. When serving, remove the paper, discard the fat, and strain the sauce through a hair sieve with some meat broth.

title: Nierenbraten auf englische Art (Kidney Roast English Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- kidney roast
- milk
- cloves
- white peppercorns
- mace
- thyme
- basil
- bay leaves
- onions, sliced
- yellow carrots
- parsley
- parsnip
- shallots
- various roots
- flour
- salt
- butter
- clear jus

## Directions

1. Place the kidney roast in a casserole and cover completely with milk.
2. Add cloves, white peppercorns, mace, thyme, basil, several bay leaves, sliced onions, yellow carrots, parsley, parsnip, shallots, and various other roots.
3. Let the roast stand overnight with the herbs.
4. The next day, 2 hours before serving, remove it from the milk and roast it on a spit.
5. Sprinkle it with flour and salt, and baste it frequently with butter so it achieves a beautiful color.
6. Place it on a platter and pour clear jus over it.

title: Nonnenkrapfen (Nun's Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds grated black bread
- 2 pounds grated brown Nuremberg gingerbread
- 3 knife points of ground cloves
- 1 loth ground cinnamon
- Cardamom for 6 kreuzer
- Finely ground cubeb pepper
- 1/2 schoppen honey
- Flour

## Directions

1. Toast the grated black bread on a dark baking sheet in the oven until golden
2. Grate the Nuremberg gingerbread on a grater
3. Mix bread, grated gingerbread, cloves, cinnamon, cardamom, cubeb pepper, and honey in a bowl to form a stiff dough
4. Let dough rest covered
5. Make a dough on a floured noodle board like cut noodles
6. Roll it out knife-back thick
7. Place a spoonful of the bread mixture on the dough, leaving a finger's width empty around it
8. Fold the dough over and seal
9. Cut into round fritters with a pastry wheel
10. Place on a dark baking sheet
11. Dry in the oven; they should remain completely white

title: Nudeln (Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Flour as needed
- 3 egg whites
- 1 knife-tip of salt
- 1 large hen's egg of butter
- Milk
- 1 large piece of butter
- Sour cream (optional)
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take flour as needed on the noodle board, beat 3 egg whites together with 1 knife-tip of salt, 1 large hen's egg of butter and milk until the dough can be worked
2. Roll it with the hand as thin as a quill and 1 ell long
3. Make milk hot in a plafond, add a large piece of butter along with the noodles and let them cook together
4. They should have some sauce; you can also add sour cream along with sugar and cinnamon

title: Nudelnauflauf (Noodle Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 eggs
- butter
- 4 Loth butter
- 3 tablespoons sour cream
- 6 Loth ground sugar
- 1 lemon
- 5 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make very fine noodles from 3 eggs
2. Brown them in a piece of butter and let them drain in a hair sieve
3. Melt 4 Loth of butter in a bowl
4. Mix this with 3 tablespoons of sour cream, 6 Loth of ground sugar grated on a lemon, 5 egg yolks, and finally the snow from these
5. Combine everything with the noodles
6. Fill the mass into a buttered bowl
7. Bake the soufflé beautifully golden and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Nudelnauflauf anderer Art (Noodle Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 whole egg
- 1 egg yolk
- butter
- noodle dough
- milk
- 6 peeled good apples
- 4 Loth raisins
- 4 Loth ground sugar
- butter
- 3 eggs
- half a Schoppen sour cream
- half a Schoppen sweet milk
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a noodle dough from one whole egg, one yolk, and butter the size of a hen's egg
2. Roll out the cakes as finely as possible but do not cut them very finely
3. Cook them in milk so they are moist
4. Cut 6 peeled good apples into small pieces
5. Take 4 Loth of raisins and 4 Loth of ground sugar
6. Butter a dish and lay noodles in the bottom, then cut apples and raisins, noodles, apples and raisins, and continue until everything is used
7. Beat 3 eggs in a dish, add half a Schoppen of sour cream and half a Schoppen of sweet milk
8. Stir it together, pour it over the noodles
9. Bake them and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Nudelntorte (Noodle Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 pound finely ground sugar
- 5 whole eggs
- 2 eggs for making noodles
- hot lard or butter
- 1 lemon
- 4 loth citron
- 4 loth orange peel
- 4 egg whites
- sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Coarsely ground half of the 1/2 pound blanched almonds; cut the other half lengthwise
2. Press the juice of 1 lemon onto the almonds and stir with 1/2 pound finely ground sugar and 5 whole eggs for a quarter hour
3. Make fine noodles from 2 eggs, cut as finely as possible, and fry golden yellow in hot lard or butter
4. Grate 1 lemon on a grater
5. Finely chop 4 loth citron and orange peel and mix into the mixture
6. Beat 4 egg whites into stiff peaks and fold into the mixture
7. Butter a torte mold with sugar and butter
8. Fill the mixture into the mold and bake the torte at gentle heat until thoroughly done

title: Nudelpudding mit Morcheln (Noodle Pudding with Morels)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 1 schoppen sour cream
- 3 whole eggs
- 2 cut thymus glands
- 1 large handful morels
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 1 piece of butter
- 1 finely minced onion
- Minced lemon zest
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- Cold water
- 1 ladle spoonful meat broth
- 1/2 glass wine
- Juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Make noodles from 2 eggs
2. Pour salt water over them
3. Draw them through cold water and let drain
4. Stir 1 schoppen sour cream, 3 whole eggs, 2 cut thymus glands, 1 large handful morels, salt and nutmeg together with the noodles
5. Bind everything in a wet cloth
6. Cook the dumpling for one hour in boiling salt water
7. For the butter sauce: let a piece of butter, 1 finely minced onion and minced lemon zest run in a small casserole with 1 cooking spoon flour until golden
8. Rinse it with cold water
9. Pour 1 ladle spoonful of meat broth, 1/2 glass wine and the juice of 1/2 lemon over it
10. Let everything cook together for a quarter hour
11. Place the pudding on a plate and serve the sauce with it

title: Nudelsuppe (Noodle Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 40 g shaped noodles
- 1 l water
- 1 pinch salt
- 5 g butter
- 6 drops Maggi seasoning

## Directions

1. Rinse the noodles in cold water.
2. Cook the noodles in salted water to which the butter has been added until done and drain.
3. Before serving, add the Maggi seasoning to the soup without cooking again.

title: Nürnberger Lebkuchen (Nuremberg Gingerbread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas honey
- 1/2 pound ground sugar
- 1 pound blanched almonds cut crosswise
- Zest of 2 lemons
- 4 loth citron
- 4 loth orange peel
- 1 loth ground cinnamon
- 1/2 quint cloves
- Cardamom for 1 kreuzer
- Cubeb pepper for 1 kreuzer
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 2 pounds flour
- Egg for glazing

## Directions

1. Heat honey in a brass pan; when it begins to boil add ground sugar
2. Let cook together for 8 minutes
3. Cut blanched almonds crosswise and mix in
4. Let cook for one more minute and transfer to a bowl
5. Add lemon zest, citron, orange peel (not finely chopped), cinnamon, cloves, cardamom, cubeb pepper, and grated nutmeg
6. Stir flour into the honey mixture
7. While still warm, place on a noodle board and dust the mold with flour
8. Press dough into the mold to about the thickness of a small finger, turn out, and place on a flour-dusted dark baking sheet
9. Brush with egg and bake in the oven
10. Optional: add 1 knife point potash to the dough

title: Nürnberger Traubenkuchen (Nuremberg Grape Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound fresh butter
- 1/2 pound finely powdered sugar
- 8 egg yolks
- powdered cinnamon to taste
- 6 to 8 handfuls beautiful ripe grape berries
- 8 egg whites
- 1 spoonful flour

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound fresh butter until very foamy
2. Add 1/2 pound finely powdered sugar and 8 egg yolks one after another
3. Add powdered cinnamon to taste
4. Add 6 to 8 handfuls of beautiful ripe grape berries to the mixture
5. Beat the 8 egg whites into stiff snow
6. Mix the stiff snow and 1 spoonful flour loosely into the mixture
7. Brush a baking dish with butter, fill it with the mixture, and bake until beautifully golden

title: Ochsenfleisch anderer Art (Beef Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the breastbone
- Salt and pepper
- Melted butter
- Lemon juice
- Horseradish

## Directions

1. Let a piece from the breastbone cook tender
2. Remove it
3. Sprinkle it with salt and pepper
4. Place it on a grate
5. Pour melted butter and lemon juice over it
6. Let it roast beautifully golden
7. Arrange it on a plate
8. Serve with horseradish or arrange it over the top

title: Ochsenfleisch anderer Art (Beef Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- Bacon and ham for larding
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Basil
- Celery herb
- 1 whole lemon, cut into leaves
- Bay leaves
- Provençal oil
- Water

## Directions

1. Lard a piece of beef with bacon and ham
2. Place half of the following herbs in a casserole: parsley, thyme, basil, celery herb, 1 whole lemon cut into leaves, bay leaves
3. Place the meat on top
4. Cover with the other half of the herbs
5. Pour some Provençal oil over the meat
6. Let it lie in the herbs overnight
7. The next day, pour water over it
8. Let the meat cook tender on coals
9. Arrange it
10. Pass the jus through a fine sieve over it
11. Garnish with parsley

title: Ochsenfleisch anderer Art (Beef Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the ribs or the so-called baron
- Various herbs and roots
- 1 glass of wine
- Half a glass of vinegar
- Half a tablespoon of meat broth or water
- Meat broth for finishing
- Lemon

## Directions

1. Take a piece of beef from the ribs or the so-called baron
2. Remove the bones nicely
3. Place the meat in a casserole in which a piece of fat, whatever fat it may be, has melted
4. Add various herbs and roots
5. Pour 1 glass of wine, half a glass of vinegar, and half a tablespoon of meat broth or water over it
6. Let it cook slowly over weak coals
7. Turn it often during cooking
8. When it is done, remove the fat
9. Pour another tablespoon of meat broth over it
10. Pass the broth through a fine sieve
11. Let it come to a boil once more in the casserole
12. Add lemon when arranging

title: Ochsenfleisch auf böhmische Art (Beef Bohemian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the breast or from the thick loin
- Salt and pepper
- 1 tablespoon of meat broth
- White beer
- Half a Schoppen wine
- Some vinegar
- Yellow zest of half a lemon
- Juice of half a lemon
- 2 Loth (approximately 14 grams) blanched almonds
- A piece of brown gingerbread
- A little sugar

## Directions

1. Rub a piece of beef from the breast or thick loin well with salt and pepper
2. Roast it on a grate until it is half done
3. Let it cook tender in a casserole with 1 tablespoon of meat broth, white beer, half a Schoppen wine, some vinegar, the finely minced yellow zest of half a lemon, and its juice
4. Cut 2 Loth of blanched almonds lengthwise
5. Grate a piece of brown gingerbread
6. Add this and a little sugar to the meat
7. Arrange the meat on a plate and pour the sauce over it

title: Ochsenfleisch auf englische Art (Beef Cooked English Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from the brisket
- 2 Maas water
- 3 onions
- 2 bay leaves
- celery root, cut into pieces
- yellow turnips
- whole cloves
- 3 Maas sweet cream
- parsley for garnish

## Directions

1. Boil a piece of beef brisket in 2 Maas water.
2. Add 3 onions, 2 bay leaves, a celery root cut into pieces, yellow turnips, and whole cloves.
3. When the meat is half tender, pour 3 Maas sweet cream over it.
4. Let it finish cooking in the cream.
5. Arrange it on a platter.
6. Pass the sauce through a hair sieve over it and garnish the platter with parsley.

title: Ochsenfleisch auf italienische Art (Beef Italian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef from the inner thigh or a fatty tail piece
- Bacon and ham for larding
- Salt and pepper
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 onions
- Yellow carrots
- Celery
- 1 Schoppen (approximately 0.95 liters) wine
- Half a Maass (approximately 1.9 liters) water
- A piece of bacon
- A piece of ham
- 2 cloves of garlic
- Basil
- Thyme
- Tarragon
- Chervil
- 1 tablespoon of jus
- 1 tablespoon flour
- Butter
- Juice of half a lemon

## Directions

1. Lard a piece of beef from the inner thigh or a fatty tail piece with bacon and ham
2. Turn it in salt and pepper
3. Place the meat in a casserole with 2 bay leaves, 3 onions, yellow carrots, and celery
4. Set it over a strong coal fire
5. Pour 1 Schoppen wine and half a Maass water over it
6. Let it stand for 2 to 3 hours
7. Pound very finely a piece of bacon, a piece of ham, 2 cloves of garlic, basil, thyme, tarragon, and chervil
8. When the meat is tender, place it in another casserole
9. Remove the fat from the meat broth
10. Pour the broth back over the meat
11. Add the pounded mixture and 1 tablespoon of jus
12. Let everything come to a boil together
13. Arrange the meat and pour the sauce over it
14. For a French and German style variation: let 1 tablespoon of flour turn yellow in a piece of butter, pour 1 Schoppen wine and 1 tablespoon of good jus over it, let it cook strongly with the meat, arrange the meat, pass the sauce through a fine sieve over it, and squeeze the juice of half a lemon over it

title: Ochsenfleisch auf polnische Art (Beef Polish Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- A few onions, one of which is studded with 4 cloves
- A celery root
- 1 bay leaf
- A little mace (nutmeg flower)
- Bacon to cover the meat
- Salt
- 2 tablespoons of water
- Several heads of savoy cabbage
- A little jus

## Directions

1. Place a piece of beef in a dish
2. Add a few onions, one studded with 4 cloves, a celery root, 1 bay leaf, and a little mace
3. Cover the meat with bacon
4. Salt it
5. Pour 2 tablespoons of water over it
6. Let it simmer slowly together
7. Bind several cleaned savoy cabbage heads tightly together with string so they remain whole
8. Blanch them slightly
9. Place them in fresh water for a while
10. Add them to the beef when it is somewhat tender
11. Let everything cook together until only a little jus remains with the meat
12. Place the meat on a plate
13. Let the cabbage drain on a sieve
14. Remove the strings
15. Arrange the heads around the meat
16. Pour a little jus over the dish

title: Ochsenfleisch gut zu sieden (Beef Boiled Well)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beef from the brisket or chuck
- cold water
- onions
- yellow turnips
- celery
- salt
- jus

## Directions

1. Pound the beef slightly and tie it with twine.
2. Place it in cold water on the fire and cover it.
3. When it is time to skim, remove the foam from the top until it no longer foams.
4. Cut onions, yellow turnips, and celery into it and let it cook very slowly until tender.
5. Salt it to taste half an hour before serving.
6. Pour a ladleful of jus onto the platter before arranging the meat on it.

title: Ochsenfleisch mit einer Kruste (Beef with a Crust)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Breast or rib piece of beef
- One-quarter pound of rice
- Fatty meat broth
- One-quarter pound Parmesan cheese
- One-quarter pound crayfish butter
- Three cleaned and boned anchovies
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Remove the flap from the bottom and boil the meat until very tender
2. Prepare crayfish butter as described elsewhere in the cookbook
3. Heat one-quarter pound of rice in a casserole with a little fatty meat broth and let it swell
4. Add a little more meat broth and when soft, pound it in a mortar to a paste
5. Transfer to a bowl and mix in one-quarter pound Parmesan cheese and one-quarter pound crayfish butter
6. Pound three cleaned and boned anchovies in a mortar and add them to the mixture
7. Stir everything together for seven and a half minutes
8. Rub the beef on a board with salt, pepper, and nutmeg
9. Spread the rice mixture the thickness of a knife-back onto the beef, on all sides and on top
10. Place the meat on wooden pieces in a roasting pan
11. Roast until beautifully browned in the oven and pour a tablespoon of meat broth over it

title: Ochsenfleisch mit feinen Kräutern (Beef with Fine Herbs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of beef
- 6 shallot onions
- Yellow zest and marrow of 1 lemon
- Basil
- Thyme
- Tarragon
- 2 tablespoons of capers
- Butter
- 3 fingers' breadth of flour
- Half a tablespoon of meat broth
- Melted consommé

## Directions

1. Cook a piece of beef tender
2. Mince finely 6 shallot onions, the yellow zest and marrow of 1 lemon, basil, thyme, tarragon, and 2 tablespoons of capers
3. Braise the minced mixture with a piece of butter and 3 fingers' breadth of flour
4. Place the meat on the herbs
5. Let everything braise together for half an hour
6. Pour half a tablespoon of meat broth over it
7. Arrange the herbs on a plate
8. Place the meat on top
9. Coat it with melted consommé

title: Ochsenfleisch mit Kräutersauce (Beef with Herb Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A good piece of beef
- Butter
- Yellow zest of 1 lemon
- Marrow of 1 lemon
- A handful of black bread
- A handful of chervil
- Parsley
- Celery
- 1 Schoppen red wine
- 1 tablespoon of jus or meat broth
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Cut a good piece of beef into finger-thick pieces
2. Pound them slightly
3. Place them with a piece of butter in a casserole
4. Finely mince the yellow zest and marrow of 1 lemon, leaving away only the white skin
5. Grate a handful of black bread
6. Mince finely a handful of chervil with parsley and celery
7. Mix this with the grated bread
8. Add it to the meat
9. Pour 1 Schoppen red wine and 1 tablespoon of jus or meat broth over it
10. Add salt, pepper, and nutmeg, but very little
11. Set it to the fire and let it cook for one hour
12. After arranging, pour the sauce over it
13. Note: all meat prepared in this or similar manner is more flavorful if you do not cook it too long and do not salt it heavily

title: Ochsenfleisch mit Sauerampfer (Beef with Sorrel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A juicy piece of beef
- Cold water
- Salt
- Pepper
- Four anchovies
- Lemon
- A large piece of fat
- Sliced onion
- Sorrel
- Butter
- Minced onion
- Parsley
- One tablespoon of flour
- Nutmeg
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Put the beef in cold water and bring to a boil; when cooked, remove to a board
2. Rub with salt and pepper
3. Clean four anchovies, make holes in the meat with a knife, and place one anchovy and a piece of lemon in each hole
4. Heat a large piece of fat in a casserole, add sliced onion and the meat
5. Roast until beautifully browned in the oven
6. Pour hot water over the sorrel, let it stand for fifteen minutes, and press it out
7. Mince the sorrel finely
8. Heat a piece of butter in a casserole with minced onion, parsley, and one tablespoon of flour
9. When brown, add the sorrel and turn everything over
10. Add grated nutmeg, salt, and pepper to taste, and a tablespoon of meat broth
11. Let it come to a boil once more
12. Arrange on a platter and place the roasted meat on top
13. The sauce can also be served separately

title: Ochsenfleisch mit Trüffeln (Beef with Truffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beef
- Potatoes
- Butter
- One Schoppen of sour cream
- Three to four large truffles

## Directions

1. Roast the beef beautifully in a casserole or in the oven
2. Peel potatoes raw and wash them clean
3. Cook them half-soft with a piece of butter
4. Pour one Schoppen of sour cream over them
5. Clean three to four large truffles with a brush
6. Cut off the rough skin and slice the truffles into thin pieces
7. Add the truffles to the potatoes
8. Let everything cook for another fifteen minutes
9. Arrange on a platter, place the meat on top, and pour some of the sauce over the meat

title: Ochsenmarkküchlein (Beef Marrow Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beef marrow
- 4 eggs
- flour
- ham
- bread
- hot lard for frying

## Directions

1. Melt a quarter pound of beef marrow
2. Strain through a sieve into a bowl
3. Beat it very foamy
4. Add 4 eggs one after another
5. Mix in 4 spoonfuls of flour and a quarter pound of minced ham
6. Grate bread onto a board
7. Make round cakes from the mass
8. Fry them in lard until golden yellow

title: Ochsenzungen gut zu räuchern (Ox Tongues for Smoking)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ox tongues
- Rock salt
- Saltpeter
- Pounded pepper
- Juniper berries
- Thyme
- Basil
- Celery
- All kinds of herbs

## Directions

1. Cut the gullet from the ox tongues
2. Roast rock salt and saltpeter together with pounded pepper and juniper berries
3. Rub the tongues thoroughly with this mixture
4. Place them in a dish
5. Add all kinds of herbs, thyme, basil, and celery
6. Leave them in the brine for 4 weeks
7. Weight them down with a well-fitting lid
8. Hang them in a large intestine casing in smoke to be smoked
9. To cook them, soak overnight in cold water
10. Cook them with cold water, onions, and all kinds of herbs until tender
11. Serve cold or warm

title: Oelsauce für 4 Personen (Oil Sauce for 4 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 g oil
- 20 g onions
- 20 g flour
- 3/4 l broth
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 tablespoon herbs, chives, parsley
- salt to taste
- 1 pinch white pepper

## Directions

1. Weigh the herbs and blanch them. Cut the onions into small pieces.
2. Sweat the oil, onions, and flour together, cook with 1/4 l broth and 1 tablespoon vinegar, strain through a sieve, and cool while stirring. Add the herbs and enough broth so the sauce has the right thickness.
3. Season with salt, pepper, and vinegar.
4. The sauce can be refined with chopped capers, pickles, mustard, and beaten egg yolk.

title: Oesterreichischer Pfannenkuchen (Austrian Pancake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- 2 to 3 onions
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- Milk
- 3 eggs
- Meat of any kind

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into cubes and let it melt in a pancake pan
2. Cut 2 to 3 onions lengthwise and add to the bacon
3. Mix 1 cooking spoon flour with milk and beat 3 eggs into it
4. Cut meat of any kind into small pieces and lay it in the pan
5. Pour the dough over the meat
6. Cover the pan and bake the pancake on both sides

title: Ofenküchlein (Oven Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen water
- 4 loth butter
- 2 loth sugar
- 10 loth fine flour
- 4 to 5 eggs
- Jam or chopped almonds
- Sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Boil water, butter, and sugar together in a brass pan
2. Stir in fine flour and cook until the dough pulls away from the pan
3. Transfer dough to a bowl and stir in eggs one at a time
4. Place on a dark, flour-dusted baking sheet
5. Sprinkle with jam, chopped almonds, and sugar
6. Bake in gentle heat

title: Ordinäre Wassersuppe (Ordinary Water Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- bread
- boiling water
- lard
- onions
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut very fine slices of bread, lay them in the bowl
2. Pour boiling water over them
3. Let the soup slowly come to a boil
4. Finish it with hot lard in which onions have been roasted golden beforehand

title: Panadesuppe (Panade Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Mundbrot (mouth bread)
- Cold water
- 3 Loth butter
- Eggs
- Milk
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Chives

## Directions

1. Cut 4 Mundbrot as for a soup
2. Put it in a casserole, pour cold water over it and let it cook with 3 Loth butter for half an hour
3. Pass it through a hair sieve into the casserole and let it come to a boil once more
4. Add salt and nutmeg
5. Beat 2 eggs into the soup bowl
6. Pour a little milk over them
7. Serve the soup over and sprinkle with cut chives and nutmeg

title: Pariser Kugelhopf (Parisian Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- 2 spoons of good beer yeast
- 3 tablespoons of lukewarm milk
- one loth of bitter almonds
- one schoppen of milk
- 10 loths of butter
- 6 eggs
- 4 loths of ground sugar
- one knife point of salt
- sugar and almonds for dusting

## Directions

1. Place one pound of flour in a bowl
2. Stir a small dough in the center of the bowl from 2 spoons of good beer yeast and 3 tablespoons of lukewarm milk
3. Let it rise in gentle warmth
4. Grind one loth of bitter almonds very fine with one schoppen of milk
5. Pour through a hair sieve, pressing the almonds firmly
6. Set the almond milk in a warm place
7. Stir 10 loths of butter in a bowl until foamy
8. Gradually stir in 6 eggs, 4 loths of ground sugar, and one knife point of salt
9. Complete the dough with the almond milk
10. Beat for a quarter hour
11. Brush a kugelhopf mold with butter
12. Dust with sugar and almonds
13. Fill the dough into it
14. Bake in the oven

title: Pastete auf englische Art (Pastry in the English Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- hare meat
- mutton fat from a leg
- half a pound beef marrow
- 1 pound tender veal
- 2 lemons
- pepper
- nutmeg
- nutmeg flower
- 2 to 3 cleaned truffles
- 4 to 6 mushrooms
- 2 eggs
- salt
- butter pastry
- bacon strips
- game salami sauce

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from the hare meat
2. Cut the fat from a mutton leg
3. Chop half a pound of beef marrow and 1 pound of tender veal together into a paste and put in a bowl
4. Peel 2 lemons, finely chop the zest and pulp and add to the mixture
5. Add 2 knife-tips each of pepper, nutmeg and nutmeg flower
6. Add 2 to 3 cleaned truffles, not too finely pounded, and 4 to 6 chopped mushrooms
7. Beat in 2 eggs, salt to taste and work everything together well
8. Line a melon mold with butter pastry
9. Fill in the mixture, cover the top with bacon strips
10. Cover with a lid, seal with a pastry crimper and bake in the oven until golden
11. Turn onto a plate and make a small opening on top
12. Cook a good game salami sauce, pour it around the pastry and serve warm

title: Pastete auf italienische Art (Pastry in the Italian Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- lamb breast
- butter
- 1 onion
- fine flour
- good bouillon
- parsley roots
- water
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Lightly boil lamb breast
2. Cut into small pieces
3. Braise in a casserole with butter and 1 whole onion
4. Cook with fine flour and good bouillon
5. Cut parsley roots into small pieces, boil slightly in water
6. Add to the meat along with a little nutmeg and salt
7. Ensure the sauce and meat are white and fill into the pastry

title: Pastete mit weißer Farce (Pastry with White Farce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- poultry
- bouillon
- béchamel

## Directions

1. Prepare the farce from poultry, as often explained
2. Make round or long dumplings from it, boil them in bouillon
3. Place them dry with béchamel in a casserole so they become quite hot and place them in the pastry

title: Pastete von Makaroni (Macaroni Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound macaroni
- butter
- 1 pound finely chopped lean ham
- 1 pint sour cream
- 3 eggs
- quarter pound grated Parmesan cheese
- butter pastry
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Boil half a pound of macaroni with a piece of butter until very soft
2. Drain
3. Mix together 1 pound finely chopped lean ham, 1 pint sour cream, 3 eggs and quarter pound grated Parmesan cheese
4. Stir everything together with the macaroni
5. Line a mold with butter pastry
6. Fill with the mixture, make a lid
7. Brush with egg yolk, bake well in the oven and turn the pastry onto a plate

title: Pellkartoffeln (Potatoes Boiled in their Skins)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) potatoes
- 1/4 l water
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Wash the potatoes (preferably of equal size) thoroughly with cold water and place in a pot (approximately 1-1/2 l capacity) with cold water and salt.
2. Cook covered until done, drain, shake in the pot, and place for 1/2 minute on the hot plate with the lid half-open so the potatoes dry and burst.
3. New potatoes require about 20 minutes to cook, old potatoes 1/2 hour or more. Test by piercing with a fork to ensure they are soft throughout.

title: Petersilie in Salz (Parsley in Salt)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Fresh parsley
- Salt

## Directions

1. Lay fresh parsley in the sun for 1 day and then pack it firmly layer by layer with salt in glasses, which are sealed according to instructions.

title: Petersiliensauce (Parsley Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l water, broth, fish water, or vegetable water
- 20 g flour
- 20 g margarine
- 10 g onions
- 1 teaspoon parsley
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Weigh the onion and parsley.
2. In the margarine, first sweat the onion until cooked but only light yellow, then add the flour. Slowly stir in the liquid, cook the whole into a bound white sauce, add the parsley, and season with pepper and salt.
3. If you use 1 teaspoon of chopped dill instead of parsley, you get dill sauce. Adding chopped chives or chives mixed with parsley gives chive sauce, and mixed herbs give herb sauce.

title: Petersiliensauce (Parsley Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one handful of fresh, cleaned parsley
- half a stirring spoonful of flour
- a piece of butter
- good meat broth
- egg yolks

## Directions

1. Chop the parsley very finely
2. Knead the flour into a piece of butter
3. Mix the parsley into the butter mixture
4. Stir everything over the fire with good meat broth
5. Let it cook a little and finish with egg yolks before serving
6. This sauce is useful for many meat dishes

title: Pfaffenbrod (Priest's Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg white
- 3 egg yolks
- 8 loth finely ground sugar
- 8 loth blanched and finely chopped almonds
- 5 loth flour
- Zest of 1/2 lemon
- Flour and sugar for the board
- Lard for the baking sheet

## Directions

1. Beat the egg white into stiff peaks
2. Stir in egg yolks and finely ground sugar and beat together for a quarter hour
3. Fold in blanched almonds, flour, and lemon zest
4. Dust the noodle board with flour and sugar and roll out dough knife-back thick
5. Cut into finger-long pieces and two fingers wide
6. Place on a lard-greased sheet and let stand for a quarter hour so the sugar rises
7. Bake in gentle oven heat

title: Pfaffenkappen (Priest's Caps)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound blanched almonds
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 2 eggs
- 2 egg yolks
- Zest of 1 lemon
- Butter dough
- Egg for glazing
- Coarsely ground sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat eggs and egg yolks with almonds and sugar and mix with lemon zest until thick like an almond cake
2. Roll out butter dough knife-back thick
3. Cut with a warm knife into strips 3 fingers wide and half a quarter-ell long
4. Place a spoonful of almond mixture in the center of each strip
5. Fold the two opposite corners diagonally over the center
6. Brush with egg
7. Fold the other two corners diagonally over the center to form a cap shape
8. Brush again with egg and sprinkle with coarse sugar
9. Place on a dark baking sheet and bake

title: Pfannenkuchen mit Gänseleber (Pancakes with Goose Liver)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- goose liver
- 6 to 8 shallot onions
- a handful parsley
- half a lemon
- 4 rolls
- 1 Schoppen milk
- butter
- 3 finely chopped shallot onions
- 1 spoonful flour
- 4 eggs
- salt
- lard

## Directions

1. Chop the goose liver fine, peel 6 to 8 shallot onions and a handful of parsley, steam these together for a while.
2. Add the chopped goose liver and steam everything once more.
3. Press the juice from half a lemon and the chopped yellow from another half lemon to it.
4. Cut 4 rolls as fine as for soup.
5. In a brass pan let 1 Schoppen milk with a piece of butter boil up.
6. Pour this onto the cut rolls, cover and let them stand a little.
7. Take 3 finely chopped shallot onions and 1 spoonful flour to the rolls, also 4 eggs, one after another, and a little salt.
8. Make lard hot in a flat pan, pour half the batter into it.
9. In the middle comes the goose liver, on top the other half of the batter.
10. Bake all like a cake; when done on one side, turn it and bake it on the other side also.

title: Pfefferlinge oder Steinpilze (Chanterelles or Porcini Mushrooms)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 lb) chanterelles or porcini mushrooms
- 20 g salt
- 2 l water for blanching
- 30 g fat or margarine
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- 5 g flour
- 1/2 tablespoon chopped parsley

## Directions

1. Clean the mushrooms, rinse quickly, cut small if necessary (but do not soak in water), place in hot salted water, blanch, and drain as dry as possible on a sieve.
2. Pour the blanched mushrooms into hot fat, dust with flour, add pepper and salt, and braise the dish for a few minutes.
3. Stir the parsley with the mushrooms and serve.

title: Pfeffernüßchen (Pepper Nuts)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound sifted sugar
- Zest of 1 lemon
- 2 loth citron and orange peel
- 2 knife points cinnamon
- 1 knife point cloves
- 1/2 pound fine flour

## Directions

1. Beat egg whites into stiff peaks
2. Stir in egg yolks, then sifted sugar, and beat together for a quarter hour
3. Add lemon zest, citron and orange peel, cinnamon, cloves, and fine flour
4. Form into balls the size of hazelnuts
5. Grease a dark baking sheet with lard
6. Bake the balls until golden in the oven

title: Pfennigkuchen (Penny Cakes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g Sugar
- 125 g Flour
- 2 Eggs
- 1 pinch Cardamom
- 1 Piece of bacon rind

## Directions

1. Line a baking sheet with paper and then grease it with bacon rind. Break the eggs into a bowl.
2. Whip the eggs in the bowl with the finely sifted sugar for about 1/4 hour until foamy, then add the flour and cardamom and stir well with the foamy egg.
3. Using a teaspoon, drop walnut-sized portions onto the baking sheet and let the cakes stand outside the oven for 5 minutes so a thin skin forms on the surface.
4. Place the cakes in a not-too-hot oven and bake them in 10-15 minutes to a nice light brown color.
5. You can also use anise, vanilla sugar, or lemon sugar instead of cardamom as the spice.

title: Pflaumen in Essig (Plums in Vinegar)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 kg (6 lbs) plums
- 1-1/4 l wine vinegar
- 1-1/4 kg (2-1/2 lbs) sugar
- 16 g cinnamon in a muslin bag
- 8 g cloves in a muslin bag

## Directions

1. Wipe the plums clean, prick them several times with a new wooden toothpick, and place them in a stone pot.
2. Cook the vinegar with sugar and spices, skim, and pour over the fruit after cooling, which you leave covered for 8-14 days.
3. Then pour them onto a colander, let the juice drain, and heat it.
4. Cook the plums in the juice for a few minutes until they get light cracks.
5. Then place them in glasses or pots, cook the juice until the second degree, and pour it over the fruit after cooling, place small cross-sticks over them to keep them under the juice, and bind the vessels with parchment paper.

title: Pflaumen mit Speck (Plums with Bacon)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g fresh or dried plums
- 100 g bacon
- 1/2 kg (1 lb) potatoes
- 1 l water
- 40 g sugar
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 1 teaspoon salt

## Directions

1. Prepare the plums according to instructions.
2. Prepare as for Pears with Bacon.

title: Pflaumenkuchen (Plum Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- Plums
- Sugar
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Distribute plums, sugar, and cinnamon on top
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Pickled Ox Tongues (Geräucherte Ochsenzungen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ox tongues
- Salt
- Saltpeter
- Pepper, ground
- Juniper berries
- Thyme
- Basil
- Celery herb
- Various herbs
- Cold water
- Onions

## Directions

1. Cut off the throat from the ox tongues.
2. Roast a part of salt and a part of saltpeter together with ground pepper and juniper berries.
3. Rub the tongues well with this mixture.
4. Lay them in a dish, add all kinds of herbs, thyme, basil, and celery herb.
5. Let them lie in the brine for 4 weeks, press them down firmly with a well-fitting lid.
6. Hang them afterwards in a large casing, into which they are inserted, in smoke.
7. When you wish to cook them, lay them overnight in cold water.
8. Cook them with cold water, onions, and all kinds of herbs until soft.
9. Serve them cold or warm.

title: Pike Ragout (Fish Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 half- or three-quarter-pound pike, cleaned and prepared
- salt
- pepper
- butter
- onions
- lemons
- 1 calf's thymus (sweetbread)
- 8 large cooked crayfish
- 1/4 pound butter
- 1 roll (Mundbrod)
- milk
- 2/3 of the crayfish butter
- nutmeg
- 2 eggs
- flour
- 1/2 glass wine
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned pike into finger-width pieces and rub them well with salt and pepper
2. Place a piece of butter in a casserole with the fish, some onions and lemons, cover and let steam for half an hour
3. Prepare the calf's thymus by removing the throat, blanch it briefly, cut into pieces and add to the fish
4. From 8 large cooked crayfish, remove the tails and claws, clean the bodies carefully so the upper shell remains whole
5. Pound the remaining crayfish bodies with 1/4 pound butter together, place in a casserole, steam until it foams, then strain through a sieve so the butter runs off well
6. Grate a roll on a grater and soak it in milk
7. Stir 2/3 of the crayfish butter lightly, squeeze out the milk-soaked bread and add it to the butter with salt and nutmeg
8. Stir in 2 eggs one after another
9. Remove the long beard and claw from the crayfish shells, fill the shells with the mixture and place them upside down in a dish
10. Pour a little water or meat broth around them, cover and cook on coals
11. Sprinkle 3 finger widths of flour strongly over the fish, pour on 1/2 glass wine and some meat broth, add the crayfish tails and claws
12. Arrange on a plate and lay the crayfish shells and ragout on top
13. Place the remaining crayfish butter on the ragout

title: Pistazientorte (Pistachio Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 loth pistachios
- 12 loth blanched and finely ground almonds
- 1/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 16 egg yolks
- 4 loth citron
- 4 loth orange peel
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- 8 egg whites
- butter

## Directions

1. Soak 8 loth pistachios in cold water overnight
2. Beat together 12 loth blanched and finely ground almonds and 1/4 pound finely ground sugar with 16 egg yolks and stir together for half an hour
3. Peel the pistachios and chop them on a chopping board
4. Chop 4 loth citron and 4 loth orange peel together, not very finely
5. Finely chop the zest of 1 lemon and add its juice
6. Add the pistachios to this mixture and stir for a while longer
7. Beat the whites from 8 eggs into stiff peaks and slowly fold into the mixture
8. Butter a tin form and bake the mixture in the oven
9. Bake for one hour

title: Plisson von einem Hasen (Hare Forcemeat)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- meat from a hare roasted the day before
- bread soaked in water
- onions, finely cut and steamed
- zest of one lemon
- nutmeg
- pepper
- salt
- 3 eggs
- butter
- tenderloin of the hare
- olives
- olive stones
- jus

## Directions

1. Take the meat from the hind legs of a hare that was roasted the day before and mince it as finely as possible
2. Mix in bread worth 2 kreuzers that has been soaked in water and pressed out, finely cut and steamed onions, the zest of one lemon, nutmeg, pepper, salt, and 3 eggs
3. Rub everything in a mortar until foamy, put it in a bowl and beat in another egg if necessary, mix it once more
4. Butter a mold, fill it with the mixture and cook it in steam
5. Meanwhile, prepare the ragout: take the tenderloin of the hare, cut small neat pieces from it and make an olive sauce as follows: carefully cut olives so they look whole in the sauce, pound the pits very finely in a mortar
6. Put a piece of butter in a saucepan with the pits and finely cut onions, roast them together for a while, sprinkle 3 finger-fulls of flour over, extinguish it with a spoonful of jus, let it boil once more
7. Add the juice of half a lemon, pour the sauce through a hair sieve, then back into the saucepan with the meat and olives
8. Arrange the plisson on a plate with the ragout arranged piece by piece around the plate and the olives on top

title: Plisson von Hirn (Brain Forcemeat)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- brain
- butter
- onion
- lemon
- bread soaked in milk
- 5 egg yolks
- salt
- zest of one lemon
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Skin the brain carefully
2. Place it with a piece of butter in a saucepan, mince an onion and a lemon very finely and let them steam with the brain for a quarter hour
3. Put the brain in a bowl, add bread worth 3 kreuzers that has been grated, soaked in milk and pressed out firmly
4. Beat 5 egg yolks, one after another, and add salt
5. Mince the zest of one lemon
6. Beat the whites of the 5 eggs into stiff snow
7. Mix everything together lightly
8. Butter a tin mold, fill it with the mixture, place it in a saucepan with boiling water
9. Cover with a lid with coals on top and let it cook for half an hour
10. Can be served with chicken fricassée

title: Plumpudding (Plum Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound beef kidney fat
- 1 pound grated white bread
- 3/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 3/4 pound small raisins
- 3/4 pound washed large raisins
- zest of 1 lemon, chopped
- 1 quint ground cinnamon
- 2 knife-points ground nutmeg
- 1 glass cognac
- 13 eggs
- salt to taste
- sugar for serving
- arak for serving

## Directions

1. Carefully skin the beef kidney fat, cut it into very small pieces
2. Add 1 pound grated white bread, 3/4 pound finely ground sugar, 3/4 pound small raisins and the same amount of washed large raisins, the chopped zest of 1 lemon, 1 quint ground cinnamon, 2 knife-points ground nutmeg, and 1 glass cognac
3. Mix everything together with 13 eggs
4. Bind the pudding in a damp cloth and cook it in boiling, lightly salted water for 4 to 5 hours
5. Turn it out onto a plate, cut a small opening in the top of the pudding
6. Pour sugar into the opening, pour in arak, light it, and serve the pudding burning

title: Pomeranzenauflauf (Bitter Orange Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound butter
- 1 bitter orange
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 4 Loth sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- 2 tablespoons fine flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir half a pound of butter until foamy
2. Grate 1 bitter orange on 1/4 pound of sugar, grind this finely, and cook with the above
3. Then let 4 Loth of sugar in a brass pan cook until it spins threads
4. Cook this again with the above for a quarter hour
5. Beat in 6 egg yolks and add the snow from the 6 egg whites along with 2 tablespoons of fine flour
6. Butter a porcelain dish, fill the soufflé into it and bake it beautifully golden in the oven

title: Pomeranzenauflauf anderer Art (Bitter Orange Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 tablespoons flour
- milk
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 bitter orange
- 4 Loth sugar
- 1 Schoppen milk
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir 4 tablespoons of flour smooth in a brass pan with milk
2. Beat in 6 egg yolks
3. Grate 1 bitter orange on 4 Loth of sugar and add this to the pan along with the juice of the bitter orange
4. Pour in 1 Schoppen of milk and cook a thick porridge of everything
5. Stir it (removed from the fire) until it cools a little
6. Finally add the snow from the 6 egg whites and bake the soufflé in a buttered porcelain dish beautifully golden in the oven

title: Pomeranzencrême (Orange Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- juice of 3 oranges
- sugar rubbed with zest of 2 oranges
- 1 schoppen white wine
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg

## Directions

1. Mix the juice of 3 oranges with sugar that has been rubbed with the zest of 2 oranges and 1 schoppen white wine until the sugar dissolves
2. Beat 6 egg yolks and 1 whole egg and add to the mixture
3. Strain the cream through a hair sieve into a designated bowl
4. Place this bowl over boiling water and cover with a lid topped with coals until the cream becomes firm
5. Serve either cold or warm

title: Pomeranzengefrorenes (Orange Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 oranges
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 2 to 3 tablespoons of water
- 1 schoppen of nutmeg wine

## Directions

1. Rub the zest of 3 oranges into the sugar
2. Cook 1/2 pound sugar in a brass pan with 2 to 3 tablespoons of water until it forms threads
3. Press the juice of 3 oranges and 1 schoppen of nutmeg wine through a sieve into the pan with the sugar
4. Let it come to a boil once more
5. Strain through a sieve
6. Let it become completely cold
7. Put it in the freezing tin
8. Work it according to the method given in No. 767

title: Pomeranzengelée (Orange Jelly)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 veal feet with bones removed
- 4 Maas of water
- 3 juicy bitter oranges
- half a pound of sugar
- juice of 3 lemons
- 2 Schoppen of Markgräfler wine

## Directions

1. Cook the veal feet with 4 Maas of water down to 1 Maas, then draw the aspic through a hair sieve and let it stand overnight.
2. The next day, cook the aspic with 3 juicy bitter oranges rubbed with half a pound of sugar, from which the juice and pulp are taken along with the juice of 3 lemons.
3. Skim the fat from the aspic and cook everything together with 2 Schoppen of Markgräfler wine in a brass pan with a lid covered with coals.
4. Let the jelly stand for a quarter hour and pour it through a felt hat into a dish; if the jelly is still not clear enough, let it run through once more.
5. Dip an aspic mold in cold water, pour the jelly into it, place it on ice and turn it out onto a plate.

title: Pomeranzenküchlein (Orange Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 loth citron and orange peel
- 3 loth blanched almonds
- Zest of 1/2 lemon
- 6 loth fine flour
- Sugar for topping

## Directions

1. Beat egg yolks with finely ground sugar in a bowl for a quarter hour
2. Weigh citron and orange peel, blanched almonds, and lemon zest together, not too finely
3. Mix into the egg mixture
4. Stir in fine flour
5. Place stiff paper on a dark baking sheet and sprinkle with sugar
6. Place spoonfuls of batter on the paper
7. Test bake to see if they spread; if they do, add more flour
8. Bake until done

title: Preiselbeeren (Lingonberry Compote)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g lingonberries
- 3 tablespoons sugar
- 2 l water

## Directions

1. The lingonberries are sorted through and briefly poured into boiling water so that the tart taste is lost; then the water is drained off.
2. The sugar is added to the drained berries and they are stewed until they are transparent; cooled, they are arranged for serving.

title: Preisselbeeren (Lingonberries)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-1/2 kg (7 lbs) lingonberries
- 1/2 l soft, cold water
- 1-3/4 kg (3-1/2 lbs) sugar
- White paper, 1 tablespoon rum

## Directions

1. Prepare the berries as for compote.
2. Cook the berries that fall to the bottom and put them in a bag, which you put into the pot (the juice promotes gelling).
3. Let the berries swimming on top drain well and pour them into the sugar that has meanwhile been cooked with the water and juice until the first degree.
4. As soon as they are clear and skimmed, that is, have a transparent, light red color, remove them with a skimming spoon and let the juice simmer for another 15-20 minutes, pour it over the berries and stir with a spoon for 1/4 hour so that they fill with juice.
5. Fill the berries in glasses or pots, cover with rum paper after they have cooled, and bind with parchment paper.
6. Diced pumpkin, apples or pears can be cooked very advantageously with the lingonberries; carrot cubes are also used for increase, but these must first be cooked separately in water, drained, then added to the raw berries.

title: Preserved Lemons (Citronen aufzubewahren)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Lemons
- Dry salt

## Directions

1. Wrap the lemons in paper.
2. Lay dry salt in a dish, then lemons, then salt and lemons again.
3. Fill the dish in this manner.
4. Bind it closed and place it in a dry location.
5. In this way, you always have fresh lemons.

title: Preserved Venison (Aufbewahrung des Schwarzwildbrets)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Venison pieces and legs (not heavier than 5-6 pounds each)
- Vinegar (2 parts)
- Poor quality wine (1 part)
- Water (1 part)
- Calf feet (6-8), hacked up
- Salt
- Juniper berries, crushed
- Various herbs

## Directions

1. Take the best pieces of venison and the strongest legs.
2. Set aside as much as needed for pickling.
3. Cook 2 parts vinegar, 1 part poor quality wine, 1 part water with 6 to 8 hacked calf feet, add salt, a handful of crushed juniper berries, and various herbs.
4. Cook everything together until the feet are soft.
5. Remove the feet and herbs.
6. Strain the broth and let it cool.
7. Bring the venison into a pot as tightly as possible.
8. For this purpose, strew the bottom with salt, juniper berries, and some herbs, lay a layer of venison on top, then sprinkle again, and continue until all venison is in the pot.
9. Pour the cooled broth over it.
10. After the pot has stood for a time, cover it with a board and press down firmly.
11. In this way, the venison keeps for a whole year.

title: Preßkopf (Head Cheese)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Bacon
- Pork ears
- Some pork jowl
- 4 calf feet
- 2 pork feet
- Salt
- Pepper
- Minced lemon peel
- Cloves
- Ginger
- Mace

## Directions

1. Cook bacon, pork ears, some pork jowl, 4 calf feet, and 2 pork feet until very soft with salt and pepper
2. Cut everything into small pieces
3. Mix thoroughly in a bowl with minced lemon peel, pepper, cloves, ginger, mace, and salt
4. Fill the mixture into a cleaned pork stomach
5. Sew it shut
6. Simmer for one hour, longer if it is large
7. Place between two boards and let it rest overnight

title: Prinzessin Louis (Princess Louis)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen water
- 4 loth fresh butter
- 6 loth sugar
- fine flour
- 1 lemon
- 12 egg yolks
- preserved gooseberries
- apricot jam
- raspberries
- sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1 schoppen water, 4 loth fresh butter, and 6 loth sugar together in a brass pan.
2. Stir in fine flour until thick, transfer to a bowl, and grate 1 whole lemon over a grater and mix with 12 egg yolks into the batter.
3. Bake 1 ladle-full of batter in an iron pan the size of a plate until golden on one side, turn the cake and bake the other side.
4. Grease the plate for serving with butter and place the cake on it.
5. Spread with preserved gooseberries, bake a similar cake and place on the first.
6. Spread the second with apricot jam, bake the third and place it on the second, spread with raspberries, and continue this way until the batter is finished (making approximately 4 to 5 cakes).
7. Beat stiff snow from 12 egg whites, make a wreath around the cakes, sprinkle sugar over for a beautiful shine, let everything bake golden in the oven for half an hour, and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Prunellensuppe mit Hafergrütze für einen Kranken (Prune and Oat Gruel Soup for an Invalid)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 25 g oat gruel
- 50 g dried prunes
- 15 g sugar
- 1 small piece cinnamon
- 1 teaspoon lemon sugar
- 1 egg yolk
- 1/8 l white wine
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Wash the oat gruel and the chopped prunes.
2. In an earthenware pot, cook the oat gruel and prunes covered with water, sugar, cinnamon and lemon sugar until soft, press through a hair sieve or cloth and finish with the egg yolk stirred in wine.

title: Pudding von geriebener Gerste (Grated Barley Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- flour
- 1 pint milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 4 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 2 loth blanched pounded almonds
- 6 egg yolks
- 6 egg whites
- butter
- sugar
- 1/4 pound cherries
- 1 pint water
- 1 pint wine
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- cinnamon
- cloves
- sugar

## Directions

1. Make a dough of 2 egg yolks and as much flour as these absorb, chop it very finely, cook it in 1 pint of milk and let it cool.
2. Beat together 1/4 pound butter, 4 loth sugar with lemon zest grated on it, 2 loth blanched pounded almonds, 6 egg yolks and the snow beaten from 6 egg whites.
3. Fill the mass into a pudding mold brushed with butter and sprinkled with sugar.
4. Cook the pudding one hour in boiling water.
5. For the sauce: pound 1/4 pound cherries together with the pits thoroughly, cook them half an hour in 1 pint of water, press through a sieve, add 1 pint of wine, along with 1 cooking spoon of flour, cinnamon and cloves, stir everything smooth and let it boil with a piece of sugar.
6. Pour the sauce over the arranged pudding.

title: Pudding von süßen Nudeln (Pudding of Sweet Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 1 egg yolk
- flour as much as the egg and yolk will take up
- 1 pound butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 1 pound chopped citron and orange peel
- half a lemon peel, finely chopped
- 4 loth sugar
- 2 apples, finely cut
- egg whites from 5 eggs
- flour
- a small piece of butter
- wine
- sugar
- cinnamon to taste
- nutmeg to taste

## Directions

1. Make a stiff dough from 1 egg, 1 yolk, and flour
2. Roll out cakes from the dough and cut them 2 knife-backs thick
3. Cook them in salted water, draw through cold water and let drain
4. Stir 1 pound butter until creamy
5. Stir in 5 egg yolks, the noodles, 1 pound chopped citron and orange peel, finely chopped lemon peel, 4 loth sugar, 2 finely cut apples, and the snow from 5 egg whites
6. Fill the mass into a damp cloth, tie it tightly, cook the pudding 5 quarter-hours in boiling water
7. For the wine sauce: let 1 small spoonful of flour with a piece of butter turn golden, deglaze with wine, add sugar, a knife-point of cinnamon and the same of nutmeg
8. Let everything come to boil together and pour this sauce over the pudding

title: Punsch (Punch)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Half a Maas Arak
- 8 lemons
- Sugar
- 2 bottles wine
- One Schoppen good green tea
- One pound sugar

## Directions

1. Rub half of the lemons on sugar
2. Squeeze the juice from the other half of the lemons
3. Pour 2 bottles of wine, one Schoppen good green tea, and one pound sugar into a brass pan
4. Heat only until it begins to boil
5. Pour through a hair sieve directly into the punch bowl
6. Add another half Maas of Arak

title: Punsch auf andere Art (Punch Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One Maas water
- 8 lemons
- 2 oranges
- Sugar
- One Schoppen Arak

## Directions

1. Rub 4 lemons on sugar
2. Scrape the sugar into the water
3. Boil the remaining sugar in a brass pan with one spoonful of water until it turns yellowish
4. Pour the juice from the lemons and oranges through a hair sieve onto the sugar
5. Add one Maas of water
6. Let it boil together
7. Pour into the bowl and add one Schoppen Arak

title: Punsch auf Stuttgarter Art (Punch Stuttgart Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 large lemons
- 6 oranges
- One Schoppen water
- 1.5 pounds sugar
- 2 bottles white Burgundy wine
- 2 Schoppen Arak

## Directions

1. Press the juice from 6 large lemons and 6 oranges
2. In a brass pan, boil one Schoppen water and 1.5 pounds sugar together until the sugar spins threads
3. Skim off the black foam carefully
4. Pour the pressed juice through a hair sieve onto the sugar
5. Let it boil together again
6. Add 2 bottles of white Burgundy wine
7. Let it heat in the pan until it begins to boil
8. Pour everything into the bowl
9. Add 2 Schoppen Arak
10. If not strong enough, strengthen it further with Arak

title: Punsch Warm (Warm Punch)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 teaspoons tea
- 3/4 liter boiling water
- 1 bottle red wine
- 1/4 bottle rum
- 125 g sugar

## Directions

1. Prepare 3/4 liter of tea according to instructions and pour through a tea sieve
2. Place wine, rum and sugar in an earthenware pot over gentle heat and bring to just before boiling point, that is until white foam appears
3. Mix with the tea
4. Check the finished punch for sweetness

title: Punschgefrorenes (Punch Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 1 measure of water
- 1 lemon
- 8 to 12 lemons for juice
- 24 loth or 1/2 bottle of fine arak

## Directions

1. Clarify 1 pound sugar with 1 measure of water as described in No. 817
2. Peel the yellow zest very thinly from 1 lemon
3. Let it boil several times with the clarified sugar, then remove it as this frozen dessert acquires a certain bitterness from it
4. Once the sugar is removed from the fire, add the juice of 8 to 12 lemons, depending on how much juice the fruits contain
5. Press the whole mixture through a sieve
6. Once the syrup has cooled, add 24 loth or 1/2 bottle of fine arak
7. Let the mixture freeze as usual
8. It takes always half an hour to freeze when the ice is freshly salted
9. It is more difficult to freeze than all other types due to the spirits in the arak

title: Punschkuchen (Punch Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- quarter pound almonds
- one egg
- quarter pound sugar
- 8 egg yolks gradually added
- half pound melted butter
- one spoonful flour
- some arak
- 8 egg whites snow

## Directions

1. Pound quarter pound almonds with one egg
2. Stir in quarter pound sugar and gradually 8 egg yolks
3. Stir half pound melted butter with one spoonful flour until foamy
4. Add some arak
5. Add the snow from the 8 egg whites
6. Mix with the almonds and sugar
7. Coat a springform with butter
8. Fill with the mass
9. Bake at gentle heat

title: Punschtorte (Punch Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound butter
- 3/4 pound finely ground sugar
- 18 egg yolks
- egg whites from 18 eggs
- zest of 1 lemon
- 26 loth flour
- 2 tablespoons arrack
- raspberries
- 2 tablespoons arrack
- currants
- 2 tablespoons arrack
- apple preserves
- wafers
- arrack
- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- arrack
- candied fruits

## Directions

1. Stir 3/4 pound butter until foamy
2. Add 3/4 pound finely ground sugar, 18 egg yolks, the beaten whites from the eggs, grated zest of 1 lemon, and 26 loth flour to the butter
3. Fill one tablespoon into 4 plate-sized equal forms and bake them golden yellow, then let cool
4. Mix 2 tablespoons arrack with raspberries in one dish, 2 tablespoons arrack with currants in a second dish, and arrack with apple preserves in a third dish
5. Place a piece of paper on a springform, put one baked small cake on it, spread with raspberries
6. Place the second cake on the first, spread with currants, place the third on the second, spread with apple preserves, place the fourth cake on top
7. Cut the torte into a round shape; if necessary, moisten a piece of wafer with arrack and arrange it nicely around the torte
8. Make an arrack icing: pour arrack into 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar until it is liquid enough to coat the torte, but the icing should not run
