9. Pour the icing over the torte and garnish with candied fruits

title: Quails in Sauce (Wachteln in Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- quails, cleaned and plucked
- ham
- salt
- pepper
- 1 to 2 glasses wine
- flour
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1/2 nutmeg, grated

## Directions

1. Remove the breastbone from each cleaned and well-prepared quail
2. Fill each with ham that has been turned in salt and pepper
3. Dress them nicely, place them in a casserole
4. Pour 1 to 2 glasses of wine over them (depending on how many quails)
5. Cover and let them steam until soft
6. Arrange them on a plate
7. Add 2 knife points of flour to the sauce, the juice of 1 lemon and half a grated nutmeg
8. Strain the sauce through a sieve over the quails

title: Quitten (Quince Preserves)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ripe quinces
- Sugar (same weight as the quinces)
- Water

## Directions

1. Remove the core from ripe quinces
2. Peel them
3. Cut them into 4 parts
4. Simmer them soft in water
5. Lay them on a hair sieve
6. Take as many pounds of sugar as you have pounds of quinces
7. Simmer the sugar in the water in which the quinces were cooked soft until it spins threads
8. Put the quinces in
9. Let them cook with the sugar
10. Preserve them in a stone pot

title: Quittencompote (Quince Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- quinces
- water
- sugar
- lemon peel

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces and cut them in half
2. Cook them soft in water and place on a hair sieve
3. Cook the peels and cores soft in water and also strain through a hair sieve
4. Combine in a pan with the quinces, sugar, and lemon peel

title: Quittencompote (Quince Compote)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Quinces
- water
- sugar (12 loth)
- lemon peel
- wine (half a schoppen)
- raisins

## Directions

1. Peel the quinces and cut them in half
2. Cook them soft in water
3. Place them on a hair sieve
4. Cook the skins and seeds soft in water and also pour through a hair sieve
5. In a saucepan combine the quinces, the seed/skin mixture, 12 loth sugar, a lemon peel and half a schoppen wine
6. Cook together until the syrup is thick
7. Form a melon shape from the quinces on a plate
8. Pour the syrup over it
9. Insert large raisins that have been cooked in the syrup into the figure

title: Quittenlatwerge (Quince Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe quinces
- three-quarters pound sugar per pound of pulp
- water

## Directions

1. Peel beautiful ripe quinces
2. Cook them in water until soft
3. Scrape off the pulp
4. Force it through a hair sieve (to one pound of pulp add three-quarters pound of sugar)
5. Cook everything together in a pan until no juice is visible
6. Store it in a porcelain container

title: Ragout von einem Fisch (Fish Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 red fish or pike
- small potatoes
- lard
- parsley, finely washed
- half a pound of butter

## Directions

1. Boil a nice red fish or pike blue
2. Peel small potatoes, fry them with lard until golden (they must be half soft boiled beforehand)
3. Garnish the fish with them
4. Mince a handful of finely washed parsley very finely
5. Heat half a pound of butter in a saucepan, add the parsley and cook the fish in it

title: Ragout von hartgesottenen Eiern (Ragout of Hard-Boiled Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 8 hard-boiled eggs
- 2 onions
- butter
- salt
- nutmeg
- sour cream
- 3 shallot onions
- lemon zest
- flour
- meat broth
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. For 4 persons, allow 8 hard-boiled eggs
2. Press the yolks of hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve
3. Slice 2 onions and steam them in a casserole with a piece of butter until soft but not yellow
4. Add the egg yolks to the onions with salt, nutmeg, and a spoonful of sour cream
5. Fill the egg whites with this mixture
6. For the sauce: let 3 chopped shallot onions and the zest of half a lemon lightly color in a casserole with melted butter and chopped ingredients, with 3 fingers-worth of flour
7. Pour in a spoonful of meat broth, add salt, nutmeg, and the juice of half a lemon
8. Let this come to a boil together
9. Arrange the sauce on a plate and place the eggs in it

title: Rahmgefrorenes (Cream Frozen Dessert)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 measure of thick cream
- 1/2 measure of raspberries
- 8 loth sugar or enough to sweeten

## Directions

1. Whip 1/2 measure of thick cream very firmly with a small whisk
2. Lay it on a sieve
3. Press 1/2 measure of raspberries through the sieve
4. Add 8 loth sugar or enough until it is sweet enough
5. Mix the whipped cream into it
6. Put it in the freezing tin without stirring it however

title: Rahmgefrorenes (Frozen Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 Maas thick cream
- 1/2 Maas raspberries
- 8 Loth sugar (or more to taste)

## Directions

1. Beat the cream very firmly with a small whisk
2. Lay it on a hair sieve
3. Force the raspberries through the sieve
4. Add 8 Loth of sugar or as much as is needed until they are sweet enough
5. Mix the whipped cream underneath
6. Place it in the freezing bucket, but do not churn it

title: Rahmkuchen (Cream Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half pound flour
- 4 loth butter
- 2 egg yolks
- half glass wine
- 2 loth finely ground sugar
- 6 egg yolks
- one schoppen sour cream
- one schoppen sweet cream
- 3 loth ground sugar
- half a lemon zested
- quarter pound raisins and dried plums
- 4 egg whites snow
- one cooking spoon fine flour
- milk

## Directions

1. Make a dough from half pound flour, 4 loth butter, 2 egg yolks, half glass wine and 2 loth finely ground sugar on a pastry board like a butter dough
2. Roll it out
3. Fold it over itself
4. Repeat this once more
5. Lay the dough in a springform coated with butter
6. Mix together 6 egg yolks, one schoppen sour and equal amount sweet cream, 3 loth ground sugar, half a zested lemon, quarter pound raisins and dried plums, and the snow from 4 egg whites
7. Mix in one cooking spoon fine flour that has been smoothed with milk
8. Fill this onto the cake
9. Bake in the oven

title: Rahmmuß (Cream Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a Vierling finely grated sugar
- 1 egg white
- 1 tablespoonful water
- flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Stir into half a Vierling finely grated sugar, 1 egg white and 1 tablespoonful water for half an hour so much flour that it becomes as thick as pap.
2. Coat a tile with butter.
3. Fill the stirred mixture into it.
4. Place it in an oven or between coals and bake it a nice yellow.

title: Rahmstrauben (Cream Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen of cream
- 4 whole eggs
- fine flour
- 1 knife-tip of salt
- 2 eggs
- hot lard
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Stir fine flour into 1 schoppen of cream and 4 whole eggs until it becomes a dough
2. Add a knife-tip of salt and stir in 2 more eggs
3. When the dough is so thin that it flows through a fritter funnel, let it run into a pan of hot lard
4. Bake it until beautifully golden and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Rahmwürste (Cream Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 rolls
- milk
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- kidney fat
- salt
- nutmeg
- 3 eggs

## Directions

1. Cut the crust from 4 rolls
2. Cut thin slices from the rolls
3. Cook them in milk to a thick porridge
4. Let cool
5. Cook the bread with a piece of butter, steamed onions, parsley, and some kidney fat in a casserole
6. Season with salt
7. Grate nutmeg on it
8. Add 3 eggs
9. Make long sausages from the cooked mixture
10. Dip them in eggs
11. Roast them in butter

title: Reformirter Thee (Reformed Tea)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Milk
- Cinnamon
- Lemon peel
- Green tea
- 6 egg yolks

## Directions

1. Pour milk into a pan as much as needed
2. Add a piece of cinnamon and some lemon peel
3. Take as much green tea as can be grasped with three fingers
4. Let it boil together for 6 minutes
5. Beat 6 egg yolks in a pot
6. Stir them smooth with cold milk
7. Slowly pour the hot milk through a hair sieve into the egg yolks
8. Put it back in the pan and let it set

title: Regenwürmer (Worms)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- flour
- egg whites
- salt
- butter
- milk
- sour cream
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Take flour as needed on a noodle board, beat in 3 egg whites with one knife-tip of salt, egg-sized butter and milk, until you can work the dough.
2. Roll it out by hand as thin as a quill feather and one ell long.
3. Heat milk in a pan, add a large piece of butter along with the noodles and let them cook together.
4. They should have some broth; you can also add sour cream along with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Regenwürmer mit Brandteig (Worms with Choux Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas water
- 1 piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
- Fine flour
- 2 to 3 eggs
- Salt
- Sugar
- Flour for the board
- Butter for the plafond
- Milk

## Directions

1. Let 1/2 maas water and 1 piece of butter the size of a hen's egg boil in a pan
2. Stir in fine flour until it forms a thick dough
3. In a bowl, beat 2 to 3 eggs, salt and sugar together
4. Sprinkle flour on a noodle board and make finger-thick noodles from the dough
5. Brush a plafond with butter, pour in milk to the height of a fingernail
6. Set the noodles side by side, but not too close together
7. Cover the plafond and place it on coals or in a baking oven
8. Let the noodles cook until no more sauce is visible
9. Turn them with a baking spatula and sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Rehblatt, Geschmortes (Braised Venison Shoulder)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 large venison shoulder
- 1 medium-sized onion
- 1 carrot
- 1 spice dose
- 1/4 l broth, milk or water
- 3 tablespoons vinegar
- 30 g bread crust
- 50 g margarine
- 30 g bacon
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. The venison shoulder is freed as much as possible from the bones (except the leg, which is only shortened), beaten, washed, skinned, drawn together with needle and thread into good shape, larded and salted. The bones are broken into small pieces.
2. The bones are placed in the braising pot after the shoulder is browned in hot fat, add onion, carrot slices, spices and bread crust, gradually pour vinegar and broth or milk or water after browning and braise the shoulder slowly in 1 1/2-2 hours under frequent basting until soft. Strain the sauce through a sieve, coat the finished shoulder with sauce and serve the remaining sauce on the side.

title: Rehschlägel in Rahmsauce (Venison Haunch in Cream Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- venison haunch
- various roots and onions
- hot lard
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- jus
- vinegar or wine
- sour cream, half a schopp
- flour, 3 finger-widths

## Directions

1. Remove the shank bone and end bone from the haunch, skin it well, and lard it.
2. Place it on a plafond, cut various roots and onions alongside it.
3. Pour hot lard over it, add salt, pepper, and cloves.
4. Roast until beautifully golden, then add a little jus, a glass of vinegar or wine, and baste it frequently.
5. Mix together half a schopp of good thick sour cream and 3 finger-widths of flour.
6. Add this mixture with a little jus to the roast and bring to a boil again.
7. Strain the sauce through a hair sieve and pour it over the roast.

title: Reinesclaudes (Greengage Plums)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Beautiful green reinesclaudes, not overripe
- Sugar (same weight as the reinesclaudes)

## Directions

1. Cook beautiful green reinesclaudes that are not overripe in a non-tinned casserole with as much sugar as the reinesclaudes weigh until it reaches the bubble stage, or as long as it takes to hard boil eggs
2. Take a silver spoon and lift them out so they do not break
3. Let the syrup cook down a little
4. Pour it over them
5. Let it stand overnight
6. Repeat this process for 3 days in a row
7. Each time a non-tinned casserole must be used, otherwise they will lose their beautiful green color

title: Reis mit getrockneten Pflaumen (Rice with Dried Plums)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g rice
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 l water for blanching
- 40 g plums
- 3/8 l water
- 10 g sugar
- 5 g margarine

## Directions

1. Prepare the plums according to instructions.
2. Cook the plums slowly with 3/8 l water and 10 g sugar until soft and pour over the rice, which is prepared as water rice.

title: Reis mit Kirschen (Rice with Cherries)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g sour cherries or 40 g dried cherries
- 20 g sugar
- 3 tablespoons water
- 5 g salt
- 125 g rice
- 1/2 l water for blanching
- 5 g margarine
- 1 piece orange peel

## Directions

1. Wash the sour cherries, remove stems and pit them if desired. Prepare dried cherries according to instructions.
2. Stew the cherries with sugar, orange peel and 3 tablespoons water and add to the rice, which is prepared as water rice.

title: Reis mit Kürbis (Rice with Pumpkin)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 piece pumpkin
- 1/2 l water for blanching
- 125 g rice
- 5 g salt
- 10 g sugar
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 5 g margarine
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. Peel the pumpkin, cut into cubes and rinse; wash the rice.
2. Cook both together in water, fat, salt and sugar until done and season with vinegar.

title: Reis mit Rosinen (Rice with Raisins)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g rice
- 1/2 l water for blanching
- 1/2 l water
- 5 g fat or margarine
- 20 g large raisins
- 1 piece orange peel
- 5 g salt
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the picked-over raisins in water and then drain.
2. Add the raisins to the rice, which is prepared as water rice; season the dish with vinegar and sugar.

title: Reis mit Rosinen (Rice with Raisins)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 l water for blanching
- 1/2 l water
- 5 g fat or margarine
- 20 g large raisins
- 1 piece orange peel
- 5 g salt
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 2 tablespoons sugar

## Directions

1. Soak the cleaned raisins in water and then drain them.
2. Add the raisins to the rice, which is prepared as water rice.
3. Season the dish with vinegar and sugar.

title: Reisauflauf anderer Art (Rice Soufflé, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice
- boiling water
- half a Maas milk
- 4 Loth butter
- 12 Borsdorfer apples
- a glass of wine
- butter
- 2 Loth orange peel and citron
- citron peel
- 6 peeled almonds
- a handful of raisins
- 5 egg yolks
- egg whites from the eggs
- 4 Loth ground sugar
- butter

## Directions

1. Blanch 1/4 pound of rice in boiling water 2-3 times and let it drain through a hair sieve
2. Cook it with half a Maas of milk and 4 Loth of butter in a casserolle
3. Peel 12 Borsdorfer apples and remove the core
4. Braise the apples in a casserolle with a glass of wine in butter until half soft and lay them on a plate
5. Make a filling: weigh 2 Loth of orange peel and citron, some citron peel, 6 peeled almonds not very finely, add a handful of raisins and fill the apples with this
6. Stir 5 egg yolks, the snow from 6 egg whites, and 4 Loth of ground sugar into the rice mass
7. Put half of this into a buttered baking sheet and lay the filled apples on top

title: Reisflomeriewein (Rice with Wine)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one-quarter pound rice
- half Maas milk
- 1 teaspoon cinnamon
- 4 Loth pounded sugar
- 1 lemon
- 2 Loth blanched bitter almonds
- milk
- red wine
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Scald one-quarter pound rice and strain through a hair sieve
2. Cook with half a Maas milk until thick
3. Add 1 teaspoon cinnamon, 4 Loth pounded sugar, 1 lemon rubbed with sugar, and 2 Loth blanched bitter almonds that have been pounded fine with milk
4. Stir everything together
5. Fill the mass into chocolate cups
6. Let the cream cool thoroughly
7. When serving, turn it out onto a plate
8. Pour red wine over it and sprinkle sugar and cinnamon on top

title: Reispanadesuppe für Kranke (Rice Panade Soup for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Loth cleanly washed rice
- Water
- Meat broth
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon sweet cream

## Directions

1. Tie 4 Loth of cleanly washed rice in a linen cloth, leaving 3 finger-widths empty
2. Boil the rice in a pot with half water and half meat broth for 2 to 3 hours
3. Stir it very finely with 1 egg yolk and 1 tablespoon of sweet cream
4. Let it come together once more and give to the sick person one spoonful at a time

title: Reispudding (Rice Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound rice, well washed
- 1/2 maas milk
- 1/4 pound butter
- 8 loth finely ground almonds
- 6 loth sugar
- egg yolks
- 3 spoonfuls apricot jam
- 1 schoppen Rhine wine

## Directions

1. Cook the rice very soft with half a maas milk, press it through a hair sieve
2. Stir 1/4 pound butter lightly in a bowl, add 8 loth finely ground almonds and 6 loth sugar
3. Lay 1 spoonful of rice in the butter and 1 egg yolk to it, continue this way to the end
4. Also stir in the almonds, beat the egg whites to stiff peaks, stir them slowly into the mass
5. Brush a melon mold with butter, put the mass in it, place in the oven and let bake well
6. The pudding is baked in about one hour
7. For the apricot sauce: make from 3 spoonfuls of apricot jam and 1 schoppen Rhine wine, let it come to boil together and pour it over the pudding

title: Reisschleim für Kranke (Rice Gruel for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Loth cleanly washed Carolina rice
- Water
- Egg yolk
- Sweet cream
- Sugar or salt

## Directions

1. Pound 4 Loth of cleanly washed Carolina rice very finely
2. Let it cook in a small pot with 1 Maas water until it begins to thicken
3. Press the rice through a linen cloth that is not very tight
4. Stir in an egg yolk and a spoonful of sweet cream
5. Let the gruel come together once more
6. Sweeten or salt it and give to the sick person one spoonful at a time

title: Reissuppe (Rice Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Rice
- Boiling hot water
- Very fatty meat broth
- Parmesan cheese

## Directions

1. Clean the rice and pour boiling hot water over it
2. Let it stand for a quarter hour, then drain and pour boiling water over it again
3. Let it stand another quarter hour
4. Place it in a casserole and pour very fatty meat broth over it
5. Slowly bring to a boil over low heat
6. When serving, Parmesan cheese can be added to the bowl or served separately

title: Reissuppe anderer Art (Rice Soup, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Marrowfat peas
- Onion
- Parsley
- Butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Meat broth
- Rice

## Directions

1. Shell the marrowfat peas and braise with onion, parsley, and butter until soft
2. Stir in one tablespoon of flour and add some meat broth
3. Let cook for a quarter hour or until soft, then press through a fine sieve
4. Prepare the rice as in recipe No. 35
5. Arrange both together

title: Reissuppe mit Schinken (Rice Soup with Ham)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound of rice
- Meat broth
- 1/2 pound of ham
- 1/4 pound of Parmesan cheese
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over the rice and let stand for half an hour
2. Drain and pour boiling water over again, let stand another quarter hour
3. Wash it thoroughly
4. Pour meat broth over it and cook until soft
5. Grate the Parmesan cheese on a grater
6. Place 1/2 pound ham and 1/4 pound grated Parmesan cheese together in the soup bowl
7. Arrange the rice over it and grate nutmeg on top

title: Remoladesauce (Remoulade Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- some peeled shallots
- 2 spoonfuls of capers
- 4 clean washed anchovies
- the yolk of 2 hard-boiled eggs
- one spoonful of Provençal oil
- some tarragon
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Mince the peeled shallots, capers, anchovies, and hard-boiled egg yolks together
2. Take one spoonful of Provençal oil, some tarragon, vinegar, pepper, and salt to taste
3. Stir everything well together
4. Serve with any meat

title: Rice Poulards with Ragout (Reispfauzen mit Ragout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound cleaned rice
- rich meat broth or butter
- 2 eggs
- butter for coating mold
- lamb meat
- finely chopped onions
- parsley
- lemons
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Place 1/4 pound cleaned rice on the fire with rich meat broth or butter and let it cook soft (it should have no more broth)
2. Stir 2 eggs into it in a bowl
3. Coat a melon mold with butter, put the rice in it, press it firmly against the mold so it gets a hollow in the middle, and let it bake in the oven
4. For the ragout: cut lamb meat into pieces and steam it in a covered casserole with finely chopped onions, parsley and lemons for half an hour
5. Sprinkle one cooking spoon of flour over it, add the juice of half a lemon, some meat broth, salt, pepper and nutmeg
6. Arrange the baked rice on a plate and fill the ragout into it

title: Rinder- und Hammelfett auslas sen (Rendering Beef and Mutton Fat)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) fat
- 1/8 liter water

## Directions

1. Cut the beef fat into small cubes and soak in water for a day or night so any blood particles can be removed
2. Drain the water, place the fat in a braising pot and pour 1/8 liter water over 1/2 kg (1 pound) fat, cover and let it cook slowly
3. The water should loosen the cells so they release the fat better
4. Once the fat becomes transparent in the heat, uncover it and let it braise openly until the cracklings are evenly browned
5. Let the fat cool somewhat, then strain through a sieve
6. Gently press out the cracklings and set aside covered for use in potato soup, legumes, or finely chopped for meatballs, beefsteak, etc.
7. Store the rendered fat in clean tied stone pots
8. For mutton fat: render the same way; when the fat becomes glassy, add 1 cup of milk, which reduces the mutton flavor

title: Rinderrouladen (Beef Rolls)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g beef
- 1 small onion
- 30 g bacon, scraped
- 20 g beef fat
- 1 teaspoon salt
- a pinch of pepper
- 20 g flour
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Beef slices the size of a palm and 3/4 cm thick are beaten, the onions finely chopped; the bacon is scraped. Bacon, onions, salt and pepper are mixed, the slices are brushed with this, then rolled together, bound with cotton and rolled in flour. The beef fat is placed in a pot.
2. The fat in the pot is made steaming, the rolls rolled in flour are placed in it and nicely browned on all sides; 1/4 l water is gradually added, the pot is covered again, and the roulades are slowly braised for 2 hours. The roulades are occasionally turned and basted. If liquid is still missing, a little more water is added. The soft roulades are removed from the pot, placed in a bowl, the cotton is removed, the sauce is seasoned and poured over the roulades; then they are served.

title: Rinderschmorbraten (Braised Beef Roast for 6 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 kg (3 lbs) beef
- 30 g fatty bacon
- 20 g flour
- 1 spice dose
- 30 g bread crust
- 30 g fat or margarine
- 20 g onions
- 15 g salt
- water as needed

## Directions

1. The meat is beaten and tied with twine into good shape. The bacon is cut into 4 cm long, 8 mm thick strips and turned in the crushed spice mixed with salt. With a pointed wooden stick, the bacon strips are inserted into the meat piece at equal distances and this is then turned in flour mixed with a little salt.
2. In the meantime, fat—also bacon scraps—is made hot and brown in an earthenware pot, the meat is browned on all sides, using a skimming spoon and a ladle for turning, then onions, crumbled bread crust and the remaining spice are added, gradually 1/4 l hot water is poured on, the pot is tightly covered, weighted with a bolt and the meat is slowly braised at the side of the fire. Every half hour it is turned to another side and the reduced sauce is replenished with hot water. For this replenishment, one can add beer or, if the piece lay in a vinegar marinade, some of it as desired. After 3 hours, the soft braised piece is removed from the pot, covered with a deep bowl, the sauce is degreased and checked for its binding, according to which it is either thinned with any liquids or thickened with a little cornstarch, mixed cold. Then it is stirred through a sieve and after everything that has stuck in the braising pot is cooked off, the sauce is returned to the braising pot.

title: Rindfleisch mit Brühkartoffeln (Beef with Boiled Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g Rindfleisch (beef)
- 3/4 kg geschälte Kartoffeln (peeled potatoes)
- Wasser und Salz zum Abwellen (water and salt for blanching)
- 15 g Suppengrün (vegetable soup greens)
- 3/4 l Wasser (water)
- 10 g Salz (salt)
- 1 Teel. Petersilie oder 1/4 Teel. getrockneter Majoran (1 teaspoon parsley or 1/4 teaspoon dried marjoram)
- 1 Prise Salz (pinch of salt)

## Directions

1. Wash and pound the meat; blanch the sliced potatoes in salted water and drain on a sieve.
2. Put 3/4 l water and 10 g salt on the stove to heat.
3. Place the meat in the boiling salted water; after it has cooked for 1/2 hour, skim it; add the vegetable soup greens and cook slowly with the meat for another 1/2 hour.
4. Add the blanched potatoes, parsley or marjoram; cook everything together for another 1/2 hour.
5. Season with salt and pepper to taste and serve.

title: Rindfleisch und Rindfleischbrühe (Beef and Beef Broth)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g Rindfleisch, Schild oder Dünnung (beef shoulder or brisket)
- 2 Essl. Suppengrün (2 tablespoons vegetable soup greens)
- 10 g Salz (salt)
- 1-1/4 l Wasser (water)

## Directions

1. Wash and pound the meat; set it on the stove with the water and salt; clean the vegetable soup greens, cut into even pieces, and wash.
2. Cook the meat in the salted water, skim it, add the vegetable soup greens, and cover the pot.
3. The meat must now steep rather than boil for 2-1/2 hours.
4. To use the broth as soup, remove the meat, strain the broth through a sieve or remove the vegetable soup greens, degrease the broth, and pour some spoonfuls of fat over the meat. Return the vegetable soup greens to the degreased broth.

title: Ringäpfel, Amerikanische (American Ring Apples as Dried Fruit)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 65 g American ring apples
- 25 g sugar
- 4 g potato flour
- 1 piece of orange peel
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. American ring apples must be cooked alone, because they require less time and would also discolor; they are only washed several times while warm. They are not soaked.
2. Otherwise they are prepared like other dried fruit; for example, they make a good compote for the sick.

title: Rissolen (Meat Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat
- one onion
- one Kreuzerbrod (small bread roll)
- 2 to 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- 2 to 3 handfuls of flour
- half a schoppen of sour cream
- 2 loth of butter
- lard for frying

## Directions

1. Chop the leftover meat finely with one onion
2. Soak a Kreuzerbrod in water, then press it out firmly
3. Braise this with butter in a casserole for a quarter hour
4. Stir in 2 to 3 eggs and sprinkle salt and nutmeg over it
5. On a noodle board, make a dough from 2 to 3 handfuls of flour, 2 eggs, half a schoppen of sour cream, and 2 loth of butter, as for noodles
6. Roll out the dough, fold it over once more, roll it out again, but not as thin as noodles
7. Brush the dough with eggs, lay the meat mixture on top
8. Fold it over, cut it out with a mold, and bake the pieces in lard until golden yellow

title: Rissolen (Rissoles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- leftover meat
- onion
- Kreuzer bread
- water
- butter
- 2 to 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- 2 to 3 handfuls flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 half Schoppen sour cream
- 2 Loth butter

## Directions

1. Take leftover meat and weigh it finely with an onion
2. Soak a Kreuzer bread in water and press it out firmly
3. Braise this with butter in a casserole for a quarter hour
4. Stir in 2 to 3 eggs
5. Sprinkle salt and nutmeg over it
6. Make a dough on a noodle board from 2 to 3 handfuls flour, 2 eggs, half a schoppen sour cream and 2 loth butter, like for noodles
7. Roll it out, fold it over again, roll it out again, but not as fine as noodles
8. Brush the sheet with eggs
9. Place the mixture on it
10. Fold it over

title: Rosenkohl (Brussels Sprouts)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Brussels sprouts
- boiling water
- onions
- parsley
- fat
- flour
- salt
- pepper
- nutmeg
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the Brussels sprouts carefully.
2. Add boiling water and cook until tender.
3. Pour into a colander, pour cold water over them.
4. In a casserole, steam the onions and parsley in a piece of fat.
5. Cook the Brussels sprouts in this for a quarter hour.
6. Add flour, salt, pepper, nutmeg, and meat broth.
7. Finish cooking until done.

title: Rosinen Sauce (Raisin Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/3 l water (preferably broth)
- 5 g salt
- 1 1/2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 1/2 tablespoons syrup
- 20 g large raisins
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 pinch pepper and salt
- fat
- flour
- 1 onion

## Directions

1. Peel the onion and cut into cubes. Clean the raisins according to instructions and soak until plump. Season 1/3 l liquid with salt, vinegar and syrup.
2. In an earthenware pot, heat the fat, add the sugar for browning, add the flour and onion cubes and let them brown as well. Stirring constantly, pour in the liquid and bring everything to a boil. Add the drained raisins; season with a bit of salt and pepper, and serve the sauce with beef.

title: Rosinensauce (Raisin Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 10 g onions
- 20 g fat or margarine
- 20 g flour
- 1/3 l water (better: broth)
- 5 g salt
- 1 1/2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 1/2 tablespoons syrup
- 20 g large raisins
- 1 teaspoon sugar
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Peel and cut the onion into cubes. Clean and soak the raisins according to instructions. Season 1/3 l of liquid with salt, vinegar, and syrup.
2. In an earthenware pot, let the fat cook, add the sugar for browning, add the flour and onion cubes and let them brown as well. Stirring, pour in the liquid and bring to a boil.
3. Add the drained raisins, season with salt and pepper, and serve the sauce with beef.

title: Rossoli von Mandeln (Almond Rossoli)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 Loth sweet almonds
- 4 Loth bitter almonds or 4 Loth peach kernels
- Half a Loth galangal
- One orange peel
- Red sandalwood (one Kreuzer worth)
- One Maas first runoff or strong fruit brandy
- One Schoppen cinnamon water

## Directions

1. Remove the skin from bitter almonds or peach kernels
2. Pound them finely with the sweet almonds
3. Cut half a Loth galangal into small pieces
4. Place all of this with one orange peel and red sandalwood in a glass flask
5. Pour one Maas first runoff or strong fruit brandy and one Schoppen cinnamon water into the flask
6. Bind the flask tightly
7. Leave it in the sun or warmth for 12 to 14 days
8. Strain the Rossoli through a felt hat
9. Store it

title: Rostbraten (Grilled Roast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- piece from the rump, hind rib
- salt
- pepper
- lemon butter
- lemon

## Directions

1. Take a piece from the rump (hind rib), remove the rib bone and the shank below.
2. Cut the meat into half-finger-thick slices.
3. Pound them lightly, rub them with salt and pepper.
4. Roast them with lemon butter on the grill until beautifully golden.
5. They will be done in a quarter hour.
6. Place them on a platter and serve with lemon.

title: Rote Grütze (Red Berry Pudding)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1-1/2 lb) currants or other fruit
- 100 g sugar according to taste
- 65 g coarse semolina or ground rice
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Wash and stem the currants and place in a pot with 1/4 l water, covered
2. Prepare cups or bowls by rinsing with water
3. Stir semolina with cold water and let drain
4. Cook the currants for 1/2 hour, then strain through a sieve or cloth and press
5. Mix the strained fruit with sugar and return to the stove
6. The liquid should measure about 1/2 l; bring to a boil and pour in semolina or rice
7. Cook slowly for 10-15 minutes while stirring, pour into moulds or cups and let cool before turning out

title: Rote Rüben (Pickled Beets)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2-1/2 kg (5 Pfd) rote Rüben (beets)
- 8 g Kümmel (caraway seeds)
- 3 tablespoons horseradish chunks
- 1/2 l vinegar
- 10 g sugar
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Clean the beets carefully with a brush, being careful not to tear the skin or break off the root end, otherwise the beets will become pale in cooking water.
2. Place them carefully in boiling water and cook until soft, which takes varying time depending on the size of the beets.
3. Place them briefly in cold water and slip the skin off all the beets smoothly.
4. Before cutting the prepared beets, wash your hands so nothing from the outside of the beet gets onto the beet slices.
5. Cut the beets over the pot in which they should remain, in slices the thickness of a coin, and sprinkle cleaned caraway seeds and small horseradish chunks thinly between the layers.
6. Bring the vinegar, which should not be too sharp, to a boil with sugar and salt, and after cooling pour it over the beets, which should be weighted down so they remain under the liquid.
7. Tie the pot with parchment paper. The small heart leaves are suitable for salad.

title: Rothes Eis (Red Icing)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound grated fine sugar
- 1 egg white
- cochineal dye or peas juice

## Directions

1. Beat one egg white into one quarter pound grated fine sugar
2. Stir together for a quarter hour
3. Add cochineal dye or pea juice drop by drop until it is beautifully red and use as desired

title: Rothwildbret (Red Game Meat)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Game meat of desired size
- One quarter pound bacon
- Salt
- Cloves
- Pepper
- Fat
- Onions
- Lemons
- Bay leaves
- Celery
- Meat broth
- Vinegar (unless long marinated)
- Black bread
- Lard
- Flour
- Wine
- Jus
- Mace
- Cardamom

## Directions

1. Wipe the game meat clean with a cloth and clean it of hair.
2. Cut one quarter pound of bacon into long pieces, turn them in salt, cloves, and pepper, pierce holes in the meat with a knife, and insert a piece of cut bacon into each hole until all the bacon is used.
3. Place the game meat in a casserole with a large piece of fat over the fire, add sliced onions, lemons, bay leaves, and celery, salt to taste.
4. Pour a spoonful of meat broth and some vinegar over it and let it cook covered for 2 hours (but if the game has been marinating for a long time, do not add vinegar).
5. Remove the crust from black bread and bake it in lard until light golden; once the meat is browned, remove it and put the light golden bread crust into the casserole, sprinkle a little flour in it.
6. Pour a quart of wine, a ladleful of jus, and some meat broth over it and let it cook together for a quarter hour.
7. Pass the broth through a fine sieve, pour it back into the casserole, place the meat back into it, and add the grated zest and juice of half a lemon, mace, pepper, and some ground cardamom.

title: Rotkohl (Red Cabbage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) red cabbage
- 1/2 tablespoon vinegar
- 50 g fat
- 1/8 l vinegar
- 30 g sugar
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 spice dose
- 1 pinch cinnamon
- 1 pinch pepper
- 10 g salt
- 2 l water

## Directions

1. Remove the outer leaves from the cabbage and cut it in half, then into very fine strips on a cutting board.
2. Pour boiling salted water with 1/2 tablespoon of vinegar over it, drain well, and mix it in a bowl with several spoonfuls of fresh vinegar until it is nicely red.
3. Heat the fat (goose, pork fat, or lard) with the remaining vinegar, salt, and sugar in an earthenware pot.
4. Add the vinegar-refreshed cabbage, mix well once the liquid boils, and let it braise slowly covered for 2.5 hours.
5. At the very end, add a little pepper, ground spices, and cinnamon; however, this is a matter of taste.
6. Before cooking, adding spices causes a poor color.
7. Before serving, adjust the seasoning with sugar, vinegar, and salt, as vinegar evaporates more or less.

title: Russische Crême (Russian Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Loth isinglass
- 1 schoppen water
- half Maas milk or cream
- one-quarter pound sugar rubbed with 1 orange
- 6 egg yolks
- milk
- juice of 2 oranges
- half schoppen Arak
- 1 schoppen whipped cream
- anise oil

## Directions

1. Cook 2 Loth isinglass with 1 schoppen water until dissolved
2. Strain the water through a hair sieve into a bowl
3. Cook half a Maas milk (or cream is better) with one-quarter pound sugar rubbed with 1 orange
4. Beat 6 egg yolks smooth with a little milk
5. Slowly stir the boiling milk and isinglass water into the egg yolks
6. Add the juice of 2 oranges
7. Let everything come together once more in the pan
8. Strain the cream through a hair sieve into a porcelain bowl
9. Add half a schoppen Arak and 1 schoppen whipped cream
10. Fill the cream into a mold brushed with anise oil
11. Let it cool on ice

title: Russische Crême anderer Art (Russian Cream, Another Kind)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 spoonfuls sieved sugar
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls Arak
- 3 egg whites

## Directions

1. Beat 3 spoonfuls sieved sugar and 3 egg yolks together very thick
2. Pour 2 spoonfuls Arak to it
3. Stir for another quarter hour
4. Finally mix in the snow from 3 egg whites
5. Arrange the cream on a salad bowl
6. Whipped cream may be added if desired

title: Russischer Pudding (Russian Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cleaned beef marrow
- 6 loth grated white bread
- 7 cooking spoonfuls fine flour
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 pound large raisins
- 1/2 pound small raisins
- 1 glass strong arak
- zest of 1 lemon, chopped
- cinnamon to taste
- 10 eggs
- 1 orange
- zest of 1 orange
- 1 lemon
- juice of 1 lemon
- 1 bottle wine
- 3 egg yolks
- flour
- cold water

## Directions

1. Cut the cleaned beef marrow into small pieces
2. Mix it well with 6 loth grated white bread, 7 cooking spoonfuls fine flour, 1/2 pound sugar, 1/2 pound large raisins and the same amount of small raisins, 1 glass strong arak, the chopped zest of 1 lemon, cinnamon, and 10 eggs
3. Bind it in a damp cloth, but not tightly, and cook 2 hours in boiling water
4. For the sauce: grate 1 orange on sugar, pull off the white skin, remove the pits, cut the flesh into pieces
5. Let this come to boil together with the grated zest, the juice of 1 lemon and 1 bottle of wine
6. Beat 3 egg yolks in a pot, add 3 fingers-full of flour, stir it smooth with cold water
7. Add the boiling spiced wine to it, let it come to boil once more while it bubbles strongly and pour the sauce over the arranged pudding

title: Rührkartoffeln (Mashed Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) potatoes
- 1/4 l water
- 1/2 l whole milk
- 5 g salt

## Directions

1. Peel the potatoes and cook them like salted potatoes.
2. Mash the cooked potatoes very finely or pass them through a sieve.
3. Beat them very hot with milk and salt, which is added gradually, until they reach a boil.
4. If desired, a small piece of butter can be beaten in.

title: Sagosuppe (Sago Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound sago
- Clear meat broth
- Two egg yolks
- Two tablespoons of sweet cream
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pour clear meat broth into a casserole and slowly let the sago run into it
2. Cover and let cook slowly, stirring often
3. Pour in as much meat broth as needed for the soup
4. Beat two egg yolks into the soup bowl and add two tablespoons of sweet cream
5. Arrange the sago over it and sprinkle nutmeg on top. 1/4 pound of sago is sufficient for 8 people

title: Salat à la patrie (Salad à la Patrie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one roasted chicken or veal roast
- 2 hard-boiled eggs
- boiled pears
- steamed apples
- plums
- cherries
- sour cherries
- herring
- anchovies
- endive
- capers
- head lettuce
- 2 spoonfuls of tarragon vinegar
- 3 spoonfuls of Provençal oil
- salt
- pepper
- yolk of 6 hard-boiled eggs
- red beet broth
- muscat nutmeg
- one beautiful plum or preserved nut
- preserved cherries

## Directions

1. Cut the roasted chicken or veal roast into small pieces.
2. Clean and cut the herring and anchovies.
3. Combine in a bowl the roasted meat, hard-boiled eggs, boiled pears, steamed apples, plums, cherries, sour cherries, herring, anchovies, endive, capers, and head lettuce.
4. Stir together 2 spoonfuls of tarragon vinegar, 3 spoonfuls of Provençal oil, salt and pepper.
5. Take a not very deep salad bowl, arrange the salad beautifully like a pyramid.
6. Press the yolk of 6 hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve, cut the whites into desired pieces, lay them in red beet broth.
7. Stir everything together for a quarter hour, pour over the stirred mixture one tablespoon of vinegar, 3 drops of oil, a knife's point of salt, some muscat nutmeg.
8. Cover the salad completely with this and place on top a beautiful plum or preserved nut, around it the eggs which have lain in the red beet broth and preserved cherries.

title: Salat, Grüner (Green Salad)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 heads green lettuce
- 40 g bacon
- vinegar to taste
- 3 g salt
- 2 tablespoons sugar or 1 tablespoon syrup

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into cubes and brown it.
2. Clean the lettuce by removing the thickest stems, wash it several times until it is free of sand, place it in a clean cloth, and shake it dry by gathering the ends of the cloth and swinging it.
3. Mix all ingredients and adjust the seasoning of the liquid.
4. Add the lettuce and mix it with 2 spoons.
5. Taste it; if it is too sour, add 1 more tablespoon of water.
6. Do not assemble the salad long before the meal, as it will otherwise become unattractive and soft.

title: Salmigondis für Fastenpasteten (Salmigondis for Fasting Pies)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cooked carp roe
- pike liver
- crayfish tails
- mushrooms
- morels
- shallot onions
- parsley
- thyme
- butter
- salt
- pepper
- several tablespoons good meat broth
- egg yolks
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Cut cooked carp roe, pike liver, and crayfish tails into small diced pieces.
2. Braise mushrooms and morels, finely cut, with shallot onions, parsley, and thyme in a piece of butter for several minutes.
3. Mix the prepared ingredients together.
4. Add salt, pepper, and several tablespoons of good meat broth.
5. Let everything cook together for several minutes.
6. Stir in egg yolks and some lemon juice.
7. Let it cool and use as needed for pies on fasting days.

title: Salmigondis zu Fastenpasteten (Salmigondis for Fasting Pastries)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Boiled carp roe
- Pike liver
- Crayfish tails
- Mushrooms
- Morels
- Shallot onions
- Parsley
- Thyme
- Butter
- Salt
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Cut boiled carp roe, pike liver, and crayfish tails into small diced pieces
2. Steam mushrooms and morels, finely cut, with shallot onions, parsley, and thyme in a piece of butter for several minutes
3. Mix the above with it
4. Add salt, pepper, and a few tablespoons of good

title: Salted Ham (Schinken einzusalzen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ham
- Salt
- Pepper, ground
- Juniper berries
- Saltpeter (4 loth)
- Garlic
- Onions

## Directions

1. Remove the hip bone from the ham.
2. Roast in an iron pan salt as much as needed, together with ground pepper and juniper berries.
3. Add 4 loth of saltpeter and rub the ham with it, especially where the hip bone was removed.
4. The salt must be as warm as one can bear it in the hand.
5. Lay the hams next to each other in a stand with a tap at the bottom (pork can be laid between them in the gaps).
6. Cut garlic and onions and add to it.
7. Close the stand firmly and press it down.
8. Let everything stand for 3 days.
9. Cook 2 pounds of salt in 2 maas of water, let it cool, pour it over the hams, and let it stand for another 8 days.
10. Repeat this for 4 to 5 weeks, cooking the brine fresh every 8 days.
11. After 5 weeks, hang the hams in the smoke chamber.

title: Salzgurken (Salt Cucumbers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Halbgewachsene Gurken (Half-grown cucumbers)
- Salz (Salt)
- Pfeffer (Peppercorns)
- Lorbeerblätter (Bay leaves)
- Traubenlaub (Grape leaves)
- Grüner Fenchel (Green fennel)
- Citronenkraut (Lemon balm)
- Wasser (Water)

## Directions

1. Soak half-grown cucumbers in salt water for 24 hours
2. Dry them with a clean cloth
3. Line the bottom of the designated small barrel with coarsely crushed peppercorns, bay leaves, and grape leaves
4. Layer cucumbers, then a layer of the spice mixture, alternating until all is used
5. Top with green fennel, lemon balm, and bay leaves
6. Cook water and salt together (for one measure of water, use one large handful of salt)
7. Let cool, then pour over the cucumbers
8. Seal the barrel securely and pitch it inside and out
9. Place in the sun for 8 days and shake daily
10. In 14 days the cucumbers are ready to eat and will keep well for a whole year

title: Salzgurken (Salted Pickles)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 60 medium cucumbers
- 50 g grape leaves
- 30 g dill
- 7-1/2 l water
- 100 g salt
- 1 small barrel

## Directions

1. Soak the cucumbers for 24 hours in spring water, wipe them off, and pack them with the washed leaves and dill into a very clean barrel.
2. Bring water and salt to a boil and pour it hot over the cucumbers; repeat this procedure on the following two days.
3. The liquid must cover the cucumbers well.
4. After cooling, bung the barrel and turn it frequently until use.

title: Salzkartoffeln (Boiled Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg (1.5 pounds) raw unpeeled potatoes
- 5 g salt
- 1/2 l water

## Directions

1. After cleaning, peel the potatoes in water, round in shape; leave smaller ones whole and halve larger ones.
2. After peeling, wash the potatoes and place them in cold water with salt.
3. Cook the potatoes well covered for 20 minutes to an hour, depending on age and variety.
4. Drain the water completely.
5. Dry-steam the potatoes for several more minutes covered on a hot plate with frequent shaking.
6. Open the pot briefly and serve the potatoes.

title: Salzküchlein (Salt Biscuits)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg yolks
- 1 pound sugar
- 6 loth almonds ground with rose water
- oblaten (wafer paper)
- coarsely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Whisk 3 egg yolks with 1 pound sugar for a quarter hour
2. Stir in 6 loth almonds ground with rose water for a quarter hour
3. Cut square pieces from oblaten
4. Spread the mixture thickly on each piece (about the thickness of a knife back)
5. Place on a black baking sheet
6. Sprinkle coarsely ground sugar on top
7. Bake

title: Sandtorte (Sand Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/2 pound fine sugar
- 1 lemon
- 6 egg whites
- 1/2 pound flour

## Directions

1. Melt 1/2 pound butter in a bowl
2. Stir in 6 egg yolks, 1/2 pound fine sugar, the grated zest of 1 lemon, the beaten whites from 6 eggs, and 1/2 pound flour very thick with the butter
3. Fill the mixture into a mold and bake the torte in the oven

title: Sardellen mit verlornen Eiern (Anchovies with Poached Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- poached eggs
- black bread
- lard
- brown gingerbread (Lebkuchen)
- wine
- broth
- lemon
- anchovies

## Directions

1. Prepare poached eggs as described in the egg preparation instructions and place them on a plate.
2. Bake a piece of black bread nicely golden in lard and cook it in a casserole with a piece of brown gingerbread, one pint of wine, and some broth.
3. Press through a hair sieve, return to the casserole with the juice and finely-chopped lemon zest, and bring to a boil.
4. Take half a pound of cleaned anchovies, remove the bones, cut them into small pieces, and lay them on the plate with the eggs.
5. Arrange the sauce over them and serve at table.

title: Sauerampfersuppe (Sorrel Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One handful of clean washed sorrel
- Two shallot onions
- A piece of butter
- One tablespoon of flour
- Meat broth or jus
- Two egg yolks
- Two tablespoons of sweet cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Finely chop the sorrel and shallot onions together
2. Place a piece of butter in a casserole and braise everything together
3. Stir in one tablespoon of flour, pour in meat broth or jus
4. Let it come to a boil once more
5. Arrange over soup dumplings (described below)
6. In a small dish, stir two egg yolks with two tablespoons of sweet cream and add to the soup
7. Add salt and nutmeg

title: Sauerkohl (Sauerkraut)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small head cabbage or 1/2 kg (1 pound) sauerkraut
- 50 g pork or goose fat or lard
- 15 g onions
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 8 g salt
- 5 g flour
- 1 pinch pepper
- 5 g sugar
- 1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Prepare fresh cabbage as for red cabbage. Loosen sauerkraut with a fork while washing it.
2. In an earthenware pot in which the fat with an onion has been brought to a boil, pour the cabbage over with salt water and some fresh vinegar and braise it covered for several hours.
3. Whisk a teaspoon of the fat with flour, mix with vinegar and pepper, pour it over the cabbage, and cook it through.
4. The cabbage must look completely white and have a beautiful sour taste, which is only subdued by sugar.
5. The cabbage must be white and have a nice sour flavor, which is only tempered by sugar.

title: Sauerkraut (Sauerkraut)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Cabbage
- Salt (as much as needed)
- 2 handfuls of juniper berries

## Directions

1. Cut the stalk and ribs from the cabbage
2. Cut this finely in the usual way
3. Mix with salt as much as needed
4. Add 2 handfuls of juniper berries
5. Put the cabbage in a clean vessel into which cabbage leaves have been previously laid
6. Press the cabbage firmly together with a wooden pestle
7. Lay cabbage leaves on top
8. Cover with a clean cloth and the vessel's lid
9. Weight it well
10. In 14 days the cabbage is already edible

title: Sauerkraut mit Feldhühnern (Sauerkraut with Partridges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Pork fat
- Onions
- 1 glass wine
- Partridges
- Bacon
- Salt
- Pepper
- Lemons
- Cloves
- Mace
- Bay leaves
- Yellow turnips
- Celery
- Lemon cabbage
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Cook the sauerkraut soft in a casserole with pork fat, onions and a glass of wine.
2. Dress the partridges, wrap them in bacon, add salt, pepper, lemons, cloves, mace, bay leaves, onions, yellow turnips, celery and lemon cabbage, pour a little meat broth over and braise covered in a casserole for an hour.
3. Remove the lid from the casserole, let the hens brown beautifully yellow, cut off the breast, arrange the sauerkraut, place the breasts nicely on top and pour the fat in which they were fried over the sauerkraut.

title: Sauerkraut mit Hecht (Sauerkraut with Pike)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Wine, cognac, or arak
- Pike
- Freshly boiled potatoes
- Half a Vierling of butter
- 1 cooking spoon of flour
- 1 egg

## Directions

1. Cook the sauerkraut soft with wine, cognac, or arak, then drain it well in a sieve.
2. If you have leftover pike, use it; otherwise, cook a pike blue, remove the bones and cut it into small pieces.
3. Peel fresh boiled potatoes, cut them into slices, and layer them in a baking pan alternately with sauerkraut and pike, repeating until full.
4. Stir together half a Vierling of butter and 1 cooking spoon of flour with an egg, pour this over the sauerkraut, bake in the oven, and turn it out onto a plate before serving.

title: Sauerkraut mit Lerchen (Sauerkraut with Larks)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Pork fat
- Onions
- 1 bottle Champagne
- Larks
- Fat
- Bay leaf
- Whole pepper
- Cloves

## Directions

1. Cook the sauerkraut soft with pork fat, onions, and a bottle of Champagne.
2. Pluck the larks, remove the eyes, place the head on the breast, twist the feet, thread 6-7 larks onto a wooden skewer and cook them in a casserole with a piece of fat, onions, bay leaf, whole pepper and cloves for an hour.
3. When they are beautiful yellow, arrange the sauerkraut, place the larks nicely on top so their heads are visible, and cover with sauerkraut again, but the heads must always remain visible.

title: Sauerkraut mit saurem Rahm (Sauerkraut with Sour Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Sauerkraut
- Bacon, cut into pieces
- 2 large onions, finely cut
- A piece of hare, wild duck, or smoked meat
- Sour cream
- Flour
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Press the sauerkraut firmly and place it in a casserole with a piece of cut bacon, 2 large finely cut onions, and a piece of hare, wild duck, or smoked meat.
2. Braise everything together; if the sauerkraut runs out of juice, sour cream must be poured over it.
3. A quarter hour before serving, dust 3 fingers' worth of flour and 1 knife-tip of pepper over the sauerkraut and serve it with the meat.

title: Sauerkrauttorte (Sauerkraut Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sauerkraut
- cold water
- zest of 3 lemons
- 3 loth blanched almonds
- 3 loth citron
- 3 loth orange peel
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/2 glass water
- butter dough
- egg
- raw potatoes
- juice of 2 lemons

## Directions

1. Squeeze the juice from 1/2 pound sauerkraut, place it in cold water, and add fresh water daily, repeating this 5 to 6 days
2. Cook the sauerkraut soft, let it drain in a sieve, and press it firmly
3. Chop the zest from 3 lemons, cut 3 loth blanched almonds, 3 loth citron, and orange peel lengthwise
4. Cook 1/2 pound sugar with 1/2 glass water in a brass pan until the sugar forms bubbles
5. Add the sauerkraut and the chopped ingredients, let everything cook 2 to 3 minutes
6. Line a plafond with butter dough, brush with egg, place raw potatoes in the sheet so the dough does not shrink, and bake golden yellow
7. Press the juice from 2 lemons on it, remove the potatoes, fill the mixture into the sheet, and let the torte bake a quarter hour in the oven

title: Saure Rahmnudeln (Sour Cream Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen of sour cream
- flour
- eggs
- 1 maas of milk
- butter
- 2 eggs
- sour cream

## Directions

1. Let 1 schoppen of sour cream simmer in a brass pan
2. Stir in flour until it becomes a thick porridge and remove from heat
3. Beat in eggs until it becomes a firm sparrow dough
4. Set 1 maas of milk on the fire
5. Scrape noodles from the dough (laid on a sparrow board) into the boiling milk
6. Cover and let them cook well
7. Remove with a skimming spoon and let them drain well on a board
8. Coat a dish to be served with butter and lay the noodles crosswise over the top
9. Stir 2 eggs together with a little milk and sour cream, pour over the noodles and bake them beautifully golden in the oven

title: Saure Rahmstrudel (Sour Cream Strudel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- approximately 1/2 pound flour
- 2 egg whites
- butter the size of a walnut
- lukewarm milk
- thick sour cream
- 2 knife-tips of salt
- 4 eggs
- 1 schoppen of milk
- 1 small piece of butter

## Directions

1. Put approximately 1/2 pound flour on a noodle board, add 2 egg whites, butter the size of a walnut, and a little lukewarm milk
2. Make a dough like for noodles but not as stiff
3. Work it very thoroughly for a quarter hour and let it rest for half an hour if time allows
4. Roll out the sheets as fine as possible and stretch the strudel on a cloth as fine as postpaper
5. Let it dry well
6. Mix 2 knife-tips of salt into thick sour cream
7. Beat 4 eggs into stiff snow and fold it into the cream
8. Place the strudel on the noodle board, coat it with cream, and roll it up
9. Put 1 schoppen of milk and a small piece of butter in a frying pan and let them boil together
10. Place the strudel in it, put the dish in an oven and let it rise

title: Schalotteis mit Schlagrahm (Shallot Ice with Whipped Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound bacon rinds
- Cold water
- 1 maas water
- 6 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 3 schoppen thick sweet cream
- 1 schoppen whipped cream
- Sugar bread (Zuckerbrod)

## Directions

1. Place 1/2 pound bacon rinds with cold water on the fire and let cook for two minutes
2. Pour off the water, place the rinds on a clean board and scrape off the fat
3. Put the rinds again in a clean dish and pour 1 maas water over
4. Let it reduce to 3 schoppen
5. Strain through a hair sieve or woolen cloth and let stand for a quarter hour
6. Pour into a brass pan, add 6 loth sugar with a lemon rind grated into it
7. Add 3 schoppen thick sweet cream and let cook together as long as a hard egg
8. Strain through a hair sieve and let cool
9. Meanwhile, beat 1 schoppen whipped cream in a bowl with a white whisk or chocolate whisk until very thick
10. Place on a hair sieve and let drain
11. When the crème is cold, fold in the snow
12. Line a smooth deep form throughout with sugar bread so that one piece touches another, as with apple shallot
13. Fill the crème into it, let it set firm and turn it out onto a plate

title: Scharmützeln (Scharmützeln)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 egg whites
- 1 pound finely ground sugar
- firm paper

## Directions

1. Beat 5 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Whisk with 1 pound finely ground sugar for half an hour until it forms a thick icing
3. Cut round pieces from stiff paper
4. Form small capsules from the paper and stretch them over a round stick or thimble
5. Fill the capsules halfway full with the meringue mixture
6. Place on a black baking sheet
7. Put in a cooled oven and bake until completely white

title: Schellfisch, Gekocht (Haddock, Boiled)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 small haddock of 1/2 kg (1 pound)
- 40 g salt
- 2 l water

## Directions

1. Scale the fish by laying it on a moistened board, holding it by the tail with a cloth, and using a knife held at an angle upward to remove the scales. Hold the knife at an angle so the scales do not scatter around the kitchen.
2. Gut the fish by cutting it open, finding the beginning and end of the intestines, loosening them with the knife, and removing all innards with your index finger.
3. If the interior of the fish is clean and the black skin has been removed, wash it carefully with water.
4. Trim the fins and tail neatly with scissors.
5. Place the fish on a plate, put this in the cooking pot and pour enough water over it so it is covered. For 1 l water, use 20 g salt.
6. Bring the prepared whole fish slowly to a boil; let whole fish come to a boil only once and then finish cooking gently.
7. The fish is done when the fins on the back can be pulled out easily and the flesh at the gills is white.
8. Remove the plate with the fish using a skimming spoon, pour off the water from the plate, wipe it clean and serve the fish with brown butter.

title: Schlacht bei Austerlitz (Battle of Austerlitz Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 stale biscuit
- butter
- raisins
- preserved nuts
- apricots
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- 2 loth blanched almonds
- milk
- 1 schoppen sweet cream
- 2 loth sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1/2 glass maraschino

## Directions

1. Cut 1 stale biscuit into finger-length pieces.
2. Grease a metal mold with butter and lay alternating layers: biscuit and raisins, biscuit and preserved nuts, biscuit and apricots, continuing until the form is full but leaving a finger's breadth empty at the top.
3. Mix 2 egg yolks with 1 schoppen sweet cream and pour over the mixture.
4. Seal the cover tightly and boil the pudding for half an hour.
5. For the sauce: pound 2 loth blanched almonds very fine with milk, add 1 schoppen sweet cream and 2 loth sugar grated from a lemon, bring to a boil, add 1/2 glass maraschino, and pour over the pudding.

title: Schlotzerbrod (Schlotz Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 egg yolks
- 1 lemon
- 18 Loth flour
- bacon
- anise

## Directions

1. Stir together for a quarter hour: 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar, 2 whole eggs, 3 egg yolks, and the yolk of 1 lemon (grated on a grater)
2. Mix in 18 Loth flour
3. Form finger-long small sticks on the noodle board that are somewhat thinner in the middle than at the ends
4. Brush a black sheet with bacon
5. Sprinkle it with anise
6. Place the little breads on it, not too close together
7. Bake them beautifully golden in the oven

title: Schmales Anisbrod (Thin Anise Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound sugar
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites
- 1 lemon
- 1 pound flour
- 2 handfuls anise, cleaned

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 pound sugar, 10 egg yolks, and the snow from 10 egg whites strongly for half an hour
2. Add the zest of 1 lemon (grated on a grater), 1 pound flour, and 2 handfuls of clean anise
3. Sprinkle flour on a black sheet
4. Form long, hand-width strips of dough and place them on the sheet
5. Bake in the oven
6. When cooled, cut into slices
7. Toast in a not-too-hot oven

title: Schmorwurst (Braised Sausage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g Bratwurst (bratwurst sausage)
- 10 g Mehl (flour)
- 15 g Fett oder Margarine (fat or margarine)
- 25 g Brotrinde (bread crust)
- 1 Gewürzdosis (1 spice dose)
- 15 g Zwiebeln (onions)
- 1 Prise Salz (pinch of salt)
- 1/4 l Weissbier oder Wasser (white beer or water)
- 5 g Kartoffelmehl (potato starch)
- 2 Tropfen Speisefarbe (2 drops food coloring)

## Directions

1. Brown the bratwurst in the fat, dust with flour, then braise in a braising pot in white beer or water with the remaining ingredients (except potato starch and food coloring) for 3/4 hour over low heat.
2. Place the sausage on a serving dish.
3. Degrease the sauce and pass through a sieve; cook 3 tablespoons of it with potato starch mixed in water, and pour over the arranged sausage.
4. Color the remaining sauce with food coloring and serve on the side.

title: Schnecken mit Eiern (Snails with Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Snails
- Butter
- Parsley
- Water (half a Maas)
- Chives
- 6 Eggs
- 3 spoonfuls of sour cream

## Directions

1. Boil the snails as described in recipe 419
2. Put them in a casserole and cook them for half an hour with a piece of butter, some parsley, and water
3. Chop a handful of parsley and chives if available, and braise the chopped herbs in a piece of butter
4. Take 6 eggs and 3 spoonfuls of sour cream and add them
5. Let the snails drain well, stir them together with the braised herbs over the fire
6. When the mixture is thick, serve it

title: Schneckenbrei (Snail Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- 1/2 lemon peel
- 1 onion
- a handful of parsley
- 1/8 pound butter
- 3 fingers flour
- 1 spoon meat broth
- salt
- pepper
- 1/2 lemon juice

## Directions

1. Prepare the snails as previously described, then weigh them very fine with 1/2 lemon peel, 1 onion and a handful of parsley
2. Let 1/8 pound butter melt in a casserole, braise the weighed snails in it, sprinkle 3 fingers flour on top
3. Pour 1 spoon meat broth to it, add salt and pepper along with the juice of 1/2 lemon and let everything come to a boil together once more

title: Schneckennudeln mit Mandeln (Spiral Noodles with Almonds)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds fine flour
- 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast
- 1 schoppen of lukewarm milk
- 1/4 pound blanched almonds
- egg white
- 3 loth of ground sugar
- 2 egg yolks
- zest of 1 lemon
- 4 loth of butter
- sour cream
- melted butter

## Directions

1. With 2 pounds fine flour, 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast, and 1 schoppen of lukewarm milk make a dough in a bowl and let it rise in warmth
2. Pound 1/4 pound of blanched almonds finely with egg white
3. Add 3 loth of ground sugar, 2 egg yolks, and the zest of 1 lemon and stir everything together for a quarter hour
4. Add 4 loth of butter and work everything together with the dough well (if still too stiff, pour in sour cream)
5. Beat it for a quarter hour, cover it and let it rise well
6. When ripe enough, make noodles the size of a hen's egg, stretch them out so they become finger-long
7. Coat them first with melted butter and the stirred almonds knife-back thick, roll them together like spirals
8. Place them in a dish where butter has been melted beforehand, let them stand in it for another quarter hour and bake them in an oven until beautifully golden
9. Serve with compote of sloes

title: Schneckensuppe (Snail Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 50 snails
- Parsley
- Chervil
- Sorrel
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth
- Egg yolks
- Sour cream
- Toasted bread slices
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil the snails as usual, clean them, remove the slime, and cook for another half hour, then chop
2. Finely chop 1 handful each of parsley, chervil, and sorrel together
3. Steam the snails with the chopped herbs in melted butter for a quarter hour
4. Bring to a boil with 1 tablespoon of flour and 1 ladle of meat broth, adding more meat broth as needed
5. Arrange the soup over toasted bread slices
6. Stir in 2 egg yolks with 2 tablespoons of sour cream
7. Season to taste with salt, nutmeg, etc.

title: Schnee anderer Art (Snow of Another Kind)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen thick sweet cream, skimmed with a spoon
- 6 loth pounded sugar
- half a glass of Champagne wine

## Directions

1. Mix the pounded sugar and Champagne wine into the thick sweet cream
2. Whip the entire mixture in a wide pot with a chocolate whisk until foamy
3. Bring the foam to a fine sieve with a spoon and continue until all is done
4. Place the foam on a plate and sprinkle with sugar

title: Schnee anderer Art (Snow, Another Kind)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schoppen thick sweet cream
- 6 Loth pounded sugar
- half glass champagne wine

## Directions

1. Mix 6 Loth pounded sugar and half a glass champagne wine into 1 schoppen thick sweet cream that has been lifted with a spoon
2. Whip the whole mixture in a wide pot with a chocolate whisk
3. Bring the foam to a hair sieve with a spoon and continue until everything is done

title: Schneeballen (Snowballs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 egg yolks
- 2 spoons crushed sugar
- Grated lemon peel
- 2 spoons thick sour cream
- 6 lots flour

## Directions

1. Put all ingredients together on a noodle board
2. Roll out a dough as for noodles
3. Form about 4 inch large circles with the pastry wheel
4. Draw 6 lines in each circle, leaving a small finger-width border
5. Lift the second, fourth and sixth strips upward so the others sink down
6. Bring the balls into a not too large pan with boiling lard
7. Bake them a beautiful yellow and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Schneegans (Snow Goose)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- snow goose
- herbs
- vinegar
- salt
- pepper
- onion with cloves
- lemon
- bacon
- sauce

## Directions

1. Pluck the goose, singe it, and lay it for 4 days with all manner of herbs in vinegar.
2. Cut off the head and neck.
3. Rub the goose with salt and pepper.
4. Place an onion studded with cloves and a piece of lemon inside it.
5. Cut off the feet and stick them into the skin.
6. Wrap the goose in bacon, roast it in the oven until beautifully golden.
7. Place a sheet of paper over it and baste it well with sauce.

title: Schneehäufchen (Snow Peaks)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 egg whites
- 1/2 pound sugar
- 3 to 4 drops anise oil
- wax

## Directions

1. Beat 3 egg whites to stiff peaks
2. Mix in 1/2 pound sugar and 3 to 4 drops anise oil (the mixture should remain firm, not liquid)
3. Brush a black baking sheet with wax
4. Drop small mounds of the mixture onto the sheet
5. Bake in a cooled oven

title: Schneidebohnen (String Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) string beans
- 2 l water for blanching
- 1/2 l mutton broth
- 1 stalk parsley
- 1 tablespoon melted beef fat or margarine
- 6 g flour
- 1 teaspoon chopped parsley
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cut the string beans instead of breaking them, and prepare them otherwise the same way as green beans.

title: Schnepfenpastete auf französische Art (Woodcock Pastry, French Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 cleaned woodcocks
- Butter pastry dough for a 3-inch-high pastry
- Butter
- Onions
- Juniper berries
- Bay leaves
- Lemons
- 2 shallot onions
- Parsley
- Red wine, 1 glass
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Mace
- Good jus, 2 tablespoons

## Directions

1. Make a 3-inch-high pastry from butter dough
2. Tuck the heads of 3 cleaned woodcocks onto their breasts
3. Lard the woodcocks and cut them into 4 parts
4. Cut the breast from one woodcock
5. Steam the woodcocks in a casserole with a piece of butter, onions, juniper berries, bay leaves, and lemons for half an hour
6. Press out the woodcock intestines firmly, then mince them together with the woodcock breast, 2 shallot onions, half a lemon peel, and parsley
7. Remove the steamed woodcocks from the casserole
8. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve
9. Return the sauce to the casserole with the minced woodcock mixture
10. Cook for only 1 minute more with the juice of half a lemon, 1 glass red wine, salt, pepper, cloves, mace, and 2 tablespoons good jus
11. Pour half the sauce into the pastry
12. Lay the cooked woodcocks inside
13. Pour the remaining sauce over them
14. Cover with a butter dough lid
15. Brush with egg
16. Bake the pastry in the oven

title: Schnepfenpastete auf Wiener Art (Woodcock Pastry in the Viennese Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 woodcock, cleaned and larded with bacon
- butter
- onions
- heart, liver and cleaned intestines of woodcock
- half a lemon zest and pulp
- pepper
- nutmeg flower
- half a nutmeg
- salt
- 4 handfuls flour
- 12 loth butter
- 12 loth lard
- 2 egg yolks
- salt

## Directions

1. Braise 3 to 4 cleaned and bacon-larded woodcock with a piece of butter and some onions until half-cooked
2. Finely chop the heart, liver, cleaned intestines, half a lemon zest and pulp
3. Remove the woodcock from the casserole, turn the chopped mixture in it a few times, remove from heat
4. Add a knife-tip each of pounded pepper, nutmeg flower, half a nutmeg and salt
5. Make the dough: work together 4 handfuls flour, 12 loth butter, 12 loth lard, 2 egg yolks and a little salt into firm dough on a board
6. Roll out finger-thick and make a pastry of desired shape
7. Place the woodcock on it, pour the woodcock preparation inside
8. Cover with a lid made from the same dough
9. Brush the pastry with egg and bake in the oven

title: Schnitten zu Krebssuppe (Slices for Crayfish Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound of butter
- 8 egg yolks
- half a schoppen of thick sour cream
- 8 spoonfuls of fine flour
- salt
- mace
- 8 egg whites

## Directions

1. Lightly beat the butter
2. Gradually add 8 egg yolks
3. Add half a schoppen of thick sour cream in the same manner
4. Add 8 spoonfuls of fine flour, salt, and mace
5. Finally fold in the snow from 8 egg whites
6. Brush a tin mold with butter
7. Bake the mixture in the mold at gentle heat
8. Cut into slices and add to crayfish soup

title: Schnitten zu Krebssuppe auf andere Art (Slices for Crayfish Soup, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 soaked and squeezed rolls
- one quarter pound of butter
- crayfish butter
- 3 eggs
- salt
- nutmeg
- spinach juice

## Directions

1. Grate 3 soaked and squeezed rolls, each separately in a bowl
2. Lightly beat each with one quarter pound of butter in a bowl (one bowl must have crayfish butter)
3. Beat 3 eggs, salt, and nutmeg into each bowl
4. Color the first bowl white, the second (with crayfish butter) red, and the third with previously described spinach juice green
5. Brush a tin mold with butter
6. Fill in the white mixture and spread it evenly
7. Fill in the crayfish mixture, then the green
8. Seal the mold and cook everything for one hour
9. Cut into slices and place on crayfish soup

title: Schokolade mit Milch (Chocolate with Milk)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 g chocolate per cup
- 1/4 liter milk per cup

## Directions

1. Break the chocolate and warm it with a few spoonfuls of water in an earthenware pot until soft
2. Then boil it with the milk
3. Alternatively, milk can be omitted entirely and water used instead (Water Chocolate)

title: Schotensuppe (Pod Soup)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 lbs) pods
- 30 g fat
- 40 g flour
- 8 g salt
- 1-1/4 l water

## Directions

1. Split the pods, break the pod shells, wash them and put them in a pot.
2. Cook the washed pod shells and the thick pod kernels with water and salt until soft and press the mixture through a sieve.
3. Meanwhile, make a flour roux. Slowly add the strained pod water to the roux with stirring.
4. Cook the fine pod kernels in the soup until soft, optionally cook flour dumplings in it as well and serve everything.

title: Schwartenmagen (Pork Stomach with Pork Rind)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds pork meat from the jaw
- 2 pounds pork from the neck
- 1 pound pork forcemeat
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Marjoram
- Ginger
- 1 glass pork blood
- Pork stomach or wide beef casing

## Directions

1. Simmer the jaw and neck pork until soft
2. Cut into long pieces and place in a bowl
3. Add 1 pound pork forcemeat
4. Season with salt, pepper, cloves, marjoram, and ginger as desired
5. Add a glass of pork blood
6. Mix everything together
7. Fill a pork stomach or wide beef casing with the mixture
8. Tie it securely closed
9. Scald it like any other sausage and prick it a little with a fork
10. When finished, place it between 2 wooden plates, put a stone on top, and hang it in smoke for 4 days

title: Schwarzbrodauflauf (Black Bread Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 8 loth black bread
- 1 glass red wine
- 1 grated nutmeg
- 1 knife point of cinnamon
- 1 knife point of mace
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 4 loth citron peel and orange peel, finely chopped
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter in a bowl until foamy and gradually stir in 6 egg yolks.
2. Grate 8 loth black bread on a grater and moisten it with 1 glass of red wine.
3. Add a grated nutmeg, a knife point of cinnamon, the same amount of mace, 1/4 pound sugar with a lemon zest grated into it, 4 loth citron peel and orange peel finely chopped.
4. Beat the egg whites to a stiff snow and add to the mixture.
5. Mix the mass well together and bake the soufflé in a porcelain bowl brushed with butter in the oven until nicely golden.

title: Schwarzbrodpudding (Black Bread Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 loth black bread, not sour
- 1 whole nutmeg
- 2 knife-points ground cinnamon
- 6 loth grated, ground almonds
- 1 knife-point ground cloves
- 1 glass Malaga wine
- 1/4 pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound ground sugar
- zest of half a lemon, grated
- snow from 6 egg whites
- 1 bottle wine
- 8 loth sugar
- 1 piece whole cinnamon
- 6 whole cloves

## Directions

1. Grate the black bread and nutmeg together
2. Mix in the cinnamon, ground almonds, and cloves
3. Moisten everything with a glass of Malaga wine
4. Lightly stir the butter in a bowl
5. Add all the above to the butter, then add the egg yolks, ground sugar, and grated lemon zest
6. Beat the egg whites to stiff peaks and fold in
7. Bake a thin pancake
8. Cut this into strips and lay them crosswise in a damp cloth
9. Fill the mass onto the strips and bind the cloth tightly
10. Leave in boiling water for one hour
11. For the mulled wine sauce: reduce one bottle of wine with 8 loth sugar to one schoppen in a pot
12. Arrange the pudding and pour the wine through a sieve containing one piece whole cinnamon and 6 cloves over the pudding

title: Schwarzbrodpudding (Black Bread Pudding)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 loth black bread (not sour)
- 1 whole nutmeg
- 2 knife tips of pounded cinnamon
- 6 loth grated and pounded almonds
- 1 knife tip of pounded cloves
- 1 glass Malaga wine
- 1/4 pound

## Directions

1. Grate 5 loth black bread that is not sour
2. Also grate 1 whole nutmeg
3. Add 2 knife tips of pounded cinnamon, 6 loth grated and pounded almonds and 1 knife tip of pounded cloves
4. Dampen everything with 1 glass of Malaga wine
5. Continue with 1/4 pound (recipe incomplete)

title: Schwarzbrodtorte (Black Bread Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely ground sugar
- 1/2 pound blanched almonds
- 1/2 pound unblanched almonds
- 12 loth stale black bread, grated
- 1 grated nutmeg
- zest of 1 lemon, minced
- 2 knife-points of ground cinnamon
- 2 knife-points of mace
- 2 knife-points of ground cloves
- 2 knife-points of cardamom
- 2 knife-points of cubeb
- 24 egg yolks
- snow of 10 egg whites
- 1/4 pound citron peel
- 1/4 pound orange peel
- butter and bread crumbs for the pan

## Directions

1. Finely pound the blanched almonds with egg white
2. Pound the unblanched almonds less finely
3. Stir together the sugar, both types of almonds, grated bread, nutmeg, lemon zest, cinnamon, mace, cloves, cardamom, and cubeb with the 24 egg yolks for half an hour with strong stirring
4. Mix in the snow of 10 egg whites along with the citron peel and orange peel
5. Brush a cake pan with butter and sprinkle with bread crumbs
6. Fill the mass into the pan
7. Bake the torte in a hot oven

title: Schwarze Würste (Black Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pork fat from the loin
- 8 large onions
- Half pound butter
- Half a maas of blood
- One schoppen sweet cream
- Basil
- Thyme
- Marjoram
- Pepper
- Salt
- Sausage casings
- Fresh butter

## Directions

1. Skin the pork fat and cut it into small cubes
2. Cut 8 large onions very finely and let them brown over steady heat with half a pound of butter
3. Pour half a maas of blood and one schoppen of sweet cream over them
4. Add the cut fat and chopped basil, thyme, and marjoram
5. Season with pepper and salt
6. Mix everything well together
7. Fill the casings with this mixture
8. Put the sausages in boiling salted water and let a broth cook over them
9. Allow them to cool again
10. Before serving, brush them with fresh butter and grill them on a grate

title: Schwarzwildbret (Wild Boar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- wild boar meat
- meat broth
- wine
- vinegar
- herbs and roots
- onions
- sugar
- fat
- flour
- red wine
- jus
- spices
- lemon
- tarragon vinegar or raspberry vinegar

## Directions
