1. Cook a piece of wild boar in meat broth, wine, and vinegar with various herbs and roots until not very tender.
2. Chop 3 to 4 large onions.
3. In a casserole, let a piece of sugar with fat become golden, then add the onions and let them brown with the sugar.
4. Add flour according to how much sauce is needed.
5. Deglaze with the broth in which the meat was cooked and let everything cook together for one hour.
6. Add a pint of red wine and some jus to it.
7. Add various spices, the zest of one grated lemon, and some tarragon or raspberry vinegar.
8. Let it cook for a long time.

title: Schwarzwurzeln (Salsify)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Flour
- Half a Vierling butter
- Flour
- Meat broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Scrape the roots clean and place them in a dish with cold water in which a handful of flour has been added.
2. After all roots are scraped, rub them clean by hand (they should only be cut to half-finger length), place them in a casserole, knead half a Vierling of butter with a handful of flour, place it on the salsify, pour a little meat broth, salt and nutmeg to taste over and cook until soft.

title: Schwarzwurzeln anderer Art (Salsify, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Salsify roots
- Salt water
- Potatoes
- Butter
- Sweet milk
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Half a schoppen sour cream

## Directions

1. Wash, clean and prepare the salsify, and cook it soft in salted water.
2. Peel potatoes, cut them into 4 parts, place a piece of butter in the casserole with the potatoes and a little sweet milk and braise them soft, adding salt and nutmeg to taste.
3. When everything is soft, add the salsify to the potatoes and pour half a schoppen of sour cream over.

title: Schwedisches Brod (Swedish Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound almonds with rose water
- 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1 Quint cinnamon
- 1 Quint cloves
- 1 Quint cardamom
- mace
- pepper
- 4 Loth fine flour
- 1 egg
- wax
- 1 egg white
- 4 Loth sugar
- lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pound 1/4 pound almonds with rose water not too finely and mix with 1/2 pound finely sifted sugar, 1 Quint cinnamon, 1 Quint cloves, 1 Quint cardamom, mace, a knife-tip of pepper, and 4 Loth fine flour
2. Beat in 1 egg to form a dough
3. Roll it out finger-thick
4. Cut pieces with a small drinking glass
5. Place them on a sheet brushed with wax
6. Bake in the oven
7. For the icing: Stir 4 Loth sugar and some drops of lemon juice into 1 egg white
8. Stir this together for a time
9. Brush the breads with it using a brush
10. Place in the oven to set

title: Schwedisches Brod (Swedish Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound almonds
- rose water
- half pound finely sifted sugar
- one quint cinnamon
- one quint cloves
- one quint cardamom
- mace
- a knife point of pepper
- 4 loth fine flour
- 1 egg
- egg white
- 4 loth sugar
- a few drops of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Pound the almonds with rose water not too finely and mix them with the half pound finely sifted sugar, one quint cinnamon, one quint cloves, one quint cardamom, mace, a knife point of pepper and 4 loth fine flour
2. Beat one egg into this mixture and make a dough from it
3. Roll it out finger thick and cut out pieces with a small drinking glass
4. Place them on a baking sheet brushed with wax and bake in the oven
5. For the icing: stir 4 loth sugar and a few drops of lemon juice into one egg white, stirring together for some time
6. Brush the breads with this icing using a brush and let them bake completely in the oven

title: Schweine-, Gänse- und Entenfett auslassen (Rendering Pork, Goose, and Duck Fat)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) back fat or lard
- 1 piece of apple
- 1 onion
- 1 sprig of marjoram

## Directions

1. After washing and scraping the back fat or lard, cut it into small cubes and place in a pot

title: Schweinebauch, gekocht (Boiled Pork Belly)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 300 g magerer Schweinebauch (lean pork belly)
- 8 g Salz (salt)
- 15 g Zwiebel (onion)
- 1 Gewürzdosis (1 spice dose)
- 1 Stiel Majoran (1 sprig marjoram)
- 3/4 l Wasser (water)

## Directions

1. Scrub the pork belly thoroughly in water like all pork and rub well with salt.
2. Place in cold water, bring to a boil and skim; after adding onion, spice dose, and dried marjoram, cook covered slowly for 2 hours.
3. Slice the meat and serve with vegetables; the broth is tasty with all root vegetable dishes, sauerkraut, and legumes; degrease before use.

title: Schweinebraten für 6 Personen (Roast Pork for 6 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg Schweinerücken (pork loin)
- 5 g Salz (salt)
- 1 Zwiebel (onion)
- 1 Gewürzdosis (1 spice dose)
- 4 Essl. saure Milch oder leichtes Bier (4 tablespoons sour milk or light beer)
- 10 g Kartoffel- oder Weizenmehl (potato or wheat flour)
- 1/2 l Wasser (water)

## Directions

1. Wash the pork loin quickly, dry, pound, and trim the fat to two straws' width; score diagonal lines first to the right, then to the left to form small squares; dust the meat with salt.
2. Bring the oven to an even, not too strong heat; peel the onion and place with the spice dose in the pan; stir the flour with 4 tablespoons cold water.
3. Place the prepared roast in the pan, pour 4 tablespoons boiling water over it (so the whole surface is covered) and place in the oven.
4. For a roast this small, allow 20 minutes per 1/2 kg; for a larger one, 18 minutes.
5. Baste the roast every 5-8 minutes with the fat that collects; meanwhile add a few spoonfuls of liquid and scrape the pan often to form a brown sauce.
6. The finished roast should be a nice brown color and crisp; remove from the pan, place on the serving dish, and keep warm.
7. Degrease the sauce, cook through with beer or milk, add the dissolved flour while stirring; the sauce must come to a proper boil, taste, and serve through a sieve with the roast.

title: Schweinefleisch, gekochtes, mit Weisskohl und Kartoffeln (Boiled Pork with White Cabbage and Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g Schweinekamm (pork neck)
- 8 g Salz (salt)
- 3/4 l Wasser (water)
- 3/4 kg Weisskohl (white cabbage)
- Wasser und 1 Prise Salz (water and 1 pinch salt)
- 250 g geschälte Kartoffeln (peeled potatoes)
- Wasser und 1 Prise Salz (water and 1 pinch salt)
- 1 Gewürzdosis (1 spice dose)
- 10 g Fett (fat)
- 10 g Mehl (flour)
- Salz und Pfeffer (salt and pepper)

## Directions

1. Wash and pound the meat; put 3/4 l water with 8 g salt on the stove to heat.
2. Remove the outer leaves and thick ribs from the cabbage; after dividing the head into 8 or 16 pieces, blanch in salted water and drain; cook the potatoes whole or cut almost until done.
3. Place the meat in the boiling salted water; after it begins to cook, continue as directed in the preparation.

title: Schweinefleisch, Gekochtes, mit Weisskohl und Kartoffeln (Boiled Pork with White Cabbage and Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 200 g pork shoulder
- 8 g salt
- 3/4 l water
- 3/4 kg white cabbage
- water and 1 pinch salt
- 250 g peeled potatoes
- water and 1 pinch salt
- 1 spice mixture portion
- 10 g fat
- 10 g flour
- salt and pepper

## Directions

1. Wash and pound the meat. Place 3/4 l water with 8 g salt on the stove to heat. Remove the outer leaves and thick leaf ribs from the cabbage after dividing the head into 8 or 16 pieces, then blanch in salted water and drain; boil the potatoes whole or cut into pieces until almost cooked.
2. Place the meat in the boiling salted water; after foam forms and simmers for a few minutes, skim it off, add the spice mixture, then the white cabbage and braise the dish approximately 1 hour covered. Now add the potatoes, cook the whole thing slowly for another 1/4 hour, make a roux with fat and flour, combine it with the dish, and season with pepper and salt.

title: Schweinekoteletten (Pork Cutlets)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g pork cutlets
- 1/2 teaspoon pepper and salt
- 2 tablespoons grated breadcrumbs
- 1 teaspoon flour
- 1/2 egg white
- 1 tablespoon lard
- 1 tablespoon soup vegetables
- 10 g flour
- 10 g onion slices
- 1/2 tablespoon rendered fat
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 3/8 l water
- 1/2 teaspoon mustard

## Directions

1. Trim the meat to a straw's width of fat, remove and chop the backbone (not the rib bone), cut into small pieces and brown; cut the separated fat into cubes and render it, prepare the onion, clean and wash the soup vegetables and cut into pieces, whisk the egg white with 1/2 teaspoon water, mix flour and breadcrumbs.
2. Heat 1/2 tablespoon of the rendered fat in a pot, add the bones, soup vegetables, onion and salt, brown the bones while stirring, add 3/8 l water, cover and let simmer slowly. Lightly wash the pork cutlets, pound them on both sides, sprinkle with pepper and salt, pull them through the beaten egg white on a fork and turn them in the mixture of breadcrumbs and flour. Melt the remaining rendered fat and 1 tablespoon lard in a sauté pan and heat until steaming. Place the breaded cutlets in it and fry them slowly on both sides while turning until a nice color, place them on a hot plate but uncovered so they do not become soft. From the fat in which the cutlets were fried and the flour make a roux, mix some mustard with it and slowly pour in the broth from the boiled bones while stirring, season the sauce with pepper and salt and serve the cutlets with the sauce.

title: Schweinekoteletten, Geschmor (Braised Pork Cutlets)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g pork shoulder
- 1 pinch salt and pepper
- 5 g flour
- 1/2 spice mixture portion
- 10 g onions
- 1 teaspoon mustard
- 4 g potato starch
- 1 tablespoon vinegar
- 3/16 l water

## Directions

1. Cut off as much of the outer fat as possible and preferably use the piece from the neck because it is more marbled and therefore stays juicier. Cut the pieces 2 cm thick, salt and pepper them and lightly coat them in flour. Dice the onions.
2. Place the cutlets side by side in a wide earthenware braising pot in which you have rendered the fat scraps, brown them on both sides after removing the cracklings, add spices, onions and 1/8 l water, cover the pot and let the cutlets braise slowly for 1 hour, adding a little water from time to time. Degrease the sauce, mix with mustard, potato starch and vinegar, season, strain through a sieve and pour over the cutlets.

title: Schweinenieren, Saure (Sour Pork Kidneys)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g pork kidneys
- 5 g salt
- 10 g flour
- 25 g margarine
- 1/4 l broth or meat extract broth
- 1/2 tablespoon vinegar

## Directions

1. Wash the kidneys thoroughly, cut them lengthwise into 1/2 cm thick pieces, remove the sinews and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
2. Make a roux with flour and margarine in a saucepan until golden, add the kidneys, brown them, add the broth and braise while stirring constantly, as they easily become tough, for approximately 2-3 minutes until they no longer look red. Then add the vinegar and serve quickly, preferably with fried potatoes.

title: Schweinepökelﬂeisch oder Eisbein (Pickled Pork or Pig's Knuckle)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg pickled pork or pig's knuckle
- 1/2 spice mixture portion
- 15 g onions
- 1 l water

## Directions

1. Pickled pork and pig's knuckle are cooked like pork belly.

title: Schweineschinken, Frischer, mit Brotkruste für 12-14 Personen (Fresh Pork Ham with Bread Crust for 12-14 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pork leg from a young pig, 4 kg
- 10 cloves
- 60 g grated bread
- 25 g sugar
- 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon
- 10 g salt
- 30 g bread rind
- 1 spice mixture portion
- 1 small onion
- 1 pinch salt
- 1-1/2 l water

## Directions

1. From a pounded pork leg, cut off the skin together with a large part of the fat and rub the leg with salt.
2. Place the leg in the pan, gradually pour 1/2 l boiling water over it, add bread rind, spice mixture and onion and let the leg roast gently for 3 hours under frequent basting, replenishing the sauce from time to time with water and carefully skimming off the fat. 3/4 hours before serving, thickly coat the leg with a mixture of bread, sugar, cinnamon, crushed cloves and a pinch of salt, press the mixture in everywhere with a knife, drizzle it with fat and let the crust brown and harden in the oven, drizzling it with fat from time to time. Then arrange the roast and cook the sauce by scraping the pan; strain the sauce through a sieve.

title: Schweins-Cotelettes (Pork Chops)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- ribs of a young pig
- salt
- pepper
- vinegar
- water

## Directions

1. Remove the sinew from the bottom of the ribs of a young pig, but do not strip the bone
2. Pound it a little, sprinkle it with salt and pepper
3. Lay it in a roasting pan, pour a glass of vinegar and a glass of water over it
4. Let it braise until tender
5. Serve it with sauerkraut or beans

title: Schweinsbratwürste (Pork Bratwurst)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- about 4 pounds of finely chopped pork from the shoulder or the so-called separated piece
- 3 quarter-pounds of bacon cut into cubes
- one schoppen of water
- salt
- pepper
- ginger

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into cubes and knead it together with the finely chopped pork in a bowl by hand along with one schoppen of water
2. Cover and let it stand overnight
3. The next day, add salt, pepper, and ginger to taste
4. Work in another half schoppen of water
5. Fill the mixture into casings of your choice
6. Fry the sausages fresh or hang them to smoke

title: Schweinskopf für große Tafeln (Roasted Pig's Head for Large Tables)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 pounds butter
- 5 pounds pork lard
- chocolate
- saffron
- spinach cheese

## Directions

1. Stir 5 pounds butter and 5 pounds pork lard together for half an hour until they become like pomade
2. On a large long platter, build a kind of altar from the majority of this mass
3. Decorate it with columns formed from the rest of the mass rolled round, which can be colored brown with chocolate, yellow with saffron, or green with spinach cheese
4. Between two such columns you can also place a figure of a hunter, a pilgrim, or some other form shaped from the same mass
5. Place the pig's head on this altar

title: Schweizer Butterlaibchen (Swiss Butter Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- one quarter pound sugar
- one quarter pound almonds
- one quarter pound butter
- 1.5 quarter pounds flour
- lemon peel
- ground cinnamon
- 3 eggs
- half pound flour
- 8 to 10 blanched almonds
- 3 loth candied lemon peel

## Directions

1. Stir together 2 eggs, one quarter pound sugar, one quarter pound almonds and one quarter pound butter
2. Add 1.5 quarter pounds flour, and if desired, finely cut lemon peel and ground cinnamon
3. Sprinkle a baking sheet with flour and place cakes from the dough, somewhat larger than a pepper nut, onto it using a spoon
4. Bake in the oven
5. They can be brushed with egg, sprinkled with sugar and ground almonds, or alternatively the almonds can be left out of the dough and instead 3 eggs, half pound flour, 8 to 10 blanched almonds and 3 loth cut candied lemon peel can be added to the butter and sugar; otherwise they are made like the previous cakes

title: Schweizer Speise (Swiss Dish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Peeled rind of 3 Wecken or milk breads
- Hot lard
- 1 schoppen wine
- 6 loth finely crushed sugar
- 1 orange
- 4 to 5 egg yolks
- Egg whites
- Puff pastry
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 2 tablespoons water
- 4 egg whites
- 1 orange

## Directions

1. Bake the peeled rind of 3 Wecken or milk breads in hot lard and cook it with 1 schoppen wine in a casserolle like a porridge
2. In a bowl, beat it with 6 loth finely crushed sugar, 1 orange rind grated on a grater, 4 to 5 egg yolks and the snow from the eggs together
3. Bake this mass in a baking sheet into which puff pastry has been placed beforehand
4. Let 1/4 pound sugar cook with 2 tablespoons water until it spins threads
5. Beat a stiff snow from 4 egg whites, add the juice of 1 orange, stir together
6. Turn the baked cake onto a plate and pour the ice over it
7. This dish can also be cooked as a pudding in steam and an orange sauce added to it

title: Schweizer Torte (Swiss Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 Loth lard
- 3 quarter-pound peeled almonds, finely ground
- zest of 2 lemons, grated on a grater
- half a pound finely ground sugar
- quarter-pound fine flour
- butter
- eggs

## Directions

1. Stir the lard in a bowl until it is completely white
2. Grind the peeled almonds finely
3. Add the grated lemon zest and half a pound of finely ground sugar
4. Mix with a quarter-pound of fine flour for half an hour, stirring well
5. Butter a springform pan
6. Put two-thirds of the mixture into it and coat with eggs
7. Make a grid pattern from the remaining third of the mixture and place it on top
8. Coat the grid with eggs as well
9. Bake in the oven until beautifully golden
10. When the cake comes out of the oven, place it on the plate on which it will be served to the table
11. Fill the grid openings with some preserves

title: Schweizer Waffeln (Swiss Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 measure thick cream
- 4 egg yolks
- 3 handfuls fine flour
- 6 loth melted butter
- 1 knife-point of salt
- 4 egg whites
- sweet cream (optional)

## Directions

1. Beat 1/2 measure of thick cream in a bowl with a small white whisk to a thick snow.
2. Whisk 4 egg yolks into it.
3. Stir in 3 handfuls fine flour, 6 loth melted butter, and 1 knife-point of salt gradually.
4. Beat the whites of 4 eggs to a stiff snow and mix with the dough.
5. If the dough is still too heavy, it can be thinned with sweet cream.
6. Bake the waffles in hot iron and sprinkle sugar on both sides.

title: Schüsselpastete (Casserole Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Young chickens
- Pigeons
- Birds
- Sweetbreads
- Veal
- Bratwurst
- Meat dumplings
- Butter
- 1 shallot onion
- Parsley
- Capers
- Morels
- Mushrooms
- Truffles
- Pastry dough
- Flour, 3 fingers' worth
- Meat broth, 1/2 ladle full
- Lemon, half

## Directions

1. Cut the chickens and pigeons into pieces
2. Braise them in butter
3. Add 1 shallot onion and parsley, finely cut
4. Let them braise together
5. Place them in a serving dish
6. Lay the braised meat in it
7. Add capers, morels, mushrooms, and truffles
8. Cover with pastry dough
9. Place in an oven and bake the pastry
10. For the sauce: sprinkle 3 fingers' worth of flour into the casserole where the chickens were steamed
11. Stir it around
12. Pour 1/2 ladle full of meat broth to it
13. Let it boil up
14. Strain the broth through a fine sieve
15. Pour it over the pastry
16. Squeeze the juice of half a lemon over it

title: Seeforellen (Lake Trout)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- lake trout
- Burgundy wine
- lesser wine
- vinegar
- lemon
- cloves
- pepper
- bay leaves
- herbs
- flour
- water
- egg yolks
- butter
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Scale the fish thoroughly and remove the gills and innards
2. Rub strongly with salt
3. Place in a fish kettle
4. Pour 1 bottle of Burgundy, 1 bottle of lesser wine, and vinegar over it
5. Add lemon, cloves, pepper, bay leaves, and all kinds of good herbs
6. Cover the kettle tightly and let the fish simmer for 1 hour
7. Place on a long platter
8. For the Dutch sauce: mix 1 cooking spoon of flour smoothly with cold water
9. Beat in 3 egg yolks, butter the size of a chicken egg, and some of the fish stock
10. Cook this sauce well with meat broth and pour over the fish or serve separately

title: Selleriesauce (Celery Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 clean, trimmed celery roots
- a piece of butter
- a small tablespoonful of flour
- a little cold water
- one ladle of meat broth
- vinegar
- pepper
- salt

## Directions

1. Cut the celery roots as finely as noodles
2. Braise them gently in a casserole with a piece of butter, covered
3. Sprinkle a small tablespoonful of flour over them and shake
4. Pour a little cold water over them
5. Add one ladle of meat broth and some vinegar
6. Season with pepper and salt to taste

title: Selleriesuppe (Celery Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of butter
- 3 to 4 clean washed celery roots
- Two finely chopped shallot onions
- Two chopped leek stalks
- As much flour as can be held between three fingers
- Meat broth or jus
- Toasted bread crumbs
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole
2. Cut the celery as finely as noodles and place with the shallot onions and leek stalks in the casserole
3. Cover and let the celery braise until soft
4. Sprinkle with as much flour as can be held between three fingers
5. Pour in meat broth or jus as needed for the soup
6. Let cook another quarter hour
7. Arrange over toasted bread crumbs and grate nutmeg on top

title: Semmelklösse (Breadcrumb Dumplings)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 5 Old bread rolls
- 1/8 l Whole milk
- 1/8 l Water
- 1 pinch Ground cinnamon
- 15 g Salt
- 40 g Sugar
- 15 g Fat or margarine
- 1 Egg
- 5 g Fat or margarine

## Directions

1. Grease a shallow dish with a little fat, bring water to a boil for cooking the dumplings, grate the bread, and melt 15 g fat.
2. Whisk together the milk, water, cinnamon, salt, sugar, melted fat, and egg, stir in the breadcrumbs, pour the mixture into the greased dish, place it in boiling water, cover it, and cook for 1-1/2 hours.
3. Remove the dumplings with a spoon, plate them on a hot dish, and serve with stewed fruit or sauce.
4. You can refine these dumplings with 6 grated almonds.

title: Senf auf französische Art (French-Style Mustard)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 beautiful peeled quinces
- 1 half pound quince pulp
- 1 half pound yellow mustard flour
- Cooked must
- 1 quint coarsely ground cloves
- Fennel
- Anise
- Coriander

## Directions

1. Cook 3 to 4 beautiful peeled quinces very soft in water
2. Scrape off the pulp
3. Take 1 half pound quince pulp and 1 half pound yellow mustard flour
4. Cook the mustard flour in cooked must
5. Let the quince pulp also cook along for 6 minutes
6. Stir in 1 quint coarsely ground cloves, fennel, anise, and coriander thoroughly
7. Put it in a stone pot and preserve it

title: Senf mit Essig (Mustard with Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 2 cloves of garlic
- 1 half maas wine vinegar
- 4 bay leaves
- 6 cloves
- 6 coarsely ground peppercorns
- 4 loth brown mustard flour
- 4 loth yellow mustard flour

## Directions

1. Cut 2 onions and 2 garlic cloves very small
2. Pour 1 half maas wine vinegar in a pan
3. Add 4 bay leaves, 6 cloves, 6 coarsely ground peppercorns
4. Let it cook for half a quarter hour
5. Take 4 loth brown and as much yellow mustard flour in a bowl
6. Remove the vinegar from the fire
7. Pull it through a hair sieve into the flour
8. Stir the mustard for half an hour as this makes it finer
9. Fill it in a vessel as desired
10. Bind it up and serve it with beef

title: Senf mit Essig (Mustard with Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 onions
- 2 garlic cloves
- half a Maas of wine vinegar
- 4 bay leaves
- 6 cloves
- 6 coarsely crushed peppercorns
- 4 Loth brown mustard flour
- 4 Loth yellow mustard flour

## Directions

1. Chop the onions and garlic cloves very finely
2. Pour half a Maas of wine vinegar into a pan, add 4 bay leaves, 6 cloves, and 6 coarsely crushed peppercorns
3. Let it cook for half a quarter hour
4. Take 4 Loth brown mustard flour and the same amount of yellow mustard flour in a bowl
5. Remove the vinegar from the fire and strain it through a hair sieve onto the flour
6. Stir the mustard for half an hour, as this makes it finer
7. Fill it into a container as desired, bind it and serve with beef

title: Senf mit süßem Most (Mustard with Sweet Must)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Sweet must
- Yellow mustard flour (8 loth)
- Brown mustard flour (8 loth)

## Directions

1. In autumn take completely sweet must
2. Cook it down well (from 3 maas must, 1 maas can be cooked down)
3. Pour it into a pot
4. Let it stand overnight
5. In the morning put it in a bottle
6. Cork it well and set it aside for use
7. To make mustard, take some of it in a brass pan
8. Let it cook
9. Take 8 loth yellow mustard flour in a bowl, 8 loth brown mustard flour
10. Scald this with the boiling must
11. Stir it thoroughly
12. Heat a small arch piece of steel until glowing
13. Dip it in the mustard
14. Stir this until it is completely cold
15. Preserve it in a stone pot, bind it up and serve it with beef

title: Senfgurken (Mustard Pickles)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3-1/2 kg (7 Pfd.) cucumbers, weighed after salting
- salt as needed
- beer vinegar as needed
- 1 l wine vinegar
- 10 g salt
- 10 g sugar
- 60 g shallots, peeled and scalded once with warm vinegar
- 30 g pearl onions, peeled and scalded once with warm vinegar
- 3 tablespoons horseradish chunks
- 4 dill flowers
- 1 chili pepper
- 3 muslin bags with various spices
- 4 tablespoons mustard seeds

## Directions

1. Wash the cucumbers thoroughly, peel starting from the tip, cut them through, and remove the seeds with a tablespoon, then cut into uniform pieces of your choice.
2. Salt them with 50 g salt per 1 kg (2 Pfd.) of cucumber pieces by mixing well and let stand until the next day.
3. After they have dried, pour cooled, lukewarm beer vinegar over them; boil the same vinegar again the next day and pour it cooled over the cucumbers.
4. On the next day, bring wine vinegar with 10 g salt and 10 g sugar to a boil per 1 l, remove the cucumbers from the beer vinegar, layer them with all the carefully cleaned various ingredients in glasses or pots, and fill the cooled wine vinegar over them, place cross-sticks on top, and tie the glasses with parchment paper.
5. You can greatly accelerate softening by pouring the vinegar hot over them once.
6. After 8 days, pour off the vinegar again, bring it to a boil, and pour it back over the cucumbers after cooling.

title: Snails in Anchovy Sauce (Schnecken in Sardellensauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- snails
- 4 pieces anchovies
- 1 onion
- zest of 1 lemon
- butter
- flour
- meat broth
- 1/2 glass wine
- juice of 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Prepare the snails as described in the 'filled snails' recipe
2. Chop finely 4 anchovies, 1 onion and the zest of 1 lemon
3. Let a piece of butter with a little flour turn yellow in a casserole
4. Add the chopped mixture and turn it a couple of times in the butter
5. Pour on one spoon of meat broth, 1/2 glass wine and the juice of 1 lemon
6. Add the snails to the sauce and let everything boil together once more

title: Sodaseife zu machen (Making Soda Soap)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound pure soap
- half a meschen ox bile (which refreshes the color)
- 2 loth honey
- 3 loth sugar
- half a loth turpentine

## Directions

1. Take 1 pound pure soap, half a meschen ox bile which refreshes the color, 2 loth honey, 3 loth sugar and half a loth turpentine
2. Mix everything together and let it melt very gently in an earthenware crucible
3. When everything is mixed, pour the mass back into a crucible onto a linen cloth which has previously been dipped in hot water and then in cold water
4. On the following day you will have the most beautiful soap, which is excellent for silk washing, for ribbons and for flowers made of cotton

title: Sommerpastete (Summer Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender butter dough
- Broccoli peas, 1 pint, half-grown
- Parsley, 1 handful
- Shallot onions
- 2 to 3 cleaned chickens
- Butter
- Jus
- Flour, 3 fingers' worth
- Crushed sugar, 1 handful
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Mace
- Sour cream, 1/2 pint

## Directions

1. Roll out tender butter dough into 2 knife-back-thick cakes
2. Place the first cake in a deep cake tin
3. Lay paper (which must be made hollow and round so it can be removed after baking) on top
4. Lay an equally large cake (actually the lid) on the paper
5. Brush both cakes with water
6. Encase them with a 2-finger-wide ring of butter dough
7. Cut them smooth
8. Brush with egg
9. Poke the pastry lid with a warm knife so it gets openings while baking
10. Bake it in the oven until golden
11. For the ragout: clean 1 pint of half-grown broccoli peas
12. Steam them for a quarter hour with a handful of parsley and shallot onions
13. Lard the breast and drumsticks of 2 to 3 cleaned chickens
14. Mince the neck and everything else together
15. Steam this with the chickens in a casserole with a small piece of butter
16. Baste the chickens often with jus during steaming
17. When the chickens are tender, remove them
18. Strain the sauce through a fine sieve
19. Pour another 1/2 ladle full of meat broth to it
20. Return the chickens to the sauce
21. Let them cook a while longer so they get a nice glaze
22. Sprinkle 3 fingers' worth of flour, 1 handful of crushed sugar, salt, nutmeg, mace, and 1/2 pint of sour cream over the broccoli peas
23. Fill them into a plate
24. Lay the chickens on top
25. Cover the pastry with the baked lid
26. Serve it at table

title: Spanferkel (Roast Suckling Pig)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- salt
- pepper
- good rice
- bouillon
- ham
- Parmesan cheese
- bratwurst
- fat
- Provençal oil

## Directions

1. Bone out the suckling pig and rub it with salt and pepper.
2. Cook good rice in bouillon with a piece of ham; the rice must be quite hard and dry.
3. Remove from heat and when cold, rub a piece of Parmesan cheese into it.
4. Fry some bratwurst, cut them into small pieces and remove the skin.
5. Remove the ham from the rice, cut it into small pieces, and stir the rice well.
6. Fill the suckling pig with the rice mixture.
7. Place the pig in a casserole with a piece of fat and put it in the oven for one hour before serving.
8. Brush it with Provençal oil so it gets a nice color.
9. Place the roasted pig on a plate and pour a little jus over it.

title: Spanferkel (Suckling Pig)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig, 4 to 5 weeks old
- salt
- white pepper
- brown beer, half a maas
- bacon
- onion

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape the suckling pig thoroughly and remove the entrails with the spleen.
2. Make 3 to 4 cuts on the forehead and thread the spleen through them.
3. Rub it with salt and white pepper.
4. Roast it on a spit if possible.
5. Heat half a maas of brown beer with a piece of bacon in a casserole and pour this broth over the pig.
6. Be careful that it does not blister; to prevent this, make holes in it with a large carving fork.
7. Dress the feet and place a smaller dish under the roast to catch drippings.
8. Cut an onion into the platter.
9. When the pig is done, make a cut in its neck so it roasts nicely.
10. Serve the jus separately.
11. With good heat, a suckling pig will be done in one hour.

title: Spanferkel auf italienische Art (Suckling Pig Italian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- suckling pig
- salt
- pepper
- good rice
- bouillon
- ham
- Parmesan cheese
- bratwurst sausages
- Provençe oil

## Directions

1. Bone out the suckling pig and rub it with salt and pepper.
2. Cook good rice in bouillon with a piece of ham; the rice must be quite hard and dry.
3. Remove it from the heat and when it is cold, grate some Parmesan cheese into it.
4. Fry some bratwurst sausages, cut them into small pieces, and remove their casings.
5. Remove the ham from the rice, cut it small, and stir the rice well.
6. Add the sausage pieces along with the fat they were fried in.
7. Fill the suckling pig with this mixture.
8. Place it in a casserole with a piece of fat and put it in the oven for one hour before serving.
9. Brush it with Provençe oil so it gets a beautiful color.
10. Place the roasted pig on a platter.

title: Spanische Torte (Spanish Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- preserved currants
- apple jam
- preserved raspberries
- apricot jam
- wafer
- egg
- 4 Loth sugar
- 2 small tablets chocolate
- 1 egg white
- quarter-pound finely ground sugar
- juice of half a lemon
- fruits for garnish

## Directions

1. Bake 5 to 6 plate-sized cakes from good butter dough
2. Place one on the plate to be served at table
3. Coat it not very thickly with preserved currants
4. Place the second cake on top and coat with apple jam
5. Coat the third with preserved raspberries
6. Coat the fourth with apricot jam
7. Place the fifth on top
8. Coat a piece of wafer with egg and arrange it beautifully around the cake
9. Prepare chocolate icing by cooking 4 Loth sugar in a brass pan with 2 small tablets of chocolate
10. Coat the cake with this chocolate icing in a ring around the top
11. Prepare lemon icing by whisking together 1 egg white, quarter-pound finely ground sugar, and the juice of half a lemon until white
12. Pour this lemon icing into the inner edge after the chocolate icing using a piping device
13. Dry the cake in the oven and garnish with fruits

title: Spanisches Brod (Spanish Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 egg
- 3 egg yolks
- one quarter pound finely sifted sugar
- a piece of pounded vanilla
- one quarter pound fine flour
- a little sugar

## Directions

1. Stir together the egg, egg yolks, finely sifted sugar and pounded vanilla for a strong quarter hour
2. Mix in one quarter pound fine flour
3. Place stiff paper on a black baking sheet and sprinkle it with a little sugar
4. Using a coffee spoon, place small heaps of the mixture onto it
5. Let them bake in a cooled oven

title: Spargel (Asparagus)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 pound) asparagus spears
- 5 g salt
- 1 l water
- 3 g sugar
- 50 g butter
- 1 tablespoon grated bread crumbs

## Directions

1. Wash the asparagus and peel it with a fine knife, starting from the tip and peeling more strongly toward the bottom, keeping it nicely round.
2. Bundle asparagus of equal length and thickness, about 6-8 together.
3. Put thinner asparagus in the cooking water later than thicker ones.
4. Cook the asparagus in boiling salted water with sugar for 20-30 minutes until soft.
5. Longer cooking makes it yellow and unpalatable.
6. Remove it carefully with 2 ladles.
7. Remove the string and arrange the asparagus with the tips overlapping.
8. Serve with melted or brown butter in which some bread crumbs have been fried, alongside or on top.
9. For broken asparagus, divide the spears into equal pieces, cook as for whole asparagus spears, drain, and mix with parsley sauce prepared without onion using asparagus water.

title: Spargeln (Asparagus)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Flour
- Cold water
- 3 egg yolks
- Juice of half a lemon
- 3 tablespoons thick sour cream
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean and scrape the asparagus, cut off the white part, and cook soft in boiling salted water in a brass pan, then arrange on a plate.
2. Make the following sauce: stir 2 cooking spoons of flour smooth with cold water in a brass pan, beat in 3 egg yolks, the juice of half a lemon, three spoons of thick sour cream, salt, nutmeg, some meat broth, and bring to a boil on the coals while stirring constantly so it does not curdle.

title: Spargelsuppe (Asparagus Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Asparagus
- Salt
- Butter
- Two chopped shallot onions
- One tablespoon of flour
- Clear meat broth
- A knife's point of ground mace
- Two egg yolks
- Two tablespoons of sour cream

## Directions

1. Cut off the white part of the asparagus and scrape slightly, bind again in bundles and simmer soft in boiling salt water
2. Remove them, cut off the tips and put in the soup tureen, place the rest in a mortar and pound
3. In a casserole, braise the pounded asparagus with butter, add two chopped shallot onions
4. After braising for a quarter hour, stir in one tablespoon of flour
5. Pour in clear meat broth and let cook together for a quarter hour
6. Press through a fine sieve, return to the casserole and bring to a boil once more
7. Add a knife's point of ground mace
8. In a small dish stir two egg yolks with two tablespoons of sour cream, pour into the soup tureen with the asparagus and arrange the soup

title: Speck in Semmelbrösel (Bacon in Breadcrumbs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 egg white
- 1/2 teaspoon water
- 1 scant tablespoon flour
- 2 tablespoons grated breadcrumbs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 20 g lard or margarine
- bacon

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into 1/2 cm thick slices so that the strips run lengthwise, and soak in 3/8 l water.
2. Beat egg white with 1/2 teaspoon water using a fork on a saucer.
3. Mix grated breadcrumbs with salt and flour; place the fat in a pan and set on the stove.
4. Dry the prepared bacon, roll it in the beaten egg white and then coat evenly in the breadcrumb mixture, fry in steaming fat from the pan, turning frequently, to a golden-brown color and arrange on a plate.

title: Speckknopf (Bacon Dumpling)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound bacon
- 2 kreuzer worth white bread
- 4 eggs
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- 2 small cooking spoons flour
- Hot meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut 1/2 pound bacon into small cubes and place half in a bowl
2. Let the other half melt in a casserole and roast 2 kreuzer worth white bread cut into cubes in it
3. Cut another 2 kreuzer worth white bread into cubes
4. Dampen them with hot meat broth
5. Mix together the roasted bread, 4 eggs, salt, nutmeg and 2 small cooking spoons flour
6. Bind everything in a wet cloth
7. Cook the dumpling for one hour
8. Serve it with braised veal

title: Speckkuchen (Bacon Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound flour
- good beer yeast
- milk
- quarter pound butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 2 knife points salt
- green bacon
- salt
- caraway

## Directions

1. Put one pound flour in a bowl
2. Make a small dough from good beer yeast with milk and let it rise at gentle warmth
3. Stir a quarter pound butter lightly
4. Add 4 egg yolks and 2 knife points salt
5. Add the small dough to the butter with flour
6. Work the dough until it throws bubbles
7. Put it back in the bowl and let it rise
8. When ripe, take it to the pastry board
9. Roll it out 2 fingers thick
10. Fold it in 2 fingers wide at the edge
11. Brush it with beaten egg
12. Cut green bacon into cubes
13. Mix it with salt and caraway
14. Sprinkle it over the cake
15. Coat a sheet of paper with butter
16. Lay the cake on it
17. Put it on a black baking sheet
18. Bake it nicely yellow in the oven

title: Specksauce, Braune (Brown Bacon Sauce)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 20 g lean bacon
- 15 g onions
- 20 g flour
- 1/4 l water
- 2 tablespoons vinegar
- 1 crushed spice portion
- salt to taste
- 15 g sugar or 1 1/2 tablespoons syrup
- 1-2 drops food coloring

## Directions

1. Cut the bacon into small cubes, chop the onions finely.
2. In an earthenware pot, fry the bacon with the onion until light yellow, sweat it through, then cook the flour in it and cook down with the water. Season the sauce with vinegar, spice, salt, sugar or syrup and serve it.
3. Remark: This sauce is suitable as an accompaniment to potatoes in their skins and also to stewed cucumbers. Mixed with 2 tablespoons diced pickles: cucumber sauce.

title: Speckscheiben Einzuwälzen und zu Braten (Bacon Slices Breaded and Fried)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 100 g lean bacon
- 3/8 l water
- 1/2 egg white, 1/2 teaspoon water
- 1 scant tablespoon flour
- 2 tablespoons grated breadcrumbs
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 20 g lard or margarine

## Directions

1. Cut bacon into 1/2 cm thick slices so that the stripes run lengthwise and soak in 3/8 l water. Beat the egg white with 1/2 teaspoon water with a fork on a saucer. Mix the grated breadcrumbs with salt and flour; put the fat in a pan and place on the stove.
2. Dry the prepared bacon, roll it in the beaten egg white and then coat it evenly in the breadcrumb mixture, fry it in steaming fat from the pan while turning until golden brown in color and serve.

title: Speckwürste (Bacon Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Half pound fresh lard
- 3 kreuzer rolls of bread
- Water
- Onions
- Parsley
- A piece of fat
- 3 to 4 eggs
- Salt
- Pepper
- Mace
- Bratwurst casings
- Hot water

## Directions

1. Cut half a pound of fresh lard into small cubes
2. Soak 3 kreuzer rolls of bread in water, press them out firmly
3. Chop onions and parsley and braise them in a piece of fat
4. Remove from heat
5. Take the lard, 3 to 4 eggs, salt, pepper, and mace
6. Mix everything well together
7. Press it into bratwurst casings not too firmly
8. Place the sausages in hot water and fry them until golden brown

title: Spiegelküchlein (Mirror Biscuits)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- oblaten (wafer paper)
- jam or preserves
- 1/2 schoppen milk
- 1/2 loth butter
- 4 cooking spoonfuls flour
- 3 eggs
- 1 pinch salt
- lard for frying
- sugar and cinnamon

## Directions

1. Cut oblaten into pieces 2 fingers wide and 2 fingers long
2. Spread some jam on one piece and cover with another piece to form a sandwich
3. Heat 1/2 schoppen milk and 1/2 loth butter until boiling
4. Mix 4 cooking spoonfuls flour with the milk until smooth
5. Add 3 eggs and 1 pinch salt
6. Dip the wafer sandwiches into the batter
7. Fry in hot lard
8. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 pounds) spinach
- 8 g salt
- 2 l boiling water
- 1 tablespoon flour
- 15 g fat or margarine
- 5 g grated onions
- 1/8 l broth
- 1 pinch pepper and salt

## Directions

1. Cut off the bottom of the leaf stems; sort the spinach, wash it often, blanch it several times in boiling salted water, skim it, drain it, cool it with fresh water, drain it well, press it gently on a colander, and pass it through a fine sieve or weigh it.
2. Sauté the onions in fat, then cook the flour with it, but do not brown it.
3. Stir the spinach puree into it and add the broth.

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kg (2 Pfd.) Spinat
- 8 g Salz
- 2 l kochendes Wasser
- 1 Essl. Mehl
- 15 g Fett oder Margarine
- 5 g geriebene Zwiebeln
- 1/8 l Bruehe
- 1 Prise Pfeffer und Salz

## Directions

1. Die Blattstiele werden unten abgeschnitten; der Spinat wird verlesen, oft gewaschen, in wallendem Salzwasser einige Male ueberkocht, geschaeumt, abgegossen, mit frischem Wasser gekuehlt, gut abgetropft, auf einem Durchschlag leise gedrueckt und gewiegt oder durch ein Haarsieb gerieben.
2. Man schwitzt die Zwiebeln in Fett, dann das Mehl damit gar, aber nicht braun. Das Spinatmus wird damit verruehrt, die Bruehe dazugeruehrt, mit Pfeffer und Salz abgeschmeckt und nicht zu lange gekocht, weil der Spinat sonst die Farbe verliert; man haelt ihn lieber heiss, indem man den Topf damit in kochendes Wasser stellt.

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt water
- 1 onion
- 2 spoonfuls of flour
- Butter
- 1 spoonful of meat broth
- Nutmeg (optional)

## Directions

1. Clean and wash the spinach
2. Cook it in salt water until soft
3. Soak it in cold water for one hour
4. Press it firmly
5. Mince it finely with one onion
6. Roast 2 spoonfuls of flour in a piece of butter until light yellow
7. Steam the spinach in this mixture
8. Pour 1 spoonful of meat broth over it and cook
9. Grate nutmeg over it if desired

title: Spinat (Spinach)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Salt water
- Cold water
- Onion
- Flour
- Butter
- Meat broth
- Optional: Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Clean and sort the spinach, wash it, cook it soft with salted water, place it in cold water for an hour, press it out firmly.
2. Mince finely with an onion, toast 2 spoonfuls of flour in a piece of butter until light yellow, braise the spinach in it, pour a spoonful of meat broth over and cook. Nutmeg can also be grated over it.

title: Spinatknopf (Spinach Dumpling)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls spinach
- Salt water
- Cold water
- 1/2 pound veal or pork roast
- 1/2 lemon
- 2 rolls
- 1 finely minced steamed onion
- 6 loth butter
- 6 egg yolks
- Salt
- Nutmeg
- Snow from 6 egg whites
- 1 cooking spoon flour
- Cold water
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 ladle spoonful meat broth
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream

## Directions

1. Blanch 2 handfuls spinach in salt water
2. Pour it into a strainer and run cold water over it so it remains green
3. Press it firmly and mince it finely
4. Mince 1/2 pound veal or pork roast with 1/2 lemon very finely
5. Rub off 2 rolls, soak and press them
6. Stir 6 loth butter until foamy in a bowl
7. Add the spinach, roast, rolls, steamed onion, 6 egg yolks, salt, nutmeg and the snow from 6 egg whites
8. Bind the mass in a wet cloth and cook for one hour in boiling salt water
9. For the fricassee: stir 1 cooking spoon flour smooth with cold water in a brass pan
10. Beat 2 egg yolks into it
11. Cook 1 ladle spoonful meat broth and 2 spoonfuls sour cream with it
12. Arrange this over the pudding

title: Spinatkuchen (Spinach Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 handfuls spinach
- 2 mouth-sized pieces bread
- boiling milk
- 4 egg yolks
- salt, 1 knife point
- 2 loth sugar
- 1/2 nutmeg, grated
- 4 egg whites

## Directions

1. Chop 2 handfuls spinach very finely
2. Scald 2 pieces of bread with boiling milk, then drain the milk
3. Add the spinach to the bread
4. Beat 4 egg yolks, 1 knife point salt, 2 loth sugar and 1/2 grated nutmeg with the spinach mixture until foamy
5. Fold in the stiffly beaten egg whites
6. Butter a baking sheet, fill with the mixture and bake the cake in the oven

title: Spinatkuchen anderer Art (Spinach Cake, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- spinach
- water
- salt
- onions
- parsley
- 2 mouth-sized pieces bread
- 4 eggs
- 1/2 vierling butter
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash the spinach carefully, place in boiling water on the fire, add salt and cook through
2. Let drain through a sieve, pour cold water over, press firmly dry
3. Chop as finely as possible with onions and parsley
4. Rub 2 pieces of bread on a grater, soak in water, press dry and mix with the spinach
5. Beat in 4 eggs one by one
6. Melt 1/2 vierling butter and pour under the mixture
7. Add salt and nutmeg, stir for a quarter hour
8. Butter a baking sheet (pottage sheet), fill with the mixture and bake on coals or in the oven

title: Spinatschnitten (Spinach Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3 to 4 handfuls of spinach
- salt
- 2 rolls
- one cleaned calf's brain
- butter
- 4 loth of melted butter
- 5 eggs

## Directions

1. Blanch 3 to 4 handfuls of spinach in salted water
2. Pour into a sieve and pour cold water over it
3. Press it firmly dry
4. Grate 2 rolls, soak them, press them out and add to the spinach
5. Braise one cleaned calf's brain in butter
6. Beat 4 loth of melted butter and 5 eggs with everything for half an hour
7. Cook this mixture in a tin mold brushed with butter in a steam pot
8. When cooled, cut into slices and add to soup

title: Squabs in Salmis Sauce (Tauben in Salmisauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- squabs
- bacon
- onions
- juniper berries
- 1 bay leaf
- lemon
- basil
- piece of pork fat
- salt
- flour
- 1/2 schoppen red wine
- jus or meat broth
- mace
- lemon, chopped
- pepper

## Directions

1. Pluck the still-warm squabs, singe them at the fire and rub with a clean cloth, then gut them but do not wash
2. Cut the wings at the second joint and the feet
3. Wrap the squabs in bacon with the feet and wings, onions, juniper berries, a bay leaf, lemon and basil and a piece of pork fat in a casserole
4. Salt, cover and steam for one hour
5. When the squabs are nicely yellow, place them in a dish to keep warm
6. Take everything else from the casserole, pound it as finely as possible in a mortar and put it back in the casserole
7. Sprinkle one spoon of flour over it, pour on 1/2 schoppen red wine and one ladle of jus or meat broth
8. Cook for a quarter hour, strain through a sieve and pour back into the casserole
9. Put the squabs back in as well, add a knife point of mace, chopped lemon, salt and pepper to taste

title: Stachelbeerkompott, Rhabarberkompott (Gooseberry Compote, Rhubarb Compote)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 250 g unripe gooseberries or rhubarb
- 1 pinch of soda
- 90 g clarified sugar
- 4 tablespoons water
- 1 piece of lemon peel
- 1 g cinnamon
- 2 g cornstarch
- 2 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. The gooseberries are freed from blossom and stem, washed and drained. Rhubarb is peeled if necessary, otherwise cut into pieces about 3 cm long and washed. Both are blanched in boiling water, the berries receive an addition of soda.
2. Gooseberries or rhubarb are poured into the clarified sugar and must steep in it with lemon peel and cinnamon pieces in a covered earthenware pot for 1-1/2 hours. Then they are thickened with cornstarch; the peel and cinnamon are removed when arranging for serving.

title: Stachelbeerkuchen (Gooseberry Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- plafond with short dough
- gooseberries
- quarter pound finely ground sugar
- 2 knife points cinnamon
- 6 egg whites
- half a lemon juice

## Directions

1. Line a plafond with short dough
2. Cut the stems, cores and fuzz from the gooseberries
3. Cover the sheet with them
4. Stir a beautiful white icing from quarter pound finely ground sugar, 2 knife points cinnamon, 6 egg whites and the juice of half a lemon
5. Pour it over the berries
6. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Stampfkartoffeln (Mashed Potatoes)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 Pfd.) Salzkartoffeln
- 1 Essl. Speckwuerfel
- 1 Teel. Zwiebeln

## Directions

1. Die Kartoffeln werden mit einer Keule gestampft und mit den Zwiebeln, welche mit den Speckwuerfeln braun gebraten werden, vermischt. Man kann auch 1/2 Teelöffel feingeschnittenen Schnittlauch und 1/2 Teelöffel Salz hinzufuegen.
2. Mussten die Kartoffeln stehen so ist es ratsam sie mit einem Holzloeffel zu zerdruecken und mit einem Loeffel zerlassenen Fettes durchzuruehren.

title: Stockfischpastete (Dried Cod Pastry)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter pastry dough
- 2 pounds dried cod or a piece of ling
- Salt water, boiling
- Onions, minced, 1 handful
- Parsley, 1 handful
- Butter
- Flour, 1 spoon full
- Light pea broth, 1/2 ladle full
- Salt
- Pepper
- Nutmeg
- Sour cream, 1/2 pint

## Directions

1. Make a pastry from butter dough as previously explained
2. Place 2 pounds dried cod or a piece of ling in a casserole with boiling salt water
3. Cover the casserole and set it to warmth
4. For the sauce: steam a handful of minced onions and parsley in butter
5. Let it brown lightly with 1 spoon full of flour
6. Pour 1/2 ladle full of light pea broth to it
7. Add salt, pepper, and nutmeg
8. Stir it smooth with 1/2 pint of sour cream
9. Let everything cook together
10. Pour the dried cod into a sieve
11. Clean it
12. Cook it a couple of minutes in the sauce
13. Fill it with the sauce into the pastry
14. Cover with the lid
15. Serve it at table

title: Stockfischpastete anderer Art (Dried Cod Pastry, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender butter dough
- Dried cod
- Salt water, boiling
- 2 cleaned herrings
- Roe and milk
- Parsley, 1 handful
- 1 onion
- Butter
- 3 hard-boiled eggs
- Thick sour cream, 1/2 pint
- Red beet broth

## Directions

1. Make a ring from tender butter dough
2. Lay it on a black sheet and bake it golden
3. Treat the dried cod as in No. 286
4. Cut 2 cleaned herrings into small pieces with roe and milk
5. Mince a handful of parsley with 1 onion finely
6. Lay a piece of butter in a casserole
7. Add the herrings to it
8. Pour the dried cod into a sieve
9. Clean it
10. Lay it with the herrings
11. Shake them often so everything mixes together
12. Press the yolk of 3 hard-boiled eggs through a sieve
13. Stir 1/2 pint of thick sour cream under it
14. Arrange the dried cod on a plate
15. Lay the ring around it
16. Arrange the stirred mixture over the dried cod
17. Cut the white of the eggs into leaves and cut it out with a tin cutter as desired
18. Lay the cut-outs in red beet broth
19. Put the pastry in the oven and let it brown
20. When it is time to serve, remove the pastry from the oven and garnish it with the eggs lying in red beet broth

title: Stockfischpastete auf böhmische Art (Dried Cod Pastry, Bohemian Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Tender butter dough
- A piece of ling
- Salt water, boiling
- 2 to 3 handfuls Bohemian peas
- Potatoes, baked
- Parsley, 1 handful
- Shallot onions
- Lard

## Directions

1. Roll out tender butter dough finger-thick
2. Make a bottom and a lid for a pastry from it
3. Brush the lid with egg
4. Bake the pastry golden
5. When it comes from the oven, remove the lid
6. Prepare the ragout: treat a piece of ling as described in No. 286
7. Cook 2 to 3 handfuls of Bohemian peas until the skins come off
8. Pour off the water
9. Set them to warmth
10. Pour the ling into a sieve
11. Clean it
12. Lay the pastry on a plate
13. Put some of the ling in it, then some of the peas, some baked potato, and continue until everything is used
14. Render the ling with a handful of parsley and shallot onions that have been steamed in hot lard
15. Lay the lid on the pastry
16. Serve it at table

title: Strauben (Fried Pastry Strands)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 lots butter
- Half a maa water
- Flour
- 6 to 7 eggs

## Directions

1. Let 4 lots butter, half a maa water and flour cook together in a brass pan until the dough peels off from the pan
2. Stir in 6 to 7 eggs until the dough is so thin that it runs
3. Brush a strauben funnel with hot lard
4. Let the dough run through the funnel into hot lard
5. Turn the strauben over and bake on the other side
6. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Straßburger Gansleberpastete (Strasbourg Goose Liver Pie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 goose livers
- salt
- half pound pork forcemeat
- 2 eggs
- 1 soaked and pressed rusk
- half pound crushed truffles
- half pound cut truffles
- 1 quint crushed mace
- 1 crushed nutmeg
- half loth pepper
- a handful of chopped and yellow-braised shallot onions
- 3 lemons
- half Württemberg Metze flour
- three quarters pound lard
- half pound butter
- cold water
- 4 loth grated Parmesan cheese
- bacon slices
- mushrooms

## Directions

1. Place 6 goose livers on a sheet of paper on a rack.
2. Sprinkle salt on the livers to draw out water.
3. Pound 3 of them as fine as a paste and cut the other 3 into small pieces, placing them in a dish.
4. Rub half pound pork forcemeat, 2 eggs, and 1 soaked and pressed rusk on a grater to a foam.
5. Add this to the pounded goose liver, half pound crushed and half pound cut truffles, 1 quint crushed mace, 1 crushed nutmeg, half loth pepper, salt, a handful of chopped and yellow-braised shallot onions, the juice from 3 lemons.
6. Mix everything together well and set aside.
7. Make a dough from half Württemberg Metze flour, three quarters pound lard, half pound butter, salt, and cold water.
8. Work it together firmly.
9. Roll out a base the thickness of a small finger and make it plate-sized.
10. Press the edge down firmly with the finger, keeping the dough finger-thick.
11. Make the pie 2 to 3 hands high.
12. Sprinkle 4 loth grated Parmesan cheese on the pie.
13. Place bacon slices and a handful of the pounded goose liver on top.
14. Then a layer of truffles.
15. Place the cut goose livers on the truffles, putting a mushroom on each piece, and continue until all is used.
16. Make a finger-thick lid from the butter dough.
17. Crimp it firmly with a pie crimper, but leave the edge 2 fingers width empty.
18. Make a small opening in the top of the lid and insert a plug, which remove when the pie is baked.
19. It is better to bake the pie the next day.
20. Brush it with eggs, make various flowers in the lid with the pie crimper.
21. Place it in a plafond so the fat does not run in the oven.
22. Let it bake 2 to 3 hours and serve it cold.

title: Straßburger Waffeln (Strasbourg Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 6 eggs
- 10 loth flour
- lukewarm milk
- 1 knife-point of salt

## Directions

1. Beat 1/2 pound butter until foamy and gradually beat in 6 eggs.
2. Make a thin dough from 10 loth flour with lukewarm milk and 1 knife-point of salt.
3. Stir it under the butter for a quarter hour.
4. Bake the waffles as usual on both sides and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Streuselkuchen (Crumb Sheet Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- Napfkuchen dough without raisins
- 100 g flour
- 50 g sugar
- 1 pinch ground cinnamon
- 50 g fat or lard
- 1 pinch salt

## Directions

1. Spread the same dough as for Napfkuchen, but without raisins, on a clean sheet pan
2. Roll out with a rolling pin to desired thickness
3. Let rise again
4. Prepare streusel: pour the hot fat into the remaining ingredients and distribute the resulting crumbs on the sheet cake
5. Let rise for another 1/2 hour with the toppings before baking in the oven until done

title: Stuttgarter Blutwürste (Stuttgart Blood Sausage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- pork cheeks, ears, and a piece of belly flap
- marjoram
- pepper
- spice blend
- salt
- 4 maas of blood
- 1 maas of milk
- fat from the above
- bacon

## Directions

1. Simmer the pork cheeks, ears, and belly flap until very tender
2. Cut into small cubes and mix with marjoram, pepper, spice blend, and salt
3. Mix 4 maas of blood with 1 maas of milk
4. Take the fat from the cooked meat and add it to the blood
5. Pass the blood through a hair sieve into a clean bowl
6. Fill clean prepared pork casings: put one handful of the cut bacon pieces into each, pour blood over it, and fill the casing
7. Tie the casings firmly and continue until all blood and bacon are in casings
8. Place the sausages in lukewarm water; when they rise to the surface, they are done

title: Stuttgarter Gansleberpastete (Stuttgart Goose Liver Pie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- semi-short dough
- bacon
- 3 goose livers
- 1 pound pork forcemeat
- quarter pound bacon
- 1 rusk
- quarter pound cleaned truffles
- 2 goose livers
- 2 eggs
- 4 loth lean ham
- a handful of chopped onions and parsley
- salt
- nutmeg
- mace
- pepper
- 3 chopped lemon peels
- marrow from lemons
- butter dough

## Directions

1. Make a semi-short dough and brush a melon mold with bacon.
2. Line the mold with the dough.
3. Weigh 3 goose livers finely.
4. Rub 1 pound pork forcemeat and quarter pound bacon on a grater.
5. Peel 1 rusk, soak it, and press it out.
6. Pound quarter pound cleaned truffles finely.
7. Cut 2 goose livers into cubes.
8. Beat 2 eggs together.
9. Weigh 4 loth lean ham.
10. Braise a handful of chopped onions and parsley.
11. Mix all of this together with salt, nutmeg, mace, pepper, 3 chopped lemon peels, and the marrow from the lemons in a bowl.
12. Fill the mass into the melon mold.
13. Make a smooth butter dough lid on top.
14. Cut some holes in it, brush the lid with eggs.
15. Bake the pie for 2 hours in the oven.
16. Let it cool and turn it out onto a plate.

title: Stuttgarter Kugelhopf (Stuttgart Kugelhopf)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one pound of flour
- 3 spoons of good beer yeast
- half a schoppen of cream
- one quarter pound of butter
- 4 egg yolks
- one loth of ground sugar
- one knife point of salt
- lukewarm milk (as needed)
- 4 loths of melted butter
- 10 loths of large and small raisins

## Directions

1. Place one pound of flour in a bowl
2. Make a small dough in the center of the bowl from 3 spoons of good beer yeast and half a schoppen of cream
3. Let it rise
4. Cut in one quarter pound of butter
5. Add 4 egg yolks, one loth of ground sugar, and one knife point of salt
6. Make a good, somewhat firm dough from everything
7. If necessary, add a little more lukewarm milk
8. Work it for a quarter hour
9. Let it rise beautifully
10. When ripe, roll out a little on a pasta board
11. Brush the dough with 4 loths of melted butter
12. Sprinkle 10 loths of large and small raisins over it
13. Roll it up like a sausage
14. Brush a Turkish mold with butter
15. Place the sausage in it
16. Let it rise well
17. Bake

title: Stuttgarter Speise (Stuttgart Dish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 maas water
- 3 loth butter
- 1 loth sugar
- Flour
- 6 to 8 egg yolks
- Lard
- 1/2 maas milk
- 3 loth sugar
- 1/2 lemon peel
- 5 egg yolks
- 3 fingers-full flour
- Cold milk
- 10 egg whites
- Sugar

## Directions

1. Cook 1/2 maas water, 3 loth butter, 1 loth sugar and flour to a thick dough
2. Put it in a bowl and beat 6 to 8 egg yolks in
3. Make walnut-sized balls from the dough and bake them in lard
4. For the crème: cook 1/2 maas milk with 3 loth sugar and 1/2 lemon peel
5. Smooth 5 egg yolks with 3 fingers-full flour and cold milk, stir in the boiling milk
6. Let everything come to the boil once more in the pan
7. Pour part of the crème through a hair sieve onto a plate
8. Make a pyramid from the baked puffs in the crème, pour the other half also on
9. Beat a snow from 10 egg whites and make a hand-high ring of it around the pyramid
10. Sprinkle sugar on it and place the puffs for a short time in the oven

title: Stuttgarter Springerlein (Stuttgart Springerlein)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 5 eggs
- 1 whole lemon, zested on a grater
- 1 pound flour
- fine flour
- white silk gauze
- bacon fat
- anise seeds

## Directions

1. Whisk together 1 pound finely sifted sugar and 5 eggs in a bowl for half an hour
2. Add the zest of 1 whole lemon grated on a box grater and mix with 1 pound flour
3. Wrap fine flour in a piece of white silk gauze and use to dust the molds well
4. Press dough into the molds and cut away excess with a knife
5. Transfer to a pastry board and continue until all dough is used
6. Let stand overnight
7. On the second day, brush a black baking sheet with bacon fat and sprinkle anise seeds on it
8. Place the springerlein on the sheet and bake

title: Stuttgarter Springerlein (Stuttgart Springerlings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound finely sieved sugar
- 5 eggs
- zest of 1 whole lemon
- 1 pound flour
- fine flour
- white gauze fabric
- bacon
- aniseed
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 2 egg whites
- juice of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir the finely sieved sugar with 5 eggs in a bowl for half an hour.
2. Grate the zest of a whole lemon on a grater and mix it with 1 pound of flour.
3. Bind fine flour in a piece of white gauze fabric, dust the molds well with it, press the dough into the molds, cut off the pressed dough with a knife, lay it on the noodle board, and continue until everything is finished.
4. Let it stand overnight.
5. The next day, coat a black baking sheet with bacon, sprinkle aniseed on it, lay the springerlings on it.
6. Stir a thick icing from 1/4 pound sugar, 2 egg whites, and the juice of 1/2 lemon.
7. Bake the springerlings halfway, coat them with the icing, and let them finish baking in the oven.

title: Stuttgarter Waffeln (Stuttgart Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound butter
- 9 egg yolks
- 1/4 pound pounded almonds
- 9 tablespoons rose water
- 4 tablespoons cherry spirits
- 9 egg whites
- 1/4 pound fine flour
- zest of 1/2 lemon

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound butter white and foamy in a bowl.
2. Stir in 9 egg yolks, one after another, 1/4 pound pounded almonds, 9 tablespoons rose water, and 4 tablespoons cherry spirits.
3. Beat the whites of 9 eggs to a stiff snow, mix with 1/4 pound fine flour, and combine with the previous mixture.
4. Add the zest of 1/2 lemon grated on a grater.
5. Bake golden in a waffle iron and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon.

title: Sulz für Kranke (Jelly for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 old hen, cleaned
- 2 calf's feet
- 1 bone
- 1/4 pound grated deer horn

## Directions

1. Chop the cleaned hen finely
2. Add 2 calf's feet and 1 bone
3. Bind the 1/4 pound grated deer horn in a cloth and cook it together with the other ingredients for 2 to 3 hours
4. Strain the broth through a cloth
5. Use as soup

title: Sulz mit Brustkräutern (Jelly with Chest Herbs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- white marshmallow roots, one handful
- lungwort, one handful
- liverwort, one handful
- hearts of blue cabbage, one handful
- 3 spoonfuls fine semolina
- 1 chopped calf's foot and bones
- a knife point of mace
- a little butter

## Directions

1. Cut the marshmallow roots, lungwort, liverwort, and cabbage hearts very finely
2. Add the semolina, chopped calf's foot and bones, mace, and a little butter
3. Cook everything together for 3 to 4 hours
4. Strain through a cloth and let it set in the cellar
5. The next day, remove the fat
6. Give the patient a spoonful morning and evening, mixed with meat broth

title: Sulz von geraspeltem Hirschhorn (Jelly of Grated Deer Horn)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loth deer horn
- 4 loth ivory
- handful of chest herbs
- watercress and chervil
- 1 1/2 maas water
- 3 loth raspberry juice
- 1 loth red cornflower juice

## Directions

1. Cut the deer horn, ivory, chest herbs, watercress, and chervil into small pieces together
2. Pour 1 1/2 maas water over them
3. Reduce by cooking until 1/2 maas of liquid remains
4. Strain through a hair sieve
5. Add 3 loth raspberry juice and 1 loth red cornflower juice
6. Give to children with fever and teething pain, one spoonful morning and evening; can also be given in tea

title: Sulz von Habergrütze (Jelly of Oat Groats)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound oat groats
- water
- peel and juice of 2 oranges
- juice of 1 lemon
- sugar

## Directions

1. Pour 1 schoppen water onto 1/4 pound oat groats
2. Let it stand for 3 days, adding 2 maas fresh water each day
3. On the fourth day, push it through a hair sieve
4. Cook the orange peel and juice, lemon juice, and a piece of sugar with the strained liquid for a quarter hour
5. Strain the jelly through a hair sieve
6. Give to the patient one spoonful at a time

title: Sulz von isländischem Moos (Jelly of Icelandic Moss)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound Icelandic moss
- 3 maas boiling water
- 1/2 pound candy sugar

## Directions

1. Pour 3 maas boiling water over 1/4 pound Icelandic moss
2. Let it stand for half an hour
3. Push the moss through a hair sieve
4. Pour the same amount of boiling water over it again and let stand another half hour
5. Push through the sieve again and press out the liquid
6. Let the liquid simmer in the pan for a quarter hour
7. Strain through a hair sieve again
8. Cook the liquid with 1/2 pound candy sugar until only 1 strong schoppen remains
9. Skim off the white foam from the top as it cooks
10. Let the jelly cool in a porcelain dish, cover it, and give to the chest patient one spoonful in the morning when fasting, at midday one hour after eating, and at night before bed

title: Sulz von Krebsen und Schnecken für Kranke (Jelly of Crayfish and Snails for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 30 snails (garden snails are best)
- 3 crayfish, roe and gall removed
- 1/2 calf's lung
- 1 skinned calf's foot
- 1/2 pound chervil
- salt
- mace
- 2 maas water

## Directions

1. Pound the snails and crayfish very finely
2. Cut the calf's lung and foot into small pieces
3. Cook together with the snails and crayfish, finely weighed chervil, salt, mace, and 2 maas water
4. Reduce by cooking until 1 maas of liquid remains
5. Strain through a wool cloth
6. Give the patient 2 spoonfuls to drink in the morning when fasting, at midday, and in the evening
7. The jelly can be cooked with meat broth if serving it warm

title: Suppe für Genesende (Broth for Convalescents)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one fresh egg
- 1 quart water
- 1 quart wine
- sugar
- cinnamon
- toasted bread slices

## Directions

1. Boil a fresh egg, peel it, and press it through a hair sieve.
2. Cook it in 1 quart water, 1 quart wine, sugar, and cinnamon for a quarter hour.
3. Arrange it over toasted bread slices.
4. If the wine is still too strong for the patient, meat broth may be used instead.

title: Suppe mit Rosolo (Soup with Rosolo)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Spinach
- Onions
- Cold roasted meat or two hard-boiled eggs
- Two eggs
- Flour for noodle dough
- One egg
- One egg yolk
- Meat broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Wash spinach, press out and finely chop with onions
2. Alternatively, finely chop some cold roasted meat or two hard-boiled eggs if roasted meat is not available
3. Make a noodle dough from two eggs and roll out finely
4. Stir the spinach with one egg and the yolk of one egg
5. Place one tablespoon of the mixture on each noodle cake, fold over, cut out and cook in meat broth
6. Add salt and nutmeg to the spinach

title: Suppe à la patrie (Soup à la Patrie)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 half Schoppen cream
- 1 whole egg
- Yolk of 1 egg
- Flour
- Lard
- Semolina flour
- Spinach
- Fish sausages
- Spinach dumplings
- Beef forcemeat
- Italian macaroni
- Butter
- Fine flour (for sauce)
- Water
- Meat broth
- Salt
- Nutmeg

## Directions

1. Stir together half a Schoppen cream, one whole egg and the yolk of one egg with enough flour to make a thick dough
2. Roll it out knife-back thick on a noodle board
3. Cut out various larger and smaller figures with small cookie cutters and fry them golden in hot lard
4. Make boiled and cooked dumplings from semolina flour, spinach fritters, fried rosettes, small fish sausages, green spinach dumplings, crayfish slices, fried egg slices, boiled beef forcemeat dumplings, fried Italian macaroni, egg cheese
5. Place all these properly boiled, cooked and fried items together in the soup bowl
6. For the sauce: melt 1 quarter pound butter in a casserole
7. Stir in 4 tablespoons of fine flour and let it brown slightly
8. Pour in half a ladle full of water and stir smooth
9. Add meat broth as needed
10. Let it come to a boil once more and pour into the soup bowl with salt and nutmeg

title: Suppe à la Reine (Queen's Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 old hen's breast
- Remaining body of the hen, chopped
- Some veal
- Parsnip
- Celery
- Carrots
- Leek
- Onions
- Butter
- Flour
- Meat broth
- 1 egg
- Nutmeg
- Chives

## Directions

1. Cut the breast from an old hen and set aside
2. Chop the remaining body and combine with some veal, and various root vegetables: parsnip, celery, carrots, leeks, and onions
3. Steam in 1/8 pound of butter in a covered casserole for 1 hour
4. Sprinkle a small handful of flour on top, turn, and pour meat broth over it
5. Let the whole thing cook for another half hour
6. Finely chop the hen's breast, pound it with an egg and some butter in a mortar, and form small dumplings
7. Cook the dumplings for 1/8 hour
8. Strain the glaze through a hair sieve and add the dumplings, some nutmeg, and chives

title: Sülze (Pork Aspic)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 kg pork shoulder
- 1 pork ear
- spice mixture
- 40 g onions
- 20 g salt
- 1-1/2 l water
- 1/8 l vinegar
- 65 g cucumber cubes

## Directions

1. After pork shoulder and pork ear are thoroughly washed, place them with spice mixture, onion slices, salt and 1-1/2 l water in a covered pot.
2. Skim thoroughly and let the pork shoulder simmer slowly for 1 hour and the pork ear for 2-1/2 hours until both are very soft.
3. Cut the meat into small cubes, pour the broth through a sieve, add vinegar and taste; after adding all ingredients to the broth, adjust seasoning once more.
4. Rinse the molds; fill with the mixture and after setting, turn out. You can line the bottom of the molds with cucumber, capers and lemon and let a few spoonfuls of broth set on top, then fill the aspic on top.
5. Serve with salad, vinegar and oil, oil sauce, and also fried potatoes.

title: Süße Milchsuppe (Sweet Milk Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Loth blanched almonds
- 3 Loth sugar
- Cinnamon
- 1 half Maas milk
- Fresh bread

## Directions

1. Take 2 Loth blanched almonds, pound them very finely
2. Stir them together with 3 Loth sugar and some cinnamon with half a Maas milk in a brass pan
3. Let it come to a boil together
4. Cut fresh bread into fine slices and serve the milk over them

title: Süßer Kartoffelauflauf (Sweet Potato Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound cooked cold potatoes
- 1/4 pound peeled almonds
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 lemon
- 1/4 pound butter
- 6 egg yolks
- half a Schoppen sour cream
- egg whites from the eggs
- butter
- sugar

## Directions

1. Grate 1 pound of cooked cold potatoes
2. Pound 1/4 pound of peeled almonds and 1/4 pound of sugar grated on a lemon very finely
3. Add this to the potatoes
4. Melt 1/4 pound of butter in a bowl and add the above mixture with 6 egg yolks and half a Schoppen of sour cream
5. Stir everything together for a quarter hour
6. Fold in the snow from the 6 egg whites
7. Let the soufflé rise golden in a buttered bowl and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Tabaksrollen (Tobacco Rolls)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter dough or other short pastry
- Eggs
- Quarter pound dried grapes and raisins
- 1 glass wine
- 2 lots blanched almonds
- 1 lot citron peel

## Directions

1. Roll out the dough strongly to knife back thickness
2. Cut into strips 3 fingers wide and half a quarter long
3. Brush with eggs
4. Cook quarter pound dried grapes and raisins in a small dish with 1 glass wine for a quarter hour until no liquid remains
5. Mix this with 2 lots blanched almonds and 1 lot citron peel
6. Spread the mixture on the strips
7. Brush the strips with egg and roll them on wooden sticks that are also buttered
8. Tie them together with thread and bake them quickly in hot lard until yellow
9. Remove the thread, let them drain on bread slices, pull out the sticks and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Tarragon Vinegar, French Style (Estragonessig auf französische Art)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Tarragon, tender leaves (1 strong handful)
- Good wine vinegar
- Pimpinella (6 pieces)
- Shallot onions (6)
- Cloves (8)
- White peppercorns (8)
- Ginger, whole (1 piece)
- Cinnamon, whole (1 piece)
- Lemon (a piece)

## Directions

1. Take a strong handful of tender tarragon leaves.
2. Pour good wine vinegar over them in a clean dish, together with 6 pimpinellas, 6 shallot onions, 8 cloves, 8 white peppercorns, a piece of whole ginger, a piece of whole cinnamon, and a piece of lemon.
3. Bind the dish tightly closed.
4. Place it in the sun for 14 days or 3 weeks, or otherwise in a warm place.
5. After 3 weeks, the vinegar is exceptionally good.
6. Fill it into bottles, close them tightly.
7. The vinegar can be used for sauces and salads.

title: Tauben, Junge (Young Squabs)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 young squabs
- 30 g butter or margarine
- 5 g potato starch
- 2 tablespoons sour milk or cream
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1/8 l water or broth
- 1 bread roll
- 1/2 tablespoon fat
- 1 tablespoon currants
- 1 pinch sugar and salt
- 1 egg yolk or egg white
- 9 tablespoons skim milk

## Directions

1. Pluck, singe, clean and lightly wash the squabs; remove wings, neck and legs (to the knee joint). Clean liver, stomach and heart. Rinse the currants and let them swell with a little water on the stove, grate the bread roll, break it into pieces and soften it in milk. Melt the fat and mix all ingredients together well.
2. Rub the squabs with salt and fill them with the stuffing. Sew the openings closed and tie the squabs into good shape.
3. Brown butter or margarine in a pot, place the squabs in and brown them well on all sides. Add water or broth gradually by spoonfuls and braise the squabs slowly covered for 1-1/2 hours.
4. Finally add the milk with potato starch stirred into it and let the sauce cook through. Divide the squabs lengthwise in halves and arrange them side by side on a platter.
5. Taste the sauce, strain through a sieve if necessary, and serve with the squabs.

title: Theekuchen (Tea Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 spoonfuls beer yeast
- milk
- flour
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 spoonfuls sour cream
- lemon zest
- salt
- 2 loth finely ground sugar
- quarter pound butter
- melted lard
- blanched almonds
- coarsely ground sugar

## Directions

1. Make a small dough from 2 spoonfuls beer yeast and milk in measured flour
2. When it has risen, add 2 whole eggs, 3 spoonfuls sour cream, some lemon zest, salt and 2 loth finely ground sugar
3. Make everything into a dough
4. Work this on the pastry board like a butter dough
5. Make it a bit firm
6. Roll it out
7. Cut a quarter pound butter into it
8. Fold it over
9. Roll it until the butter is well worked in
10. Roll it out 2 fingers thick
11. Let it rise on a butter-coated sheet
12. Brush the cake with melted lard
13. Sprinkle it with minced almonds and coarsely ground sugar
14. Then bake it

title: Timbal von Makaroni (Macaroni Timbale)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound Italian macaroni
- 1 pound lean, finely chopped ham
- 1 schoppen sour cream
- 3 eggs
- 1/4 pound grated Parmesan cheese
- butter dough

## Directions

1. Boil 1/2 pound Italian macaroni until quite soft.
2. Place in a bowl and mix with 1 pound lean, finely chopped ham, 1 schoppen sour cream, 3 eggs, and 1/4 pound grated Parmesan cheese.
3. Line a melon mold with butter dough.
4. Fill with the mixture, bake in the oven, and then turn out onto a plate.

title: Torte auf englische Art (English-Style Torte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 3/4 pound butter
- 1/4 pound sifted sugar
- 1/2 pound almonds
- zest of 1/2 lemon
- 1/2 pound flour
- 1 egg white
- 1/8 pound sugar
- red sanding sugar
- lemon or orange cream
- apple sauce
- other creams for filling

## Directions

1. Cream the butter in a bowl until foamy
2. Add the sifted sugar, almonds, and grated lemon zest, and stir everything together well
3. Line a dark baking sheet with paper, dust with a little flour
4. Work 1/2 pound flour into the mixture
5. Make 4 to 6 cakes, as large as desired for the torte
6. Bake them in the oven until nicely golden yellow
7. Once all cakes are baked, place one cake on the serving plate
8. Spread it with lemon or orange cream, lay the second cake on top, spread it with apple sauce, the third with another cream, and so forth (but the top one must remain empty)
9. Make the torte nicely round
10. Beat an egg white until stiff, add 1/8 pound sugar, stir together until thick
11. Spread this mixture over the torte, sprinkle red sanding sugar over it
12. Place in the oven and let it dry

title: Trauben oder Kirschen auf französische Art (Grapes or Cherries in the French Manner)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe grapes (black Muscat are the best) or cherries
- quarter pound of crushed sugar per half Maas glass
- coarsely crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- cherry schnapps

## Directions

1. Cut the stems from the grapes or cherries
