2. Place them in a sugar glass
3. For each half Maas glass, add one quarter pound of crushed sugar, some coarsely crushed cinnamon and cloves
4. Fill the glass completely with cherry schnapps
5. Bind it with a bladder and store it
6. This preserve is very good for baking

title: Traubenkuchen (Grape Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- good butter dough
- grapes
- half pound finely cut almonds
- quarter pound ground sugar
- half loth ground cinnamon
- 12 egg whites snow

## Directions

1. Lay good butter dough in a baking sheet
2. Place picked grapes over it
3. Mix half pound finely cut almonds, quarter pound ground sugar, half loth ground cinnamon, the snow from 12 egg whites together
4. Pour the mass over the grapes
5. Bake the cake in a not too hot oven

title: Trisenetschnitten (Trisenet Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds flour
- 2 spoonfuls beer yeast
- warm milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 3 loth finely powdered sugar
- 1 knife point salt
- 3 tablespoons raspberry juice
- 1 pound finely powdered sugar
- 1 loth red sandalwood
- 1 loth Trisenet
- 1 loth powdered cinnamon
- 2 finely grated nutmegs
- 1 small knife point cloves
- 1 bottle wine

## Directions

1. In a bowl with 2 pounds flour, make a small dough with 2 spoonfuls beer yeast and warm milk
2. Let it rise and add 2 egg yolks, 3 loth finely powdered sugar, 1 knife point salt, and 3 tablespoons raspberry juice
3. Make a dough from all of this
4. Work it a quarter hour on the noodle board
5. Let it rise again in the bowl
6. When fully ripe, make 2 loaves on the noodle board
7. Roll them out long, place on a flour-dusted sheet, let them rise again
8. Brush with eggs, bake in the oven, and let them sit 2 to 3 days
9. Cut them into regular slices
10. Mix 1 pound finely powdered sugar, 1 loth red sandalwood, 1 loth Trisenet, 1 loth powdered cinnamon, 2 finely grated nutmegs, and 1 small knife point cloves together
11. Pour 1 bottle wine into a dish
12. Dip 4 to 6 slices at once in the wine, rub them well with the mixed spices, and place them in another dish
13. Continue until all are rubbed
14. Let them stand covered overnight
15. The next day, dip and rub them again in the same way
16. Let them dry slowly on a sheet of paper in a cooled oven, and store them in a box
17. They can be kept this way for half a year

title: Tropfsuppe (Drop Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 spoonfuls of flour
- milk or water
- meat broth or water
- chives
- nutmeg
- salt

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 2 eggs, 2 spoonfuls of flour and milk or water
2. Heat meat broth or water until simmering
3. Let the dough run into the pot over a cooking spoon or through a skimming spoon
4. When serving, add chives, nutmeg and salt

title: Tropfsuppe (Drop Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 eggs
- 2 tablespoons flour
- Milk or water
- Meat broth or water
- Chives
- Nutmeg
- Salt

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 2 eggs, 2 tablespoons of flour and milk or water
2. Bring meat broth or water to a boil
3. Let the dough run into the casserole over a cooking spoon or through a skimming spoon
4. When serving add chives, nutmeg and salt

title: Turtle Ragout (Ragout von einer Schildkröte)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 turtle, head and feet
- hot water
- roots
- onions
- salt
- cloves
- 1/8 pound butter
- 6 to 8 onions, cut
- flour
- 1 drinking glass wine
- meat broth or pea broth
- 1 schoppen Madeira wine
- Spanish pepper
- 1/4 pound truffles, cleaned and prepared

## Directions

1. Cut off the head and feet of the turtle
2. Scald the turtle with hot water and clean the shell well
3. Cook the turtle on the fire with some roots, onions, salt and cloves until the shell can be lifted (takes about one strong hour)
4. Remove the hard skin from the top, also the liver and eggs
5. In a casserole, let 6 to 8 cut onions turn yellow in 1/8 pound butter, stir in one cooking spoon of flour
6. Let it turn yellow, pour on 1 drinking glass of wine and some meat broth (pea broth is also good)
7. Let it boil together for half an hour, press through a sieve
8. Pour it back into the casserole with 1 schoppen Madeira wine, a knife point of Spanish pepper, 1/4 pound cleaned and prepared truffles cut into leaves
9. Let it cook together for another quarter hour
10. Arrange the turtle on a plate and serve the sauce separately

title: Tyrolerknöpflein (Tyrolean Dumplings)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 cut rolls
- 1 pint hot milk
- 1 pound smoked bacon, finely chopped
- 1 pound smoked meat, finely chopped
- 1 handful onions steamed in bacon
- 3 spoonfuls fine flour
- salt
- 7 to 8 eggs

## Directions

1. Moisten 6 cut rolls in a bowl with 1 pint hot milk
2. Add 1 pound finely chopped smoked bacon and 1 pound finely chopped smoked meat
3. Add 1 handful onions steamed in bacon, 3 spoonfuls fine flour, and salt
4. Mix 7 to 8 eggs through everything
5. Let it stand covered for half an hour so it draws
6. Make round balls from the mixture
7. Boil them for half an hour in salt water
8. They can be added to stewed chicken

title: Tyrolerkrapfen (Tyrolean Fritters)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound almonds
- 1/4 pound butter
- 6 loth sugar
- 1/4 pound flour
- some aniseed
- 2 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon, zest grated
- 2 tablespoons wine

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 1/4 pound almonds, 1/4 pound butter, 6 loth sugar, 1/4 pound flour, some aniseed, 2 egg yolks, and half a lemon zest.
2. Add 2 tablespoons wine.
3. Roll out the dough, cut triangular pieces from it.
4. Lay the fritters on a flour-dusted baking sheet.
5. Coat them with egg and sprinkle coarse pounded sugar on top.
6. Bake them.

title: Tyrolernudeln (Tyrolean Noodles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pounds flour
- 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast
- 3 egg yolks
- 1/2 schoppen of sour cream
- 3 loth of sugar
- 1/4 pound of small raisins
- hot lard
- sugar
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Make a firm dough from 1-1/2 pounds flour, 3 spoonfuls of beer yeast, 3 egg yolks, 1/2 schoppen of sour cream, 3 loth of sugar, and 1/4 pound of small raisins
2. Work it for a quarter hour and let it rise in warmth
3. Make small cakes with a spoon, place them on a floured cloth
4. Form them into finger-long batons and bake them in hot lard
5. Sprinkle sugar and cinnamon over them

title: Uebrig gebliebener Braten mit Béchamelle (Leftover Roast with Béchamel)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cold roast
- 4 loth butter
- 2 cooking spoonfuls fine flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- lemon juice and zest
- half a pint sweet cream
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Cut the cold roast into desired pieces
2. Melt 4 loth butter in a casserole and stir in 2 cooking spoonfuls of fine flour
3. Add salt, nutmeg, juice and grated zest of half a lemon, and half a pint of sweet cream
4. Stir smooth with some meat broth and let it come to a boil together
5. Place the meat in it and let everything cook for another quarter hour

title: Vanillebrei (Vanilla Porridge)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 tablespoons grated roll flour
- 2 tablespoons fine flour
- cold milk
- 1 schoppen cream
- a piece of vanilla
- sugar

## Directions

1. Stir 2 tablespoons grated roll flour and 2 tablespoons fine flour smooth with cold milk
2. Let 1 schoppen cream and a piece of vanilla cook together for a quarter hour, pour the stirred mixture to it, let it cook well together
3. Arrange it on a plate and sprinkle sugar on top

title: Vanillegefrorenes (Vanilla Frozen)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 Schoppen milk
- 1 piece vanilla
- 12 egg yolks

## Directions

1. In a brass pan, let 1-1/2 Schoppen milk with a piece of vanilla cook as long as hard-boiled eggs
2. Beat 12 egg yolks in a pot
3. Stir the boiling milk to them
4. Let it draw once more in the pan
5. Force it through a hair sieve
6. Let it cool
7. Place it in the freezing bucket and work it according to the rule given in No. 767

title: Verdeckte Eier (Covered Eggs)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- butter
- oat flour (Geigenmehl)
- milk
- three whole eggs
- chives
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Boil eggs hard, peel them, place them in cold water for a while, and cut them into 4 parts.
2. Butter the dish to be served at table and place the hard-boiled eggs in it.
3. Take 3 handfuls of oat flour, stir it smooth with one pint of milk.
4. Beat in 3 whole eggs and a handful of finely-cut chives, stir everything well together.
5. Add salt and nutmeg, carefully pour it over the eggs.
6. Place the dish in a baking oven and let it rise nicely golden.

title: Verlorene Eier in einer Sauce (Poached Eggs in Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- water
- vinegar
- eggs
- salt
- sorrel
- butter
- sour cream
- egg yolk

## Directions

1. Heat equal parts water and vinegar in a pan, salt the water
2. Carefully crack the eggs into it (the water should not boil hard)
3. When the egg whites are hard, remove them and place in cold water
4. For the sauce: pound 2 handfuls of washed sorrel in a mortar and press the juice firmly through a cloth
5. Melt a piece of butter in a casserole
6. Take a tablespoonful of sour cream and 1 egg yolk, stir in the sorrel juice
7. Let it warm over charcoal fire
8. Arrange the eggs on a plate and pour the sauce over them

title: Verlorene Eier mit Jus (Poached Eggs with Jus)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- eggs
- water
- vinegar
- strong jus
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. In a brass pan with half water and half vinegar, when the water boils, carefully beat fresh eggs into it.
2. Remove them with a slotted spoon, place them in cold water, and continue until the eggs are done.
3. When serving, remove the eggs, trim them, place them on the plate for serving, pour a slotted spoonful of strong jus over them, let them come together, and place pepper on top.
4. The water must be salted.

title: Verlorene Eiersuppe (Lost Egg Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- boiling water
- black bread, finely cut
- lard
- eggs (as many as persons at table)
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over finely cut black bread that has been roasted golden in lard
2. Bring to a boil
3. Break as many eggs as there are persons at table into the soup
4. Let the soup draw a little, salt it and sprinkle nutmeg on top

title: Verlorene Eiersuppe (Lost Egg Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Water
- Black bread
- Lard
- Eggs

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over finely cut black bread that has been roasted golden in lard and bring to a boil
2. Beat in as many eggs as there are people at the table

title: Vermischter Salat (Mixed Salad)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- red beets
- potatoes
- beans
- bean kernels
- endive
- head lettuce
- ox mouth
- pickled cucumbers
- celery
- yellow beets
- soft corn
- yolk of 3 hard-boiled eggs
- 3 spoonfuls of Provençal oil
- good vinegar
- salt
- pepper

## Directions

1. Put red beets, potatoes, beans, bean kernels, endive, head lettuce, ox mouth, pickled cucumbers, celery, yellow beets, and soft corn in a bowl.
2. Press the yolk of 3 hard-boiled eggs through a hair sieve and dress the salad with 3 spoonfuls of Provençal oil, some good vinegar, salt and pepper.

title: Wachsbohnen (Wax Beans)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 kg (1 Pfd.) Wachsbohnen
- 1 1/2 l Wasser zum Abwellen
- 1/4 l Hammelbruehe
- 1 Stiel Pfefferkraut
- 1 Essl. (knapp) zerlassenes Rinderfett od. Margarine
- 4 g Mehl
- 1 Teel. gewiegte Petersilie
- 1 Prise Pfeffer und Salz

## Directions

1. Die Wachsbohnen werden wie Bohnen, gruene, bereitet.

title: Waffeln mit Bierhefe (Waffles with Beer Yeast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound flour
- 2 spoonfuls thick good beer yeast
- warm milk
- 1 knife point salt
- 1/2 pound butter
- 6 eggs
- bacon rind for brushing the waffle iron
- sugar and cinnamon for sprinkling

## Directions

1. Make a dough from 1/2 pound flour, 2 spoonfuls thick good beer yeast, warm milk, and 1 knife point salt
2. Let it rise in gentle warmth
3. In the meantime, lightly stir 1/2 pound butter in a bowl
4. Add 6 eggs and knead under the dough
5. Beat the dough strongly, let it rise once more
6. Bake it in hot waffle irons brushed with bacon rind on both sides
7. Sprinkle the waffles on the plate with sugar and cinnamon

title: Waffeln mit spanischem Wein (Waffles with Spanish Wine)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound flour
- Spanish wine
- 4 loth melted butter
- 4 egg yolks
- 1 knife-point of pounded cinnamon
- 1 knife-point of salt
- 4 egg whites
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- bacon

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound flour smooth with Spanish wine.
2. Pour 4 loth melted butter on it and stir in 4 egg yolks, one after another, along with 1 knife-point of pounded cinnamon and as much salt.
3. Add the stiff snow of 4 egg whites and 2 tablespoons sour cream.
4. Coat the hot waffle iron inside with a strip of bacon and fill with dough.
5. Bake the waffle on both sides and sprinkle with sugar.

title: Waffeln von saurem Rahm (Waffles with Sour Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound flour
- sour cream
- 6 egg yolks
- 1 knife-point of salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar
- 6 loth blanched and finely pounded almonds
- 6 to 8 bitter almonds
- 6 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir 1/2 pound flour smooth in a bowl with sour cream.
2. Add 6 egg yolks, one after another, 1 knife-point of salt, 1 tablespoon sugar, and 6 loth blanched and finely pounded almonds, among which 6 to 8 bitter almonds must be included.
3. Stir everything together well.
4. Beat the whites of 6 eggs to a stiff snow and slowly mix under the batter.
5. Bake in a waffle iron on both sides.

title: Warme Senfsauce (Warm Mustard Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Butter
- One minced onion
- Lemon
- One small tablespoon of flour
- One ladle-full of meat broth
- One tablespoon of mustard
- Two egg yolks
- Cold water

## Directions

1. Heat butter in a casserole with one minced onion and some lemon and let it cook slightly
2. Sprinkle one small tablespoon of flour over it
3. Let it steam for a couple of minutes
4. Pour in one ladle-full of meat broth and one tablespoon of mustard
5. Let everything cook together
6. Beat two egg yolks in a dish with one tablespoon of cold water
7. Slowly strain the sauce through a hair sieve onto the eggs

title: Wasserreis (Water Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g Reis
- 1/2 l Wasser zum Abwellen
- 1/2 l Wasser
- 5 g Salz
- 1 Stueck Apfelsinenschale
- 10 g Butter od. Margarine
- 2 Essl. Zucker und Zimt

## Directions

1. Man bereitet den Reis vor wie Milchreis.
2. Wie Milchreis anstatt Milch nimmt man Wasser und Fett.

title: Wassersuppe mit Grieß, Mehl, Stärkemehl, Graupen, Reis (Water Soup with Semolina, Flour, Cornstarch, Pearl Barley, Rice)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 l water
- 1 pinch salt
- 30 g sugar
- 10 g butter
- 1/4 lemon peel
- 1 small piece cinnamon
- 40 g semolina, 30 g pearl barley, rice, flour or cornstarch
- 1 egg yolk
- 1 tablespoon water

## Directions

1. Clean the pearl barley, rice and semolina by rinsing in cold water. Mix flour or cornstarch with 6 tablespoons cold water.
2. Bring 1 l water to a boil with salt, sugar, butter and spices, add the ingredients and cook until done under frequent stirring.
3. Pass the stirred egg yolk through a sieve into the soup; it can also be omitted.

title: Weichselkuchen (Sour Cherry Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1-1/2 pound sour cherries
- 10 loth melted butter
- 6 rolls or milk breads
- water
- 10 loth ground almonds
- 6 whole eggs
- 4 egg yolks
- cinnamon
- cloves
- 4 egg whites snow
- plafond with butter dough

## Directions

1. Remove stems and stones from 1-1/2 pound sour cherries
2. Put 10 loth melted butter in a bowl
3. Cut the crust from 6 rolls or milk breads
4. Soak them in water and press out firmly
5. Stir 10 loth ground almonds, 6 whole eggs, 4 egg yolks, a little cinnamon and cloves with all the above for half an hour
6. Mix the snow from 4 egg whites with it
7. Line a plafond with butter dough
8. Pour the stirred mixture onto it
9. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Weichsellatwerge (Sour Cherry Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pounds genuine, very ripe sour cherries
- 2 pounds sugar
- 1 Schoppen water

## Directions

1. Pit 2 pounds of genuine, very ripe sour cherries
2. Force them through a fine hair sieve
3. Cook 2 pounds of sugar with 1 Schoppen of water in an earthenware container until the sugar spins a very thick thread
4. Add the cherry pulp and cook it with the sugar under constant stirring until no juice is visible
5. Store the paste in a porcelain, bound pot like all preserves
6. It is particularly good for the sick

title: Weichsellatwerge anderer Art (Sour Cherry Paste Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe sour cherries (wine cherries are even better)
- half pound sugar per pound of pulp

## Directions

1. Pit beautiful ripe sour cherries (wine cherries are even better)
2. Force them through a hair sieve
3. For every amount of pulp, take the same amount in half-pounds of sugar
4. Cook them together in a pan until no juice is visible, then let it cool
5. Store it in wooden boxes or in a porcelain container

title: Weichselnauflauf (Sour Cherry Soufflé)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 6 loth almond macaroons
- 3 cooking spoonfuls fine flour
- 3 loth sugar
- 4 egg yolks
- 1/2 schoppen sweet cream
- 4 loth butter
- 5 egg yolks
- 1/2 lemon
- pitted sour cherries

## Directions

1. Place 6 loth almond macaroons in warmth to be roasted, then pound them fine in a mortar, place them in a brass pan
2. Stir them smooth with 3 cooking spoonfuls fine flour, 3 loth sugar, 4 egg yolks and 1/2 schoppen sweet cream and cook to a thick crème
3. Stir 4 loth butter and the crème together in a bowl, then add 5 egg yolks one after another, beat the whites of these eggs to snow and fold into the mass
4. Add 1/2 lemon grated on a grater, butter a baking sheet, place some of the mass in it, a layer of pitted sour cherries on top, pour some of the crème again, sour cherries again on top, the remaining crème over it
5. Place in oven and let it rise

title: Weichselsuppe für Kranke (Morello Cherry Soup for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Dried morello cherries
- Warm water
- Sugar
- Lemon
- Wine
- Cinnamon
- Bread slices

## Directions

1. Wash dried morello cherries thoroughly in warm water
2. Pound them very finely in a mortar
3. Place in a casserole, pour water over as much as needed for the soup
4. Let cook together for half an hour
5. Press through a fine sieve, return to casserole and bring to a boil once more
6. Rub a piece of sugar slightly on the lemon
7. If the illness permits, wine and cinnamon can be added
8. Arrange over poached bread slices

title: Weihnachtskuchen, Braune (Brown Christmas Cake)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 125 g Margarine
- 125 g Pork lard
- 150 g Brown syrup
- 125 g Sugar
- 1-1/8 kg (2-1/4 pounds) Flour
- 60 g Sweet grated almonds or citron cubes
- 5 Bitter almonds
- 10 Crushed cloves
- 1 teaspoon Cinnamon
- 10 g Chemically pure potash
- 20 g Baking powder
- 2 tablespoons Warm water
- 1 Piece of bacon rind for the baking sheet

## Directions

1. Bring the margarine, lard, syrup, and sugar to a boil and, after cooling, stir in the flour mixed with the spices.
2. Dissolve the potash and baking powder in warm water, strain through muslin, and combine with the dough.
3. Cover the dough and set it in a warm place for 4 days, then roll it out, cut it into desired shapes, and bake it slowly on a greased baking sheet in the oven.
4. Before baking, you can garnish the cakes with almond halves, or after they have cooled, you can brush them with icing.
5. Icing: 3 tablespoons powdered sugar stirred with 2 teaspoons rose water or water. Must not stand long.

title: Weinbeerkuchen (Bilberry Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 16 loth grated black bread
- one grated nutmeg
- 2 knife points cloves
- half loth ground cinnamon
- butter dough
- 12 egg yolks
- half pound ground sugar
- 8 loth melted butter
- half pound raisins
- 12 egg whites snow

## Directions

1. Mix 16 loth grated black bread, one grated nutmeg, 2 knife points cloves, half loth ground cinnamon together
2. Cover a cake sheet with butter dough
3. Stir 12 egg yolks, half pound ground sugar and 8 loth melted butter together for a quarter hour
4. Add half pound raisins and the snow from the 12 egg whites
5. Mix the bread with the rest
6. Fill everything onto the butter dough
7. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Weinbeerschnitten (Bilberry Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- 2 whole eggs
- 3 pinches ground cinnamon
- 4 loth small black bilberries
- 1 pinch cloves
- flour
- bacon fat

## Directions

1. Whisk together 1/2 pound sugar and 2 whole eggs until thick
2. Add 3 pinches ground cinnamon, 4 loth small black bilberries, and 1 pinch cloves
3. Work in enough flour until it forms a not-very-firm dough
4. Form a long sausage-like loaf on a pastry board
5. Place on a black baking sheet brushed with bacon fat
6. Bake slowly at gentle heat in a baking oven
7. Cut into slices when cooled
8. Keep for 2 to 3 weeks

title: Weinschaumsauce (Chaudeau) im Wasserbade (Wine Foam Sauce in a Water Bath)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg yolks
- 1 whole egg
- 40 g sugar
- 1/8 l apple wine, white wine or malt wine
- 1 teaspoon lemon sugar
- 1 tablespoon lemon juice

## Directions

1. Stir eggs with sugar cold, add wine, lemon sugar and lemon juice, place in water bath and beat until thick. To increase the amount of sauce, one can also cook 10 g flour separately with 1/8 l wine and stir into the above mixture. The sauce is to be served warm or cold.
2. Remarks: Instead of lemon, orange or both mixed can be used. If 3 tablets of dissolved gelatin are added and the mixture is then poured into a bowl, when cooled you have wine foam dessert.

title: Weinstrauben (Wine Strauben)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 schopen wine
- 2 lots butter
- A pinch of cinnamon
- 12 lots flour
- 2 lots sugar
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Stir together 1 schopen wine, 2 lots butter, a pinch of cinnamon, 12 lots flour and 2 lots sugar until smooth
2. Thin the dough with egg white so it runs well
3. Brush the strauben funnel with hot lard
4. Let the dough run through it into a pan with hot lard
5. Bake the strauben yellow, turn them over and bake on the other side
6. Curl them over a wooden stick and sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Weinsuppe (Wine Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure of wine
- 1 cooking spoonful of flour
- cold water
- 4 eggs
- 3 lots of sugar
- zest of half a lemon
- roasted bread pieces

## Directions

1. Pour one measure of wine into a pan
2. Stir one cooking spoonful of flour mixed with cold water into it
3. Beat in 4 eggs
4. Add 3 lots of sugar with the zest of half a lemon mixed in
5. Pour in half a measure of cold water
6. Let it cook while constantly stirring
7. Serve over roasted bread pieces

title: Weinsuppe anderer Art (Wine Soup Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter
- 1 cooking spoonful of flour
- cold water
- 1 measure of wine
- 3 lots of sugar
- cinnamon stick
- zest of 1 lemon
- roasted bread pieces

## Directions

1. Let a piece of butter melt in a pot
2. Add 1 cooking spoonful of flour and let it brown
3. Deglaze with cold water
4. Pour in one measure of wine
5. Add 3 lots of sugar, a piece of cinnamon and the zest of a lemon
6. Let it all come to a boil together
7. Serve over roasted bread pieces

title: Weinsuppe mit süßem Rahm (Wine Soup with Sweet Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 measure of cream
- 1 measure of wine
- half a vierling of sugar
- zest of 1 lemon
- 4 egg yolks
- butter
- diced rolls
- saffron
- salt

## Directions

1. Warm 1 measure of cream and 1 measure of wine separately
2. Cook half a vierling of sugar, mixed with zest of a lemon, together with 4 egg yolks and the wine and cream
3. Roast diced rolls in lard
4. Lay them in the prepared bowl
5. Pour the cooked mixture over them
6. Sprinkle saffron on the soup

title: Weissbiergelee (Wheat Beer Jelly)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 tablets red gelatin
- 1 bottle wheat beer
- 1/2 cm cinnamon
- 1/2 tablespoon lemon sugar
- 100 g sugar
- 1 tablespoon rum

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatin in 8 tablespoons wheat beer while lukewarm.
2. In the remaining wheat beer, add cinnamon, lemon sugar, sugar, rum and the gelatin poured through a sieve, stir everything thoroughly, remove the cinnamon, fill the jelly into small glasses, a porcelain or glass bowl and chill well.

title: Weissbiergelee für 4 Personen (Wheat Beer Jelly for 4 Persons)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 9 Sheets Red gelatin
- 1 Bottle Wheat beer
- 1/2 cm Cinnamon
- 1/2 tablespoon Lemon sugar
- 100 g Sugar
- 1 tablespoon Rum

## Directions

1. Dissolve the gelatin in 8 tablespoons wheat beer at warm temperature.
2. Add cinnamon to the remaining wheat beer, add lemon sugar, sugar, rum, and the gelatin strained through a sieve, stir everything well, remove the cinnamon, pour the jelly into small glasses, a porcelain or glass dish, and chill thoroughly.
3. Instead of wheat beer, you can also use cider or light white wine (wine jelly). The rum can also be omitted.

title: Weisskohl (White Cabbage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 kleiner Kopf Kohl
- 10 g Salz
- 1 Prise Natron
- 3 l Wasser
- 40 g Bouillonfett oder Bratenfett oder Margarine
- 15 g Zwiebeln
- 2 g Kuemmel
- 2 Pfefferkoerner
- 5 g Salz
- 3/8 l Bruehe
- 5 g Mehl

## Directions

1. Der Kohlkopf wird in 16 Teile geschnitten; nachdem die aeusseren Blaetter und Struenke entfernt sind, wird er gewaschen, in kochendem Salzwasser mit einer Prise Natron offen abgewellt, in kaltem Wasser abgekuehlt und gut abgetropft. Der Kuemmel wird vorbereitet.
2. 20 g Bouillon- oder Bratenfett laesst man zergehen, schmort die feingeschnittenen Zwiebeln darin, legt das Kraut dicht aneinander und streut den Kuemmel, die Pfefferkoerner und das Salz dazwischen, uebergiesst den Kohl mit Bruehe und daempft ihn zugedeckt langsam gar. Dann schwitzt man Mehl und die uebrigen 20 g Fett auf einer Untertasse und verbindet das Gemuese damit erst wenn der Kohl gar ist, was durch den Mehlzusatz von Anfang an verzoegert wuerde. Ist keine Bruehe zur Hand so kann auch Wasser genommen werden.

title: Weiße Brodsuppe für Kranke (White Bread Soup for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 rolls
- 3 lot almonds
- rose water
- 1 measure almond milk
- cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak 2 rolls and press them out firmly
2. Pound 3 lot almonds very finely with rose water
3. Make 1 measure of almond milk as usual
4. Mix it well with the rolls and cook
5. Sprinkle cinnamon on the soup when serving

title: Weiße Brodsuppe für Kranke (White Bread Soup for the Sick)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 Wecken (bread rolls)
- 3 Loth almonds
- Rosewater
- 1 Schoppen almond milk
- Cinnamon

## Directions

1. Soak the bread rolls and press them out firmly
2. Pound the almonds very finely with rosewater
3. Make 1 Schoppen almond milk in the usual way
4. Stir it well with the bread rolls and cook
5. When the soup is served, sprinkle cinnamon on top

title: Weiße Mandellebkuchen (White Almond Gingerbread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound blanched almonds
- 8 egg whites
- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1 quint cardamom
- 4 loth citron
- 1 lemon zest, finely minced
- 1/2 loth ground cinnamon
- 1 pinch cloves
- 1 pound fine flour
- oblaten (wafer paper)

## Directions

1. Cut the blanched almonds as finely as possible and let them turn golden on a baking sheet in the oven
2. Whisk together 8 egg whites and 1 pound finely sifted sugar for a quarter hour
3. Mince 1 quint cardamom, 4 loth citron, and 1 lemon zest very finely
4. Add 1/2 loth ground cinnamon and 1 pinch cloves
5. Mix the almonds and 1 pound fine flour with the above ingredients
6. Make a dough and beat it flat on a pastry board with a knife
7. Form into biscuits 3 fingers wide and 1 finger long
8. Place them on oblaten and bake in the oven
9. For the glaze: cook 1/4 pound sugar and 2 tablespoons water together
10. Add the juice of 1 lemon and let it come to a boil once more
11. Brush the glaze onto the cakes with a pastry brush using the cooked mixture
12. Dry them in the oven

title: Weiße Mandelschnitten (White Almond Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/2 pound sugar
- 1/4 pound finely ground almonds
- 1 whole lemon, zested on a grater
- 6 eggs
- 2 handfuls fine, grated violin flour
- 3 fingers' worth white flour
- butter

## Directions

1. Whisk together 1/2 pound sugar, 1/4 pound finely ground almonds, 1 lemon grated on a box grater, and 6 eggs for half an hour
2. Mix in 2 handfuls fine grated violin flour and 3 fingers' worth white flour
3. Butter a long metal form and fill with the mixture
4. Bake at gentle heat
5. On the second day, cut the cake into slices and bake them on a black baking sheet in the oven until nicely golden

title: Weiße Rüben (White Turnips)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- White turnips
- Fat
- Flour
- 2 tablespoons sour cream
- 2 tablespoons sweet cream

## Directions

1. Scrape the turnips, cut them fine and long like noodles, wash them, and braise them in a casserole with a piece of fat until soft.
2. Dust a little flour over them and pour 2 tablespoons of sour cream and the same amount of sweet cream over them.

title: Weiße Rüben anderer Art (White Turnips, Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- White turnips
- 3 Loth sugar
- Fat
- Flour
- Meat broth

## Directions

1. Prepare the turnips as in recipe No. 127.
2. Toast 3 Loth of sugar in a casserole with fat until beautiful yellow, add the turnips and let them cook for an hour.
3. When soft, dust a spoonful of flour over them and mix with a cooking spoon, pour a spoonful of meat broth over them and let them cook for another hour.

title: Weiße Rüben auf polnische Art (White Turnips, Polish Style)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- White turnips
- Lard
- Bouillon or jus
- Flour
- Sugar
- Optional: carbonade, mutton, or pork

## Directions

1. After peeling and cutting the turnips, fry them in hot lard.
2. Cook them in a casserole with bouillon or jus, dust a little flour over them, add sugar and bring to a boil once more.
3. Carbonade, mutton, or pork can be added if desired.

title: Weiße Würste (White Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Ingredients as in recipe 369
- White and soft-boiled offal
- Onions
- Butter
- Marjoram

## Directions

1. Make the sausages as in recipe 369, but instead of sweetbreads and udder use a beautiful white and properly soft-boiled offal, but no butter except that in which the onions have been fried
2. Add a little chopped marjoram
3. Serve them warm

title: Weißes Eis (White Icing)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound sugar
- 1 egg white
- 1 lemon

## Directions

1. Pound one quarter pound sugar very finely
2. Pass it through a hair sieve
3. Beat one egg white into it and add the juice of one lemon
4. Stir for a quarter hour until it is completely white and use as desired

title: Weißes Kraut mit saurem Rahm (White Cabbage with Sour Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 to 3 cabbage heads
- fresh water
- salt water
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- chervil
- tarragon
- thyme
- basil
- smoked meat
- jus
- flour
- salt
- nutmeg
- pepper
- half measure of thick sour cream

## Directions

1. Remove the core from 2 to 3 cabbage heads and soak them in fresh water for one hour.
2. Cook them in salt water until half tender, drain in a sieve.
3. Cut each head in two parts, bind it with string to hold it together again.
4. Place the cabbage in a casserole with a piece of butter.
5. Add minced onions, parsley, chervil, tarragon, thyme, and basil, also a piece of smoked meat.
6. Cover the casserole, turn the cabbage often, and pour a little jus over it if needed.
7. When the cabbage is almost tender, sprinkle a little flour over it, salt it, add nutmeg and pepper.
8. Half an hour before serving, pour half a measure of thick sour cream over it.
9. Remove the meat, cut it into pieces, arrange the cabbage, remove the strings, and garnish with the smoked meat.

title: Welsche Nüsse aufzubewahren (Preserving Walnuts)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- walnuts with still green shells
- dry salt

## Directions

1. Lay the walnuts, which still have green shells, in a pot on dry salt
2. Press them together with the hand
3. Cover them with salt
4. Bind a cloth over it
5. Bury the pot in the earth, but in such a way that frost does not reach it
6. Thus one can preserve them for a long time

title: Welsche Suppe (Turkey Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Turkey feet, neck, head, and wings from the second joint
- Butter
- Onions
- Milk bread
- Meat jus
- Egg yolks
- Sour cream

## Directions

1. Take the feet, neck, head, and wings from the second joint of a turkey and chop everything very finely
2. Steam cut onions, the chopped mixture, and milk bread cut into 2 kreuzer worth of pieces with butter for half an hour
3. Pour in good jus and let it cook together for one hour
4. Beat 3 egg yolks with 2 tablespoons of sour cream in the soup bowl
5. Strain the soup through a hair sieve once more

title: Welsche Suppe (Welsh Chicken Soup)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- feet, neck, and head of a Welsh chicken
- wings from the second joint
- butter
- onion, sliced
- milk bread, sliced, worth 2 kreuzer
- good jus
- 3 egg yolks
- 2 spoonfuls sour cream
- salt
- nutmeg

## Directions

1. Take the feet, neck, and head of a Welsh chicken, and also the wings from the second joint, and chop everything very finely.
2. Braise sliced onion, the chopped chicken pieces, and the sliced milk bread in a piece of butter for half an hour.
3. Pour good jus over it and let it cook together for one hour.
4. Beat 3 egg yolks with 2 spoonfuls of sour cream in the soup bowl.
5. Pass the soup through a hair sieve once more.
6. Let it heat again in the saucepan, stir it into the soup bowl, and season with salt and nutmeg.

title: Welschkorn (Corn)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- corn cobs
- bay leaves
- coarsely crushed pepper
- salt water

## Directions

1. Blanch the corn cobs when they are as thick as a small finger
2. Place them in salt water for one day
3. Remove and dry them
4. Lay bay leaves and coarsely crushed pepper in a stone pot
5. Place the corn cobs on top, then pepper and bay leaves again
6. Continue alternating until the pot is full
7. Bind it tightly and store it
8. Serve with beef

title: Wiener Hippen (Viennese Wafers)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 vierling flour
- 4 loth lard
- 4 loth ground almonds
- 1/4 pound sifted sugar
- 4 eggs
- zest and juice of 1 lemon
- 1 spoon sweet cream

## Directions

1. Work all ingredients into a smooth dough
2. Bake in a wafer iron
3. Roll the wafers while still hot

title: Wiener Kuchen (Viennese Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 strong cooking spoonfuls flour
- one schoppen milk
- 6 egg whites
- quarter pound butter
- sugar to taste
- lemon zest (optional)

## Directions

1. Stir 4 strong cooking spoonfuls flour with one schoppen milk cold
2. Then stir thick on the fire
3. Add 6 egg whites, quarter pound butter, sugar to taste (one can also add some lemon zest)
4. Fill the mass into any desired form
5. Bake it

title: Wiener Speise (Viennese Dish)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Apples of equal size
- 1 piece butter
- 1 piece sugar
- 2 beaten eggs
- Finely grated bread
- 10 loth almonds in the shell
- 1/4 pound crushed sugar
- 1 nutmeg
- 1 knife-tip crushed cloves
- 4 loth grated black bread
- 10 egg yolks
- 10 egg whites

## Directions

1. Peel apples and remove the core
2. Steam them halfway done in a piece of butter and a piece of sugar
3. When cold, turn them in 2 beaten eggs, then in finely grated bread
4. Place in a cake sheet and bake until golden in the oven
5. Crush 10 loth almonds in the shell very fine, add 1/4 pound crushed sugar, 1 nutmeg, 1 knife-tip crushed cloves, 4 loth grated black bread
6. Beat 10 egg yolks in and stir everything together as strongly as a cake mixture
7. Finally add the snow from 10 egg whites
8. Place the apples on the plate, fill the beaten mixture on top and let it bake golden in the oven

title: Wiesbadner Brod (Wiesbaden Bread)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound butter
- half pound flour
- 6 loth sugar
- zest of one lemon
- 6 egg yolks
- lemon or chocolate icing

## Directions

1. Mix together one quarter pound butter, half pound flour, 6 loth sugar, lemon zest grated on a grater, and 6 egg yolks to form a dough
2. Form finger-long sticks from this dough
3. Tear them onto a black baking sheet brushed with lard using a knife
4. Bake in the oven
5. Pour lemon or chocolate icing over them

title: Wilde Enten (Wild Ducks)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Wild duck
- Salt
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Nutmeg
- Bacon
- Lemon
- Pork fat
- Onions
- Carrots
- Bay leaves
- Tarragon vinegar

## Directions

1. Pluck the duck, remove the skin, cut off the head and neck
2. Soak it in cold water for 3 days, changing the water daily
3. Dry it carefully with a cloth
4. Rub it with salt, pepper, cloves, and nutmeg
5. Bind it with bacon and lemon
6. Braise it in a covered casserole with pork fat, onions, carrots, bay leaves, and a little tarragon vinegar for half an hour
7. Uncover the casserole and roast the duck until golden yellow
8. Add the juice of half a lemon, arrange the duck on a platter, pour the sauce through a hair sieve over it, and leave the bacon on the duck
9. Note: The sauce should not be treated as with other wild game because the liver and heart are unusable due to their unpleasant fishy moss smell

title: Winterkohl (Winter Cabbage)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- cabbage
- fat
- boiling water
- onions
- pork or goose fat
- 3 spoonfuls flour
- meat broth
- salt
- nutmeg
- glazed chestnuts

## Directions

1. Strip, wash the cabbage and add a piece of fat with boiling water.
2. Cook until tender, strain, and let sit in cold water for a quarter hour.
3. Press out firmly and finely mince together with onions.
4. In a casserole with a piece of pork or goose fat in which 3 spoonfuls of flour have been roasted, steam for a quarter hour.
5. Add a ladle of meat broth, salt, and nutmeg.
6. Serve on the platter garnished with glazed chestnuts if desired.

title: Wips (Wips)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- One Maas wine
- One pound sugar
- 24 eggs

## Directions

1. Put one Maas wine, one pound sugar, and 24 eggs in a pan
2. Beat this on the fire with a small whisk until it begins to boil
3. Remove the drink from the fire
4. Continue beating for a quarter hour
5. The Wips is then ready
6. Champagne can be used instead of ordinary wine

title: Wirsing anderer Art (Savoy Cabbage Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- savoy cabbage
- butter
- onions
- parsley
- flour
- pepper
- nutmeg
- meat broth

## Directions

1. Clean the savoy cabbage and cut off the ribs and wash it
2. Braise it for a quarter hour in a piece of butter with onions and parsley
3. Sprinkle a little flour, pepper, and nutmeg over it
4. Pour meat broth over it

title: Wirsing mit geräuchertem Fleisch und Feldhühnern (Savoy Cabbage with Smoked Meat and Partridges)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- savoy cabbage
- smoked meat
- partridges

## Directions

1. Cook the savoy cabbage as for blue cabbage
2. Serve the partridges whole and add some smoked meat to make it more flavorful

title: Wirsing mit Schweinsfüßen und Ohren (Savoy Cabbage with Pig's Feet and Ears)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- savoy cabbage
- butter or lard
- shallot onions
- bay leaves
- cloves
- raw ham
- meat broth
- flour
- nutmeg
- salt
- pig's feet and ears

## Directions

1. Cut the stem and ribs from the savoy cabbage
2. Boil it in water for only two broths, then place it in cold water
3. In a casserole with butter or lard, add chopped shallot onions, lay them with two bay leaves, four cloves and the squeezed-out cabbage heads cut into four parts
4. Cover and braise strongly on coals
5. Pour in one spoonful of bouillon, add a piece of raw ham and let everything cook soft together
6. Add another piece of butter and another spoonful of flour, some nutmeg and salt
7. Arrange the cabbage on a plate, pass the sauce through a hair sieve onto the cabbage and garnish the plate with pig's feet and ears

title: Wirsingkohl (Savoy Cabbage)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 mittelgr. Kopf Wirsingkohl
- 2 l Wasser, 5 g Salz zum Abwellen
- 30 g Hammel- oer Rinderfett
- 1/2 Teel. Zwiebelwuerfel
- 1/2 Gewuerzdosis
- 10 g Mehl, 1/2 l Wasser
- 1 Teel. Salz
- 1 Prise Pfeffer

## Directions

1. Die schlechten Kohlblaetter entfernen, den Kopf achtmal durchschneiden, den dicken mittleren Strunk entfernen und den Kohl waschen. Die Kohlstuecke werden nach und nach in das kochende Salzwasser getan und abgewellt, dann abgegossen, kalt abgeschreckt, abgetropft.
2. Das Fett wird im irdenen Topf zerlassen; die Zwiebelwuerfel werden darin weich geschmort, Gewuerze hinein, Kohl darauf gelegt, mit Mehl, Pfeffer und Salz bestaeubt, Wasser dazugegossen und alles zugedeckt 1 1/2 Stunde langsam weichgeschmort. Abgeschmeckt wird der Kohl in einem Napf zu Tisch gegeben. Soll der Kohl mit Kartoffeln gemischt sein, muessen diese 3/4 Stunden vor dem Anrichten dazugetan werden.

title: Wohlfeile und brauchbare Hefe (Inexpensive and Usable Yeast)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 loth grated, mealy, cooked potatoes
- 1 loth pounded sugar
- a teaspoon full of yeast
- a schoppen of water

## Directions

1. Place 4 loth grated, mealy, cooked potatoes, 1 loth pounded sugar, and a teaspoon full of yeast in a pot together with a schoppen of water
2. Set it in gentle warmth
3. After one hour the yeast can be used

title: Würstchen, Geräucherte, Warm zu Machen (Smoked Sausages, Heated)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 pairs sausages
- 1/2 l cold water

## Directions

1. Place Jauersche, garlic or Wiener sausages in a covered pot with plenty of cold water.
2. When the water is hot, just before boiling, the sausages are ready. If the sausages boil, they become hard and dry.

title: Würste auf bürgerliche Art (Bourgeois-Style Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- all intestines from 2 pigs
- pig ears
- pig tongues
- salt
- pepper
- cloves
- caraway seeds
- bouillon

## Directions

1. Wash all the intestines from 2 pigs in salt water
2. Weigh the ears and tongues of the pigs with the intestines
3. Add salt, pepper, cloves and caraway seeds
4. Mix everything well together
5. Fill the mixture into intestines and tie them well
6. Place the sausages with some feet into a braise (broth)
7. Fill this with bouillon
8. Let them cook on steady fire and afterwards cool
9. They can be kept in the cellar for 14 days
10. Before use, remove the aspic from them, wrap each sausage separately in paper brushed with butter, fry them on the grill until golden and serve warm

title: Würste auf italienische Art (Italian-Style Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Pork or veal brain
- Some veal udder
- Some butter
- Egg yolks
- A little saffron

## Directions

1. Make the sausages from pork or veal brain with some veal udder and some butter added
2. The difference is that no bread is used, but only the brain itself and some egg yolks, with a little saffron added
3. Serve them warm

title: Würste auf italienische Art (Italian-Style Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 pig ears
- 4 pig skins with some fat still attached
- 1 glass muscatel wine
- chopped basil
- thyme
- lemon peel
- pepper
- cloves (coarsely ground)
- salt

## Directions

1. Chop 4 pig ears and 4 pig skins with fat attached
2. Pour a glass of muscatel wine over them
3. Add some chopped basil, thyme, lemon peel, pepper, coarsely ground cloves, and salt
4. Mix everything well together
5. Fill into intestines very firmly like salami and tie tightly
6. Hang them in a dry place with air circulation until they are quite hard
7. Before serving, simmer them with 2 bay leaves until soft and serve with vegetables
8. They can be kept a long time and sent far distances

title: Würste von Hasen (Hare Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Hare meat, cleaned and skinned
- 1 pound lard
- Several shallot onions
- Basil
- Thyme
- A little lemon zest
- Red wine
- Pepper
- Salt
- Bratwurst casings

## Directions

1. Chop the hare meat, lard, onions, basil, thyme, and lemon zest very finely
2. Pour a small glass of red wine over it
3. Add pepper and salt
4. Mix everything well together
5. Fill into bratwurst casings
6. Before serving, fry them like bratwurst sausages, but not too dry to avoid them becoming brittle

title: Würste von Kalbshirn (Veal Brain Sausages)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Veal brain
- Ingredients as in recipe 421

## Directions

1. Make the sausages as in recipe 421, but use veal brain instead of offal
2. Serve them warm

title: Würste von weißem Geflügel mit Rahm (White Poultry Sausages with Cream)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Half-roasted poultry (capon or poulard)
- Béchamel
- Egg yolks
- Pepper
- Salt
- Butter
- Milk

## Directions

1. Skin the half-roasted poultry and remove all meat
2. Cut the meat into fine fillets
3. Mix with a good amount of béchamel, some egg yolks, and a little pepper and salt
4. Fill the mixture into casings
5. Poach the sausages gently in salted water
6. Cool in milk
7. Place on paper brushed with butter
8. Fry until golden brown over low heat

title: Zimmteis (Cinnamon Icing)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one quarter pound finely grated sugar
- 1 egg white
- 1 loth ground cinnamon
- a couple drops of lemon juice

## Directions

1. Beat one egg white into one quarter pound finely grated sugar
2. Stir together for a quarter hour
3. Add 1 loth ground cinnamon and a couple drops of lemon juice

title: Zimmtküchlein (Cinnamon Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- sugar
- 1 loth cinnamon
- wax

## Directions

1. Beat 2 egg whites to a stiff snow.
2. Stir sugar and 1 loth cinnamon under it.
3. Coat a black baking sheet with wax.
4. Set small heaps the size of a hazelnut from the mixture on it and bake them in a cooled oven.

title: Zimmtschnitten (Cinnamon Slices)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast dough
- 3 loth finely powdered sugar
- 3 knife points cinnamon
- eggs for brushing
- sugar and cinnamon for sprinkling
- 1/2 pound finely powdered sugar
- 1 loth powdered cinnamon
- 1 whole grated nutmeg
- 1 knife point cardamom

## Directions

1. Make a not very strong yeast dough as previously described
2. Let it rise, take 3 loth finely powdered sugar and 3 knife points cinnamon
3. Work the dough with this
4. Let it rise until ripe
5. Make 3 sausages on a noodle board
6. Place them on a black flour-dusted sheet, let them rise once more in warmth
7. Brush with eggs, scatter sugar and cinnamon on top, and bake until beautifully golden
8. Let them cool
9. Cut them into slices 2 knife-back thicknesses thick
10. Sprinkle each with a mixture of 1/2 pound finely powdered sugar, 1 loth powdered cinnamon, 1 whole grated nutmeg, and 1 knife point cardamom, and rub in well
11. Layer them all on top of each other, let sit for a quarter hour
12. Brush again, but only very lightly, with the above-described mixture
13. Place on a black sheet with paper on it and dry in a cooled oven
14. The slices can be served for dessert or with cold punch

title: Zimmtstern (Cinnamon Stars)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1 pound unpeeled almonds
- 6 egg whites
- 1 pound finely sifted sugar
- 1 loth cinnamon

## Directions

1. Clean the almonds with a cloth, grind them finely and pass through a fine sieve, grinding again what remains
2. Beat the egg whites into snow
3. Mix the almonds and snow together with the finely sifted sugar and cinnamon
4. Roll out the dough to the thickness of a knife back and cut out stars with a medium-sized star mold
5. Brush a black baking sheet with bacon fat, place the stars on it not too close together
6. Bake in gentle heat until yellow
7. Can be served with lemon ice or other ice, or placed on wafer cookies if desired

title: Zimmtwaffeln (Cinnamon Waffles)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 4 loth finely pounded almonds with skin
- 4 loth fine flour
- 1/2 loth finely pounded cinnamon
- bacon

## Directions

1. Stir 1/4 pound butter to foam.
2. Stir together 2 eggs, 1/4 pound sugar, and 4 loth finely pounded almonds with skin, and 4 loth fine flour and 1/2 loth finely pounded cinnamon.
3. Put portions the size of a walnut in the middle of a hot waffle iron coated inside with bacon and bake on both sides.
4. These waffles can be kept for 2 to 3 weeks.

title: Zimtküchlein (Cinnamon Cakes)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 2 egg whites
- sugar
- 1 loth cinnamon

## Directions

1. Beat the egg whites into a stiff snow
2. Stir sugar and cinnamon underneath
3. Brush a black baking sheet with wax
4. Place spoonfuls of the mixture the size of a hazelnut on the sheet
5. Bake in a cooled oven

title: Zitronenspeise (Lemon Dessert)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- 4 eggs
- 200 g sugar
- 6 tablespoons lemon juice
- 1 tablespoon lemon sugar
- 3 tablets white gelatin
- 2 tablespoons water

## Directions

1. Beat the egg yolks with the sugar for 1/2 hour until foamy, gradually combine with lemon juice and lemon sugar, finally add the prepared gelatin and carefully fold in the snow of the egg whites. Fill into a glass bowl.

title: Zitronenzucker (Lemon Sugar)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- lemon zest
- lump sugar

## Directions

1. Rub the yellow of the lemon peel onto lump sugar.
2. Pound the sugar in a mortar.

title: Zucker zu Läutern (Clarified Sugar)
author: Hedwig Heyl
body:
## Ingredients

- sugar
- water

## Directions

1. Dip the sugar in water until no more bubbles rise.
2. Place the pieces thoroughly soaked with water in a clean pot or kettle.
3. Heat gently until the sugar is completely melted.
4. Only then bring the mixture to a boil; if done earlier, the sugar will crystallize.
5. Bring the sugar to a boil, skim, and use further.

title: Zucker zu läutern (Purifying Sugar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- half a pound sugar
- half a schoppen water
- one egg white

## Directions

1. Take half a pound sugar in a brass pan and break it into 3 to 4 pieces
2. Add half a schoppen water and let it boil until it throws bubbles
3. Beat one egg white to snow, add it under the sugar, and add a spoonful of water if the sugar wants to rise
4. When the sugar is quite clear, wet a cloth that is not very tight, pour the sugar into it and let it run through
5. Then preserve it

title: Zuckerbretzeln (Sugar Pretzels)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- 1/4 pound butter
- 1/2 pound flour
- 1/4 pound sugar
- 1 egg
- 1 spoonful thick sour cream

## Directions

1. Mix butter, flour, sugar, egg and thick sour cream into a dough
2. Make small pretzels from the dough
3. Place on a black baking sheet
4. Brush with egg
5. Sprinkle with finely pounded almonds and some sugar
6. Bake

title: Zuckerstrauben (Sugar Strauben)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Quarter pound flour
- 6 lots sugar
- 3 spoons wine
- 3 spoons rose water
- Egg white

## Directions

1. Stir together quarter pound flour, 6 lots sugar, 3 spoons wine and 3 spoons rose water with egg white
2. Make the dough somewhat thinner than usual strauben dough
3. Let hot lard run through the strauben funnel
4. Bake the strauben through the funnel in hot lard
5. While still hot, press them over a corrugated rolling pin
6. Sprinkle with sugar and cinnamon

title: Zwetschgen in Essig (Plums in Vinegar)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- fresh plums
- coarsely crushed cinnamon
- cloves
- vinegar
- sugar

## Directions

1. Place fresh plums in a sugar glass so that all stems point upward
2. Add coarsely crushed cinnamon and cloves
3. Fill the glass with plums
4. Cook as much vinegar as needed with sugar (for half a Maas of vinegar, 12 Loth of sugar are required)
5. When the vinegar is still lukewarm, pour it over the plums
6. Let them stand overnight
7. The next day, pour off the vinegar, cook it again in the pan, and repeat this a third time
8. Pull a paper through cherry schnapps, lay it on the plums
9. Bind the glass and store it in a dry place

title: Zwetschgenkuchen (Plum Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- butter dough
- plums
- 3 loths of ground sugar
- 2 knife points of cinnamon
- 2 loths of butter

## Directions

1. Line a baking sheet with butter dough
2. Pit the plums
3. Cover the cake thickly with plums, arranged so that the cut side faces upward
4. Sprinkle with 3 loths of ground sugar and 2 knife points of cinnamon
5. Cut 2 loths of butter into small pieces over the top
6. Bake the cake

title: Zwetschgenkuchen mit Hefenteig (Plum Cake with Yeast Dough)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- yeast dough (as for steam noodles)
- plums, pitted
- 3 eggs
- half a schoppen of sour cream
- one handful of ground sugar
- one handful of grated bread

## Directions

1. Make yeast dough as for steam noodles
2. Roll it out half a finger thickness
3. Place in a baking sheet
4. Arrange the pitted plums thickly on the dough
5. Stir together 3 eggs, half a schoppen of sour cream, one handful of ground sugar and one handful of grated bread
6. Pour this mixture over the plums
7. Bake the cake in the oven

title: Zwetschgenlatwerge (Plum Paste)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- beautiful ripe plums
- sugar
- boiling water

## Directions

1. Pour boiling water over beautiful ripe plums and peel off the skin
2. Let them steam in a covered casserole for a quarter hour
3. Force them through a hair sieve (for every pound of pulp, add the same amount of sugar)
4. Cook the sugar in a brass pan until it spins threads
5. Cook the pulp with it until everything is thick, then let it cool
6. Store it in a wooden box

title: Zwiebelsauce (Onion Sauce)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- Two large handfuls of shallot onions
- A piece of butter
- Some sugar
- Three finger-widths of flour
- Vinegar
- Meat broth
- Pepper

## Directions

1. Steam the shallot onions in a small casserole with a piece of butter and some sugar until they turn yellow
2. Sprinkle three finger-widths of flour over the onions
3. Add vinegar, meat broth, and pepper
4. Cook everything together until time to serve

title: Zwiebelsauce anderer Art (Onion Sauce Another Way)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- A piece of lard
- About 1 Loth of sugar
- One handful of finely cut onions
- Two tablespoons of flour
- One ladle-full of meat broth
- Good vinegar
- Juice and yolk of half a lemon
- Pepper
- Cloves
- Bay leaf

## Directions

1. Heat a piece of lard in a casserole until very hot
2. Add about 1 Loth of sugar and let it turn dark brown over the heat
3. Throw in one handful of finely cut onions and brown them very dark
4. Stir in two tablespoons of flour, one ladle-full of meat broth, and some good vinegar
5. Press everything through a hair sieve
6. Add the juice and yolk of half a lemon to the strained mixture
7. Season with pepper, cloves, and a bay leaf

title: Züricher Kuchen (Zurich Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- a strong handful of fine flour
- a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg
- one schoppen of milk
- 5 egg yolks and one whole egg
- one loth of sugar
- the yellow of one lemon
- 2 coffee cups of sweet cream (if needed to thin)
- one quarter pound of confectionery (e.g. one quarter pound of macaroons)
- 2 loths of citron and orange peel
- 5 egg whites

## Directions

1. Stir together a strong handful of fine flour and a piece of butter the size of a hen's egg with one schoppen of milk
2. Place on the fire and continue stirring until it becomes a thick paste
3. Let it cool
4. Stir together 5 egg yolks and one whole egg with one loth of sugar and the yellow of one lemon
5. If too thick, thin with 2 coffee cups of sweet cream
6. Cut one quarter pound of confectionery (such as macaroons) and stir in along with 2 loths of citron and orange peel into the mass
7. Grease a springform pan with butter
8. Beat 5 egg whites into stiff peaks and fold into the mass
9. Immediately bake in the oven until beautifully golden yellow

title: Züricher Kuchen (Zurich Cake)
author: Viktorine Schiller
body:
## Ingredients

- one strong handful fine flour
- one piece butter size of a hen's egg
- one schoppen milk
- 5 egg yolks
- one whole egg
- one loth sugar
- yellow of one lemon
- 2 coffee cups sweet cream (optional)
- quarter pound confections such as macarons
- 2 loth citron and orange peel
- 5 egg whites snow

## Directions

1. Stir a strong handful fine flour and one piece butter in the size of a hen's egg with one schoppen milk well
2. Place it on the fire and continue stirring until it is a thick porridge
3. Let it cool
4. Stir 5 egg yolks and one whole egg with one loth sugar and the yellow of one lemon into it
5. If too thick, thin with 2 coffee cups sweet cream
6. Cut a quarter pound confections (such as macarons)
7. Stir 2 loth citron and orange peel under the mass
8. Coat a springform with butter
9. Stir a stiff snow from 5 egg whites under the mass
10. Put it in the oven immediately
